blocks:
Ayah

الٓمٓ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Mim. (1)

Key Points

Regarding the disconnected letters (al-Huroof al-Muqatta'ah), various opinions have been stated, including:

  1. The Qur'an, the divine miracle, is composed of these very letters of the alphabet, which are available to everyone. If you can, you should also produce miraculous speech using these letters.

  2. These letters are the name of the Surah in which they appear at the beginning.

  3. These letters refer to the Greatest Name of Allah (Ism al-A'dham).(98)

  4. These letters constitute a type of oath or swearing by Allah.(99)

  5. These letters are among the secrets between Allah and the Prophet.(100) And according to some narrations, they are codes that no one knows except Allah.(101)

However, perhaps the best view is the first one. This is because among the one hundred and fourteen Surahs of the Qur'an, 29 of which begin with the disconnected letters, in twenty-four instances, the discussion immediately following these letters concerns the Qur'an and its miraculous nature. Just as in this Surah, following "الم" (Alif, Lam, Mim), "ذلك الكتاب" (That is the Book) appears, referring to the greatness of the Qur'an.

At the beginning of Surah Ash-Shura, the disconnected letters "حم عسق" (Ha, Mim, 'Ayn, Sin, Qaf) appear, after which He says: "كذلك یُوحى الیك والى الّذین مِن قبلك اللّه العزیزُ الحكیم" (Thus does Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise, inspire [revelation] to you and to those before you). This means that Allah's revelation also utilizes these very letters—letters that are accessible to all human beings. However, Allah has revealed a Book using these letters that is a miracle. Can man also produce such a Book?!

Yes, Allah reveals a miraculous Book from the letters of the alphabet, just as He creates hundreds of types of fruits, flowers, and plants, and creates man from the heart of the earth. But the limit of human artistry is to make bricks and tiles from earth and clay!

(98) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

(99) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 88, p. 7.

(100) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 89, p. 384.

(101) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Baqarah

This Surah, which has two hundred and eighty-six verses, was revealed in Medina and is the largest Surah in the Qur'an. The reason for naming it Al-Baqarah (The Cow) is the very beautiful story of the Cow of the Children of Israel, which is narrated in verses 67 to 73. Due to its length, the content of the Surah encompasses various theological, jurisprudential (fiqhi), and devotional topics, such as Monotheism (Tawhid), cosmology, the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and life after death, as well as the legislation of many devotional, ethical, individual, and social laws. It also includes issues related to revelation, the miraculous nature of the Divine Book, and social services such as giving, spending (infaq), benevolence (ihsan), war and struggle (jihad) against enemies, and the law of retribution (Qisas). This Surah contains the Verse of the Throne (Ayat al-Kursi) and the Verse of Debt (Ayat al-Dayn), which is the longest verse in the Qur'an, the incident of the change of the Qibla, the excuses made by the Children of Israel, the creation of Adam and the disobedience of Satan, and excerpts from the history of several Prophets such as Adam, Abraham, and Moses علیهم السلام, and the stories of Harut and Marut, and Talut and Jalut (Saul and Goliath). Since the two Surahs, Al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran, contain many jurisprudential rulings and luminous Divine Names and Attributes, they are referred to as "Az-Zahrawan," meaning the two very brilliant Surahs. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله was asked which Surahs of the Qur'an were superior to all others. He replied: Surah Al-Baqarah. And they asked which verse was superior? He replied: Ayat al-Kursi.(97)

(97) Tafasir Noor al-Thaqalayn and Majma' al-Bayan.

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ (۲)
This is the Book, there is no doubt in it, a guidance to the Godwary, (2)

Key Points

«لاریب فیه» means there is no doubt that the Quran is from Allah. This is because its contents are such that they leave no room for this doubt and uncertainty. If any doubt exists, it is due to the suspicion and obstinacy of individuals. As the Quran states: «فهم فى رَیبهم یَتردّدون»(۱۰۲) They are bewildered in the doubt that they themselves create.

The goal of the Quran is the guidance of people, and if it refers to issues such as the creation of the heavens and the earth, plants, animals, and so on, it is so that people's attention to them leads to attention to the knowledge, power, and wisdom of Allah.(۱۰۳)

The Quran is a means of guidance for all people; «هدىً للنّاس»(۱۰۴). It shines upon everyone like the sun, but only those benefit from it who possess a pure nature and are submissive before the truth; «هدىً للمتّقین». Just as sunlight only passes through clean glass, not through brick and mud. Therefore, the wicked (۱۰۵), the wrongdoers (۱۰۶), the disbelievers (۱۰۷), the dead-hearted (۱۰۸), the extravagant, and the deniers (۱۰۹) do not benefit from the guidance of the Quran.

Question

This Ayah says about the Quran and its contents: «لاریب فیه» (there is no doubt in it). While the Quran itself expresses the doubt and uncertainty of opponents regarding this: In one place it says: «انّنا لفى شكّ ممّا تَدعونا الیه مُریب»(۱۱۰) (We are in doubt concerning that to which you invite us). And regarding doubt in revelation and prophethood, we read: «بل هم فى شك من ذكرى»(۱۱۱). Regarding the Day of Judgment, it also states: «لنعلم من یؤمن بالاخرة ممن هو منها فى شك»(۱۱۲). Therefore, how can it say: «لاریب فیه» (there is no doubt in the Quran and its content)?

Answer

The meaning of the phrase «لاریب فیه» is not that no one has doubted it or will doubt it, but rather the intention is that the truthfulness of the Quran is so firm that there is no room for doubt, and if someone doubts, it is due to his own blindness of heart. As He says in Ayah 66 of Surah An-Naml: «بل هم فى شك بل هم منها عمون»(۱۱۳).

۱۰۲) Tawbah, 45.

۱۰۳) The Quran refers to natural, cosmic, historical, philosophical, political, and industrial subjects, but the main goal is guidance.

۱۰۴) Al-Baqarah, 185.

۱۰۵) «لایهدى القوم الفاسقین» Tawbah, 80.

۱۰۶) «لایهدى القوم الظالمین» Al-Ma'idah, 51.

۱۰۷) «لایهدى القوم الكافرین» Al-Ma'idah, 67.

۱۰۸) «لایهدى من هو كاذب كفار» Az-Zumar, 3.

۱۰۹) «لایهدى مَن هو مسرف كذّاب» Ghafir, 28.

۱۱۰) Hud, 62.

۱۱۱) Sad, 8.

۱۱۲) Saba, 21.

۱۱۳) Nukhbat al-Tafasir, quoting Ayatollah Jawadi.

Messages

1- The Quran holds a very high status in greatness. In Arabic literature, «ذلك» (dhālika) is a demonstrative pronoun for the distant. Here, it refers to the Quran, which is before us, using «ذلك», which indicates the unattainable greatness of the Quran.

2- A guide must be decisive and firm in his method of invitation and the content of his program. The phrase «لاریب فیه» indicates the firmness and strength of the Quran.

3- The Quran's ability to guide the pious is itself the best proof of its precision and truthfulness. «هدى للمتقین»

4- Only pure and pious individuals benefit from the guidance of the Quran. «هدى للمتقین» The purer the vessel of one's heart, the greater his benefit and reception of light.(۱۱۴)

۱۱۴) Guidance has stages and is subject to increase and decrease. «والذین اهتدوا زادهم هدى»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ (۳)
who believe in the Unseen, maintain the prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them; (3)

Key Points

The Quran divides existence into two parts: the unseen world (Ghayb) (115) and the manifest world (Shahadah). The pious (Muttaqin) believe in the entirety of existence, but others only accept what is tangible to them. They even expect to see God with their eyes, and because they do not see Him, they do not believe in Him. Just as some said to Prophet Moses: «لن نؤمن لك حتّى نَرى اللّه جهرة» (116) We will never believe you until we see Allah manifestly.

Such individuals also say regarding the Day of Judgment: «ما هى الاّ حیاتنا الدنیا نموت و نحیا و ما یهلكنا الاّ الدّهر» (117) There is no other world except this worldly life in which we live; we die and we live, and only time destroys us.

Such individuals have not yet moved beyond the realm of animals; they limit the path of knowledge to the tangible and seek to perceive everything through the senses.

The pious (Muttaqin) believe in the unseen world (Ghayb), which is superior to knowledge and transcends it. Within faith (Iman) are hidden love, affection, reverence, sanctification, and connection, whereas these matters are absent in mere knowledge.

115) Ghayb (the unseen) is applied to Allah the Almighty, the angels, the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام.

116) Al-Baqarah, 55.

117) Al-Jathiyah, 24.

Messages

1- Faith is not separate from action. Alongside belief in the unseen (Ghayb), the practical duties and obligations of the believer are stated. «یؤمنون... یقیمون... ینفقون»

2- The most fundamental principle in the divine worldview is that existence is not limited to the tangible. «یؤمنون بالغیب»

3- After the principle of faith, establishing prayer (Salat) and spending (Infaq) are among the most important actions. «یؤمنون... یقیمون... ینفقون» (In a divine society that moves and journeys toward Allah, spiritual and psychological anxieties, disorders, and moral deficiencies are strengthened and treated through prayer, and economic voids and the resulting instability are filled and resolved through spending.)

4- The performance of prayer must be continuous, not seasonal or temporary. «یقیمون الصّلاة» (The imperfect tense verb [present/future] indicates continuity and permanence.)

5- In spending (Infaq), we must also be moderate. (118) «ممّا رزقناهم»

6- We should spend on others from everything Allah has bestowed (knowledge, reputation, wealth, skill, etc.). «ممّا رزقناهم ینفقون» (119) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: They disseminate in society what We have taught them. (120)

7- Spending must be from lawful wealth (Halal), because Allah determines the provision (Rizq) (121) of every person from what is lawful. «رزقناهم»

8- If we believe that what we possess is from Allah, we will not become arrogant when spending. We will be better able to spend a portion of it. «ممّا رزقناهم»

118) «ممّا» is (مِن ما), and one of the meanings of «مِن» is 'some' or 'a part'. Meaning, they spend a part of what We have provided—not all of it.

119) In such cases, the word «ما» means 'everything' or 'whatever'.

120) Bihar, Vol. 2, p. 17.

121) «Rizq» (provision) refers to the continuous blessing given for the continuation of life according to need. The condition of continuity and being commensurate with need distinguishes it from the concepts of Ihsan (benevolence), I'ta' (giving), Nasib (share), In'am (favor), and Hazz (portion). Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran, Vol. 4, p. 114.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ وَبِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ (٤)
and who believe in what has been sent down to you and what was sent down before you, and are certain of the Hereafter. (4)

Key Points

The tools of human knowledge are not limited to sense and intellect; rather, revelation (Wahy) is also one of the paths to knowledge, which the pious (Muttaqin) believe in. Man, in choosing a path without a guide, falls into confusion and bewilderment. The prophets must take his hand and guide him toward true happiness through their logic, miracles, and practical conduct (Sirah). It is understood from this verse and the two preceding verses that humility before Allah the Almighty (prayer/Salat), possessing the spirit of sacrifice (Ithar), spending (Infaq), cooperation (Ta'awun), preserving the rights of others, and hoping for a bright future and great divine rewards, are among the effects of piety (Taqwa).

Messages

1- Belief in all prophets and heavenly books is necessary, because they all pursue a single goal. «یؤمنون... بما اُنزل من قبلك»

2- True piety does not manifest without certainty in the Hereafter. «بالاخرةهم یوقنون»

3- The respect for the Quran precedes that of other books. «...بما انزل الیك وما انزل من قبلك...»

4- The Prophet of Islam is the last divine Prophet. The word «من قبلك» (from before you) without mentioning "from after you" is a sign of the finality (Khatamiyyah) of the Prophet of Islam and the Quran.

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَلَىٰ هُدًى مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (٥)
Those follow their Lord’s guidance and it is they who are the felicitous. (5)

Key Points

The reward for the people of Taqwa (piety)—who believe in the Unseen, are devoted to prayer (Salat), charity (Infaq), and certainty in the Hereafter—is success (Rastgari) and prosperity (Falah). Success is the highest peak of felicity. This is because Allah created existence for mankind (122), and mankind for worship (123), and worship for achieving Taqwa (124), and Taqwa for achieving Falah and success (125). In the Quran, the successful possess characteristics, including: A: They engage in reform against the corruption of society (126). B: They enjoin good and forbid evil (127). C: In addition to believing in the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, they support Him (128). D: They are people of self-sacrifice (Ithar) (129). E: On the Day of Judgment, they will have a heavy balance of good deeds (Hasanat) (130). Success is not achieved without effort; it requires conditions and prerequisites. Among these, the following are mentioned in the Quran: * Purification (Tazkiyah) is necessary for Falah and success. «قد افلح من زكّاها» (131) * Striving (Jihad) is necessary for Falah and success. «جاهدوا فى سبیله لعلكم تفلحون» (132) * Humility (Khushu) in prayer, turning away from vain talk (Laghw), paying Zakat (alms), chastity, modesty, trustworthiness, fulfilling covenants, and steadfastness in prayer are necessary for Falah and success.

122) خلق لكم ما فى الارض جمیعاً» Baqarah, 29. 123) و ما خلقت الجن و الانس الا لیعبدون» Dhariyat, 56. 124) اعبدوا ربّكم... لعلكم تتقون» Baqarah, 21. 125) واتقوا اللَّه لعلكم تفلحون» Ma'idah, 100. 126) Aal-i-Imran, 104. 127) Aal-i-Imran, 104. 128) A'raf, 157. 129) Hashr, 9. 130) A'raf, 8. 131) Shams, 9. 132) Ma'idah, 35.

Messages

1- Special Divine guidance is guaranteed for true believers. «هدًى من ربّهم»

2- Faith (Iman) and Taqwa lead man to Falah (prosperity) and success. «للمتقین، یؤمنون، المفلحون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَأَنذَرْتَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تُنذِرْهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (٦)
As for the faithless, it is the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will not have faith. (6)

Key Points

"Kufr" (كفر) means covering or ignoring. The farmer and the night are called kāfir. This is because the farmer covers the seed and kernel under the soil, and the night envelops the space. Ingratitude for blessings (kufrān-i ni'mat) also means ignoring that blessing. A person who denies religion is called a kāfir because he conceals or ignores the divine truths and signs (āyāt).

After describing the righteous (muttaqīn), the Quran introduces a group of disbelievers (kuffār). These are those who are so stubborn in their misguidance and concealment of the truth that they are unwilling to accept the divine signs. Their spoken and unspoken response to the invitation of the prophets was: «سواء علینا أوَعَظتَ ام لم تكن من الواعظین»(133) Your preaching and advice have no effect on us; it makes no difference whether you preach or are not among the preachers.

If the ground is not fertile and suitable, the invitation of the prophets will also not be effective.

The rain, whose nature is pure gentleness, Grows tulips in the garden and weeds in the barren salt marsh.

(133) Ash-Shu'arā, 136.

Messages

1- Obstinacy, enmity, and ignorant fanaticism turn a person into something like an inanimate object. «سواء علیهم»

2- The method of propagation for the disbelievers (kuffār) is warning (indhār). If warning and cautioning do not affect a person, then glad tidings and promises will also not be effective. «سواء علیهم ءانذرتهم ام لم تُنذرهم»

3- Do not expect all people to believe (134). «...لا یؤمنون»

(134) In Ayah 103 of Sūrat Yūsuf, He says: «وما اكثر النّاس ولو حَرصتَ بمؤمنین» Even if you are eager and desirous, most people will not believe.

Ayah

خَتَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَعَلَىٰ سَمْعِهِمْ ۖ وَعَلَىٰٓ أَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ غِشَـٰوَةٌ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۷)
Allah has set a seal on their hearts and their hearing, and there is a blindfold on their sight, and there is a great punishment for them. (7)

Key Points

The seal of wretchedness that Allah places upon the hearts of the disbelievers is the punishment for their obstinacy. As we read: «یطبع اللَّه على كلّ قلب متكبّر جبّار»(135) Allah seals the heart of every arrogant and tyrannical person. And in Ayah 23 of Surah Al-Jathiyah, we also read: Allah seals the hearts of those who knowingly and consciously pursue their desires (or follow their whims). Therefore, the Divine seal is the result of man's own bad choice, and not a coercive or compulsory act from God.

The meaning of qalb (heart) in the Quran is the soul and the center of perception/understanding. The Quran introduces three types of hearts: 1. Qalb Saleem (Sound Heart), 2. Qalb Munīb (Repentant Heart), 3. Qalb Marīdh (Sick Heart).

Characteristics of the Sound Heart (Qalb Saleem) A: A heart in which there is none but Allah. «لیس فیه احد سواه»(136) B: A heart that follows the true guide, repents from sin, and submits to the Truth.(137) C: A heart that is safe from the love of the world.(138) D: A heart that finds tranquility through the remembrance of God.(139) E: A heart that is humble before Allah.(140)

Of course, the heart of the believer both finds peace through the remembrance of Allah and fears His wrath. «اذا ذُكر اللّه وَجِلَت قلوبهم»(141) Just like a child who is both comforted by their parents and holds them in awe.

Characteristics of the Sick Heart (Qalb Marīdh) A: A heart that is heedless of God and is unfit for leadership. «لاتطع من اغفلنا قلبه»(142) B: A heart that seeks discord and pretexts. «فامّا الّذین فى قلوبهم زیغ فیتّبعون ما تشابه منه ابتغاء الفتنة»(143) C: A heart that possesses hardness (callousness). «جعلنا قلوبهم قاسیة»(144) D: A heart that has been rusted (or stained). «بل ران على قلوبهم ما كانوا یكسبون»(145) E: A heart that has been sealed. «طبع اللّه علیها بكفرهم»(146)

Characteristic of the Repentant Heart (Qalb Munīb) The Qalb Munīb is one that, after realizing deviation and transgression, repents (tawbah) and turns back (inābah) to God. Its prominent characteristic is the change in a person's behavior and speech.

In the Quranic verses, Allah has stated nine characteristics for the hearts of the disbelievers: A: Denial of truths. «قُلوبُهم مُنكِرة»(147) B: Fanaticism/Bigotry. «فى قلوبهم الحَمیّة»(148) C: Deviation and misguidance. «صَرفَ اللّه قُلوبهم»(149) D: Hardness and cruelty. «فَویلٌ لِلقاسیَة قلوبهم»(150) E: Death. «لاتَسمعُ المَوتى»(151) F: Contamination and rust. «بَل رانَ على قلوبهم»(152) G: Sickness. «فى قلوبهم مَرض»(153) H: Constriction/Narrowness. «یجعلْ صَدره ضَیّقاً»(154) I: Sealing. «طَبع اللّه علیها بكفرهم»(155)

The human heart is changeable and susceptible to influence. Therefore, believers pray thus: «ربّنا لاتزغ قلوبنا بعد اذ هدیتنا»(156) O God! Do not let our hearts deviate after You have guided us. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Say this phrase (Ayah) often, and do not consider yourselves safe and secure from deviation.(157)

135) Ghafir, 35. 136) Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 4, p. 57. 137) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 214. 138) Tafsir Safi. 139) Al-Fath, 4. 140) Al-Hadid, 16. 141) Al-Anfal, 2. 142) Al-Kahf, 28. 143) Al ‘Imran, 7. 144) Al-Ma’idah, 13. 145) Al-Mutaffifin, 14. 146) An-Nisa, 155. 147) An-Nahl, 22. 148) Al-Fath, 26. 149) At-Tawbah, 127. 150) Az-Zumar, 22. 151) Ar-Rum, 52. 152) Al-Mutaffifin, 14. 153) Al-Baqarah, 10. 154) Al-An’am, 125. 155) An-Nisa, 155. 156) Al ‘Imran, 8. 157) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Failure to perceive the truth is perhaps the highest of Divine punishments. «ختم اللَّه على قلوبهم»

2- Disbelief (kufr) and atheism (ilhād) are the causes for the sealing of hearts and ears. «الذین كفروا... ختم اللَّه»

3- As a result of insistence upon disbelief, the fundamental privileges of man (the perception of truths and realities) are withdrawn. «الّذین كفروا... ختم اللَّه»

4- Divine punishment is commensurate with our actions. «الّذین كفروا... ختم اللَّه»

Yes, the recompense for one who understood the truth but covered it up is that God also covers his eyes, ears, soul, and intellect. In reality, man himself prepares the cause of his own wretchedness. As Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The sealing is the consequence of their disbelief.(158)

158) Tafasir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Kanz al-Daqa’iq.

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَبِٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَمَا هُم بِمُؤْمِنِينَ (۸)
Among the people are those who say, ‘We have faith in Allah and the Last Day,’ but they have no faith. (8)

Key Points

At the beginning of this Surah, four verses are dedicated to introducing the believers and two verses to identifying the disbelievers. From verse 8 to 20, it introduces a third group: the hypocrites (munāfiq). They possess neither the faith of the first group nor the courage and boldness of the second group in expressing disbelief. The hypocrite is like a field mouse that makes two escape routes for its burrow; it leaves one open and uses it for movement, and keeps the other closed. Whenever it senses danger, it opens the closed path with its head and flees. The name of the mouse's hidden hole is "Nāfiqā'," from which the word munāfiq (hypocrite) is derived. (159) Although the meaning of nifāq (hypocrisy) in these verses refers to disbelief in the heart coupled with the outward expression of faith, in the narrations (riwāyāt), nifāq has a broader meaning: anyone whose words and actions are not consistent has a share of hypocrisy. We read in a Hadith: If we betray a trust, lie in our speech, and fail to keep our promises, we are hypocrites, even if we perform the prayer (namāz) and fast (rawza). (160) Hypocrisy (nifāq) is a type of practical and ideological lie, and ostentation (riyā’) is also a type of hypocrisy. (161)

159) Qāmūs and Mufradāt. 160) Safīnat al-Bihār, Vol. 2, p. 605. 161) Tafsīr-e Namūneh.

Messages

1- Faith is a matter of the heart and does not depend on human declarations. «ماهم بمؤمنین»

2- The foundation of faith is belief in the Origin (Allah) and the Return (the Hereafter). «آمنا باللَّه و بالیوم الآخر»

3- Allah is aware of the inner state of man. «وما هم بمؤمنین»

Ayah

يُخَـٰدِعُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَمَا يَخْدَعُونَ إِلَّآ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۹)
They seek to deceive Allah and those who have faith, yet they deceive no one but themselves, but they are not aware. (9)

Key Points

The word "شعور" (consciousness/perception) originates from "شَعر" (hair). A person who possesses precise and meticulous understanding is considered to have perception and consciousness. Therefore, the hypocrite imagines that he is deceiving others because he lacks proper understanding. «لایشعرون» (they perceive not).

The intent behind the deceit and cunning of the hypocrites towards Allah is either their trickery and deception regarding the laws of Allah and the Divine religion, which they mock and treat as a game, or it means deception directed towards the Prophet of Allah. That is, just as obedience and allegiance to the Messenger of Allah is obedience and allegiance to Allah (162), and deceit towards the Messenger of Allah is equivalent to deceit towards Allah, it is clear that this kind of trickery and deception involving religion is deceit and trickery towards oneself.

Just as if a doctor prescribes medication and the patient falsely claims to have taken it, he thinks he has deceived the doctor, but in reality, he has deceived himself, for deceiving the doctor is deceiving oneself.

Islam's treatment of the hypocrite is similar to the hypocrite's treatment of Islam. He outwardly embraces Islam, and Islam outwardly considers him a Muslim. He lacks faith internally and is a disbeliever (Kafir), and Allah will resurrect him with the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment.

In a narration from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, we read: Hypocrisy (Riya') is deceit towards Allah. (163)

The Quran considers the reflection of a person's good and bad deeds to be for himself. As it states in this verse: Deceit involving religion is deceit towards oneself, not Allah. And elsewhere it states: «اِن أحسنتم أحسنتم لانفسكم و اِن أسأتم فلها» (164) If you do good, you do good to yourselves; and if you do evil, it is for yourselves. Or elsewhere it states: «ولا یَحیق المَكر السیّى ء الاّ بَاهلِه» (165) Evil plotting encompasses none but its people.

162) «من یطع الرّسول فقد اطاع اللّه» Whoever obeys the Messenger has certainly obeyed Allah. An-Nisa, 80. «انّ الّذین یُبایعونك انّما یبایعون اللّه» Indeed, those who give allegiance to you are only giving allegiance to Allah. Al-Fath, 1. 163) Tafsir Noor Ath-Thaqalayn. 164) Al-Isra, 7. 165) Fatir, 43.

Messages

1- Trickery is a sign of hypocrisy. «یخادعون اللّه»

2- The hypocrite is always thinking of inflicting harm. «یخادعون» (The word khid'ah (deceit) means concealing one matter and manifesting another, with the aim of inflicting harm.) (166)

3- The effects of trickery revert to the trickster himself. «وما یَخدعون الاّ انفسهم»

4- The hypocrite lacks perception and does not understand that his opponent is Allah, Who knows all his inner secrets (167) and Who will expose his deeds on the Day of Judgment (168). «وما یشعرون» (169)

5- Deceit and trickery are not signs of intellect or perception. «یخادعون... لایشعرون» We read in narrations: True intellect is that by which a person worships Allah.

166) Tafsir Rahnama. 167) «یعلم خائنة الاعین و ماتخفى الصدور» He knows the deceit of the eyes and what the breasts conceal. Ghafir, 19. 168) «یوم تبلى السرائر» The Day when secrets will be tested. At-Tariq, 9. 169) The phrase «و ما یشعرون» can be interpreted in two ways: first, that they do not perceive that Allah knows their secrets; and second, that they do not perceive that they are truly harming themselves.

Ayah

فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ فَزَادَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مَرَضًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْذِبُونَ (۱٠)
There is a sickness in their hearts; then Allah increased their sickness, and there is a painful punishment for them because of the lies they used to tell. (10)

Key Points

Illness (or sickness) is sometimes related to the body, such as Ayah 185 of Surah Al-Baqarah: «ومن كان مریضاً» (And whoever is ill), which concerns the rulings on fasting for the sick. And sometimes it is related to the spirit (or soul), such as this Ayah: «فى قلوبهم مرض» (In their hearts is a sickness), which concerns the sickness of hypocrisy (nifaq). The story of the hypocrite is like a foul-smelling carcass or carrion that has fallen into a water reservoir. The more water that enters it, the greater its corruption becomes, and its unpleasant odor and contamination increase. Hypocrisy is like carrion; if it remains in the spirit and heart of a person, then every Ayah and ruling that is revealed by Allah, instead of submitting to it, the person resorts to pretense and hypocrisy (riya'), adding one step to his hypocrisy. This sick spirit makes all his thoughts and actions hypocritical and pretentious, and this is a type of increase in sickness. «فزادهم اللَّه مرضاً» (So Allah increased them in sickness). Perhaps the phrase «فزادهم اللَّه مرضاً» is a curse, similar to «قاتلهم اللَّه» (May Allah destroy them), meaning: now that they have sickness in their hearts, may Allah increase their sickness.

Messages

1- Hypocrisy is a spiritual sickness, and the hypocrite is sick. Just as a sick person is neither healthy nor dead, the hypocrite is neither a believer (Mu'min) nor a disbeliever (Kafir). «فى قلوبهم مرض»

2- The essence of man is his heart and spirit. «زادهم اللَّه» (The correct grammatical form would have been to say «فزادها اللَّه مرضاً», meaning there was sickness in their hearts, so Allah increased its sickness. But He said that He increased their sickness. Therefore, the human heart is equivalent to the entire human being, because if the spirit and heart deviate, its effects are manifest in speech and action.)(170)

3- Hypocrisy is a sickness that has cancerous growth.(171) «زادهم اللَّه مرضاً»

4- Man himself creates the grounds for honor and downfall within himself. «لهم عذاب الیم بما كانوا یكذبون»

5- Lying is one of the common methods of the hypocrites. «كانوا یَكذبون»

(170) Tafsir Rahnama.

(171) In the Quran, we read verses in which praiseworthy attributes such as knowledge, guidance, and faith are introduced as being capable of increase. Examples include: «زدنى علماً» (Taha, 114), «زادتهم ایمانا» (Al-Anfal, 2), and «زادهم هدى» (Muhammad, 17). Likewise, some sicknesses and undesirable attributes such as defilement (rijs), aversion, fear, and loss are also considered capable of increase. Examples include: «زادتهم رجساً» (At-Tawbah, 125), «زادهم نفوراً» (Al-Furqan, 60), «مازادوكم الاّ خَبالاً» (At-Tawbah, 47), and «ولا یزید الظالمین الاّ خساراً» (Al-Isra, 82). Considering the aforementioned verses, it becomes clear that the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to grant freedom to both groups, the good and the evil. «كلاّ نُمدّ هؤلاء و هؤلاء» (Al-Isra, 20).

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَا تُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُصْلِحُونَ (۱۱)
When they are told, ‘Do not cause corruption on the earth,’ they say, ‘We are only reformers!’ (11)

Messages

1- Although the hypocrites are neither receptive to advice nor seeking counsel, it is better to speak to them and enjoin the good and forbid the evil. «قیل لهم»

2- Hypocrisy is a cause of corruption. «لا تفسدوا فى الارض»

3- The hypocrite considers his duplicity as diplomacy and reformism. «انمّا نحن مصلحون»

4- The hypocrite introduces only himself as a reformer. «انّما نحن مصلحون» (It is possible for someone to be at the highest level of spiritual sickness, yet imagine that he is healthy.)

5- By praising himself inappropriately, the hypocrite seeks to fool people and justify his wrongdoings. «انّما نحن مصلحون»

Ayah

أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَشْعُرُونَ (۱۲)
Behold! They are themselves the agents of corruption, but they are not aware. (12)

Key Points

In a general review of the verses of the Quran, we find that hypocrisy (nifaq) creates negative effects and consequences in the spirit, psyche, behavior, and actions of the hypocrite, which afflict him in this world and the Hereafter. The Quran says in describing them:

  1. They suffer from a lack of true perception/understanding. (172)

  2. They do not reflect or comprehend. (173)

  3. They become afflicted by confusion and bewilderment. (174)

  4. Because they lack correct heartfelt belief, they experience fear and anxiety (175) and a painful torment. (176)

172) لایشعرون (Baqarah, 12); هم السفهاء (Baqarah, 13).

173) لایفقهون (Tawbah, 87); لایعلمون (Baqarah, 13).

174) یعمهون (Baqarah, 15), لایبصرون (Baqarah, 17).

175) حذر الموت (Baqarah, 19).

176) ولهم عذاب الیم (Baqarah, 10)

Messages

  1. Muslims must be aware of the tricks and seemingly beautiful slogans of the hypocrites. «الا»

  2. The arrogant ambition and fanciful thinking of the hypocrite must be shattered. «انّهم هم المفسدون»

  3. Hypocrites are constantly engaged in corruption. «المفسدون»

  4. Cleverness, if not in the path of truth, is a lack of perception. «لا یشعرون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ ءَامِنُوا۟ كَمَآ ءَامَنَ ٱلنَّاسُ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنُؤْمِنُ كَمَآ ءَامَنَ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۳)
And when they are told, ‘Believe like the people who have believed,’ they say, ‘Shall we believe like the fools who have believed?’ Behold! They are themselves the fools, but they do not know. (13)

Messages

1- The guidance and invitation of the friends of Allah are ineffective on the hypocrites. «قیل...أنؤمن»

2- Hypocrites possess a spirit of seeking privilege and self-superiority. «انؤمن»

3- Belittling the believers is one of the methods of the hypocrites. «كما آمن السفهاء»

(Having faith and submitting to Allah is considered light-mindedness in the view of the hypocrites.)

4- Muslims must be vigilant so they are not deceived by appearances. «الا»

5- In the culture of the Quran, failing to submit to the truth is foolishness. «انّهم هم السفهاء»

6- The arrogant pride of the hypocrite must be broken, and they must be countered in kind. «انّهم هم السفهاء»

7- Exposing the false face of the hypocrite is necessary for the Islamic society. «هم السفهاء»

8- Worse and more painful than any pain is ignorance of that pain. «لكن لا یعلمون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا لَقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ شَيَـٰطِينِهِمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۱٤)
When they meet the faithful, they say, ‘We believe,’ but when they are alone with their devils, they say, ‘We are with you; we were only deriding [them].’ (14)

Key Points

"Shaytan" (Satan) is derived from "shatan," meaning "one who is far removed from goodness." It is applied to anyone who inspires deviation, and it is also used for wicked people who are far from the truth. [183]

183) Mufradat al-Raghib.

Messages

1- The hypocrite is opportunistic (changes his stance based on the day's benefit). «قالوا آمنا... قالوا انّا معكم»

2- One should not place absolute trust in every declaration of faith, and one must be wary of infiltrators. «قالوا آمنا»

3- The hypocrite lacks the courage of sincerity and fears the believers. «خَلَوا» The contact and communication of the hypocrites with the believers is open and public, but their contact with the disbelievers or their own leaders and chiefs takes place secretly and confidentially, in private and seclusion.

4- The friends of the hypocrites are devilish (Shaytan-like). «شیاطینهم»

5- The disbelievers and the hypocrites have organizational ties with each other, and the hypocrites receive their ideological guidance from them. «شیاطینهم»

6- The declaration of faith by the hypocrite is temporary, but his disbelief is stable and permanent. «آمنا، انّا معكم» (Faith is expressed using a verbal sentence, while disbelief is expressed using a nominal sentence, and the nominal sentence is a sign of permanence and stability.)

7- Hypocrites are not only like-minded with the disbelievers but also their helpers. «انّا معكم» (The word "ma'a" [مع] is used where there is cooperation in addition to being like-minded.)

8- Hypocrites mock the believers. «انّما نحن مستهزؤن»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ يَسْتَهْزِئُ بِهِمْ وَيَمُدُّهُمْ فِى طُغْيَـٰنِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (۱٥)
It is Allah who derides them, and leaves them bewildered in their rebellion. (15)

Key Points

The word "y'mahūn" (یَعمهون) is derived from 'amah (عَمَه), similar to 'amā (عَمى). However, 'amā refers to physical blindness, while 'amah is spiritual or inner blindness. [184] Imam Reza عليه السلام said in the interpretation of this Ayah: Allah is not characterized by deceit (makr), trickery (khid'ah), or mockery (istihzā'), but He gives the recompense for their deceit and mockery. [185] Just as He leaves them in their rebellion and transgression, so that they become bewildered and submerged (in error). And what recompense is harder than hardness of heart, the dominance of Satan and his whispers, inclination towards sin, lack of desire for worship, association and cooperation with unworthy individuals, preoccupation with the world, and heedlessness of the Truth—all of which the hypocrites suffer from. The hypocrites behave dually, and thus they are treated dually: in this world, they are subject to the rulings of Muslims, and in the Hereafter, they will face the punishment of the disbelievers (kuffār).

184) Tafsir Kashshāf. 185) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 30.

Messages

1- Divine punishments are commensurate with the sins committed. In response to "انّما نحن مستهزؤن" (Indeed, we are mockers), "اللَّه یستهزئ بهم" (Allah mocks them) is stated.

2- The hypocrites are dealing with Allah, not with the believers. (They mock the believers, but Allah comes to their defense and gives the response to their mockery Himself.) "اللَّه یستهزئ بهم"

3- Mockery is permissible if it is in the form of a response, similar to arrogance towards an arrogant person. "اللَّه یستهزئ بهم"

4- One should not be deceived by Allah's granting of respite and His abundance (of provision). "یمدّهم فى طغیانهم"

5- Rebellion and transgression are the grounds for bewilderment and confusion. "فى طغیانهم یعمهون"

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱشْتَرَوُا۟ ٱلضَّلَـٰلَةَ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ فَمَا رَبِحَت تِّجَـٰرَتُهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُهْتَدِينَ (۱٦)
They are the ones who bought error for guidance, so their trade did not profit them, nor were they guided. (16)

Key Points

The hypocrites did not possess guidance (in the first place) that they could lose it. Thus, perhaps the meaning of the Ayah is that they lost the innate foundations and factors of guidance. Just as we read in other verses: «اشتروا الكفر بالایمان» (186) a group traded faith for disbelief. Or «اشتروا الحیوة الدّنیا بالاخرة» (187) they traded the Hereafter for the worldly life. Or «والعذاب بالمغفرة» (188) they traded divine forgiveness and pardon for His wrath and punishment. Meaning, they destroyed the potential for faith and the reception of reward and forgiveness through their own actions.

Ultimately, the divine light turned to smoke, and his truth-seeking nature became like Nimrod.

186) Aal-i-Imran, 177. 187) Al-Baqarah, 86. 188) Al-Baqarah, 175.

Messages

1- The hypocrite does not recognize his own profit and loss, and thus trades guidance for error. «اشتروا الضلالة بالهدى»

2- Man is free and possesses choice. This is because trade requires will and decision. «اشتروا الضلالة...»

3- The world is like a market, and people are traders, and the subject of the trade is our actions and choices. «اشتروا... فما ربحت تجارتهم»

4- The destiny of the believer is guidance; «على هدىً من ربّهم» and the end of the hypocrite is deviation. «ما كانوا مهتدین»

5- The hypocrites do not find a path to their goals. «ما كانوا مهتدین» (And this is confirmed) by considering the subsequent verses.

Ayah

مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ ٱلَّذِى ٱسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا فَلَمَّآ أَضَآءَتْ مَا حَوْلَهُۥ ذَهَبَ ٱللَّهُ بِنُورِهِمْ وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتٍ لَّا يُبْصِرُونَ (۱۷)
Their parable is that of one who lighted a torch, and when it had lit up all around him, Allah took away their light, and left them sightless in a manifold darkness. (17)

Key Points

Example (parable/analogy) plays an effective role in making a point understandable to people. It makes abstract concepts tangible, thereby shortening and generalizing the path (to understanding), raising the degree of certainty, and silencing the obstinate. Many examples are cited in the Quran, including: the example of truth likened to water and falsehood to the foam on the water. [189] The example of truth likened to a goodly tree (shajarah tayyibah) and falsehood to an evil tree (shajarah khabithah). [190] The comparison of the deeds of the disbelievers to ashes before a strong wind. [191] Or the comparison of their deeds to a mirage. [192] The comparison of idols and tyrants (taghuts) to a spider's house. [193] The parable of a scholar without action likened to a donkey carrying books. [194] And the parable of backbiting likened to eating the flesh of a dead brother. [195] This verse is also in the position of likening a collection of the dispositions and states of the hypocrites. They kindle a fire, but Allah takes away their light and leaves smoke, ashes, and darkness for them. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The meaning of the verse, «تركهم فى ظلمات...» (He left them in darknesses...) is that Allah abandons them to their own state. [196]

189) Ra‘d, 17. 190) Ibrahim, 26. 191) Ibrahim, 18. 192) Nur, 39. 193) ‘Ankabut, 41. 194) Jumu‘ah, 5. 195) Hujurat, 12. 196) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- To reach light, the hypocrite uses fire (nar), which also involves ashes, smoke, and burning. «استوقد ناراً»

2- The light of Islam is universal, but the light under the shade of which the hypocrites pretend to be Muslim is of a lesser radius and its brightness is unstable. «أضاءت ما حوله»

3- Islam is light and disbelief (kufr) is darkness. «ذهب اللّه بنورهم وتركهم فى ظلمات»

4- Whoever does not benefit from the single light remains in multiple darknesses. «بنورهم... فى ظلمات» (The word nur (light) is singular, and the word dhulumat (darknesses) is plural.)

5- The plans and conspiracies of the hypocrites remain incomplete by Divine Will. «ذهب اللّه بنورهم»

6- The opponent of the hypocrites is Allah. «ذهب اللّه بنورهم»

7- The outcome and future of the hypocrites are dark. «فى ظلمات»

8- Hypocrites suffer from fear and anxiety, and they are confused in long-term decision-making. «فى ظلمات لا یبصرون»

9- Sometimes, faith is genuine at the beginning, but gradually a person inclines toward deviation and becomes a hypocrite. (The word «نورهم» (their light) in this verse and the phrase «لایرجعون» (they will not return) in the next verse indicate that they had a light, but they did not return to that light.)

Ayah

صُمٌّۢ بُكْمٌ عُمْىٌ فَهُمْ لَا يَرْجِعُونَ (۱۸)
Deaf, dumb, and blind, they will not come back. (18)

Key Points

The Quran praises some prophets, stating that they possess hands and eyes: «واذكر عبادنا ابراهیم واسحاق ویعقوب اولى الایدى والابصار» (197). Perhaps the intention is that only he who possesses a hand capable of smashing idols truly has a hand, and only he who possesses an eye capable of seeing God truly has an eye. Thus, hypocrites who lack such hands and eyes are essentially like those born defective, having themselves prepared the grounds for their deficiency and lost the means of recognition. Therefore, in this Surah, terms such as «لا یشعرون», «ما یشعرون», «لا یعلمون», «لا یبصرون», «یعمعون», «صم», «بكم», «عمى», and «لا یرجعون» are used regarding the hypocrites. Looking (نظر) is different from insight (بصیرت). In Surah Al-A'raf, we read: «تریهم ینظرون الیك و هم لا یبصرون» (198). You see them looking at you, yet they do not see. That is, they lack the eye of insight to see the truth. The failure to utilize the means and faculties of recognition correctly is equivalent to downfall and the loss of humanity (199). The recompense for one who pretends to be blind, deaf, and mute in this world is blindness, deafness, and muteness in the Hereafter. «ونحشرهم یوم القیامة على وجوههم عمیاً و بكماً و صماً» (200).

197) Sad, 45. 198) A'raf, 198. 199) In Surah Al-A'raf, Ayah 179, we read: »لهم قلوب لایفقهون بها و لهم اعین لایبصرون بها و لهم اذان لایسمعون بها اولئك كالانعام بل هم اضل اولئك هم الغافلون« They have hearts with which they do not understand, they have eyes with which they do not see, and they have ears with which they do not hear the truth. These people are like cattle; nay, they are even more astray and misguided. They are the heedless ones. 200) Al-Isra, 97.

Messages

  1. Hypocrisy prevents man from perceiving divine realities and knowledge. «صم بكم عمى»
  2. He who does not utilize the divine gifts in the path of truth is like one who lacks those blessings. «صم بكم عمى»
  3. The hypocrites' inability to see the truth has two reasons: one is that their external environment is dark; «فى ظلمات», and the other is that they themselves have lost the eye of the heart. «صم بكم عمى»
  4. Hypocrites possess obstinacy and prejudice. «فهم لا یرجعون»

Ayah

أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فِيهِ ظُلُمَـٰتٌ وَرَعْدٌ وَبَرْقٌ يَجْعَلُونَ أَصَـٰبِعَهُمْ فِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِم مِّنَ ٱلصَّوَٰعِقِ حَذَرَ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ مُحِيطٌۢ بِٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱۹)
Or that of a rainstorm from the sky, wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning: they put their fingers in their ears due to the thunderclaps, apprehensive of death; and Allah besieges the faithless. (19)

Key Points

Allah has likened the hypocrite to individuals caught in the rain, overwhelmed by the difficulties of heavy rain, dark night, the deafening roar of thunder, the blinding light of lightning, and the terror and fear of death. Yet, he has neither shelter to protect himself from the rain, nor light for the darkness, nor an ear safe from the thunder, nor a soul calm from death.

Messages

1- Hypocrites become submerged in difficulties and worries, and even in this world, anxiety, distress, disgrace, and humiliation afflict them. «ظلمات و رعد و برق»

2- Hypocrites fear death. «حذر الموت»

3- Hypocrites should know that Allah encompasses them, and whenever He wills, He exposes their secrets and conspiracies. «و اللّه محیط بالكافرین» (201)

4- The ultimate end of hypocrisy leads to disbelief (kufr). «و اللّه محیط بالكافرین» Therefore, in another place in the Quran, He says: «ان اللَّه جامع المنافقین و الكافرین فى جهنم جمیعاً» (202)

201) A look at the actions and fate of the hypocrites during the Islamic Revolution of Iran is a clear sign for this Ayah. Anxiety, division, defeat, displacement, alienation, disgrace, seeking refuge with disbelievers (kuffar) and tyrants (taghuts), and espionage were the results of their deeds. They imagined victory by using religious slogans and figures; »استوقد نارا« but when their conspiracies were neutralized and the people became aware of their ill intentions, Allah afflicted them with confusion, division, and ignorance of realities and truths. »ذهب اللّه بنورهم« Because they forbid listening to news and the word of truth from scholars, they are like the deaf; because they do not express their inner perceptions and truths, they are like the mute; and because they cannot bear to see the progress and victory of Islam, they are blind. Due to obstinacy and fanaticism, they are the embodiment of »لایرجعون«. However, the victories, like lightning, and the warnings of the people and the revealing verses, like thunder and lightning bolts, have cast them into fear and anxiety.

202) An-Nisa, 140.

Ayah

يَكَادُ ٱلْبَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَـٰرَهُمْ ۖ كُلَّمَآ أَضَآءَ لَهُم مَّشَوْا۟ فِيهِ وَإِذَآ أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ قَامُوا۟ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۲٠)
The lightning almost snatches away their sight: whenever it shines for them, they walk in it, and when the darkness falls upon them, they stand. Had Allah willed, He would have taken away their hearing and sight. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (20)

Key Points

Hypocrites lack the strength and capacity to witness the luminous proofs and the radiance of the Divine verses. They are like a night traveler in the desert whose eyes are dazzled by the lightning, and he only takes a few steps. Although hypocrites in the Islamic society sometimes advance a few steps, they stop moving due to incidents or events. They have extinguished the lamp of their inner nature and remain waiting for light to arrive from external powers.

Whenever it is said that Allah is capable of all things, the intention is possible things. For example, if we say someone is a mathematician, it does not mean he can make the sum of 2+2 equal 5. Because this matter is impossible, not that the person is incapable of summing it up. Some people asked the Imam عليه السلام: Can Allah place the Earth inside an egg? The Imam first gave a convincing answer, stating that we see the vast sky through a lens of the eye, then He said: Allah is capable, but your suggestion is impossible. (203) Just like the power of a mathematician who does not solve an impossible problem.

The Portrait of the Hypocrite in the Quran The hypocrite displays reactions in belief and action, conduct and conversation, which are mentioned in this Surah and in the Surahs Al-Munafiqun, Al-Ahzab, At-Tawbah, An-Nisa, and Muhammad. What can be said here appropriately is that hypocrites do not have faith internally, but they consider themselves reformers and wise. They isolate themselves with their like-minded associates; their prayer is performed lazily, and their spending (in charity) is done reluctantly. They are fault-finders regarding the believers and harmful towards the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. They flee from the battlefield and are heedless of Allah. They are individuals who speak nonsense, are hypocritical (show-offs), spread rumors, and are keen on befriending the disbelievers. The criterion for their affection is gratification, and the criterion for their anger is deprivation. They are unfaithful regarding the commitments they have made to Allah; they are worried about the good things that reach the believers, but they are happy about the problems that befall the Muslims. They enjoin evil and forbid good. In response to all these intellectual and practical deviations, the Quran states: «انّ المنافقین فى الدّرك الاسفل من النار» (204)

203) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 39. 204) An-Nisa, 145.

Messages

  1. The hypocrite is bewildered in his path of movement. «أضاء... مشوا، أظلم... قاموا»
  2. The movement of the hypocrite is in the light of others. «اضاء لهم»
  3. Due to the deeds he commits, the hypocrite might be afflicted by Divine wrath at any moment. «و لو شاء اللّه لذهب بسمعهم»
  4. The Divine tradition is to grant freedom to everyone; otherwise, Allah could have made the hypocrites deaf and blind. «و لو شاء اللّه لذهب بسمعهم و ابصارهم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُمْ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (۲۱)
O mankind! Worship your Lord, who created you and those who were before you, so that you may be Godwary (21)

Key Points

In law books, legal articles are stated without direct address, but the Quran is a book of law that deals with the spirit and emotions of people; therefore, when stating commands, it uses direct address. Of course, the addresses in the Quran vary. [205]

The goal of the creation of the universe and humanity is the perfection (or development) of human beings. That is, the purpose of the creation of existence is for human beings to benefit [206], and the perfection of human beings depends on worship [207], and the effect of worship is attaining piety (taqwa) [208], and the ultimate result of piety is salvation (felicity). [209]

Question: Why should we worship Allah? Answer: The response to this question is given in several places in the Quran as follows: * Because Allah is your Creator and Sustainer. «اعبدوا ربّكم الّذى خلقكم» [210] * Because He is the Provider of your sustenance and security. «فلیعبدوا ربّ هذا البیت الذى اطعمهم من جوع و آمنهم من خوف» [211] * Because there is no deity except Him. «لا اله الاّ أنا فاعبُدنى» [212]

Human worship is the goal of human creation, not the goal of the Creator. He has no need for our worship; if all the people on earth become disbelievers, He is still Self-Sufficient: «ان تكفروا انتم و من فى الارض جمیعاً فانّ اللَّه لغنى...» Just as if all people build their houses facing the sun or turning their backs to it, it has no effect on the sun.

Although the worship of Allah is obligatory upon us, because He is our Creator, Provider, and Sustainer, He still grants a reward for fulfilling this duty, and this is the ultimate extent of His grace.

What compels a human being to worship includes the following matters: 1- Attention to His blessings, as He is our Creator, Provider, and Sustainer. 2- Attention to our own poverty and need. 3- Attention to the effects and blessings of worship. 4- Attention to the negative consequences of abandoning worship. 5- Attention to the fact that all existence is obedient to Him and engaged in His glorification (tasbih); why should we be the discordant element in existence? 6- Attention to the fact that love and devotion are inherent in our soul, and to whom should we show love greater than Him?

Question: It is stated in the Quran: «واعبد ربّك حتّى یاتیك الیقین» meaning, worship until certainty (yaqin) comes to you. So, if someone attains certainty, should he abandon his prayer? Answer: If we say: put up a ladder so your hand reaches the high branch of the tree, it does not mean that once your hand reaches the branch, you should remove the ladder, because you will fall. Whoever separates himself from worship is like one who falls from the sky; «فكانّما خرّ من السماء» Furthermore, those who attained certainty, such as the Messenger of Allah and the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام), never ceased worshipping for a moment. Therefore, the intent of the Ayah is to state the effects of worship, not to define the limits of worship.

The verses (Ayat) and narrations (Riwayat) have explained methods and conditions for worship, which will be discussed in their proper place. However, since this Ayah is the first Divine command addressed to humanity in the Quran, we will outline some main points to clarify how worship should be: 1- Commanded worship (following orders), meaning according to His instruction, without superstitions. 2- Conscious worship, so that we know who our addressee and object of worship is. «حتّى تعلموا ماتقولون» [213] 3- Sincere worship. «ولا یشرك بعبادة ربّه احداً» [214] 4- Humble worship. «فى صلاتهم خاشعون» [215] 5- Secret worship. The Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) said: «اعظم العبادة اجراً اخفاها» [216] 6- Loving worship. The Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) said: «افضل الناس مَن عَشق العبادة» [217]

In summary, we must state that there are three types of conditions for worship: A: Conditions for validity (sihhah), such as purity (taharah) and facing the Qiblah. B: Conditions for acceptance (qabul), such as piety (taqwa). C: Conditions for perfection (kamal), such as worship being conscious, humble, secret, and loving—all of which are conditions for perfection. [218]

205) For the general public, He says: «یا ایّها الناس», but for those who have been guided, He says: «یا ایّها الذین امنوا» 206) «سخّر لكم» (Jathiyah, 13). «خلق لكم» (Baqarah, 29). 207) «و ما خلقتُ الجنّ والانس الاّ لیَعبدون» (Dhariyat, 56). 208) «واعبدوا ربّكم ... لعلّكم تتّقون» (Baqarah, 21). 209) «واتّقوا اللّه لعلّكم تفلحون» (Baqarah, 189). 210) The polytheists accepted creation (Khaliqiyyah) but denied Lordship (Rububiyyah). In this Ayah, Allah has placed the two words «ربّكم» and «خلقكم» side by side to indicate that your Creator is your Lord. 211) Quraysh, 3-4. 212) Taha, 14. 213) Nisa, 43. 214) Kahf, 110. 215) Mu'minun, 2. 216) Bihar, Vol. 70, p. 251. 217) Bihar, Vol. 70, p. 253. 218) For further detail, refer to the author's books A Ray of the Secrets of Prayer and Tafsir al-Salat (Commentary on Prayer).

Messages

1- The call of the prophets is universal and encompasses all people. «یا ایها الناس»

2- Among the philosophies of worship is gratitude toward the Bestower of blessings. «اعبدواربّكم الذى خلقكم»

3- The first of blessings is the blessing of creation, and the first command is submission before the Creator. «اعبدوا ربّكم الذى خلقكم»

4- Let not the idol worship or deviation of our ancestors distance us from the worship of Allah, for even they were created by Allah. «و الذین من قبلكم»

5- Worship is the factor that leads to piety (taqwa). If an act of worship does not generate piety, it is not worship. «اعبدوا... لعلّكم تتقون»

6- We should not be arrogant about our worship, for not every act of worship produces piety. «لعلّكم تتقون»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ فِرَٰشًا وَٱلسَّمَآءَ بِنَآءً وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَخْرَجَ بِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ رِزْقًا لَّكُمْ ۖ فَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ لِلَّهِ أَندَادًا وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۲۲)
—He who made the earth a place of repose for you, and the sky a canopy, and He sends down water from the sky and with it brings forth crops for your sustenance. So do not set up equals to Allah, while you know. (22)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah refers to numerous blessings, each of which is the source of several other blessings. For example, the Earth being a "bed" (firaash) points to many other abundant blessings. Such as the hardness of the mountains and the softness of the plains' soil, the distance of the Earth from the sun, its temperature, climate, and air, the existence of rivers, valleys, mountains, plants, and its various movements, all of which collectively prepare the Earth to be a "bed." Just as several terms have been used for the Earth in the Quran: the Earth is both a "cradle" (mahd) [219], and "submissive" (dhalūl) [220], and "encompassing" (kifāt) [221].

The word "samā'" (sky/heaven) appears once in the Ayah opposite "ard" (earth), referring to the entire upper region, and once it refers specifically to the part from which rain descends from the clouds.

219) الذى جعل لكم الارض مهداً« Taha, 53.

220) هو الذى جعل لكم الارض ذلولاً« Al-Mulk, 15.

221) الم نجعل الارض كفاتا« Al-Mursalat, 15.

Messages

1- Remembering divine blessings is one of the best ways to invite people to worship. «اعبدوا ربّكم... الذى جعل لكم»

2- One of the best ways to know Allah is by utilizing the accessible blessings. «جعل لكم الارض فراشاً و السماء بناءً»

3- Complete harmony is evident in the system of creation. Harmony between the Earth, the sky, rain, plants, fruits, and humans. (The Proof of Order) «جعل، انزل، اخرج»

4- Every creation has been created for a purpose. «رزقاً لكم» Rain is for the growth and fruition of fruits, «فاخرج به» and fruits are for the sustenance of man. «رزقاً لكم»

5- The Earth and rain are means; the growth of plants and fruits is by the hand of Allah. «فأخرج»

6- The order and harmony of the system of creation are signs of Monotheism (Tawhid); therefore, you should also be monotheists. «فلا تجعلوا لِلّه انداداً»

7- The root of worshipping Allah lies in the innate nature (fitra) and conscience of all people. «وانتم تعلمون»

8- The creation of the Earth, the sky, rain, fruits, and the sustenance of man is a reflection of the Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah. «اعبدوا ربّكم... الذى جعل لكم...»

9- All human beings have the right to utilize and dispose of the Earth. (The word «لكم» [for you] is repeated in the Ayah.)

10- The reason your worship is obligatory is His grace, as He has placed the Earth, the sky, rain, and plants for your sustenance. «اعبدوا ربّكم... الذى جعل لكم...»

11- Allah is sovereign over natural causes. «جعل، انزل، اخرج»

12- The notion of partners for Allah stems from ignorance. «لا تجعلوا لله اندادا وانتم تعلمون»

Ayah

وَإِن كُنتُمْ فِى رَيْبٍ مِّمَّا نَزَّلْنَا عَلَىٰ عَبْدِنَا فَأْتُوا۟ بِسُورَةٍ مِّن مِّثْلِهِۦ وَٱدْعُوا۟ شُهَدَآءَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۲۳)
And if you are in doubt concerning what We have sent down to Our servant, then bring a surah like it, and invoke your helpers besides Allah, if you are truthful. (23)

Key Points

This Ayah raises the issue of the miraculous nature of the Quran. Prophets have one call, which is guidance toward Allah, and they carry this out through reasoning, admonition, and good disputation. They also have one claim: that they have come from Allah to guide people, and they bring a miracle for this claim. Thus, the miracle is for proving the Prophet's claim, not His call. [222] Allah has repeatedly challenged the opponents of Islam in the Quran, stating that if you do not consider this Book to be from Allah, but rather a fabrication made by human hands, then instead of all this war and struggle, bring a book like the Quran so that the voice of Islam may be silenced! To prove the truthfulness of His Prophet and His Book, Allah has, on the one hand, provoked the opponents, and on the other hand, offered them a concession. In one place, He says: «فاتوا بكتاب» [223] (Bring a book like the Quran). In another place, He says: «فاتوا بعشر سور مثله» [224] (Ten Surahs). And in yet another place, He says: «فاتوا بسورة مثله وادعوا من استطعتم من دون اللَّه» [225] (One Surah). Furthermore, He says: For this task, you may call upon all your powers, helpers, and like-minded individuals throughout the world. The division of the Quran and the naming of each section as a Surah occurred during the time of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and was from the side of Allah. «فاتوا بسورة» In praising the Prophets, the Quran mentions their names either after the word «عبدنا» (Our servant), such as «عبدنا ایوب» (Our servant Ayyub), or before the word «عبد» (servant), such as «ابراهیم... كل من عبادنا» (Abraham... all were Our servants). But concerning the Prophet of Islam, it uses only the word «عبد» without mentioning His name, to indicate that the absolute servant is Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله.

222) It should not be hidden that a miracle does not mean ignoring the system of cause and effect. Rather, a miracle also has a cause, but its cause is either the Divine Will or factors whose causality Allah has kept hidden from people. 223) Qasas, 49. 224) Hud, 13. 225) Yunus, 38.

Messages

1- Doubt must be removed from the minds and hearts of individuals, especially concerning matters of belief. «ان كنتم فى ریب»

2- The condition for receiving revelation is servitude to Allah. «نزّلنا على عبدنا»

3- The Quran is a book of reasoning and argumentation, leaving no room for temptation or doubt. «فاتوا بسورة»

4- Prophets must possess a miracle, and the Quran is the miracle of the Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله. «فاتوا بسورة من مثله»

5- An eternal religion requires an eternal miracle so that every human being, in any time or place, if afflicted by hesitation or doubt, can test it for themselves. «فاتوا بسورة من مثله»

6- We are so certain of the truthfulness of the Quran that if the opponents bring even one Surah like the Quran, we will accept it in place of the entire Quran. «بسورة»

7- The best judge and arbiter is the conscience. Allah has made the conscience of the opponents the arbiter. «و ادعوا شهداءكم»

Ayah

فَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلُوا۟ وَلَن تَفْعَلُوا۟ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱلنَّارَ ٱلَّتِى وَقُودُهَا ٱلنَّاسُ وَٱلْحِجَارَةُ ۖ أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۲٤)
But if you do not—and you will not—then beware the Fire whose fuel will be humans and stones, prepared for the faithless. (24)

Key Points

In Surah Al-Anbiya', we read: «انّكم و ماتعبدون من دون اللّه حصب جهنّم» (Indeed, you and whatever you worship instead of Allah are the fuel of Hell.) [226] (You and what you worship besides Allah (your idols) are the fuel of Hell.) Therefore, it is possible that the meaning of "stone" (الحجارة) in this Ayah refers to the idols that were worshipped, not just any stone, [227] so that they may know and see with their own eyes that the idols were and are incapable of doing anything. Furthermore, these stones will be, on the Day of Judgment, like a bloody knife accompanying the murderer's file—a document of their crime and sin.

Question: Since words (alphabets/language) are human inventions, how is it that man cannot produce the like of the Qur'an?

Answer: The letters of the alphabet are from man, but the method of combining them and expressing their lofty concepts arises from knowledge and art. The Qur'an was revealed based on the infinite Divine knowledge and wisdom, but every other book, regardless of who authored it, is composed based on the limited human knowledge confined by ignorance. Thus, man can never produce a book like the Qur'an.

226) Anbiya', 98. 227) Just as throughout history, among all those stones and wood, the Black Stone (Hajar al-Aswad) has never been worshipped, nor has it played the role of an idol, and it will never become the fuel of Hell. Indeed! The Black Stone is the Hand of Allah on earth, the guardian of secrets, and a witness to the confessions of human natures.

Messages

1- Certainty in the truthfulness of the path and the goal is one of the principles of leadership. «ولن تفعلوا» (And you will never do it.)

2- Now that you have felt helplessness and inability, submit to the Truth. «ولن تفعلوا فاتّقوا» (And you will never do it, so fear [the Fire].)

3- The obstinate and disbelieving person is on the same level as stone. «الناس و الحجارة» (Men and the stones.)

4- The inner wickedness of the sinful person will be embodied on the Day of Judgment and will become the fuel for the fire. «وقودها الناس» (Whose fuel is men.)

5- Disbelief and obstinacy transform a human being, who is worthy of being Allah's Vicegerent (Khalifatullah), into the fuel of Hell. «وقودها الناس» (Whose fuel is men.)

6- Salvation from the Fire of Hell depends on believing in the Qur'an and affirming the Prophet of Islam. «فان لم تفعلوا و لن تفعلوا فاتّقوا النّار... اُعدّت للكافرین» (But if you do not do so—and you will never do so—then fear the Fire... prepared for the disbelievers.)

Ayah

وَبَشِّرِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ كُلَّمَا رُزِقُوا۟ مِنْهَا مِن ثَمَرَةٍ رِّزْقًا ۙ قَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى رُزِقْنَا مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَأُتُوا۟ بِهِۦ مُتَشَـٰبِهًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِيهَآ أَزْوَٰجٌ مُّطَهَّرَةٌ ۖ وَهُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۲٥)
And give good news to those who have faith and do righteous deeds, that for them shall be gardens with streams running in them: whenever they are provided with their fruit for nourishment, they will say, ‘This is what we were provided before,’ and they were given something resembling it. There will be chaste mates for them, and they will remain therein [forever]. (25)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning of mutashābihan (similar) is that the inhabitants of Paradise, at first glance, see the fruits resembling worldly fruits and say: "This is similar to what we ate in the world," but after eating, they realize it has a fresh taste and pleasure. And perhaps the meaning is that they are given fruits that are all uniform in terms of quality, beauty, and fragrance, and unlike worldly fruits, they do not have first, second, or third grades.

In the Qur'an, faith (īmān) and righteous deeds (‘amal sālih) are usually mentioned together, but faith precedes righteous deeds. Indeed, if a room is illuminated from within, the rays of this light will shine out through the openings and windows. Faith illuminates the inner self of man, and an illuminated heart illuminates all of man's actions. The blessings of faith and righteous deeds are numerous, and they have been referred to in the verses of the Qur'an. [228]

The spouses in Paradise are of two types: A: One type is the Hūr al-‘Īn (wide-eyed maidens), who are like pearls and virgins, and are created in that very realm. «أنشاناهنّ إنشاء» B: The believing worldly spouses who will be placed beside their husbands with beautiful faces. «ومن صلح من ابائهم و أزواجهم» [229]

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about the azwāj mutahharah (purified spouses), and He said: The spouses of Paradise are not afflicted by menstruation or ritual impurity (hadath). [230]

228) The Qur'an mentions 15 blessings for faith accompanied by righteous deeds, which you can access by referring to Al-Mu'jam al-Mufahras. 229) Ra‘d, 13. 230) Tafsīr Rāhnamā and Durr al-Manthūr.

Messages

1- Glad tidings and good news accompanied by warning and caution (in the previous Ayah) is one of the principles of education/training. «فاتقوا النار... وبشّر الذین آمنوا...»

2- Inner faith must be accompanied by righteous deeds. «آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات»

3- Performing all good and worthy deeds is effective, not just some of them. «عملوا الصالحات» (Al-Sālihāt is both plural and contains the definite article alif and lām, which signifies all good deeds.)

4- Worthy and righteous deeds are valuable only if they stem from faith, not from personal desires or social attractions. First «آمنوا» (they believed), then «عملوا الصالحات» (they did righteous deeds).

5- The deprivations that the believer experiences in this world due to observing the lawful (halāl) and the unlawful (harām) will be compensated for in the Hereafter. «رزقوا»

6- In this world, we worry about losing blessings, but in the Hereafter, this worry does not exist. «وهم فیها خالدون»

7- Familiarity with the history of the blessings increases the pleasure of enjoyment. [231] «رزقنا من قبل»

8- The spouses of Paradise are also purified. «ازواج مطهّرة»

231) We listen with love and interest to the words of someone we know on the radio or television, or we look with interest at pictures of places we have previously visited.

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَسْتَحْىِۦٓ أَن يَضْرِبَ مَثَلًا مَّا بَعُوضَةً فَمَا فَوْقَهَا ۚ فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ فَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۖ وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فَيَقُولُونَ مَاذَآ أَرَادَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَـٰذَا مَثَلًا ۘ يُضِلُّ بِهِۦ كَثِيرًا وَيَهْدِى بِهِۦ كَثِيرًا ۚ وَمَا يُضِلُّ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّا ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (۲٦)
Indeed Allah is not ashamed to draw a parable whether it is that of a gnat or something above it. As for those who have faith, they know it is the truth from their Lord; and as for the faithless, they say, ‘What did Allah mean by this parable?’ Thereby He leads many astray, and thereby He guides many; and He leads no one astray thereby except the transgressors (26)

Key Points

The word ba'ūḍ (بعوض) means mosquito, and its root is from ba'ḍ (بعض), which is applied to it due to its small size. (232) The examples in the Quran are for all people, and every kind of example is mentioned therein: «ولقد ضربنا للنّاس فى هذا القران من كلّ مثل» (233) However, these examples should not be taken lightly, because the learned understand their essence: «وتلك الامثال نضربها للنّاس وما یعقلها الاّ العالمون» (234) In presenting examples, there is remembrance, comprehension, teaching, clarification, and the unveiling of hidden truths. This is frequently seen in previous heavenly books, such as the Torah and the Gospel, and in the sayings of the Messenger of Allah (Rasūl al-Akram) and the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. There is also a section in the Torah called "The Proverbs of Solomon" (Amthāl Sulaymān).

Some opponents of Islam, who were unable to counter the logical arguments and examples presented in the Quran, used the Quranic examples as an excuse, saying: The status of Allah is too exalted for Him to give examples using animals like a fly or a spider. (235) And these examples are not compatible with the majesty of Allah. They thus cast doubt upon the verses of the Quran. (236) Allah answers their excuses with the revelation of this verse.

Question

Why does Allah mislead a group of people through the Quran and its examples?

Answer

Allah does not mislead anyone. Rather, whoever stands against the truths of the Quran misguides himself, and in this sense, it can be said that the Quran became the cause of his misguidance. As He says at the end of this very verse: «وما یضلّ به الاّ الفاسقین» meaning that the disobedience (fisq) of the people is the cause of their misguidance.

Truly, is it possible for Allah to command people to believe, yet He Himself misguides them!? Is it possible for Him to send down so many Prophets and heavenly books, yet He Himself misguides people!? Is it possible for Him to criticize Iblis for misguiding others, yet He Himself misguides others!?

Although in this verse, guidance and misguidance are generally attributed to Allah, other verses clarify the matter, stating: «یهدى الیه من أناب» (237)—Allah guides those who turn towards Him. And «یهدى به اللَّه من اتّبع رضوانه» (238)—those who seek to attain His pleasure. And «الذین جاهدوا فینا لنهدینهم سبلنا» (239)—those who strive in His way.

Conversely, those who willingly choose to walk the crooked path, Allah leaves them to their own devices. The meaning of Allah misguiding is precisely this. Just as the phrase lā yahdī (He does not guide) is used concerning the disbelievers, the wrongdoers, the defiantly disobedient, and the extravagant.

In the words of Sa'di: There is the path and the pit, the seeing eye and the sun, So that man may look before his own feet. He has so many lamps yet walks astray, Let him fall and see his just recompense.

232) Mufradāt Rāghib. 233) Rūm, 58. 234) ‘Ankabūt, 43. 235) In Sūrat al-‘Ankabūt, verse 41, non-divine powers are likened to the house of the spider, and in Sūrat al-Ḥajj, verse 73, He states that others do not even have the ability to create a fly. 236) Fundamentally, why should Allah be ashamed of giving the example of a mosquito? If the creation of a mosquito was not shameful, why should giving an example of it be shameful? The smallness of the mosquito should not be an excuse, because this very mosquito possesses all the organs of an elephant in a smaller size, and in addition, it has two antennae. Its hollow proboscis is like the finest and most precise syringe, and this small mosquito can incapacitate the largest animal. It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: Do not look down upon the smallness of the mosquito, for it has everything the elephant has, plus two wings, which the elephant does not have. Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān. 237) Ra‘d, 27. 238) Mā’idah, 16. 239) ‘Ankabūt, 69.

Messages

1- Modesty and shame are appropriate when an action is religiously, rationally, or customarily reprehensible. However, in stating truths, shame and embarrassment are not commendable. «لا یستحیى»

2- Sublime and important truths can be expressed through simple language and examples. «ان یضرب مثلاً»

3- The believer trusts the word of Allah and is obedient to it. «یعلمون انّه الحق»

4- The examples in the Quran are a means of education and growth. «فیعلمون انه الحق من ربهم»

5- The examples in the Quran are truth and serve to clarify realities. «انّه الحق»

6- The truth-seeker finds the path by any light, but the fault-finder and excuse-maker criticizes every lamp. «ماذا اراد اللَّه بهذا مثلاً»

7- Disbelief and obstinacy are factors leading to excuse-making. «ماذا اراد اللَّه بهذا مثلاً»

8- Disobedience (fisq) causes misguidance and prevents the recognition of truths. «ومایضلّ به الاّ الفاسقین»

9- The examples of the Quran are a means of guidance or misguidance. «یضلّ به، یهدى به»

10- Allah considers the covenant-breaker defiantly disobedient (fāsiq), and He misguides the defiantly disobedient. «وما یضل به الا الفاسقین»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَنقُضُونَ عَهْدَ ٱللَّهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مِيثَـٰقِهِۦ وَيَقْطَعُونَ مَآ أَمَرَ ٱللَّهُ بِهِۦٓ أَن يُوصَلَ وَيُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (۲۷)
—those who break the covenant made with Allah after having pledged it solemnly, and sever what Allah has commanded to be joined, and cause corruption on the earth—it is they who are the losers. (27)

Key Points

The divine covenants mentioned in this verse are numerous: Allah took a covenant from the prophets to convey the divine verses to the people. [240] He took a commitment from the People of the Book not to conceal the truths and to act upon the glad tidings concerning the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله found in the Torah and the Gospel. [241] He also took a covenant from the general populace to act upon the divine command and abandon the path of Satan. [242]

In Islam, fulfilling a covenant is obligatory, even towards disbelievers (kuffar). A covenant-breaker is introduced as a hypocrite, even if he is a person of prayer. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «لا دین لمن لا عهد له» [243] (He who is not committed to his obligations has no share in religion.)

In this verse, Allah has commanded us to maintain connections and relationships with certain groups: «امر اللَّه به ان یوصل» (that which Allah has commanded to be joined). In narrations, these groups are introduced as: heavenly leaders, scholars, relatives, believers, neighbors, and teachers. Whoever severs his relationship with these individuals harms himself. This is because he has prevented the growth and perfection that he could have achieved through these connections.

Fulfilling a covenant is a perfection by which Allah has praised Himself: «ومن اوفى بعهده من اللَّه» [244] (And who is more faithful to His covenant than Allah?) Fulfilling a covenant is necessary even towards polytheists (mushrikin): «فاتموا الیهم عهدهم الى مدّتهم» [245] (So fulfill their covenant to them until the end of their term). Likewise, in Ayah 25 of Surah Ar-Ra'd, covenant-breakers are cursed.

Covenants are of two types: A covenant that people make with one another, which they must fulfill. And a covenant that Allah places upon a Prophet or an Imam for the leadership of a society, which is a divine station (position). «لا ینال عهدى الظالمین» [246] (My covenant does not include the wrongdoers.)

A part of the divine covenants emphasized by this verse are the innate covenants (mithaq fitrah) that Allah has placed within the nature of all human beings. Just as Hadrat Ali عليه السلام considers the philosophy of prophethood to be the flourishing of the innate covenant. «و واتر الیهم انبیائه لیستأدوهم میثاق فطرته» [247]

Question: What is the covenant of Allah? Answer: It is understood from the phrase «لا ینال عهدى الظالمین» that heavenly leadership is the covenant of Allah, and it is also understood from narrations that prayer (salat) is the covenant of Allah. Any commitment a person has between himself and God is a divine covenant. Intellectual and rational laws, as well as divine rulings (Ahkam), are also instances of the divine covenant.

Allamah Majlisi قدس سره, following the verse «ویقطعون ما امر اللَّه به ان یوصل» (and they sever what Allah has commanded to be joined), presented and discussed one hundred and ten hadiths regarding the importance of maintaining kinship ties (Silat ar-Rahim). We [248] will mention some interesting points found in those narrations here:

  • Have visits with your relatives, even if it is only to offer them a drink of water.
  • Maintaining kinship ties increases lifespan and wards off poverty.
  • Through maintaining kinship ties, sustenance (rizq) is expanded.
  • The best steps are those taken for maintaining kinship ties and visiting relatives.
  • As a result of maintaining kinship ties, you attain a special station in Paradise.
  • Go to your relatives, even if they ignore you.
  • Maintain kinship ties, even if the family member is not among the righteous.
  • Maintain kinship ties, even if it is only by greeting them (with Salam).
  • Maintaining kinship ties eases death and the reckoning on the Day of Judgment.
  • The person who severs kinship ties (Qati' ar-Rahm) will not smell the fragrance of Paradise.
  • Maintaining kinship ties causes the purification of deeds and the growth of wealth.
  • Financial aid to relatives has twenty-four times the reward of aid given to others.
  • Maintain kinship ties, even if you have to walk for a year.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: My father advised me not to befriend those who do not maintain relations with their family. [249]

240) واذ اخذنا من النبییّن میثاقهم« Ahzab, 7. 241) واذ اخذاللَّه میثاق الذّین اوتوا الكتاب« Aal-i-Imran, 187. 242) واذ اخذ ربّك من بنى آدم« A'raf, 172. 243) Bihar, Vol. 72, p. 198. 244) Tawbah, 111. 245) Tawbah, 4. 246) Baqarah, 124. 247) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 1. 248) Bihar, Vol. 71, p. 87. 249) Tafsir Rahnuma and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Covenant-breaking is the way of the wicked (fasiqin). «الفاسقین. الّذین ینقضون» (The present tense verb indicates continuity.)

2- Do not trust the covenants of the wicked. Whoever violates the covenant of Allah will not be faithful to the covenants and agreements of others. «ینقضون، یقطعون»

3- Man is responsible before Allah because, through his intellect and innate nature (fitrah), he has made a covenant and agreement with Him to act upon the rulings of religion. «عهد اللَّه»

4- Islam is opposed to isolation. «امر اللَّه ان یوصل»
