5- The covenant-breaker gradually becomes a corrupter (mufsid). «ینقضون، یفسدون»

6- The covenant-breaker harms himself, not Allah. «اولئك هم الخاسرون»

Ayah

كَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَكُنتُمْ أَمْوَٰتًا فَأَحْيَـٰكُمْ ۖ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۲۸)
How can you be unfaithful to Allah, [seeing that] you were lifeless and He gave you life, then He will make you die and then bring you to life, and then you will be brought back to Him? (28)

Key Points

The best way to know Allah is to reflect upon the creation of oneself and the universe. Prophet Abraham عليه السلام, when proving the existence of Allah to others, said: «ربّى الّذى یحیى و یمیت» [250] My Lord is the One who gives life and causes death. Reflection upon the phenomenon of life and contemplation of the issue of death make man realize that if life originated from man himself, it should be eternal. Why was it absent before, then appeared later, and is subsequently taken away?! Allah says: Now that you have seen how a lifeless being becomes living, know that your resurrection on the Day of Judgment will be similar. The reality of life is unknown, but its effects are evident in the existence of man. The reality of the Creator of this life is also incomprehensible, but His signs are clear in everything.

250) Baqarah, 258.

Messages

1- Appealing to reason and innate nature (fitra) is one of the methods of propagation and guidance. «كیف تكفرون...»

2- Reflection upon the transformations of death and life is the best proof for the existence of Allah. «كنتم امواتاً» Self-knowledge is the prelude to knowing Allah.

3- In the divine perspective, the purpose of life and death is perfection and return to the source of perfection. «ثم الیه ترجعون» Mawlawi (Rumi) says in the third volume of the Mathnawi:

I died from the mineral and became the plant; I died from the plant and emerged as the animal. I died from animality and became the human; so why should I fear? When have I ever been diminished by dying? Another time I shall soar from the angel; I shall become that which enters not the imagination.

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ لَكُم مَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰٓ إِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَسَوَّىٰهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَٰتٍ ۚ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۲۹)
It is He who created for you all that is in the earth, then He turned to the heaven and fashioned it into seven heavens, and He has knowledge of all things. (29)

Key Points

«وفرعها فى السماء» The word samā’ (سماء) in the Arabic language means "above." Sometimes it refers to a few meters above the ground, like the branches of trees that are at a low height, as mentioned (X) [251]; and sometimes it refers to the height of the clouds from which rain falls: «و نزلنا من السماء ماء» [252]; and sometimes it refers to the atmosphere surrounding the earth. «جعلنا السماء سقفاً محفوظاً» [253]

251) Ibrahim, 24. 252) Qaf, 9. 253) Anbiya, 32.

Messages

1- Disbelief in Allah, the Forgiving, the Powerful, and the Remover of needs, is astonishing. «كیف تكفرون... هو الذى...»

2- Existence was created for mankind. «خلق لكم ما فى الارض جمیعاً»

3- The cosmic order is purposeful, and wise planning and design were involved in the creation of the world. «خلق لكم»

4- No creation in nature is futile, even if we do not know how to utilize it. «خلق لكم»

5- The principle is that everything is permissible (mubah) for man, unless a specific proof negates it. «خلق لكم ما فى الارض جمیعاً»

6- The world is for man, not man for the world. «خلق لكم»

7- Man can reach a point, scientifically, where he utilizes all natural blessings, discovers the secrets of existence, and subjugates it. «لكم»

8- The utilization of the earth's blessings is for everyone. «خلق لكم ما فى الارض»

9- The seven heavens of the universe possess equilibrium and are without the slightest deviation or disharmony. «فسوّاهن سبع سموات»

10- The creation of the heaven and the earth is entirely based on Divine knowledge. «خلق لكم... علیم»

11- The creation of the earth and the seven heavens is proof of Allah's ability to resurrect the dead, which was mentioned in the previous Ayah. «ثم یحییكم... هو الذى»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِنِّى جَاعِلٌ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةً ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ أَتَجْعَلُ فِيهَا مَن يُفْسِدُ فِيهَا وَيَسْفِكُ ٱلدِّمَآءَ وَنَحْنُ نُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِكَ وَنُقَدِّسُ لَكَ ۖ قَالَ إِنِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۳٠)
When your Lord said to the angels, ‘Indeed I am going to set a viceroy on the earth,’ they said, ‘Will You set in it someone who will cause corruption in it and shed blood, while we celebrate Your praise and proclaim Your sanctity?’ He said, ‘Indeed I know what you do not know.’ (30)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that Allah created all the bounties of the earth for mankind. In this Ayah and the subsequent Ayahs, the issue of man's vicegerency (Khilafah) on earth is raised, which is followed by the angels' concern regarding human corruption, Allah's explanation and justification, and their prostration before the first man. The angels predicted human bloodshed and corruption, either through divine revelation, or by observing humans preceding Adam (عليه السلام) in other realms or in this world, or due to a correct foresight they had regarding the earthly, material nature of man and their natural conflicts. Although all humans have the potential to become Allah's vicegerent, not all are Allah's vicegerents. This is because some of them fall so low through their actions that they become baser than animals. As the Quran states: «اولئك كالانعام بل هم اضل» (254) The abode of this vicegerent is the earth, but His worthiness extends to «قاب قوسین او ادنى» (255). Allow others to ask questions. Allah permitted the angels to ask, otherwise, the angels do not speak without permission, and the angels knew that there was a sublime purpose behind every creation.

Question: Why did Allah raise the issue of man's creation with the angels? Answer: Man is a special creation whose material structure was made in the best form: «احسن تقویم» (256), and into him was breathed the Divine Spirit, and after His creation, Allah congratulated Himself: «فتبارك اللّه» (257)

Question: What need does Allah, Who is eternally Present, Observing, and Self-Subsisting (Qayyum), have for a successor or vicegerent? Answer: Firstly, man's vicegerency is not due to any need or inability on the part of Allah, but this status is due to the dignity and excellence of the rank of humanity. Secondly, the system of creation is based on intermediaries. Meaning, although Allah is directly capable of performing every task, He has appointed intermediaries for the execution of affairs. We present some examples: * Although He is the primary Planner: «اللّه الّذى... یدبّر» (258), He has appointed the angels as the planners of existence. «فالمدبّرات أمراً» (259) * Although healing is in His hand; «فهو یشفین» (260), He has placed healing in honey. «فیه شفاء» (261) * Although the knowledge of the unseen belongs exclusively to Him; «انّما الغیب للّه» (262), He reveals a part of it to some of His righteous servants. «الاّ مَن ارتضى من رسول» (263) Therefore, man can become Allah's successor, and obedience to him is like obedience to Allah. «من یطع الرّسول فقد أطاع اللّه» (264). And pledging allegiance to him is also equivalent to pledging allegiance to Allah. «انّ الّذین یبایعونك... انّما یبایعون اللّه» (265). And love for him is like love for Allah. «من احبّكم فقد احبّ اللّه» (266)

To judge creatures, one must place all their good and evil aspects side by side, and one should not judge prematurely. The angels saw themselves, noting that their glorification (Tasbih) and praise (Hamd) were greater than man's. Iblis also saw himself and said: I am made of fire and Adam is made of clay, and he refused to submit. But the Exalted Allah sees the whole picture, that man is superior, and He says: «انّى اعلم مالا تعلمون»

254) Al-A'raf, 179. 255) An-Najm, 9. 256) At-Tin, 4. 257) Al-Mu'minun, 14. 258) Yunus, 3. 259) An-Nazi'at, 5. 260) Ash-Shu'ara, 80. 261) An-Nahl, 69. 262) Yunus, 20. 263) Al-Jinn, 27. 264) An-Nisa, 18. 265) Al-Fath, 10. 266) Ziyarat Jami'ah Kabirah.

Messages

  1. Allah first provided the means of life for man, then He created him. «خلق لكم ما فى الارض جمیعاً ... اذقال ربك للملائكه» (267)

  2. The creation of the angels preceded Adam, because Allah discussed the creation of man with them.

  3. The appointment of the divine vicegerent, successor, and ruler is solely in the hand of Allah. «انّى جاعل فى الارض خلیفة»

  4. Man is the permanent successor of Allah on earth. «جاعل» (268)

  5. Man can be the noblest of creatures and worthy of the station of Allah's Vicegerent. (269) «جاعل فى الارض خلیفة»

  6. The angels considered corruption and bloodshed to be the continuous work of man. «یفسد... ویسفك»

  7. The divine ruler (270) and vicegerent must be just, not corrupt or wicked. The vicegerent must not be one who «یفسد فى الارض».

  8. Presenting one's own worthiness is permissible, provided it is not based on envy. «و نحن نسبّح بحمدك و نقدّس لك»

  9. Worship and glorification in a peaceful environment are not the sole criteria for worthiness. «نحن نسبّح»

  10. Due to the deviation or corruption of a group, the possibility of growth for others should not be prevented. Although Allah knew that a group of humans would commit corruption, He did not withhold the blessing of creation from everyone.

  11. Being obedient and submissive does not contradict asking questions to resolve ambiguity. «أتجعل فیها»

  12. Allah did not reject human corruption and bloodshed, but rather presented the more important expediency, worthiness, and superiority of man. «انى اعلم ما لاتعلمون»

  13. Do not expect all people to accept your words or actions without question, for even the angels questioned Allah. «قالوا أتجعل فیها»

  14. The knowledge and information of the angels are limited. «مالا تعلمون»

267) Imam Ali (عليه السلام) said: "فلمّا مهد ارضه و انفذ امره اختار آدم..." As soon as He prepared His earth and executed His command, He chose Adam... Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 91.

268) The word «جاعل» is an active participle (Ism Fa'il) and symbolizes continuity.

269) Of course, He has deprived the oppressors from attaining this status: «لاینال عهدى الظالمین» Al-Baqarah, 124.

270) The imperfect verb (Mudari') indicates continuity.

Ayah

وَعَلَّمَ ءَادَمَ ٱلْأَسْمَآءَ كُلَّهَا ثُمَّ عَرَضَهُمْ عَلَى ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ فَقَالَ أَنۢبِـُٔونِى بِأَسْمَآءِ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۳۱)
And He taught Adam the Names, all of them; then presented them to the angels and said, ‘Tell me the names of these, if you are truthful.’ (31)

Key Points

Allah the Almighty taught Adam the Asma (Names) [271] and the secrets of the universe, ranging from the names of His chosen saints [272] to inanimate objects. According to the expression of Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, as cited in Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan: All the lands, mountains, valleys, riverbeds, and even this very carpet beneath our feet, were made known to Adam.

271) The term Ism (Name) in our common usage only means a name or sign, but in the Quranic culture, it is a name accompanied by content (meaning). Just as "فله الاسماء الحسنى" (To Him belong the Most Beautiful Names) means that Allah possesses sublime attributes.

272) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 54, and Ikmal al-Din by Saduq, Vol. 1, p. 14.

Messages

1- The true Teacher is Allah, while the pen, speech, teacher, and book are merely the means of instruction. "عَلّم" (He taught).

2- The superiority of man over the angels is due to knowledge. "وعلّم آدم..." (And He taught Adam...).

3- Man has the potential and capacity to receive all knowledge. "كلّها" (all of them).

4- The angels had more worship, but Adam had more knowledge. The relationship between the station of Vicegerency (Khilafah) and knowledge is greater than that with worship. "نسبّح بحمدك... علّم آدم الاسماء" (We glorify [You] with Your praise... He taught Adam the Names).

5- The best way to enlighten others is by holding an examination and showcasing differences and merits. "علّم... ثم عرضهم... فقال انبئونى" (He taught... then He presented them... and said, inform Me).

6- The angels considered themselves more worthy of the station of the Vicegerent of Allah (Khalifatullah). "ان كنتم صادقین" [273] (if you are truthful).

273) It is narrated in a tradition from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that He said: "ان كنتم صادقین" means if you, the angels, are truthful in your claim of being more worthy. Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ لَا عِلْمَ لَنَآ إِلَّا مَا عَلَّمْتَنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۳۲)
They said, ‘Immaculate are You! We have no knowledge except what You have taught us. Indeed You are the All-knowing, the All-wise.’ (32)

Key Points

Iblis (Satan) and the angels each, in a way, considered themselves superior to Adam: Iblis due to his creation, saying, "انا خیر منه" (I am better than him), and the angels due to their worship, saying, "نحن نسبح بحمدك" (We glorify [You] with Your praise). However, Iblis resisted Allah's command to prostrate, but the angels, when they understood the truth, apologized and confessed their ignorance, saying, "سبحانك لا علم لنا" (Glory be to You! We have no knowledge).

Messages

1- Apologizing for questioning without knowledge is a virtue. "سبحانك"

2- Elevated statuses and feelings of superiority must be regulated. Those who spoke the words "نسبّح و نقدّس" (We glorify and sanctify) later said: "لا علم لنا" (We have no knowledge).

3- We must confess our ignorance. "لا علم لنا" The angels demonstrated the highest form of etiquette. The words: "سبحانك, لاعلم لنا, علّمتنا, انّك, انت العلیم الحكیم," are all signs of etiquette.

4- The knowledge of the angels is limited. "لا علم لنا"

5- Allah's knowledge is inherent (intrinsic); "انّك انت العلیم" (Surely You, only You, are the All-Knowing), but the knowledge of others is acquired. "علّمتنا" (You have taught us).

6- We should not consider the affairs of the universe to be accidental. "انت العلیم الحكیم" (You are the All-Knowing, the Wise).

Ayah

قَالَ يَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ أَنۢبِئْهُم بِأَسْمَآئِهِمْ ۖ فَلَمَّآ أَنۢبَأَهُم بِأَسْمَآئِهِمْ قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ إِنِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ غَيْبَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَأَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُونَ وَمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْتُمُونَ (۳۳)
He said, ‘O Adam, inform them of their names,’ and when he had informed them of their names, He said, ‘Did I not tell you that I know the Unseen of the heavens and the earth, and that I know whatever you disclose and whatever you conceal?’ (33)

Messages

1- Give worthy talents the opportunity to flourish and manifest. «انبئهم باسمائهم»

2- In the test of knowledge that Allah conducted, Adam gained superiority over the angels. «فلمّا انبئهم باسمائهم»

3- Besides what they stated, the angels were also concealing certain matters. «كنتم تكتمون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِـَٔادَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰ وَٱسْتَكْبَرَ وَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۳٤)
And when We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate before Adam,’ they prostrated, but not Iblis: he refused and acted arrogantly, and he was one of the faithless. (34)

Key Points

According to the Quran, Iblis was from the race of Jinn, who worshipped among the angels. «كان من الجنّ» (274) Since the prostration (sujud) to Adam was by Allah's command, it was, in reality, servitude and worship of Allah. (275) Because true worship is an action that Allah desires, not one that conforms to our wishes. Iblis was willing to prostrate for centuries, but not to prostrate to Adam. Iblis committed two deviations and transgressions: A: Ideological transgression; «أبى » (he refused), which caused his disobedience (fisq). (276) B: Moral transgression; «استكبر» (he was arrogant), which caused him to be destined for Hellfire. (277) The prostration to Adam was not only for his person but also for his progeny and descendants. As He says elsewhere: «خلقناكم ثمّ صوّرناكم ثمّ قلنا للملائكة اسجدوا لأدم» (278) Imam Sajjad عليه السلام says: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: The prostration to Adam was because of his offspring and progeny. (279) The angels' prostration was temporary, but their descent upon the believers and their seeking forgiveness for them is permanent. «الّذین قالوا ربّنا اللّه ثم استقاموا تتنزّل علیهم الملائكة» (280) The prostration to Adam was not because of his body, but because of his Divine Spirit. «فاذا سوّیته و نفخت فیه من روحى فقعوا له ساجدین» (281) It is unfair that all the angels prostrated to man, yet man does not prostrate to Allah.

(274) Kahf, 50. (275) ‘Uyun Akhbar al-Ridha. (276) «ففسق عن امر ربّه» (So he disobeyed the command of his Lord) Kahf, 50. (277) «فبئس مَثوى المتكبّرین» (So wretched is the dwelling of the arrogant) Zumar, 72. (278) A‘raf, 11. (279) Tafsir Safi, regarding the verse. (280) (Those who said, "Our Lord is Allah," and then remained steadfast, the angels will descend upon them.) Fussilat, 30. And elsewhere He says: «ویستغفرون للّذین آمنوا» (And they seek forgiveness for those who have believed), meaning the angels are constantly seeking forgiveness for the people of faith. Ghafir, 7. (281) Hijr, 29.

Messages

1- Angels, like humans, are subject to address, command, and prohibition. «أسجدوا لادم»

2- Merit is more important than seniority. The ancient angels had to prostrate to the newly created, yet worthy, human. «أسجدوالادم»

3- More dangerous than disobedience in action is disbelief in the command itself. «ابى و استكبر»

4- The arrogance and audacity of Iblis became the source of his wretchedness. «كان من الكافرین»

Ayah

وَقُلْنَا يَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ ٱسْكُنْ أَنتَ وَزَوْجُكَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ وَكُلَا مِنْهَا رَغَدًا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَـٰذِهِ ٱلشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونَا مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۳٥)
We said, ‘O Adam, dwell with your mate in paradise and eat thereof freely whencesoever you wish, but do not approach this tree, lest you should be among the wrongdoers.’ (35)

Key Points

| In the culture of the Quran, shajar (شجر), besides meaning tree, is also applied to a "bush" or "plant." For example, He refers to the pumpkin plant as: «شجرة من یقطین» (282). Therefore, if we read in narrations and commentaries that the intent of shajar in this Ayah is wheat, there is no objection. Jannah (جنّت) is also applied to gardens in this world, as stated in Surah Al-Qalam, Ayah 17: «انّا بلوناهم كما بلونا أصحاب الجنّة» (We tested the owners of the garden). What is understood from other verses of the Quran and narrations is that the garden where Adam resided was not the promised Paradise (Heaven), because: 1- That Paradise is for reward, and Adam had not yet performed any deed that would entitle him to a reward. «ام حسبتم ان تدخلوا الجنّة و لمّا یعلم اللّه الّذین جاهدوا منكم» (283). 2- Whoever enters that Paradise will not be expelled from it. «و ما هم بمخرجین» (284). 3- In that Paradise, there is no command, prohibition, restriction, or obligation (taklif), whereas Adam was prohibited from eating from the tree. Furthermore, it is also mentioned in the narrations of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام that Adam's Paradise was not the promised Paradise. This prohibition given to Prophet Adam was not a mandatory prohibition (nahy taklifi) whose violation would be forbidden (haram), but rather it had the nature of advice and guidance.

282) As-Saffat, 146. 283) Aal-i-Imran, 142. 284) Al-Hijr, 48.

Messages

1- Regarding residence, the wife follows the husband. «انت و زوجك» (You and your spouse) (285).

2- In the method of education, whenever you wish to restrain someone from an action or a thing, first the correct paths should be left open, and then the prohibition should be announced. First He said: «كُلا منها رغداً حیث شئتما» (Eat freely thereof wherever you wish), then He said: «لا تقربا» (Do not approach).

3- Approaching sin is the same as falling into the lap of sin. «لا تقربا فتكونا» (Do not approach, lest you become).

4- Violating Divine guidance is oppression against oneself. «فتكونا من الظّالمین» (Lest you become among the wrongdoers). Adam and his wife also said when repenting: «ظلمنا انفسنا» (We have wronged ourselves). Of course, considering the infallibility (‘ismah) of the Prophets, the intent of oppression in such cases is tark al-awla (leaving the preferable course of action).

285) In the matter of command and prohibition, temptation, and expulsion from Paradise, Adam and his wife were addressed equally: «كلا، شئتما، لاتقربا، تكونا، أزلّهما، اخرجهما» (Eat, you both wish, do not approach, you both become, He made them both slip, He expelled them both). However, regarding the matter of residence, instead of saying «اسكنا» (You both reside), He said: «اُسكن انت و زوجك...» (Reside, you and your spouse...).

Ayah

فَأَزَلَّهُمَا ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ عَنْهَا فَأَخْرَجَهُمَا مِمَّا كَانَا فِيهِ ۖ وَقُلْنَا ٱهْبِطُوا۟ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ ۖ وَلَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمَتَـٰعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (۳٦)
Then Satan caused them to stumble from it, and he dislodged them from what [state] they were in; and We said, ‘Get down, being enemies of one another! On the earth shall be your abode and sustenance for a time.’ (36)

Key Points

| From the Ayah, «انّى جاعل فى الارض خلیفة» (Indeed, I am going to place a vicegerent on the earth), which we read previously, it is understood that the primary purpose of Adam's creation was for him to live on earth. However, life on earth required a certain type of preparation, and Adam needed to know that: 1- He is not absolutely free. In his life, there are commands, prohibitions, and responsibilities (taklif). 2- Iblis is his enemy and may deceive man, perhaps through oaths and false promises. 3- Obedience to Satan is the cause of his downfall. 4- Repentance (Tawbah) is the means of compensation (or rectification). Imam Reza عليه السلام says: The incident of Adam's slip occurred before he attained the station of prophethood, and it was among the minor slips that are pardonable. [286] To cause the slip, Satan employed every type of psychological and propaganda tactic, including: A: He swore an oath to Adam and Eve. «قاسمهما» [287] B: He adopted a benevolent appearance and said: I am one of your well-wishers. «انّى لكما لمن النّاصحین» [288] C: He promised eternity, saying that if you eat this plant, you will have eternal life and dominion. «شجرة الخلد و ملك لا یبلى» [289] D: He lied and slandered the Divine Essence (of Allah). «مانهیكما ربّكما عن هذه الشجرة الا ان تكونا ملكین او تكونا من الخالدین» [290] Meaning: Your Lord only forbade you from this tree lest you become angels or attain eternal life.

286) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 50. 287) Al-A'raf, 21. 288) Al-A'raf, 21. 289) Taha, 120. 290) Al-A'raf, 20.

Messages

1- The danger of Satan exists even for the great ones. He approached Adam and Eve. «فاَزلّهما»

2- Satan is the ancient enemy of the human race. For, from the very first day, he went after our father and mother. «فاَزلّهما»

3- Man, by his very nature, is prone to error and susceptible to temptation. «اَزلّهما الشیطان»

4- Every human being is destined for Paradise due to the talents and merits he possesses, but transgressions cause his downfall. «فاخرجهما»

5- Let us learn a lesson from disobedience to Allah and the bitter consequences of Iblis's temptations. Falling under the influence of Satan is equivalent to exiting the Divine stations and being deprived of them. «فازلّهما الشیطان... فاخرجهما»

6- Life in this world is temporary. «الى حین»

Ayah

فَتَلَقَّىٰٓ ءَادَمُ مِن رَّبِّهِۦ كَلِمَـٰتٍ فَتَابَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۳۷)
Then Adam received certain words from his Lord, and He turned to him clemently. Indeed He is the All-clement, the All-merciful. (37)

Key Points

When Adam tasted the food he should not have eaten, and was deprived of all those blessings and comforts, he realized His mistake. Due to his regret and remorse, he received certain words (kalimāt) from Allah, and through those words, he repented. [291] According to what is understood from both Shi'a and Sunni narrations, the meaning of the words through which Adam repented is seeking intercession (tawassul) through the best of Allah's creation, namely, the Prophet Muhammad and his Household (Ahl al-Bayt) علیه وعلیهم السلام. Just as it is narrated in Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr from Ibn Abbas that, for the acceptance of his repentance, Adam swore by Allah using these names: «بحّق محمّد وعلىّ و فاطمة و الحسن و الحسین» [292] Of course, some believe that the meaning of the words is the phrase mentioned in Sūrat al-A'rāf: «ربّنا ظلمنا أنفسنا و ان لم تغفر لنا و ترحمنا لنكوننّ من الخاسرین» [293] O God! We have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we shall surely be among the losers.

291) Repentance (Tawbah) means return. If we attribute repentance to Allah, it means the return of His grace and mercy upon the sinful and remorseful servant; »هو التوّاب« (He is the Acceptor of Repentance). And if we attribute it to the servant, it means the return of the erring human toward Allah. »فتاب علیه« (So He turned to him). 292) Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr, Vol. 1, p. 60. 293) Al-A'rāf, 23.

Messages

1- Just as the success (tawfīq) of repentance comes from Allah, we must also receive the method and path of repentance from Him. «من ربّه كلمات»

2- The acceptance of repentance and the presentation of its path are among the affairs of His nurturing and Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «من ربّه»

3- All this grace and acceptance of repentance is solely the work of Allah. «هو»

4- If the repentance is genuine, Allah accepts it. «هو التّواب»

5- Even if we break the repentance, Allah still accepts repentance. «هو التوّاب»

6- Allah's acceptance of excuses is accompanied by mercy, not reproach or blame. «التّواب الرّحیم»

Ayahs

قُلْنَا ٱهْبِطُوا۟ مِنْهَا جَمِيعًا ۖ فَإِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُم مِّنِّى هُدًى فَمَن تَبِعَ هُدَاىَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (۳۸)
We said, ‘Get down from it, all together! Yet, should any guidance come to you from Me, those who follow My guidance shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve. (38)
وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۳۹)
But those who are faithless and deny Our signs, they shall be the inmates of the Fire and they shall remain in it [forever]. (39)

Key Points

Although Adam's confession of error and repentance caused Allah to forgive him, he will not return to Paradise again after repentance. This is because the consequential effects of the slip are separate from the Divine forgiveness. The command for descent (hubūṭ) in the previous few verses was accompanied by a kind of wrath, but in this verse, since it comes after Adam's repentance, it is presented in an ordinary manner. It also gives hope that if they follow the Divine guidance, they will have no sorrow or grief.

Messages

  1. Sometimes, the scope of influence (good and bad) of a single action extends across all ages and generations. «اهبطوا منها جمیعاً»
  2. One should not reject someone due to a single slip or error, because man is capable of guidance and accepting direction. «یأتینكم منّى هدىً»
  3. The coming of the prophets for the guidance of humanity is certain. «یأتینّكم»
  4. True guidance is only from Allah. «منّى هدىً»
  5. True security and tranquility are achieved through following the religion and the commands of Allah. «فمن تبع هداى فلا خوف علیهم و لا هم یحزنون»

Ayah

يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتِىَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَوْفُوا۟ بِعَهْدِىٓ أُوفِ بِعَهْدِكُمْ وَإِيَّـٰىَ فَٱرْهَبُونِ (٤٠)
O Children of Israel, remember My blessing which I bestowed upon you, and fulfill My covenant that I may fulfill your covenant, and be in awe of Me [alone]. (40)

Key Points

Israel is another name for Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob), which is composed of two words: "Isr" and "Il." "Isr" means servant (or slave), and "Il" means God (Allah). Therefore, linguistically, Israel means "Servant of God."

The history of the Children of Israel, their captivity by the Pharaohs, their salvation by the hand of Prophet Musa عليه السلام, and their excuses and apostasy have created a unique destiny and history for this nation. This history is also instructive and admonitory for Muslims, who, according to narrations, will face the same destiny if they are not vigilant.

Divine covenants include the rulings of the heavenly books and the innate covenants that Allah has taken from everyone. In the Quran, the issue of Imamate (leadership) is referred to as a covenant: «لاینال عهدى الظالمین» [294], meaning My covenant (the station of Imamate) does not reach the unjust individuals. Thus, fulfilling the covenant means fulfilling the commitment to the Imam and the heavenly leader, and obeying him.

Furthermore, we read in narrations: Prayer (Salat) is a Divine covenant. [295]

294) Baqarah, 124.

295) Wasā'il, Vol. 4, p. 110.

Messages

1- Remembering the blessings of the Lord leads to love for Him and obedience to Him. «اذكروا... اوفوا»

When inviting towards Allah, mention His favors so that the ground for acceptance is prepared. «اذكروا»

2- Remembering and mentioning blessings is obligatory. «اذكروا»

3- The blessings given to ancestors are considered equivalent to blessings given to the person himself. Although the blessings were given to the ancestors of the Jews during the time of the Prophet, Allah tells their descendants not to forget them. «اذكروا»

4- Fulfilling Divine covenants is obligatory. «عهدى»

5- Benefiting from Allah's favors is conditional upon taking steps in the path of Divine duties. «اوفوا بعهدى اوف بعهدكم» Yes, if we are obedient to Allah, He will also answer our prayers. «اوفوا بعهدى اوف بعهدكم»

6- In performing Divine duties, we should not fear any power or show consideration for anyone. The enemy's negative propaganda, blame, threats, and conspiracies are not important; Allah's wrath is the most important of all. «و ایّاى فارهبون»

Ayah

وَءَامِنُوا۟ بِمَآ أَنزَلْتُ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا مَعَكُمْ وَلَا تَكُونُوٓا۟ أَوَّلَ كَافِرٍۭ بِهِۦ ۖ وَلَا تَشْتَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِى ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا وَإِيَّـٰىَ فَٱتَّقُونِ (٤۱)
And believe in that which I have sent down confirming that which is with you, and do not be the first ones to deny it, and do not sell My signs for a paltry gain, and be wary of Me [alone]. (41)

Key Points

This Ayah addresses the Jewish scholars, stating: The Quran is consistent with the glad tidings that are in your Torah. [296] You scholars were once propagators and awaiters of the advent of Islam. Now, do not become the pioneers of disbelief, lest, by following you, the rest of the Jews refuse to embrace Islam. Do not sell the divine verses for the meager price of worldly leadership, and fear Allah.

296) The meaning of the consistency of the Quran with the untransformed Torah is general and overall consistency, not consistency in all rulings.

Messages

1- We should accept the correct beliefs of the people so that they, too, accept our words. «مصدّقاً لِما معكم»

2- The existence of distortion in a part of the Torah and the Gospel should not prevent the acceptance of other parts. «مصدقاً لما معكم»

3- The deviation of scholars causes the deviation of others. «ولا تكونوا اوّل كافر»

4- The secret behind the inappropriate silence and concealment by scholars is their material attachments. «ولا تشتروا بأیاتى ثمناً قلیلاً»

5- Considering that the enjoyment of the world is meager (qaleel), attaining all worldly benefits is not worth a single moment of deviation. «ثمناً قلیلاً»

6- Instead of worrying about losing wealth and status, one must fear the wrath of Allah. «ایّاى فاتّقون»

Ayah

وَلَا تَلْبِسُوا۟ ٱلْحَقَّ بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ وَتَكْتُمُوا۟ ٱلْحَقَّ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (٤۲)
Do not mix the truth with falsehood, nor conceal the truth while you know. (42)

Key Points

The word lubs (لُبْس) means covering, and labs (لَبْس) means doubt and uncertainty. Man's distinction lies in his knowledge, and those who conceal the truth from people by creating doubt, temptation, and mischief, thereby taking away correct knowledge from them, have in reality taken away the unique distinction of being human, and this is the greatest oppression. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: If falsehood is presented purely, there is no worry (because people become aware and abandon it). And if the truth is also presented purely, the tongue of the opponent is silenced. However, the danger lies where truth and falsehood are mixed together, and a part of each is presented in such a way that the ground is prepared for Satan's dominance over his followers. [297]

297) Nahj al-Balaghah, Discourse 49.

Messages

1- We should neither mix the truth with falsehood and alter it, nor present falsehood in the guise of truth. «لا تلبسوا الحقّ، لا تكتموا الحقّ»

2- Conscience and innate nature (fitrah) are the best witnesses to man's concealment of the truth. «وانتم تعلمون»

Ayah

وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَٱرْكَعُوا۟ مَعَ ٱلرَّٰكِعِينَ (٤۳)
And maintain the prayer, and give the zakat, and bow along with those who bow [in prayer]. (43)

Messages

1- Following the call to faith is the call to righteous action. «آمنوا... اقیموا»

2- Prayer (Salat) and Zakat (charity) were also present in the Jewish religion. «اقیموا الصلوة و اتوا الزكوة»

3- The relationship with Allah through prayer (Salat), helping Allah's creation through Zakat, and accompanying others form a sacred triangle. «اقیموا، اتوا، اركعوا»

4- The fundamental command regarding prayer is with congregation (Jama'ah). The foundation of religion rests upon presence in society and avoidance of isolation and seclusion. «واركعوا مع الراكعین»

Ayah

۞ أَتَأْمُرُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ بِٱلْبِرِّ وَتَنسَوْنَ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَتْلُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (٤٤)
Will you bid others to piety and forget yourselves, while you recite the Book? Do you not exercise your reason? (44)

Key Points

Jewish scholars, before the advent of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, used to invite people to believe in him and gave glad tidings of His appearance, but when He appeared, they themselves did not believe. Some of them even advised their relatives who had embraced Islam to remain Muslim, but they themselves did not embrace Islam. [298]

It is narrated in traditions: The scholar who invites others to Paradise, but is himself destined for Hell, will experience the greatest and most severe regrets. [299]

The right of recitation (of the Scripture) is action. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «كونوا دعاة النّاس باعمالكم و لاتكونوا دعاةً بالسنتكم» [300] Invite people with your actions, not just with your words.

It is narrated from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام that he said: I swear that I do not invite you to any deed unless I am the first to perform it, and I do not forbid you from any deed unless I have abandoned it before you. [301]

It is stated in Nahj al-Balaghah: Whoever appoints himself as the leader (Imam) of others must first begin by educating himself. [302]

Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: «طوبى للعلماء بالفعل و ویل للعلماء بالقول» [303] Blessings upon the scholars who act upon their words, and woe upon the scholars who only speak.

The scholar without action in the form of metaphor

A: In the Quran: The scholar without action is a donkey carrying a load of books, but deriving no benefit from them. [304]

B: In traditions: * The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: The scholar without action is like a lamp that burns itself out, but its light reaches the people. [305] * The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: The scholar without action is like an archer without a bow. [306] * Jesus عليه السلام: The scholar without action is like a lamp on a rooftop while the rooms below are dark. [307] * Ali عليه السلام: The scholar without action is like a fruitless tree and a treasure that is not spent (in charity). [308] * Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: The admonition of a scholar without action is like rain upon a stone, which does not penetrate the hearts. [309]

C: In the words of thinkers: * The scholar without action: He is a hungry person sleeping on a treasure. He is a thirsty person standing beside water and the sea. He is a physician who himself groans from pain. He is a patient who constantly reads the prescription for cure but does not act upon it. He is a hypocrite whose words and deeds are not one. He is a body without a soul.

298) Namuneh, Vol. 1, p. 214. 299) Bihar, Vol. 2, p. 37. 300) Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 57. 301) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 175. 302) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 73. 303) Bihar, Vol. 78, p. 299. 304) Surah Al-Jumu'ah, 5. 305) Kanz al-'Ummal, H. 29109. 306) Bihar, Vol. 10, p. 100. 307) Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 309. 308) Ghurar al-Hikam. 309) Bihar, Vol. 2, p. 39.

Messages

1- Those who enjoin good must themselves practice good. «تأمرون الناس بالبرّ و تنسون انفسكم»

2- If we ourselves have prepared the grounds for forgetfulness, we are not excused. The excuse of forgetfulness is accepted only for one who is blameless. «تنسون, تتلون»

3- Recitation of the Divine Book is not enough; reflection (intellect) is necessary. «تتلون الكتاب افلا تعقلون»

4- Self-forgetfulness is a sign of lack of wisdom. «افلا تعقلون»

Ayah

وَٱسْتَعِينُوا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ وَٱلصَّلَوٰةِ ۚ وَإِنَّهَا لَكَبِيرَةٌ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱلْخَـٰشِعِينَ (٤٥)
And take recourse in patience and prayer, and it is indeed hard except for the humble (45)

Key Points

Although this Ayah follows the addresses directed to the Jews, its audience is all people. We read in narrations that whenever an important matter arose for Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, he would stand for prayer and recite this Ayah. It is narrated from the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله that patience (sabr) is of three types: 1- Patience in the face of calamity. 2- Patience against sin. 3- Patience regarding worship. [310] Therefore, what is mentioned in narrations—that the intent of patience (sabr) in this Ayah is fasting—refers to one of the manifestations of patience. Prayer is the light of the eye for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and those like Him [311], but for some who lack humility (khushu'), it is a heavy burden. Humility (khushu') relates to the heart and soul, while submission (khudu') relates to the limbs of the body. Prayer performed with attention reminds man of the infinite power of Allah and makes everything other than Him appear small, whatever it may be. It increases His love in the heart, strengthens the spirit of reliance (tawakkul), and frees man from material dependencies. All these effects make man resistant in the face of difficulties. Patience and resistance are the key to all acts of worship. The angels greet the inhabitants of Paradise, but not because of prayer, Hajj, or Zakat, but because of their resistance and steadfastness: «سلام علیكم بما صبرتم» [312] Because if there is no resistance, prayer, Jihad, Hajj, and Zakat will also not exist. Even the condition for reaching the station of divine guidance and leadership is patience: «جعلنا منهم ائمّةً یهدون بأمرنا لمّا صبروا» [313] A memory concerning a poet named Najashi, who was one of the admirers of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, who drank wine during the month of Ramadan. By the Imam's order, he was given eighty lashes, and twenty additional lashes were administered for committing the sin during the month of Ramadan. He became upset, joined Mu'awiyah, and composed poetry against the Hadrat (Ali)! Najashi's associates, who belonged to an important tribe in Kufa, complained to Ali عليه السلام, asking why he did not retain his companions. Tariq ibn Abdullah, their chief, angrily told the Imam: Why do you not differentiate between friend and stranger? A person like Najashi should not be lashed; we will not tolerate such behavior. The Imam, in response, recited this Ayah: «و انّها لكبیرة الاّ على الخاشعین» Tariq also joined Mu'awiyah. Therefore, although the Ayah is about prayer, one can seek help from the verses of the Quran in similar situations.

310) Bihar, Vol. 71, p. 77. 311) Bihar, Vol. 16, p. 249. 312) Ra'd, 24. 313) Sajdah, 24.

Messages

1- Patience and prayer are two powerful levers against difficulties. «استعینوا بالصبر و...»

2- The more we express helplessness and servitude at the threshold of Allah, the more we will receive His aid and overcome difficulties. «استعینوا بالصبر و الصلوة»

3- Seeking help from Allah in the Ayah «ایّاك نستعین» does not contradict seeking help from that which is by His command. «استعینوا بالصبر»

4- The heaviness of prayer is a sign of arrogance before Allah. «لكبیرة الاّ على الخاشعین»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِ رَٰجِعُونَ (٤٦)
—those who are certain that they will encounter their Lord and that they will return to Him. (46)

Key Points

“Liqā’” (meeting/encounter) does not mean seeing, but rather means presence. Just as if a blind person goes into someone’s presence, he says he met him, even though he has not seen him. The author of Majma‘ al-Bayān also says: The intent of meeting his Lord is meeting with Allah’s reward and punishment. Perhaps the intent of meeting the Lord is the same mystical state that the humble ones (khāshi‘ūn) find in prayer, because prayer is presence before Allah. If ẓann (conjecture) and assumption are against knowledge, it is subject to criticism; «مالهم به من علم ان یتّبعون الاّ الظنّ» (314) But if the intent of assumption is assurance, it is valuable, even if it is not strong certainty. Just as Allah has also referred to the brave and sincere fighters as the possessors of assumption: «قال الذّین یظنّون انّهم ملاقوا اللّه كم من فئة قلیلة غلبت فئةً كثیرةً» (315)

314) Najm, 28. 315) Baqarah, 249.

Messages

1- Even the assumption of the existence of the Day of Judgment is sufficient for controlling behaviors. «یظنّون انّهم ملاقوا...»

2- The humble one feels the sense of meeting with Allah in prayer. «الخاشعین الّذین یظنّون انّهم ملاقوا ربّهم»

Ayah

يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتِىَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَنِّى فَضَّلْتُكُمْ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٤۷)
O Children of Israel, remember My blessing which I bestowed upon you, and that I gave you an advantage over all the nations. (47)

Key Points

This Ayah asks the children and descendants of Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob) to remember those gifts and blessings in order to gain greater knowledge of Allah, revive the spirit of gratitude, and be encouraged by divine favors. Of course, the superiority and virtue of the Children of Israel were relative to the people of their own time. Because the Quran says about the Muslims: «كنتم خیر امّة» [316] (You are the best of nations). It is also possible that the intent of superiority is the victory of Prophet Musa (Moses) and the Children of Israel over the Pharaohites, not moral or ideological superiority. [317] Because the Quran repeatedly criticizes their baseless excuses and lack of faith.

(316) Al ‘Imran, 110. (317) Jathiyah, 16.

Messages

1- Blessing and virtue are in the hand of Allah. «نعمتى، انعمت، فضّلت»

2- Salvation from the domination of the tyrant (Taghut) is among the greatest divine blessings. «نعمتى، فضّلتكم على العالمین»

Ayah

وَٱتَّقُوا۟ يَوْمًا لَّا تَجْزِى نَفْسٌ عَن نَّفْسٍ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يُقْبَلُ مِنْهَا شَفَـٰعَةٌ وَلَا يُؤْخَذُ مِنْهَا عَدْلٌ وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ (٤۸)
Beware of the day when no soul will compensate for another, neither any intercession shall be accepted from it, nor any ransom shall be received from it, nor will they be helped. (48)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to some of the false beliefs and misplaced hopes of the Jews. They imagined that their ancestors would intercede for them on the Day of Judgment, just as idolaters considered idols to be their intercessors. Some of them considered sacrificing to be an atonement for sins, and if they lacked money, they would slaughter a pair of doves. A group of them would bury gold and jewelry with the dead so that the deceased could pay it as a penalty for his sins.

They are heedless that the Day of Judgment is unlike this world, where some overcome their problems through money, connections, or gaining support from powers.

On the Day of Judgment, all means are severed; «تقطّعت بهم الأسباب» (318) and relationships are erased; «فلا أنساب بینهم» (319) and the tongue of apology is silenced; «فلا یؤذن لهم فیعتذرون» (320) wealth and children are useless; «لا ینفع مالٌ و لا بنون» (321) and kinship yields no benefit; «لن تنفعكم أرحامكم» (322) powers become void; «هلك عنّى سلطانیه» (323) and intercession is not accepted without the permission of Allah; «و لا یشفعون الاّ لمن ارتضى» (324) and ransom is not accepted. «لایؤخذ منكم فدیه» (325)

A Discussion on Intercession

Intercession (Shafa'ah) comes from the word shaf', meaning 'pair' or 'couple'. This means that individuals who possess a foundation of faith, piety, and deeds, but have a deficiency on that Day, grace is added to that foundation, and by pairing and accompanying the grace of the Divine Saints, they are saved from the wrath of Allah. Therefore, intercession only includes those who strive but have fallen short and need a power to stand beside them.

Approximately thirty verses of the Quran relate to intercession, which are divided into several categories:

  1. Verses that negate intercession, such as: «یومٌ لا بیعٌ فیه ولا خلّةٌ ولا شفاعةٌ» (326), a Day wherein there is no trade, nor friendship, nor intercession.

  2. Verses that restrict intercession exclusively to Allah, such as: «مالكم من دونه من ولّى ولا شفیع» (327), besides Him you have no protector or intercessor.

  3. Verses that mention the intercession of others with the permission of Allah, such as: «من ذا الذّى یشفع عنده الاّ باذنه» (328), Who is there that can intercede with Him except by His permission?

  4. Verses that state the conditions for those who are interceded for, such as:

A: Intercession is for those whom God approves. «ولا یشفعون الا لمن ارتضى» (329)

B: There is no friend or intercessor for the oppressors. «ما للظّالمین من حمیم ولا شفیع» (330)

C: Angels pray and seek forgiveness for those who have believed. «ویستغفرون للّذین آمنوا» (331)

Considering the above verses, it becomes clear that intercession is not unconditional, and intercession is by the permission and leave of Allah, and the one interceded for must meet certain conditions. If someone does not meet the conditions for intercession, even if she is the wife of the Prophet of God, she will not be interceded for. Just as the wives of Noah and Lot (عليهما السلام) were not included in intercession due to their transgression. «فخانتا هما فلم یغنیا عنهما من اللّه شیئاً و قیل ادخلا الّنار مع الدّاخلین» (332)

Therefore, intercession is constructive when it links a person to the Saints of God and prevents him from despair. But if belief in intercession leads to audacity in sin, just as Christians believe that Jesus was sacrificed so that our sins might be forgiven, it is never acceptable.

Question

Does the intercession of the Saints of God mean standing against the will of God? If God wills to punish someone, does the Prophet prevent that will through his intercession?

Answer

Both the punishment of the wicked and the permission for His Saints to intercede are His will and desire. Therefore, the intercession of the Saints of God is not something contrary to the Divine Will.

Question

Does the intercession of the Saints of God mean that their mercy is greater than God's? Because God wills to punish, and His Saints intercede?

Answer

Both the existence of mercy in the Saints of God and the permission to use it come from God. Therefore, it is He Who grants permission for intercession through His mercy and grace.

Question

Does intercession change the will of God?

Answer

The will of Allah is not uniform under different conditions. The will of God is to punish the sinner, but if he repents, He removes His wrath, because the condition of a sinner differs from that of a penitent. A person receives intercession due to love, devotion, and obedience to the Saints of God in this world, while another who opposed them does not.

The means of forgiveness for sins in this world are three:

  1. Repentance. «الاّ الّذین تابوا واصلحوا وبیّنوا فاولئك اتوب علیهم وأنا التّواب الرّحیم» (333)

  2. Avoiding major sins. «ان تجتنبوا كبائر ما تنهون عنه نكفّر عنكم سیّئاتكم» (334)

  3. Good deeds. «انّ الحسنات یُذهبنَ السیّئات» (335)

But in the Hereafter, the only path to forgiveness is intercession.

Messages

  1. Fear of appearing in the Divine Court, without the presence of any lawyer or intercessor, is the basis for strengthening Divine piety (Taqwa). «واتّقوا یوماً»

  2. Confront superstitious and false beliefs with decisiveness. «لایقبل، لا یؤخذ»

Ayah

وَإِذْ نَجَّيْنَـٰكُم مِّنْ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ يُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْنَآءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَآءَكُمْ ۚ وَفِى ذَٰلِكُم بَلَآءٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ (٤۹)
[Remember] when We delivered you from Pharaoh’s clan who inflicted a terrible torment on you, and slaughtered your sons and spared your women, and in that there was a great test from your Lord. (49)

Key Points

"Pharaoh" (فرعون) was the title of the kings of Rome, "Chosroes" (كسرى) was the title of the kings of Persia, and "Khagan" (خاقان) was the title of the kings of the Turks. The Pharaoh of the time of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) was named Ramses I, whose body, it is said, is kept in the Cairo Museum. «فالیوم ننجّیك ببدنك لتكون لمن خلفك آیة» [336] The word «یسومونكم» is derived from the root sawm (سوم), meaning to continuously pursue something, and «یسومونكم سوء العذاب» means they continuously afflicted you with grievous torment. One of the torments and tortures was the killing of the sons. This was either due to a dream whose interpretation had warned Pharaoh that he would be destroyed by a man from the Children of Israel, or it was to prevent the growth of the Children of Israel.

336) Yunus, 92.

Messages

1- Freedom from the domination of tyrants is one of the greatest blessings, just as Allah mentioned it among all blessings. «نجّیناكم» (We saved you).

2- Recounting the hardships and bitterness of the past multiplies the pleasure of today's freedom. «یسومونكم ... یذّبحون ... یستحیون»

3- Tyrants have no power without their entourage. «آل فرعون» (Pharaoh's people).

4- Hardship and freedom are both means of trial and training. «بلاءٌ من ربّكم» (A trial from your Lord).

5- Eliminating defensive forces and sparing forces of comfort (or luxury) is the work of a Pharaoh. «یُذبّحون ابنائكم و یستحیون نسائكم» (They slaughter your sons and spare your women).

6- Tyrants resort to every kind of torture to maintain their regime and dominance. «یسومونكم, یُذبّحون».

Ayah

وَإِذْ فَرَقْنَا بِكُمُ ٱلْبَحْرَ فَأَنجَيْنَـٰكُمْ وَأَغْرَقْنَآ ءَالَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ (٥٠)
And when We parted the sea with you, and We delivered you and drowned Pharaoh’s clan as you looked on. (50)

Key Points

The story of Bani Israel crossing the Nile River has been mentioned in several Surahs of the Quran. [337] Allah caused them to cross the sea-like Nile River by splitting the water through the striking of the staff of Prophet Moses عليه السلام. In this Ayah, three divine miracles are presented together: A: The splitting of the sea. B: The salvation of Bani Israel. C: The drowning of the Pharaoh's people.

337) Taha, 77; Ash-Shu'ara, 63; Ad-Dukhan, 24.

Messages

1- The role of means (causes) depends on the wise Will of Allah. Moses has a staff that sometimes causes water to gush forth from stone; «فاضرب بعصاك الحجر فانفجرت» and sometimes strikes the water, opening a path through it. «فرقنا بكم البحر»

2- The end of the dark night is bright (relief follows hardship). Bani Israel achieved prosperity and salvation after all that torture and affliction. «فانجیناكم»

3- Vengeance against the oppressors in the presence of the oppressed is a balm for them. «انتم تنظرون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ وَٰعَدْنَا مُوسَىٰٓ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً ثُمَّ ٱتَّخَذْتُمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ وَأَنتُمْ ظَـٰلِمُونَ (٥۱)
And when We made an appointment with Moses for forty nights, you took up the Calf [for worship] in his absence, and you were wrongdoers. (51)

Key Points

The name of Prophet Moses (Musa) عليه السلام is mentioned in thirty-four Surahs of the Quran and 136 times. The story of the appointed time (Mi'ad) of Prophet Moses is also mentioned in Surahs Al-A'raf and Taha. [338] The location of the appointment was Mount Tur (Sinai), and its duration was initially set at thirty nights. At the appointed time, ten more nights were added to it. This duration was for receiving the Book of the Torah. However, despite having a leader like Aaron (Harun), the brother of Prophet Moses, among them, the Children of Israel forgot all the blessings and divine favors and turned to calf-worship. A golden calf that had been made by an artistic sculptor named Samiri, in the absence of Moses. It is understood from the verses of the Quran that three issues were intended during this appointment: receiving the Torah, establishing the position of vicegerency (Khilafah) for Aaron, and testing the Children of Israel. Several factors played a role in the fall of the Israelite community into polytheism (Shirk): A: The absence of a leader like Prophet Moses. B: The presence of artistic deviants like Samiri. C: The use of gold and attractive ornaments. D: Loud propaganda horns (or noise). Because Samiri's calf made a sound. E: Simple, gullible people and the lack of deep faith. F: Favorable conditions and a history of cow worship.

338) Al-A'raf, 142; Taha, 86.

Messages

1- The temporary withdrawal of divine leaders from society for the purpose of worshipping Allah is a worthy and good action. [339] «واعدنا موسى اربعین لیلة»

2- Worship during forty nights, away from people, has special effects. «اربعین لیلة»

3- The number forty plays a role in receiving revelation (Wahy) and spiritual inspirations. «اربعین لیلة»

4- Polytheism (Shirk) is an injustice to humanity. Because man removes his hand from the Hand of Allah and places it in the hands of the helpless and unworthy. «و انتم ظالمون»

339) Tafsir Rahnuma.

Ayah

ثُمَّ عَفَوْنَا عَنكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (٥۲)
Then We excused you after that so that you might give thanks. (52)

Key Points

Repentance from polytheism (Shirk) is believing and declaring the two testimonies (Shahadatayn). This is because the mission of the prophets is for the salvation of humanity from polytheism and disbelief (Kufr). Therefore, the Ayah, «انّ اللّه لا یغفر أن یشرك به» [340], which states: Allah does not forgive polytheism, relates to those who die while in a state of polytheism and do not return to Monotheism (Tawhid) and the worship of the One God.

340) Nisa, 48 and 116.

Messages

1- Even for polytheism (Shirk) and calf-worship, the path of repentance and return exists. «ثم عفونا عنكم»

2- Divine forgiveness (Afw) is itself a basis for gratitude (Shukr). «عفونا... لعلّكم تشكّرون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْفُرْقَانَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (٥۳)
And when We gave Moses the Book and the Criterion so that you might be guided. (53)

Key Points

Furqan is a means that separates the truth from falsehood. Since the truth is separated from falsehood in the heavenly Book, it is called Furqan. Perhaps the intent behind Furqan, considering that it is mentioned alongside the Book, is the nine miracles or other truths that were granted to Prophet Moses besides the Torah.

Messages

1- Heavenly Books express the truth and are the source of its separation from falsehood. «الكتاب والفرقان»

2- The proof from Allah is complete, but people sometimes do not accept the truth due to their whims and desires. «لعلّكم»

3- The purpose of the revelation of heavenly Books is the guidance of people. «الكتاب... لعلكم تهتدون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنَّكُمْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنفُسَكُم بِٱتِّخَاذِكُمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ فَتُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ بَارِئِكُمْ فَٱقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ عِندَ بَارِئِكُمْ فَتَابَ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٥٤)
And [recall] when Moses said to his people, ‘O my people! You have indeed wronged yourselves by taking up the Calf [for worship]. Now turn penitently to your Maker, and slay [the guilty among] your folks. That will be better for you with your Maker.’ Then He turned to you clemently. Indeed, He is the All-clement, the All-merciful. (54)

Key Points

The meaning of «قتل نفس» (killing the self) in the phrase: «فاقتلوا انفسكم» is not suicide, but rather killing one another. Similar to the noble Ayah: «لا تلمزوا انفسكم» [341] (Do not defame one another). Or similar to the Ayah: «فسلّموا على انفسكم» [342], meaning, greet one another. Accepting this severe form of repentance was a virtue for the Jews. This is because Allah, in criticizing the hypocritical Muslims, says: «و لو انّا كتبنا علیهم ان اقتلوا انفسكم اواخرجوا من دیاركم ما فعلوه الاّ قلیلٌ منهم» [343] meaning: If We had made it obligatory upon them to kill one another or commanded them to leave their homes, only a few of them would have done it.

341) Al-Hujurat, 11. 342) An-Nur, 61. 343) An-Nisa, 66.

Messages

1- Prepare people with affection to accept divine limits (or ordinances). «یا قوم ... فاقتلوا»

2- Shirk (polytheism) is oppression against the self. «ظلمتم انفسكم باتّخاذكم العجل»

3- The more proofs, miracles, and evidence are presented, the heavier the obligation becomes, and the more dangerous the transgression. Calf-worship, after witnessing all those miracles, requires no repentance other than execution. «فتوبوا... فاقتلوا انفسكم»

4- The ruling for an apostate (murtadd) is execution. «فاقتلوا انفسكم»

5- Death within divine mercy is better than life under divine curse. «ذلكم خیرٌ لكم»

6- Implementing divine ordinances, even if it costs a person's life, is ultimately beneficial for him. «فاقتلوا... ذلكم خیر لكم»

7- The foundation of divine commands is to bring good (khayr) to humanity. «ذلكم خیر لكم»

8- For severe penalties, many blessings must be mentioned so that people are prepared to pay the price. «فاقتلوا ... ذلكم خیر، فتاب علیكم، انّه هو التّواب الرحیم»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّىٰ نَرَى ٱللَّهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ ٱلصَّـٰعِقَةُ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ (٥٥)
And when you said, ‘O Moses, we will not believe you until we see Allah visibly.’ Thereupon a thunderbolt seized you as you looked on. (55)

Key Points

The people of Prophet Moses split into two groups: A: A select group, who came with Moses عليه السلام to Mount Tur for supplication and to hear the Word of Allah, but when they heard the conversation between God and Moses, they said: How do we know this voice is from God? We must see God with our own eyes to accept it. B: The other group remained with Aaron, but in the absence of Moses, they became calf-worshippers. In Surah Al-A'raf (344), concerning those who demanded to see God, He says: «اخذتهم الرجفة» (the earthquake seized them). Perhaps the thunderbolt mentioned in this Ayah was accompanied by an earthquake, and they died by this thunderbolt. This is because the next Ayah says: We raised you up again after death.

The disposition of the questioners is not uniform. Moses and the people both requested to see God, but Moses, representing the people and with politeness, said: «أرنى» (Show me), but the people of Moses said with arrogance: «لن نؤمن لك حتّى نرى اللّه جهرة», (We will never believe you until we see Allah manifestly). Therefore, Allah said in response to Moses: «لن ترانى», (You shall never see Me). But against the people, He sent His wrath. This Ayah offers solace and comfort to the Prophet of Islam that he should not worry about the futile demands of the people, because the people made more dangerous demands of Moses.

(344) Al-A'raf, 154.

Messages

1- Take heed from the consequence of the obstinacy, audacity, impoliteness, and undue expectations of those who came before. «لن نؤمن... فاخذتكم»

2- For one group, logic, reasoning, and admonition are sufficient, but for another group, wrath and dominance are necessary. «فاخذتكم»

3- If Allah is not [visible] so that you may see Him, His signs are certainly visible. Why do you not believe upon seeing them? «فاخذتكم الصاعقة و انتم تنظرون»

Ayah

ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَـٰكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَوْتِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (٥٦)
Then We raised you up after your death so that you might give thanks. (56)

Key Points

The revival of those who perished due to the thunderbolt followed the distress, anxiety, and prayer of Prophet Moses عليه السلام, the discussion of which will come, ان شاءاللّه, in Surah Al-A'raf [345].

345) Al-A'raf, 155.

Messages

1- Return (Raj'ah) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad) are not impossible matters. Some of the dead have been revived in this very world. «بعثناكم من بعد موتكم»

2- The secret behind the occurrence of some hardships and then salvation from them is the emergence of a spirit of gratitude. «بعثناكم ... لعلكم تشكّرون»

Ayah

وَظَلَّلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْغَمَامَ وَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ ۖ كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ ۖ وَمَا ظَلَمُونَا وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (٥۷)
We shaded you with clouds, and sent down to you manna and quails [saying]: ‘Eat of the good things We have provided for you.’ And they did not wrong Us, but they used to wrong [only] themselves. (57)

Key Points

After the salvation of the Children of Israel from the domination of Pharaoh, they were commanded to enter the land of Palestine. They disobeyed, using the excuse that there were oppressors there, and they told Moses: Go fight them with your God; we are staying right here. The wrath of Allah overtook them, and they remained in the desert of "Tih" for forty years. But even there, Allah provided clouds for them as a canopy (shade) and made two types of natural and pleasant food, named Mann and Salwa, available to them. Ghamam refers to a rainless cloud that only provides shade. Rain-bearing clouds are called Sahab, Ghaym, and Muzn. Mann refers to the special sap of trees that settles upon them like drops, which is gum (resin). Some have interpreted it to mean honey or mushrooms. Salwa refers to specific birds that resemble pigeons.

Messages

1- Clouds, wind, and rain are under the command of Allah. «ظلّلنا علیكم الغمام»

2- Allah's provision (Raziq) is not limited to specific conditions. He sends natural sustenance even in a barren, waterless desert. «وانزلنا علیكم المنّ والسّلوى»

3- Allah has decreed the sustenance of man to be from lawful and pure things. «طیّبات ما رزقناكم»

4- Violation of divine commands is oppression against oneself. «انفسهم یظلمون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قُلْنَا ٱدْخُلُوا۟ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْقَرْيَةَ فَكُلُوا۟ مِنْهَا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمْ رَغَدًا وَٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْبَابَ سُجَّدًا وَقُولُوا۟ حِطَّةٌ نَّغْفِرْ لَكُمْ خَطَـٰيَـٰكُمْ ۚ وَسَنَزِيدُ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (٥۸)
And when We said, ‘Enter this town, and eat thereof freely whencesoever you wish, and enter while prostrating at the gate, and say, ‘‘Relieve [us of the burden of our sins],’’ so that We may forgive your iniquities and We will soon enhance the virtuous.’ (58)

Key Points

As mentioned in Surah Al-Ma'idah [346], the intent of al-qaryah (the town) in this Ayah is Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem). After forty years of wandering in the wilderness of Tih, the Children of Israel were commanded to enter the city of Bayt al-Maqdis and proceed to the sanctuary (temple). Upon entering the mosque, they were instructed to utter the blessed word ḥiṭṭah. Ḥiṭṭah means the shedding of sins, seeking forgiveness, and expressing repentance. In Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan, it is stated that the intent of al-bāb (the gate) is not the city gate, but the door of the mosque, which is still known today as Bāb al-Ḥiṭṭah (The Gate of Forgiveness). Furthermore, the intent of sujjadan (prostrating) is the prostration of gratitude (Sujud al-Shukr) after entering the mosque.

346) Al-Ma'idah, 21.

Messages

1- Providing for the livelihood of the people precedes the command for worship and is a prerequisite for it. «كلوا... سجّداً»

2- Special respect is necessary for entering sacred places. «ادخلوا...سجداً»

3- Forgiveness is from Him, but seeking forgiveness and pardon must come from us. «قولوا حطّة نغفر لكم»

4- We must also learn the etiquette of supplication and the method of repentance from Allah. «ادخلوا...سجّداً وقولوا حطّة»

5- Acting upon the commands of Allah is the basis for our forgiveness. «ادخلوا... قولوا... نغفر لكم»

6- Seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) is a source of pardon for the sinner and an elevation of rank for the righteous. «نغفر لكم خطاياكم و سنزید المحسنین»

Ayah

فَبَدَّلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ قَوْلًا غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى قِيلَ لَهُمْ فَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ رِجْزًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ (٥۹)
But the wrongdoers changed the saying with other than what they were told. So We sent down on those who were wrongdoers a plague from the sky because of the transgressions they used to commit. (59)

Key Points

In Ayahs 161 and 162 of Surah Al-A'raf, a similar passage to this Ayah and the previous Ayah is repeated, and the word "رجز" (rijz) is used to mean torment, the plague disease, and a state of anxiety.

The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is the descent of mercy, and for this reason, the best food, "منّ وسَلوى" (mann and salwa), was sent down for the Children of Israel. But due to their deviation, torment descends from the sky.

Messages

1- Oppression (zulm) and sin pave the way for the alteration and distortion of the law. «فبدّل الّذین ظلموا»

2- As long as the method and manner of action have not been specified, man is free to act according to his own opinion, but after the methods are stated, there is no excuse for changing them. «قیل لهم»

3- The recompense for those who distort Divine laws is wrath and torment. «رجزاً من السماء»

4- Not all rewards and punishments relate to the Hereafter; rather, some punishments occur in this very world. «فانزلنا... رجزاً من السماء»

5- If deviation becomes a habit for man, Divine wrath descends. « كانوا یفسقون»

Ayah

۞ وَإِذِ ٱسْتَسْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦ فَقُلْنَا ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْحَجَرَ ۖ فَٱنفَجَرَتْ مِنْهُ ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا ۖ قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ ۖ كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ مِن رِّزْقِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ (٦٠)
And when Moses prayed for water for his people, We said, ‘Strike the rock with your staff.’ Thereat twelve fountains gushed forth from it; every tribe came to know its drinking-place. ‘Eat and drink of Allah’s provision, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.’ (60)

Key Points

A secret is hidden in the number twelve. The number of months, the number of the chieftains (Naqibs) of the Children of Israel, the number of the disciples (Hawariyyun) of Prophet Jesus عليه السلام, and the number of the Infallible Imams علیهم السلام is twelve.

In the Ayah, the word "لاتعثوا" (do not commit mischief) is mentioned alongside the word "مفسدین" (mischief-makers). "عثوا" means severe corruption, and perhaps the juxtaposition of these two words intends to prohibit the intention, deliberation, and spirit of causing corruption. This is because an unintentional slip, without the intent to cause discord, is forgiven.

Messages

1- Prophets are also concerned with providing for the material needs of the people. «اذ استسقى موسى لقومه»

2- We should ask Allah for everything, even drinking water. «استسقى»

3- The laws of nature are subject to the Will of Allah. «اضرب... فانفجرت»

Allah is both the Creator of means (causes) and the Annihilator of means (causes). With one staff, and by the hand of one person, He dries up water one time and causes water to flow another time.

4- The prayers of the Prophets are answered. «استسقى، فانفجرت»

5- The flowing of water by striking the rock with a staff is one miracle, and the appearance of twelve springs for twelve tribes is another miracle. «اثنتا عشرة»

6- Organized, just, and calculated distribution leads to security and harmony, and prevents the emergence of conflict. «قد علم كل اناس مشربهم»

7- Utilizing divine blessings should not become a pretext for corruption. «كلوا... ولاتعثوا»

8- To prevent corruption, speak of the love and grace of Allah towards humanity. «كلوا و اشربوا... ولاتعثوا»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ لَن نَّصْبِرَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامٍ وَٰحِدٍ فَٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُخْرِجْ لَنَا مِمَّا تُنۢبِتُ ٱلْأَرْضُ مِنۢ بَقْلِهَا وَقِثَّآئِهَا وَفُومِهَا وَعَدَسِهَا وَبَصَلِهَا ۖ قَالَ أَتَسْتَبْدِلُونَ ٱلَّذِى هُوَ أَدْنَىٰ بِٱلَّذِى هُوَ خَيْرٌ ۚ ٱهْبِطُوا۟ مِصْرًا فَإِنَّ لَكُم مَّا سَأَلْتُمْ ۗ وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلذِّلَّةُ وَٱلْمَسْكَنَةُ وَبَآءُو بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا۟ وَّكَانُوا۟ يَعْتَدُونَ (٦۱)
And when you said, ‘O Moses, ‘We will not put up with one kind of food. So invoke your Lord for us, so that He may bring forth for us of that which the earth grows—its greens and cucumbers, its garlic, lentils, and onions.’ He said, ‘Do you seek to replace what is superior with that which is inferior? Go down to any town and you will indeed get what you ask for!’ So they were struck with abasement and poverty, and they earned Allah’s wrath. That, because they would deny the signs of Allah and kill the prophets unjustly. That, because they would disobey and commit transgressions. (61)

Key Points

Instead of thanking Allah for the blessings of "manna and quails" (mann wa salwa), the Children of Israel became preoccupied with excessive demands and the desire for variety. They requested earthly foods, naming some examples such as herbs, cucumbers, onions, and garlic. In response to these requests, Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), while regretting that they were exchanging good and superior blessings for simple ones, told them: If you intend to enjoy these things, you must go to the city and fight your enemies. On the one hand, you lack the spirit for Jihad (struggle), and on the other hand, you demand all the privileges of city life. Allah informs us that this nation, possessing such characteristics and traits, will fall into humiliation and disgrace and will be afflicted by divine wrath and anger. The desire for variety and excessive demands is a trap for enslaving human beings. Colonizers also exploit this characteristic of people—regarding clothing, housing, transportation, and luxuries—to lead people into captivity.

Messages

1- Gluttony (worship of the stomach) is a factor in the descent and downfall of human beings. «لن نصبرعلى طعام واحد...اهبطوا»

2- The pursuit of luxury paves the way for humiliation and disgrace. «لن نصبر... ضربت علیهم الذلّة...»

3- Impoliteness in speech is a sign of a rebellious and defiant spirit. Instead of saying «لن نصبر» (we will never endure), they could have said: The monotony of the food has become difficult for us. Or, instead of «فادع لنا ربّك» (call upon your Lord for us), they could have said: «فادع لنا ربّنا» (call upon our Lord for us).

4- The Children of Israel are a demanding and excessively ambitious race. «فادع لنا، یخرج لنا»

5- Expressing trivial demands is a sign of intense dependency and meanness of spirit. «بقل، قثاء، فوم، عدس و بصل»

6- Being content with what Allah has willed and having patience regarding it ensures the true good and welfare of man. «أتستبدلون الّذى هو ادنى بالّذى هو خیر...»

7- City life and possessing all kinds of facilities are not signs of growth and perfection; rather, they are sometimes causes of downfall and descent. «اهبطوا مصراً»

8- Dangerous actions are linked to dangerous and deviant thoughts. «یكفرون، یقتلون»

9- The history of the Prophets is intertwined with martyrdom in the way of Allah. «یقتلون النبیین»

10- Repeated transgression and disobedience lead to disbelief, and disbelief is the cause of every kind of crime. «یكفرون، عصوا»

11- Humiliation and wretchedness are not related to race, but rather to the characteristics, beliefs, and deeds of human beings. «ذلك بانّهم كانوا یكفرون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ وَٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰ وَٱلصَّـٰبِـِٔينَ مَنْ ءَامَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (٦۲)
Indeed the faithful, the Jews, the Christians and the Sabaeans—those of them who have faith in Allah and the Last Day and act righteously—they shall have their reward from their Lord, and they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (62)

Key Points

In Tafsir Namuneh, it is narrated from Jami' al-Bayan: Salman al-Farsi said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: My friends who were people of faith and prayer, but did not see you to believe in you, what will their status be on the Day of Judgment? One of those present replied: They are people of the Fire (Hell). But this Ayah was revealed, stating that every follower of the religions who acted according to their duties and the Divine command in their own era will be rewarded. Of course, this Ayah cannot be an excuse or pretext for remaining in Judaism or Christianity. Because, firstly: the Quran has invited the People of the Book to Islam, and secondly: it has warned that if they knowingly pursue a religion other than Islam, it will not be accepted. [347]

The meaning of "الّذین هادوا" (those who are Jews) is the Jews. This naming is either due to their expression of repentance, which came with the phrase "انّا هُدنا الیك" [348], or because this nation was attributed to one of the sons of Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob) who was named Yahudh (Judah).

The meaning of "نصارى" (Christians) is the Christians, who, in response to Prophet 'Isa (Jesus) when He said: "مَن أنصارى" (Who are My helpers?), replied: "نحن انصار اللّه" (We are the helpers of Allah). [349] Perhaps this naming is also due to their residence in the region of Nazareth (Nasiriyyah), the birthplace of 'Isa عليه السلام.

"صابئین" (Sabeans) refers to those who consider themselves followers of Prophet Yahya (John the Baptist) and attribute the power of governance (or planning) to the stars. The name of this group appears in Surahs Al-Baqarah, Al-Ma'idah, and Al-Hajj, alongside the Jews, Christians, Majus (Zoroastrians), and polytheists [350], and from this, it is clear that the Sabeans are distinct from the four aforementioned groups. The followers of this religion, like the rest of the People of the Book, have been invited to Islam. Due to their specific beliefs, they have few followers and do not engage in proselytizing their religion. They mostly live near rivers and seas and are reclusive and isolated. These individuals have multiple ritual baths (ghusls) that must be performed in rivers and running water, both in summer and winter. Currently, nearly five thousand of them live in Khuzestan, alongside the Karun River and in other cities of that province, and approximately eight thousand people reside in Iraq, alongside the Tigris (Dijlah) and in other cities of Iraq.

347) Al 'Imran, 85. 348) Al-A'raf, 156. 349) As-Saff, 14. 350) Al-Baqarah, 62; Al-Ma'idah, 69; Al-Hajj, 17.

Messages

  1. All heavenly religions share common principles: Monotheism (Tawhid), the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and performing righteous deeds. "آمن باللَّه و الیوم الاخر و عمل صالحاً"
  2. The most important doctrinal principle after Monotheism is the Resurrection. "ءامن باللَّه و الیوم الاخر"
  3. Followers of other religions, if they are unaware of Islam—whether before Islam or after Islam—are among those saved, provided they believe in their heavenly religion, perform righteous deeds, and are not negligent in their lack of awareness. "من آمن باللّه... لاخوف علیهم..."
  4. Man finds peace only in the light of faith in Allah, hope in the Resurrection, and the performance of righteous deeds. "لا خوف علیهم"
  5. Felicity and honor are related to faith and righteous deeds, not to the title of Muslim, Jew, Christian, or Sabean. "من آمن باللّه... لاخوف علیهم..."

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَـٰقَكُمْ وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَكُمُ ٱلطُّورَ خُذُوا۟ مَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰكُم بِقُوَّةٍ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ مَا فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (٦۳)
And when We took a pledge from you, and raised the Mount above you, [declaring], ‘Hold on with power to what We have given you and remember that which is in it so that you may be Godwary.’ (63)

Key Points

The incident of Mount Tur being uprooted from its place and positioned above the heads of the Jews is also mentioned in Surahs Al-Baqarah, [351] An-Nisa [352] and Al-A'raf. [353] Furthermore, perhaps the covenant mentioned in this Ayah is the same covenant that is mentioned in Surahs Al-Baqarah [354] and Al-Ma'idah. [355]

351) Al-Baqarah, 93.

352) An-Nisa, 154.

353) Al-A'raf, 171.

354) Al-Baqarah, 40 and 82.

355) Al-Ma'idah, 12.

Messages

1- Taking a covenant is one of the factors and motivations for action. «اخذنا میثاقكم»

2- Allah has completed the proof (Hujjah) upon the people both through sending the Prophet and by showing extraordinary deeds. «رفعنا فوقكم الطور»

3- Threatening is an educational tool for suppressing arrogant and stubborn spirits. «رفعنا فوقكم الطور»

4- Preserving the achievements of the revolution (deliverance from Pharaoh's domination, freedom from captivity, etc.) must be pursued with power and strength. «رفعنا فوقكم الطور»

5- Acting upon the Divine verses and decrees must be accompanied by seriousness, love, and determination, not jest, habit, doubt, or formality. «خذوا ما اتیناكم بقوة»

6- Religious knowledge must remain alive in the minds of the people through teaching and propagation. «اذكروا ما فیه»

7- Remembering the Divine verses and reflecting upon them paves the way for piety (Taqwa). «اذكروا ما فیه لعلكم تتقون»

Ayah

ثُمَّ تَوَلَّيْتُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ فَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ لَكُنتُم مِّنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (٦٤)
Again you turned away after that; and were it not for Allah’s grace on you and His mercy, you would have surely been among the losers. (64)

Messages

1- The heedless person forgets the most significant threats. «ثمّ تولّیتم من بعد ذلك»

2- Do not despair, because Allah deals with even the transgressors with grace and mercy. «فلو لا فضل اللّه علیكم»

3- Salvation from loss is due to Divine grace and mercy. «لولا فضل اللَّه علیكم و رحمته لكنت من الخاسرین»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱعْتَدَوْا۟ مِنكُمْ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ فَقُلْنَا لَهُمْ كُونُوا۟ قِرَدَةً خَـٰسِـِٔينَ (٦٥)
And certainly you know those of you who violated the Sabbath, whereupon We said to them, ‘Be you spurned apes.’ (65)

Key Points

One of the rulings of the Torah was the obligation to observe the Sabbath (Saturday) as a day of rest. However, greed and avarice drove a group of the Children of Israel to work, and using a trick which We will mention later, they worked on Saturdays. Therefore, Allah transformed the cunning individuals into apes, so that they might be a lesson for others. This incident is also mentioned in addition to this Ayah, in Surah Al-A'raf (7:163-166) [356]. Fundamentally, the transformation of the face (maskh) is one of the divine punishments and the realization of Allah's wrath. A group of Christians also committed disbelief after the descent of the heavenly table (Mā'idah), and they were transformed into apes and swine. «وجعل منهم القردة و الخنازیر» [357]

The word sabat (سَبت) means to cut off or cease from work. As He stated in another Ayah regarding the role of sleep: «وجعلنا نومكم سباتاً» [358]. Therefore, Saturday, the day of rest for the Jews, is called Yawm as-Sabt.
