Khāsi'īn (خاسئین) comes from the root khas'a (خسأ), meaning "to drive away" or "to repel." This word was initially used to drive away a dog, but later became used generally. In the Ayah, instead of using qiradah khāsi'ah (apes driven away), He used khāsi'īn (those driven away), which is the plural masculine adjective for rational beings. Perhaps this usage is because their bodies were transformed into apes, but not their human souls and intellects. For in this state, they would suffer greater torment.

Although some scholars, such as Al-Maraghi in his Tafsir, have considered the transformation into apes to be a metaphor, stating that this Ayah is similar to the Ayah «كمثل الحمار» (like the donkey) or «كالاَنعام» (like cattle). Meaning, their spiritual transformation (maskh ma'nawi) is intended, not a physical or apparent transformation (maskh sūri).

However, in Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan, a narration is cited from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله who said: Throughout history, Allah has transformed the faces of seven hundred nations due to their disbelief, and they were changed into thirteen types of animals. [359]

Just as we read in narrations, the scope of transformation (maskh) on the Day of Judgment will be broader, depending on the spiritual characteristics of the individuals. On the Day of Judgment, people will be resurrected in ten forms: 1. Those who spread rumors, in the form of apes. 2. Those who consume unlawful earnings (haram), in the form of swine. 3. Usurers (riba-eaters), upside down. 4. Unjust judges, blind. 5. Arrogant egoists, deaf and mute. 6. Scholars who do not practice what they preach, chewing their own tongues. 7. Those who harm their neighbors, with severed hands and feet. 8. Informers (gossips), suspended from branches of fire. 9. Hedonists (debauchees), smelling worse than a corpse. 10. The arrogant, covered in a shroud of fire. [360]

356) Al-A'raf, 163 to 166. 357) Al-Ma'idah, 60. 358) An-Naba', 9. 359) Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 787. 360) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, Nur ath-Thaqalayn, and Safi, concerning Ayah 18 of Surah An-Naba'.

Messages

  1. Take lessons from historical knowledge. «ولقد علمتم»
  2. Whoever abrogates the command of Allah has transformed himself. Changing and distorting the face of religion leads to the transformation of the face of humanity. «اعتدّوا... كونوا قِردة»
  3. Rest and worship must be part of the official schedule, and whoever occupies the time designated for rest, recreation, and worship with work is a transgressor. «اعتدّوا فى السبت»
  4. Whoever abandons the path of Allah will become ape-like, merely imitating others. «كونوا قِردة خاسئين»
  5. In the natural world, the transformation of one creature into another is possible. «كونوا قِردة»
  6. Animals are not far from the Mercy of Allah, but the transformation of a human into an animal is a sign of divine wrath and rejection. «كونوا قِردة خاسئين»

Ayah

فَجَعَلْنَـٰهَا نَكَـٰلًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهَا وَمَا خَلْفَهَا وَمَوْعِظَةً لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ (٦٦)
So We made it an exemplary punishment for the present and the succeeding [generations], and an advice to the Godwary. (66)

Key Points

Nakal (نكال) is a punishment whose effect remains and is visible so that others may see it and take a lesson. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of limā bayna yadayhā (لما بین یدیها) is the people contemporary to the descent of the affliction, and the meaning of mā khalfahā (ماخلفها) is the nations after the descent, which includes us Muslims as well.

Messages

1- Failures and victories must be a lesson for future generations. «نكالاً... لما خلفها»

2- Taking a lesson and accepting admonition requires possessing the spirit of piety (Taqwa). «موعظةً للمتّقین»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تَذْبَحُوا۟ بَقَرَةً ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ أَتَتَّخِذُنَا هُزُوًا ۖ قَالَ أَعُوذُ بِٱللَّهِ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ (٦۷)
And when Moses said to his people, ‘Allah commands you to slaughter a cow,’ they said, ‘Are you mocking us?’ He said, ‘I seek Allah’s protection lest I should be one of the ignorant!’ (67)

Key Points

This Surah has been named Surah Al-Baqarah (The Cow) because of this story. The command to slaughter a cow is mentioned in the Torah [361] as a judicial law. The summary of the incident is that a murdered person was found among the Children of Israel whose killer was unknown. Conflict and dispute began among the people, and every tribe accused another clan or tribe of the murder while exonerating themselves. They went to Prophet Moses (Musa) عليه السلام for judgment and to solve the problem. Moses عليه السلام told them: Allah has commanded you to slaughter a cow and strike the murdered person with a piece of its body so that he may come back to life and identify his killer. Upon hearing this answer, they said to Moses عليه السلام: Are you mocking us? Moses said: Mocking is the work of the ignorant (Jahilūn), and I seek refuge in Allah from being among the ignorant. In the culture of the Quran and narrations (Hadith), Jahl (ignorance) means lack of wisdom or folly, not lack of knowledge. Therefore, the word Jahl is used in contrast to Aql (intellect/reason), not in contrast to Ilm (knowledge). And since mocking others is a sign of folly, Prophet Moses sought refuge in Allah from it.

361) Torah, Deuteronomy, Chapter 21.

Messages

1- If Allah's command does not align with our minds or tastes, and we do not understand its secret, we must not deny it. «انّ اللّه یأمركم... أتتّخذنا هزواً»

2- Moses عليه السلام introduced the command to slaughter the cow as coming from Allah, hoping they would observe proper conduct and submit, but they still made excuses. «انّ اللّه یأمركم»

3- If Allah wills, a dead person can be brought back to life by the striking together of two dead things (the piece of the cow and the corpse). «تذبحوا بقرة»

4- In the slaughtering of the cow, the sanctity of the cow is crushed, similar to Abraham's عليه السلام idol-breaking and the burning of the golden calf by the Samiri. «تذبحوا بقرةً»

5- The degree of people's faith in their Prophet is understood from their reaction to his commands. «اتتّخذنا هزوا»

6- Isti'ādhah (seeking refuge) in Allah is one way of being protected. The infallibility (Ismah) of the Prophets is under the shadow of Isti'ādhah and similar acts. «اعوذ...»

7- Mocking is the work of ignorant and foolish people. «اكون من الجاهلین»

8- Jahl (folly/lack of wisdom) is a danger from which the friends of Allah seek refuge in Him. «اعوذ باللَّه ان اكون من الجاهلین»

9- In one incident, Allah proves Monotheism (Tawhid) through the demonstration of His power, Prophethood (Nubuwwah) through the miracle of Moses, and the Resurrection (Ma'ad) through the revival of the dead person.

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ ٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا هِىَ ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا فَارِضٌ وَلَا بِكْرٌ عَوَانٌۢ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ فَٱفْعَلُوا۟ مَا تُؤْمَرُونَ (٦۸)
They said, ‘Invoke your Lord for us that He may clarify for us what she may be.’ He said, ‘He says, she is a cow, neither old nor young, of a middle age. Now do what you are commanded.’ (68)

Key Points

When the Children of Israel realized that the matter was serious, they began to make excuses. And according to some commentators, it is probable that these excuses were being instilled in the people by the real killer, lest he be exposed. [362] In any case, the method of questioning used by the people indicates their spirit of obstinacy, which is alluded to in the Ayah and unveils their inner nature.

362) Tafsir Fakhr al-Razi, concerning the Ayah.

Messages

1- Ask questions with courtesy. In this Ayah, the word "لنا" (for us) is repeated twice, and the word "ربّك" (your Lord) is used instead of "ربّنا" (our Lord), which is a sign of arrogance. "ادع لنا ربّك یبیّن لنا"

2- Carry out the command of Allah quickly and avoid temptation and doubt. "فافعلوا ما تؤمرون"

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ ٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا لَوْنُهَا ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ صَفْرَآءُ فَاقِعٌ لَّوْنُهَا تَسُرُّ ٱلنَّـٰظِرِينَ (٦۹)
They said, ‘Invoke your Lord for us that He may clarify for us what her colour may be.’ He said, ‘He says, she is a cow that is yellow, of a bright hue, pleasing to the onlookers.’ (69)

Key Points

Although the command to slaughter was issued twice, it seems some of them knew the killer and did not want him to be identified. Therefore, out of stubbornness and excuse-making, they raised numerous questions, until finally they asked about the color of the cow. Allah replied to them, saying: The color of the cow should be intensely yellow, deep, and pure. A color that gladdens the beholders. Meaning, it should possess a special quality of good physique, health, color, and beauty. Perhaps if they had the means to weigh it, they would have also asked about the cow's weight! We have been forbidden from asking inappropriate questions. In Surah Al-Ma'idah, we read: «لا تسألوا عن اشیاء ان تُبدَلكم تَسؤكم» [363] Do not ask about things which, if disclosed to you, would cause you trouble. One day, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was delivering a sermon about the necessity and importance of Hajj. During the sermon, a person asked: Is Hajj obligatory every year? The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله did not answer. That person repeated his question again. He became upset and said: Why do you insist? If I say yes, your task will become difficult. When you see that I remain silent, do not insist. Then the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: One of the factors leading to the destruction of past nations was their inappropriate questions. [364] Imam Ali عليه السلام says in Nahj al-Balaghah: «و سَكت لكم عن اشیاء ولم یدعها نسیاناً فلا تتكلّفوها» [365] Allah has left the ruling regarding some matters unspoken; this silence is not due to forgetfulness, but so that you may have ease in practice. Therefore, do not scrutinize them with inappropriate questions.

363) Ma'idah, 101. 364) Tafsir-e Nemooneh, Vol. 5, p. 96. 365) Sharh Ibn Abi al-Hadid, Vol. 18, p. 102.

Messages

1- The color yellow, as mentioned in narrations, is recommended by the religion. «فاقع لونها»

2- Colors affect the human spirit (or morale/mood). «لونها تسر النّاظرین»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ ٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا هِىَ إِنَّ ٱلْبَقَرَ تَشَـٰبَهَ عَلَيْنَا وَإِنَّآ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَمُهْتَدُونَ (۷٠)
They said, ‘Invoke your Lord for us that He may clarify for us what she may be. Indeed all cows are much alike to us, and if Allah wishes we will surely be guided.’ (70)

Key Points

Moderation in every action is a virtue. Sometimes individuals attain certainty by seeing a very simple sign, but others, despite the explicit statement; «أن تذبحوا بقرةً» (that you slaughter a cow), become hesitant. One should not become hesitant regarding clear and explicit statements. It is mentioned in a hadith that the Prophet علیه السلام said: The Children of Israel were commanded to slaughter a simple and ordinary cow, but because they made things difficult and offered excuses, Allah also made things difficult for them.

Messages

[366] 1- Attention to secondary issues prevents a person from focusing on fundamental issues. «انّ البقر تشابه علینا»

2- A spirit of obstinacy causes the truth to become confusing for a person. «انّ البقرتشابه علینا»

3- Sometimes questioning is not for research, knowledge, or awareness, but rather a sign of a spirit of obstinacy and evasion. «یبیّن لنا ما هى انّ البقر تشابه علینا»

4- Divine leaders possess broad-mindedness and do not react to insolence. (The word «ربّك» (Your Lord) instead of «ربّنا» (Our Lord) was a form of insolence that was repeated many times, but Prophet Moses did not react to it.)

366) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 89.

Ayah

قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا ذَلُولٌ تُثِيرُ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَلَا تَسْقِى ٱلْحَرْثَ مُسَلَّمَةٌ لَّا شِيَةَ فِيهَا ۚ قَالُوا۟ ٱلْـَٔـٰنَ جِئْتَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ فَذَبَحُوهَا وَمَا كَادُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ (۷۱)
He said, ‘He says, She is a cow not broken to till the earth or water the tillage, sound and without blemish.’ They said, ‘Now have you come up with the truth!’ And they slaughtered it, though they were about not to do it. (71)

Key Points

«ذَلول» (Dhalūl) means a tamed animal. «تُثیر» (Tuthīr) is from «اثارة» (Ithārah), meaning plowing. «مُسلَّمه» (Musallamah) means safe/sound from any defect or physical flaw. «شِیة» (Shīyah) is from «وشى» (Washy), meaning a spot/mark, and «لاشیة» (Lā Shīyah) means it has no spot or streak of another color. The Jews were disrespectful towards their Prophet (Moses). Although they themselves were looking for excuses and ways to escape, when they were forced to carry out the command, they said to Moses: «الآن جئت بالحق» (Now you have brought the truth). It is as if he had been speaking falsehood before this.

Messages

1- When implementing plans, do not destroy active, productive, and economic elements and resources. «لاذلول... ولاتسقى»

2- Whatever is used in the way of Allah's command must be sound/intact. «مسلّمة» (Musallamah). During the Hajj pilgrimage, the pilgrim must also sacrifice a sound animal on the Day of Eid al-Adha.

3- Arrogance and desire lead a person to the point where he considers anything that suits his own wishes to be the truth. «جئت بالحق»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَتَلْتُمْ نَفْسًا فَٱدَّٰرَْٰٔتُمْ فِيهَا ۖ وَٱللَّهُ مُخْرِجٌ مَّا كُنتُمْ تَكْتُمُونَ (۷۲)
And when you killed a soul, and accused one another about it—and Allah was to expose what you were concealing— (72)

Key Points

The incident of the murder and the command to slaughter the cow were explained in detail in the previous verses. However, this verse mentions a summary of the event to serve as a renewed warning that what you were concealing, Allah exposed through the command to slaughter the cow, striking the deceased's body with a part of it, the deceased coming back to life, and identifying his killer. Thus, He unveiled your transgressions.

A Hadith is narrated in Durr al-Manthur that if a person performs an action amidst solid rocks where there is no opening, Allah will make it manifest for the people. [367]

We read in narrations: The middle-aged, yellow cow with those specific characteristics was only owned by a young man who had been offered a very profitable deal for it. However, since the key to the storehouse was under his father's head, and he did not want to wake his father, he forewent the profit. And Allah, in compensation for this service rendered by the son to his father, arranged the answer to the excuses of the Children of Israel such that the young man's cow was specified, allowing him to sell it for a high price. Prophet Moses said: «انظروا الى البّر مایبلغ باهله» (Look at the goodness, what it brings to its people.) [368]

367) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 1, p. 78.

368) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 88.

Messages

1- Whoever is pleased with the sin of others is a partner in their sin. Allah attributed the murder to the Jews of the Prophet's time, implying that they were pleased with the murder that occurred during the time of Moses. «واذ قتلتم»

2- Sometimes a person tries to cunningly deflect a crime from himself by attributing it to others. He is unaware that if Allah wills, He clarifies matters and exposes the criminal by striking one deceased person with a part of another deceased (animal). «واللّه مخرجٌ ما كنتم تكتمون»

Ayah

فَقُلْنَا ٱضْرِبُوهُ بِبَعْضِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُحْىِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَيُرِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (۷۳)
We said, ‘Strike him with a piece of it:’ thus does Allah revive the dead, and He shows you His signs so that you may exercise your reason. (73)

Messages

1- Gain the trust and confidence of the people. Moses (عليه السلام) did not personally strike the slain man with a part of the cow's body; rather, He commanded the people: Do this yourselves. «اضربوه»

2- This Ayah is an example of Allah's power to resurrect the dead in this world, known as the Return (Raj'ah), and in the Hereafter for recompense. «كذلك یحیى اللّه الموتى»

3- Seeing the signs of Divine power is intended for the continuous reasoning (ta'aqqul) of human beings, not for momentary astonishment. «لعلكم تعقلون»

4- If the spirit and mind of a person are corrupted, even seeing the Divine signs will not provoke reasoning in him. (Reasoning is used with the word «لعّل» [perhaps/so that you may]). «لعلّكم تعقلون»

Ayah

ثُمَّ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَهِىَ كَٱلْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ قَسْوَةً ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنَ ٱلْحِجَارَةِ لَمَا يَتَفَجَّرُ مِنْهُ ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنْهَا لَمَا يَشَّقَّقُ فَيَخْرُجُ مِنْهُ ٱلْمَآءُ ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنْهَا لَمَا يَهْبِطُ مِنْ خَشْيَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۷٤)
Then your hearts hardened after that; so they are like stones, or even harder. For there are some stones from which streams gush forth, and there are some of them that split, and water issues from them, and there are some that fall for the fear of Allah. And Allah is not oblivious of what you do. (74)

Key Points

The previous verses recalled many of Allah's favors upon the Children of Israel, such as: deliverance from the evil of Pharaoh, the splitting of the sea, the acceptance of their repentance from calf-worship, the sending down of the best foods, the provision of clouds as shade, and infallible leadership. Finally, they recounted the incident of the murder and how the killer was discovered through a miraculous method. Allah says in this verse: Due to your disregard for all these signs and verses, which stemmed from obstinacy and malice, hardness (Qaswah) enveloped your hearts, and you became hard-hearted, and perhaps your hearts became even harder than stone. Stones have different stages: A: They break apart, and rivers flow from them, watering people. «یتفجّر منه الانهار» B: They do not burst forth, but at least they moisten themselves and their surroundings. «یخرج منه الماء» C: Out of the fear (Khashyah) of Allah, they fall and prostrate. «یهبط من خشیة اللَّه»

Messages

1- Hardness (Qaswah) and hard-heartedness are the worst types of spiritual illness that afflict a person due to continuous obstinacy. «ثم قست قلوبكم من بعد ذلك»

2- Observing the signs and favors of Allah, in obstinate individuals, leads to hardness of heart instead of strengthening faith. [369] «قست قلوبكم من بعد ذلك»

3- Let us not act in a way that makes our descendants ashamed of our behavior. The ancestors became hard-hearted, but Allah addresses their descendants, saying: «قست قلوبكم»

4- Knowledge alone does not bring enlightenment. After seeing all those miracles, they still became hard-hearted. «قست قلوبكم من بعد ذلك»

5- Hardness has stages. «كالحجارة او اشدّ قسوة»

6- The deepest truths can be expressed using the simplest analogies. «كالحجارة»

7- Criticisms must be substantiated. When the Quran states that some hearts are harder than stone, it provides the reason: the flowing of water from the stone. «و انّ من الحجارة لما یتفجّر...»

8- Inanimate objects possess a degree of consciousness, as they experience fear (Khawf) and awe (Khashyah). «وانّ منها لما یهبط من خشیة اللّه» [370]

9- The cure for hardness of heart is attention to the knowledge of Allah. «كالحجارة... و مااللَّه بغافل»

10- Hard-heartedness affects our behavior. «قست قلوبكم ... عمّا تعملون»

11- Allah is aware of all our deeds. «وما اللَّه بغافل عمّا تعلمون»

[369] Just as the Quran is a source of healing and mercy for the believers, and a cause of loss and damage for the wrongdoers. (Al-Isra, 82). (The rain, whose nature is pure gentleness, causes tulips to grow in the garden and weeds in the salt marsh.)

[370] Considering that Khashyah (awe/fear) arises only from knowledge, and nothing else: »انّما یخشى اللَّه من عباده العلماء« (Fatir, 28). And Mawlawi (Rumi) also says: (All particles, manifest and hidden, speak to you, day and night, "We are hearing, seeing, and conscious; but with you, the strangers, we are silent." The speech of water, the speech of earth, and the speech of clay are perceptible to the senses of the people of the heart.)

Ayah

۞ أَفَتَطْمَعُونَ أَن يُؤْمِنُوا۟ لَكُمْ وَقَدْ كَانَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ كَلَـٰمَ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ يُحَرِّفُونَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا عَقَلُوهُ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (۷٥)
Are you then eager that they should believe you, though a part of them would hear the word of Allah and then they would distort it after they had understood it, and they knew [what they were doing]? (75)

Messages

1- Expecting people to believe is good, but not everyone is granted this success, so you should not expect it either. «أفتطمعون أن یؤمنوا»

2- With the existence of stubborn and dangerous scholars, there is no hope for the reform of society. «أفتطمعون ان یؤمنوا لكم و قد كان...»

3- In criticism, we must observe fairness. Not all Jews were people of distortion. «فریقٌ منهم... یحرّفونه»

4- There is hope for the reform of the corrupt common people, but there is no hope for the reform of the stubborn, deviated scholar. «بعد ما عقلوه»

5- Knowing the truth is different from accepting the truth. There are individuals who know the truth but are not willing to acknowledge it. «یحرّفونه من بعد ما عقلوه»

6- Distortions are more dangerous when they are expert and conscious. «یحرّفونه من بعد ماعقلوه»

7- The negligent ignorant person is subject to threat, not the unaware ignorant person. «من بعد ما عقلوه»

8- Distortion is the sin of the scholars and the learned. «یحرّفونه من بعد ما عقلوه و هم یعلمون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا لَقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَا بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ قَالُوٓا۟ أَتُحَدِّثُونَهُم بِمَا فَتَحَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لِيُحَآجُّوكُم بِهِۦ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (۷٦)
When they meet the faithful, they say, ‘We believe,’ and when they are alone with one another, they say, ‘Do you recount to them what Allah has revealed to you, so that they may argue with you therewith before your Lord? Do you not exercise your reason?’ (76)

Key Points

Some Jews, when they met the Muslims, would tell them: Since the descriptions and signs of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله are in our Torah, we too have believed. However, when some (other) individuals met these believers, they would reproach and censure them, asking why they were recounting the descriptions and signs of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله to the Muslims? If you inform them of what is in the Torah, they will use it against you on the Day of Resurrection.

Messages

1- When a person understands the truth, he must follow it and should not ignore the truth due to the intimidation and threats of others or the influence of this or that position. «قالوا اتحدثونهم بما ...»

It is likely that if the scholars had not concealed the truth on that day, there would not be so many Jews and Christians.

2- In the view of the deviated, hypocrisy and concealing the truth to preserve status and misplaced fanaticism is a sign of intellect and acting wisely. «أتحدّثونهم... أفلا تعقلون»

Ayah

أَوَلَا يَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا يُسِرُّونَ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ (۷۷)
Do they not know that Allah knows whatever they hide and whatever they disclose? (77)

Messages

1- Faith in the presence and knowledge of Allah restrains man from errors. «أوَلایعلمون انّ اللّه یعلم...»

2- Before Allah, the manifest and the hidden are the same. «یعلم ما یسرّون و ما یعلنون»

Ayah

وَمِنْهُمْ أُمِّيُّونَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ إِلَّآ أَمَانِىَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَظُنُّونَ (۷۸)
And among them are the illiterate who know nothing of the Book except hearsay, and they only make conjectures. (78)

Key Points

Unlike the previous group, who were scholars and were familiar with the descriptions and signs of the Prophet of Islam in the Torah, but concealed the truth, the common people, who were unaware of the content of the heavenly book, lived by wishes and imagination. They thought that the Jews were the superior race and the children and beloved ones of Allah, and would not go to Hell, and if there was any punishment for them, it would not last more than a few days. [371]

371) Examples of these wishes are mentioned in Surahs An-Nisa (4:80 and 4:120), Al-Baqarah (2:109), and Al-Ma'idah (5:21).

Messages

1- One must know his society and be familiar with the thoughts and beliefs prevailing among the people of his time. «و منهم اُمیّون»

2- Despite the existence of the Book and the teacher, being unlettered (ummi) and illiterate is a defect, and everyone must strive to attain the Book of Truth so that they are not subject to criticism. «و منهم اُمیّون لایعلمون الكتاب»

3- Illiteracy and ignorance are the grounds for the growth of misplaced imaginations and wishes. «امیّون... امانى»

4- Expectations and hopes must be based on knowledge, not imagination. «الاّ یظنّون»

5- In matters of belief, following conjecture and imagination is forbidden. «ان هم الاّ یظنّون»

Ayah

فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَكْتُبُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ ثُمَّ يَقُولُونَ هَـٰذَا مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ لِيَشْتَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۖ فَوَيْلٌ لَّهُم مِّمَّا كَتَبَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَوَيْلٌ لَّهُم مِّمَّا يَكْسِبُونَ (۷۹)
So woe to them who write the Book with their hands and then say, ‘This is from Allah,’ that they may sell it for a paltry gain. So woe to them for what their hands have written, and woe to them for what they earn! (79)

Key Points

This is the only Ayah in which the word "Wayl" (Woe) is used three times, addressing the danger posed by worldly scholars and learned individuals.

Messages

  1. Religion fabrication has been a dangerous trend throughout history. «یكتبون الكتاب...»

  2. Innovation (Bid'ah), religion fabrication, selling religion, and exploiting people's ignorance are among the dangers that threaten society from the side of corrupt scholars. «یقولون هذا من عنداللَّه»

  3. Be careful of pens, books, poisonous articles, distorting works, and innovating works, and do not trust every scholar. «یكتبون... یقولون هذا من عند اللّه»

  4. People are innately interested in religion; therefore, many charlatans present their words to the people in the name of religion. «یقولون هذا من عنداللّه»

  5. One of the motivations for innovation (Bid'ah) and slander is attaining worldly gain. «ثمناً قلیلاً»

  6. Among the worst earnings is the income derived from selling religion. «ویل لهم ممّا یكسبون»

  7. The severest punishments are directed at those who betray the thoughts and beliefs of the people. (Indicated by) the repetition of the word "Wayl."

  8. Every deviation that arises throughout history due to an innovation, its sin lies upon the innovator. «یكسبون» indicates continuity.

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ لَن تَمَسَّنَا ٱلنَّارُ إِلَّآ أَيَّامًا مَّعْدُودَةً ۚ قُلْ أَتَّخَذْتُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ عَهْدًا فَلَن يُخْلِفَ ٱللَّهُ عَهْدَهُۥٓ ۖ أَمْ تَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۸٠)
And they say, ‘The Fire shall not touch us except for a number of days.’ Say, ‘Have you taken a promise from Allah? If so, Allah will never break His promise. Do you ascribe to Allah what you do not know?’ (80)

Key Points

Among the doctrinal deviations of the Jews was their claim that even if they were sinners, their punishment would be lighter than others, and they would not be tormented for more than a few days, because they were superior. Allah, in this Ayah, invalidates this way of thinking.

Messages

1- Seeking privilege is one of the characteristics of the Jews. «و قالوا»

2- Do not leave improper thoughts and statements unanswered. «قالوا... قل»

3- Seeking superiority, racism, and wishful thinking without action are forbidden. «قالوا لن تمسّنا النار... ام تقولون»

4- Everyone is equal before the law; Allah has not given a promise or covenant for the salvation of a specific nation. «قل اتّخذتم عند اللَّه عهداً»

5- Ignorance of religious knowledge leads to attributing superstitions to the religion. «ام تقولون على اللَّه ما لاتعلمون»

Ayah

بَلَىٰ مَن كَسَبَ سَيِّئَةً وَأَحَـٰطَتْ بِهِۦ خَطِيٓـَٔتُهُۥ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۸۱)
Certainly whoever commits misdeeds and is besieged by his iniquity—such shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they will remain in it [forever]. (81)

Key Points

Contrary to the assumption of the Children of Israel (Bani Israel) who imagined that the retribution of Allah concerning them differed from His divine retribution concerning others, this Ayah states: everyone is equal before the law. And if the effects of sins so encompass a person that he has no opportunity for repentance and return, he will remain in Hell forever.

Messages

1- Confront fantasies and delusions with clarity and decisiveness. «بلى مَن»

2- Retribution and reward are not based on wishes, but on deeds. «بلى مَن كسب»

3- Only those sins incur punishment which are committed knowingly, deliberately, and voluntarily, not those based on ignorance or compulsion. «كسب»

4- The sinner commits sin imagining that he is gaining profit or benefit. «كسب سیّئة»

5- Every sin has effects and consequences, and those consequences can drown a person within themselves. «أحاطت»

6- Man is inherently pure (by fitrah), but sins and errors afflict him, encompass him, and change his essence. «احاطت به خطیئته»

7- The habit and characteristic of sinfulness lead to eternity in the fire of Hell. «مَن كسب... احاطت... خالدون»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۸۲)
And those who have faith and do righteous deeds—they shall be the inhabitants of paradise; they will remain in it [forever]. (82)

Messages

1- Alongside warning, glad tidings are necessary. The previous Ayah discussed the punishment of the sinner; this Ayah discusses the reward of the righteous. «الّذین آمنوا و عملوا... اصحاب الجنّة»

2- Faith is not separate from action. «آمنوا و عملوا...»

3- The criterion for Paradise is faith and action, not imagination or mere wishes. «الّذین آمنوا وعملوا... اصحاب الجنّة»

4- Performing a single righteous deed is not sufficient; rather, possessing the characteristic of righteousness and performing every kind of righteous deed is necessary. «عملوا الصالحات». The word «الصالحات» is plural and includes the definite article (alif and lam), hence it encompasses all good deeds.

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَـٰقَ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ لَا تَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ وَبِٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا وَذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَقُولُوا۟ لِلنَّاسِ حُسْنًا وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ ثُمَّ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنكُمْ وَأَنتُم مُّعْرِضُونَ (۸۳)
When We took a pledge from the Children of Israel, [saying]: ‘Worship no one but Allah, do good to your parents, relatives, orphans, and the needy, speak kindly to people, maintain the prayer, and give the zakat,’ you turned away, except a few of you, and you were disregardful. (83)

Key Points

Ihsan (benevolence) is the most comprehensive and broadest term regarding kindness. In the case of parental poverty, ihsan is material, and in the case of their wealth, it is spiritual ihsan. Just as ihsan towards an orphan includes; their upbringing, safeguarding their rights, affection, and their education. The fundamentals of all religions are one, because all these covenants are also present in Islam.

Messages

1- Allah takes covenants from people through intellect, innate nature (fitrah), and revelation. «أَخَذْنَا مِيثَاقَ»

2- Monotheism (Tawhid) is the cornerstone of the prophets' mission, followed by good deeds. «لَا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا اللَّهَ وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا»

3- Alongside Monotheism, benevolence towards parents is mentioned. «لَا تَعْبُدُوا... وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا»

4- Benevolence towards parents must be carried out personally, not through an intermediary. The letter bā (ب) in «بِالْوَالِدَيْنِ» signifies personal direct action. «بِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا»

5- Favoring only one of the parents is forbidden. «بِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا»

6- First needy relatives, then others. «ذِي الْقُرْبَى وَالْيَتَامَى وَ...» Of course, priorities are also maintained among relatives. «أُولُو الْأَرْحَامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلَى بِبَعْضٍ» (372)

7- Benevolence [372] must be accompanied by courtesy and without obligation (or reminding of the favor). Alongside ihsan, the phrase «قُولُوا لِلنَّاسِ حُسْنًا» (speak kindly to people) is mentioned.

8- Although one cannot show ihsan (material benevolence) to all people, one can speak well to everyone. «قُولُوا لِلنَّاسِ حُسْنًا»

9- Good conduct and kind speech are necessary not only with Muslims but with all people. «قُولُوا لِلنَّاسِ حُسْنًا»

10- In addition to paying Zakat (alms), benevolence towards the poor and orphans is also necessary. The Ayah mentions both Zakat and ihsan towards the needy. «إِحْسَانًا... وَالْمَسَاكِينِ... وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ»

11- Prayer (Salat) and the relationship with Allah are not separate from Zakat and the relationship with the poor. «أَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ»

12- Prayer and Zakat were also present in other religions. «أَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ»

13- Belief in Monotheism (Tawhid), benevolence towards parents, orphans, and the needy, along with the command to pay Zakat, treat people kindly, and establish prayer, is a sign of the comprehensiveness of Islam.

14- Fulfilling rights has ranks and stages. First, the right of Allah, then parents, then relatives, then orphans who lack affection, and finally the needy (Masakin) who lack material resources.

15- All doctrinal, ethical, and jurisprudential rulings are the covenant and promise of God with humanity. After «مِيثَاقَ» (covenant), the discussion turns to the monotheistic creed, benevolence towards parents, prayer, and Zakat.

372) Al-Anfal, 75.

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَـٰقَكُمْ لَا تَسْفِكُونَ دِمَآءَكُمْ وَلَا تُخْرِجُونَ أَنفُسَكُم مِّن دِيَـٰرِكُمْ ثُمَّ أَقْرَرْتُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَشْهَدُونَ (۸٤)
And when We took a pledge from you, [saying]: ‘You shall not shed your [own people’s] blood, and you shall not expel your folks from your homes,’ you pledged, and you testify [to this pledge of your ancestors]. (84)

Key Points

Perhaps the intent of the covenant with the Children of Israel regarding the respect for human life is Ayah 32 of Surah Al-Ma'idah, which states: We decreed upon the Children of Israel that whoever kills a soul unless for a soul or for corruption [done] in the land—it is as if he had slain mankind entirely. The right to life is the first right for every human being, and murder (killing a soul) is among the major sins. In Surah An-Nisa, Ayah 93, we read: And whoever kills a believer intentionally, his recompense is Hell, wherein he will abide eternally, and Allah has become angry with Him. Just as in Surah Al-Ma'idah, Ayah 32, killing one person without any crime is considered equal to killing all people. It is stated in a hadith: All the sins of the victim fall upon the killer. And it is also stated: If the inhabitants of the heavens and the earth participate in the killing of one person, they will all fall into Hell. The method of the tyrants (Taghuts) is to build fortresses and palaces to solidify their base, while displacing the people, subjecting them to harassment and violating their sanctity. Whereas even a creditor does not have the right to force a debtor to sell his house. And if a person takes a place for himself in the mosque, no one has the right to pray there. And whoever chooses a place for work, worship, or agriculture sooner than others is given priority, and no one has the right to interfere. The criticism of the Jews during the time of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, which is evident in the phrase «اقررتم و انتم» (you acknowledged while you were witnessing), is due to the actions of their ancestors. Yes, whoever is satisfied with an action—whether personal or communal—related to past or future times, is considered a partner in it.

Messages

1- To ensure the execution of important tasks, mere recommendation and command are not enough; a covenant (mithaq) and pledge are necessary. «اخذنا میثاقكم»

2- The security of life and homeland is the recommendation of all divine schools of thought. «لاتسفكون، لاتخرجون»

3- Love of homeland is an innate and natural right, and the deprivation of this right is manifest oppression. «ولا تخرجون أنفسكم من دیاركم»

4- Society is like a single body whose individuals are the members of this body. «دماءكم، انفسكم» [373]

373) It is inferred from the words «دماءكم» and «انفسكم» that killing or expelling another is, in reality, killing and expelling oneself. Sa'di says: The children of Adam are members of one another, having been created from one essence.

Ayah

ثُمَّ أَنتُمْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ تَقْتُلُونَ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَتُخْرِجُونَ فَرِيقًا مِّنكُم مِّن دِيَـٰرِهِمْ تَظَـٰهَرُونَ عَلَيْهِم بِٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْعُدْوَٰنِ وَإِن يَأْتُوكُمْ أُسَـٰرَىٰ تُفَـٰدُوهُمْ وَهُوَ مُحَرَّمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ إِخْرَاجُهُمْ ۚ أَفَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِبَعْضِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَتَكْفُرُونَ بِبَعْضٍ ۚ فَمَا جَزَآءُ مَن يَفْعَلُ ذَٰلِكَ مِنكُمْ إِلَّا خِزْىٌ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰٓ أَشَدِّ ٱلْعَذَابِ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۸٥)
Then there you were, killing your folks and expelling a part of your folks from their homes, backing one another against them in sin and aggression! And if they came to you as captives, you would ransom them, though their expulsion itself was forbidden you. What! Do you believe in part of the Book and deny another part? So what is the requital of those of you who do that except disgrace in the life of this world? And on the Day of Resurrection, they shall be consigned to the severest punishment. And Allah is not oblivious of what you do. (85)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah reproaches the Children of Israel because, despite the covenant and pledge they had, they killed one another and expelled one another from their lands. You were obligated to form a united society based on sound familial relations and care for the deprived, but instead, you aided and supported one another in sin and transgression against the rights of others, promoting chaos, division, and bloodshed in society. With your support, oppressive rulers gained the audacity to commit murder and expulsion. It is strange that, according to the ruling of the Torah, if you have a captive in these conflicts, you free him by paying ransom, yet you do not heed the ruling of the Torah regarding the prohibition of murder and expulsion. You are willing to kill one another with your own hands, but you are not willing to be captives of one another! If captivity is an insult, killing is worse than that, and if paying ransom and freeing captives is a command of the Torah, the prohibition of murder and expulsion is also a command of Allah! Indeed, you are not submissive to the command of Allah; rather, you accept the divine verses wherever they conform to your desires and tastes, and wherever they do not, you refuse to comply. This Ayah warns against the danger of eclecticism (Al-Iltiqat), showing how people accept a part of the religion and abandon the other part. Muslims who act upon individual rulings but are indifferent toward social issues must await humiliation and disgrace in this world and the torment of the Hereafter. How many are those who observe the conditions and rulings of prayer (Salat) and fasting (Sawm), but are not committed to the conditions and duties related to the ruler and the government.

Messages

1- The Quran refers to the killing of others as the killing of one's own self, to state that killing others is self-killing, and the individuals of a society are like the members of a single body. «تقتلون انفسكم»

2- Aiding and cooperating in sin and transgression is forbidden. [374] «تظاهرون علیهم بالاثم والعدوان»

3- The sign of faith is action, and if someone does not act upon the commands of the religion, it is as if he has no faith. «أفتؤمنون ببعض الكتاب و تكفرون ببعض» Instead of criticizing why you act upon some commands and abandon others, the Quran states: Why do you believe in some and disbelieve in others?

4- The sign of true faith is acting upon commands that go against a person's personal tastes; otherwise, performing commands that align with human desire is no indication of the extent of true faith. «تقتلون، تفادوهم» (In killing one another, you disregard the Torah, but in freeing captives, you become people of faith!)

5- Disbelief in some verses is, in reality, disbelief in all rulings. For this reason, the recompense for those who do not act upon a part of the verses will be humiliation in this world and the torment of the Hereafter. «خزى فى الحیوة الدنیا... یردّون الى اشدّ العذاب»

374) It is mentioned in the narrations (Ahadith): If someone guides or helps an oppressor in his tyranny and oppression, he will be the companion of Haman, the minister of Pharaoh, in Hellfire. Furthermore, even preparing ink and pen for the oppressor is not permissible. Imam Kadhim عليه السلام addressed one of the noble Muslims, saying: Renting a camel to the apparatus of Harun al-Rashid, even if it is for the Hajj journey, is not permissible. (Tafsir Atayeb Al-Bayan).

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱشْتَرَوُا۟ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ فَلَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ (۸٦)
They are the ones who bought the life of this world for the Hereafter; so their punishment shall not be lightened, nor will they be helped. (86)

Key Points

This verse explains the root of covenant-breaking, self-murder (or killing of souls), and failure to act upon certain verses thus: They seek worldly life and are only bound by laws that secure their interests, and they are indifferent to any law that harms their worldly interests. Therefore, the Divine punishment for these comfort-seeking, world-worshippers will not be reduced. Contrary to their imagination and assumption—where they claim they will either not be punished at all or will not be penalized for more than a few days—they, like all criminals, are responsible for their deeds.

Messages

1- Man is free and possesses the right to choose. All verses stating that man bought the world and sold the Hereafter prove that man is not compelled; rather, he has free will, and he himself weighs and chooses through thought and comparison. «اشتروا»

2- Everyone is equal before the Divine law. «فلا یخفّف» The notion that the Children of Israel consider themselves the superior race, the children and beloved of Allah, is nothing more than an assumption and an illusion. The Divine wrath makes no exception for any person or race that is on the path of obstinacy and disbelief.

3- Worldly desire is one of the motives for murder. «تقتلون انفسكم... اشتروا الحیوة الدنیا»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَقَفَّيْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ بِٱلرُّسُلِ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَا عِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَأَيَّدْنَـٰهُ بِرُوحِ ٱلْقُدُسِ ۗ أَفَكُلَّمَا جَآءَكُمْ رَسُولٌۢ بِمَا لَا تَهْوَىٰٓ أَنفُسُكُمُ ٱسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ فَفَرِيقًا كَذَّبْتُمْ وَفَرِيقًا تَقْتُلُونَ (۸۷)
Certainly, We gave Moses the Book and followed him with the apostles, and We gave Jesus, the son of Mary, manifest proofs and confirmed him with the Holy Spirit. Is it not that whenever an apostle brought you that which was not to your liking, you would act arrogantly; so you would impugn a group [of them], and slay a[nother] group? (87)

Key Points

This Ayah speaks of the continuation of Allah's grace for the guidance of people. [It mentions] that after Moses (عليه السلام), Prophets such as David, Solomon, Joshua, Zachariah, and John (عليه السلام) came, and after those noble figures, Allah sent Jesus (عليه السلام) with clear proofs, who was aided and confirmed by the Holy Spirit [375]. But the Children of Israel adopted the path of arrogance and rebellion, and instead of accepting Divine guidance, they resorted to denying and killing the Prophets. Narrating the history of the Prophets is a form of consolation for the Prophet of Islam and the believers, so that enduring hardships becomes easier for them.

375) It is understood from Ayah 102 of Surah An-Nahl that the Holy Spirit (Ruh al-Qudus) is the same as Gabriel.

Messages

1- It is Allah's tradition (Sunnah) that the heavenly leader continues to exist among the people. «قفّینا»

2- Education and training are not subject to suspension. «قفّینا» The sending of Prophets one after another is a sign of the flow of guidance throughout history.

3- Angels assist the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah). «ایّدناه بروح القدس»

4- Prophets must go to the people. «جاءكم الرسول»

5- Guidance is not compatible with whims and desires. «لاتهوى انفسكم»

6- Whoever does not submit to the truth is arrogant. «لاتهوى انفسكم استكبرتم»

7- Following desires (Hawa-parasti) leads man as far as killing Prophets. «فریقاً تقتلون»

8- The Prophets resisted until death to achieve their goals. «فریقاً تقتلون»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ قُلُوبُنَا غُلْفٌۢ ۚ بَل لَّعَنَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِكُفْرِهِمْ فَقَلِيلًا مَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۸۸)
And they say, ‘Our hearts are uncircumcised.’ Rather, Allah has cursed them for their unfaith, so few of them have faith. (88)

Key Points

| Apparently, this mocking answer was the slogan of all polytheists (mushrikūn) and rebels against the Prophets. Just as they said in response to Prophet Shu'ayb: "یا شعیب ما نفقه" [376] (We do not understand your words.) Or, in response to the verses of the Qur'an, they would say: "قلوبنا فى اكنّة" [377] (Our hearts are covered and veiled.) In this verse, the expression "قلوبنا غُلف" has also been mentioned.

376) Hūd, 91. 377) Fuṣṣilat, 5.

Messages

1- The preliminaries of wretchedness are in the hands of man himself. If a group is subjected to the curse (la'nah) and wrath of Allah, it is due to their own disbelief (kufr) and obstinacy. "لعنهم اللّه بكفرهم"

Ayah

وَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ كِتَـٰبٌ مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ مُصَدِّقٌ لِّمَا مَعَهُمْ وَكَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ يَسْتَفْتِحُونَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُم مَّا عَرَفُوا۟ كَفَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ ۚ فَلَعْنَةُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۸۹)
And when there came to them a Book from Allah, confirming that which is with them—and earlier they would pray for victory over the pagans—so when there came to them what they recognized, they denied it. So may the curse of Allah be on the faithless! (89)

Key Points

This Ayah presents another scene of the Jews' obstinacy and self-interest. Based on the glad tidings in the Torah, they were awaiting the appearance of the Prophet and even promised each other victory. According to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, one of the reasons for their residence in Medina was that they knew that city was the place of the Prophet's migration, and for this reason, they had settled there beforehand. However, after the appearance of the Prophet of Islam, despite finding His signs consistent with what was in the Torah, they committed disbelief. (378)

(378) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Divine religions confirm one another; they are not opposed to each other. "مصدّقاً"

2- One should not rely on every welcome or anticipation. Although the Jews resided in Medina for years awaiting the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, they committed disbelief in practice. (379) "كانوامن قبل... كفروا به"

3- Knowing the truth and having knowledge of it is not sufficient. Many individuals understood the truth but became disbelievers due to obstinacy. "فلمّا جائهم ماعرفوا كفروا"

(379) Do not be deceived by your love and anticipation. It is possible that a person's state changes during the stage of action. God forbid, one might recite the Du'a Nudbah today, but tomorrow...

Ayah

بِئْسَمَا ٱشْتَرَوْا۟ بِهِۦٓ أَنفُسَهُمْ أَن يَكْفُرُوا۟ بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ بَغْيًا أَن يُنَزِّلَ ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ ۖ فَبَآءُو بِغَضَبٍ عَلَىٰ غَضَبٍ ۚ وَلِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ (۹٠)
Evil is that for which they have sold their souls, by defying what Allah has sent down, out of envy that Allah should bestow His grace on any of His servants that He wishes. Thus, they earned wrath upon wrath, and there is a humiliating punishment for the faithless. (90)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the reason for the disbelief of the Jews in the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is stated. They were jealous that revelation had not been sent down upon one of the individuals of the Children of Israel. And this jealousy and disbelief was a bad price for which they sold themselves.

Messages

  1. The criterion for the value of people's religion lies in their motives. «بئسما اشتروا... بغیاً»
  2. Envy leads to disbelief (kufr). The Children of Israel wished that the promised Prophet would be from their lineage, and when they did not achieve their wish, they became envious and disbelieved. «بغیاً ان ینزّل»
  3. Prophethood is divine grace. «من فضله على من یشاء»
  4. Human dissatisfaction has no effect on the wise favors of Allah. God knows best upon whom to entrust His mission. «من یشاء»
  5. The worst transaction is for a person to give up his existence and buy divine wrath. «بئسما اشتروا... غضب، عذاب مهین»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ قَالُوا۟ نُؤْمِنُ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْنَا وَيَكْفُرُونَ بِمَا وَرَآءَهُۥ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا مَعَهُمْ ۗ قُلْ فَلِمَ تَقْتُلُونَ أَنۢبِيَآءَ ٱللَّهِ مِن قَبْلُ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۹۱)
And when they are told, ‘Believe in what Allah has sent down,’ they say, ‘We believe in what was sent down to us,’ and they disbelieve what is besides it, though it is the truth confirming what is with them. Say, ‘Then why would you kill the prophets of Allah formerly, should you be faithful?’ (91)

Messages

1- The Prophet of Islam was commissioned to invite all nations (Ummahs) to Islam. «قیل لهم»

2- One of the reasons for the disbelief of the disbelievers is racism and tribal prejudices. «نؤمن بما انزل علینا ویكفرون بما ورائه»

3- The criterion for faith is the truthfulness (Haqqāniyyah) of the religion, not race. «و هو الحقّ»

4- The Quran is entirely truth (Haqq). «انزل اللَّه... وهو الحقّ»

5- Since the Jews of the Prophet's time were satisfied with the actions of their ancestors, Allah attributed the killing of the prophets to them. «فلِمَ تقتلون»

6- A liar is disgraced. «فلم تقتلون... ان كنتم مؤمنین»

(If you believed in the prophets who were from the Children of Israel, then why did you martyr all those prophets, such as Hadrat Yahya and Zakariyya, who were also from the Children of Israel?! Indeed, your actions are a sign of your lack of faith.)

Ayah

۞ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَكُم مُّوسَىٰ بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ ٱتَّخَذْتُمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ وَأَنتُمْ ظَـٰلِمُونَ (۹۲)
Certainly Moses brought you manifest proofs, but then you took up the Calf in his absence and you were wrongdoers. (92)

Key Points

Another living proof that you have only used the Arab identity of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله as an excuse not to believe in him, is that even though Moses عليه السلام was the most prominent Prophet from your own race, and brought you so many clear proofs and miracles, yet, as soon as he went to Mount Tur for a few nights for supplication and receiving the Torah, you turned to calf-worship, wasting all the efforts of Moses عليه السلام and wronging yourselves.

Messages

1- Mentioning past records prepares the ground for correct judgment. «اتخذتم العجل»

2- Returning to polytheism (shirk) and ignorance (Jahiliyyah) is an injustice (zulm) to oneself and to future generations. «انتم ظالمون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَـٰقَكُمْ وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَكُمُ ٱلطُّورَ خُذُوا۟ مَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰكُم بِقُوَّةٍ وَٱسْمَعُوا۟ ۖ قَالُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَعَصَيْنَا وَأُشْرِبُوا۟ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ بِكُفْرِهِمْ ۚ قُلْ بِئْسَمَا يَأْمُرُكُم بِهِۦٓ إِيمَـٰنُكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۹۳)
And when We took covenant with you and raised the Mount above you, [declaring], ‘Hold on with power to what We have given you, and do listen!’ They said, ‘We hear, and disobey,’ and their hearts had been imbued with [the love of] the Calf, due to their unfaith. Say, ‘Evil is that to which your faith prompts you, should you be faithful!’ (93)

Key Points

The final word of the Jews was that if a prophet was not from the Children of Israel (Bani Isra'il), they would not believe in him, and they would only accept a book that was revealed specifically to them. The Quran presents several examples of their lies: The first example was in the previous Ayah, where He said: If you are truthful in this claim, then why did you turn away from Moses and resort to calf-worship? The second example is this very Ayah, where He says: We took a covenant from you and raised Mount Tur above you, and We said: Take the heavenly laws of the Torah with full strength, listen, and act upon them. But you said: We hear the laws, but we do not act upon them. Now, if you do not believe in the Quran and the Prophet of Islam, Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, on the pretext that he is not from among you and the Quran was not revealed to the Children of Israel, then why did you treat Moses and his Torah in that manner? The Quran explains the secret behind this lack of belief thus: Due to their disbelief, their hearts were saturated with affection for the calf, leaving no room for contemplation and faith. If the Children of Israel are sincere in their claim that they will believe in whatever is revealed to them, then how do they justify these crimes that are in their record? Is calf-worship, prophet-killing, and covenant-breaking part of faith?

Messages

1- Taking a covenant (mithaq) is one of the factors and motivations for action. «اخذنا میثاقكم»

2- Preserving the achievements of the Divine revolution is necessary, even if it comes at the cost of threat. «رفعنا فوقكم»

3- Carrying out Divine rulings and commands requires strength, seriousness, devotion, and determination, and is incompatible with jesting and formalities. Piety is incompatible with weakness, negligence, and compromise. «خذوا ما آتیناكم بقوة»

4- Excessive love and attachment are dangerous. If a person's heart is filled with attachment to something, he will not be willing to accept the truths. [380] «اشربوا فى قلوبهم العجل»

5- Behavior is the best indicator of a person's thoughts and beliefs. «بئسما یأمركم به ایمانكم»

380) As has been said: »حبّ الشى ء یعمى و یصمّ« (Love of a thing makes one blind and deaf). This means that a person is no longer willing to see or hear its faults.

Ayah

قُلْ إِن كَانَتْ لَكُمُ ٱلدَّارُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ خَالِصَةً مِّن دُونِ ٱلنَّاسِ فَتَمَنَّوُا۟ ٱلْمَوْتَ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۹٤)
Say, ‘If the abode of the Hereafter were exclusively for you with Allah, and not for other people, then long for death, should you be truthful.’ (94)

Key Points

The Children of Israel (Bani Israel) had many false claims and fantasies, some of which included: * We are the children and beloved ones of God (Allah). «نحن ابناء اللّه و احبّائه» [381] * No one shall enter Paradise except one who is a Jew or a Christian. «قالوا لن یدخل الجنّه الاّ من كان هوداً او نصارى» [382] * The Fire of Hell will not touch us except for a few days. «لن تمسّنا النّار الاّ ایّاما معدودة» This Ayah [383] refutes all these imaginary fabrications and intellectual delusions, stating: If these claims of yours are true and you truly believe these words, then you should no longer fear death or flee from it; rather, you should wish for death so that you may enter Paradise! The friends of Allah (Awliya) not only do not fear death, but they also yearn for it. Just as Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «واللَّه لابن ابى طالب آنس بالموت من الطفل بثدى امّه» meaning [384] By God, the fondness of the son of Abu Talib for death is greater than the fondness of an infant for his mother's breast. Yes, we must live in such a way that we are ready for death at any moment. [385]

381) Ma'idah, 18. 382) Baqarah, 111. 383) Baqarah, 80. 384) Bihar, Vol. 28, p. 233. 385) Death is like a journey. A driver fears the journey only if he does not know the way, or lacks fuel, or has committed a violation, or is carrying contraband, or has no place to stay at the destination. However, the true believer knows the way: »الیه المصیر« (To Him is the return); he has fuel for the journey: »عمل صالحاً« (righteous deeds); he has compensated for his violations through repentance; he carries no contraband (i.e., no unlawful deeds); and he knows his place of residence: »جنّة الماوى« (Paradise of Abode). Therefore, he does not fear.

Messages

1- Confront fantasies and delusions with clarity and frankness. «قل» (Say)

2- The scope of exclusivity and racism extends even to the Day of Judgment! «لكم الدار الاخرة خالصة...» (For you is the Hereafter exclusively...)

3- Conscience is the best judge. «إن كانت... فتمنّوا الموت» (If it is... then wish for death)

4- Readiness for death is a sign of true and sincere faith. «فتمنّوا الموت ان كنتم صادقین» (then wish for death if you are truthful)

Ayah

وَلَن يَتَمَنَّوْهُ أَبَدًۢا بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۹٥)
But they will not long for it ever because of what their hands have sent ahead, and Allah knows best the wrongdoers. (95)

Key Points

Not fearing death is a sign of truthfulness and certainty (Yaqin). When death arrives, jokes, formalities, and fantasies flee; only man remains with his deeds. At the moment of death, man believes that the provisions of this world are meager, and the Hereafter is better and everlasting. At the moment of death, man believes that the world is a bud that never fully blossoms for anyone, and worldly friends are like flies around sweetness. If a person reaches the rank of certainty, the closer he gets to death, the more he feels proximity and attainment of the Divine encounter and vision (Liqa'). For this reason, when Imam Ali عليه السلام felt the strike of the sword upon his head, he said: «فزت و ربّ الكعبة» (By the Lord of the Ka'bah, I have succeeded/triumphed). Imam Husayn عليه السلام in Karbala, the closer He got to the noon of Ashura and the time of martyrdom, the more radiant and blossoming His face became. And when His companions were asked on the last night: How is death in your view? They offered replies that demonstrated their certainty in the righteousness of their path; they considered death sweet, and some of them even joked with each other on that very last night.

Messages

1- Fear of death is, in reality, fear of the retribution for our own deeds. «بما قدّمت ایدیهم»

2- Lying claimants and those who have undue expectations are oppressors (wrongdoers). «واللَّه علیم بالظالمین»

3- You yourselves know what you have done, and Allah is also aware of them, so why all these claims?! «واللَّه علیم بالظالمین»

Ayah

وَلَتَجِدَنَّهُمْ أَحْرَصَ ٱلنَّاسِ عَلَىٰ حَيَوٰةٍ وَمِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا۟ ۚ يَوَدُّ أَحَدُهُمْ لَوْ يُعَمَّرُ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ وَمَا هُوَ بِمُزَحْزِحِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ أَن يُعَمَّرَ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بَصِيرٌۢ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ (۹٦)
Surely, you will find them the greediest for life, of all people even the idolaters. Each of them is eager to live a thousand years, though it would not deliver him from the punishment, were he to live [that long]. And Allah watches what they do. (96)

Messages

1- A long lifespan is not important; what is valuable is nearness to Allah, the blessing (barakah) in one's life, and salvation from the Fire. [386] «لویعمّر الف سنة وما هو بمزحزحه من العذاب»

2- The Jews are the most greedy and the most worldly people in the world. «احرص الناس على حیوة»

3- The Jews want to remain alive, even if the life is base in any way. «احرص الناس على حیوة» (The word "حیوة" is indefinite (nakirah) and indicates any type of life.)

4- A liar has a poor memory. On the one hand, the Jews consider Paradise exclusive to themselves, «لكم الدار الاخرة خالصة», and on the other hand, they want to live forever in this world. «یعمّر الف سنة» [387]

386) In Tafsir Fakhr Razi, a supplication is narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, who said: If life is good for me, keep me alive, and if death is better, cause me to die. Imam Sajjad عليه السلام also asks Allah in the supplication of Makarim al-Akhlaq: »الهى عمرّنى ما كان عمرى بذلةً فى طاعتك، فاذا كان عمرى مرتعاً للشیطان فاقبضنى ...« O God, if my life is a means of service in obedience to You, prolong my life, but if my life will be a pasture for Satan, then terminate it.

387) Tafsir Rahnamā.

Ayah

قُلْ مَن كَانَ عَدُوًّا لِّجِبْرِيلَ فَإِنَّهُۥ نَزَّلَهُۥ عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَهُدًى وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۹۷)
Say, ‘Whoever is an enemy of Gabriel [should know that] it is he who has brought it down on your heart with the will of Allah, confirming what has been [revealed] before it and as a guidance and good news for the faithful.’ (97)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah, it is narrated: When the Prophet of Islam came to Medina, one day Ibn Suriya (one of the Jewish scholars) came to the Prophet with a group of Jews from Fadak and asked questions. The Prophet answered all their questions and explained every sign they requested. Their last question was: What is the name of your Angel of Revelation? The Prophet replied: Jibril (Gabriel). They said: If it were Mika'il (Michael), we would believe in you, because Jibril brings difficult commands, such as Jihad (struggle), but Mika'il's commands are simple and easy!

Messages

1- The scope of human fantasy and obstinacy extends so far that it even affects the world of angels. [388] «عدوّاً لجبریل» (Enemy to Jibril).

2- It is necessary to support and defend the pure ones who are subjected to accusations. «نزّله...باذن اللَّه» (He brought it down... by Allah's permission). While condemning the assumptions of the Children of Israel, Allah honors Jibril, stating that he does nothing without Our permission and that he is trustworthy (Amin) and the link between Us and the Prophet in his mission.

3- Jibril brought a Book that confirms your Torah, so why are you hostile towards him?! «نزّله... مصدّقاً» (He brought it down... confirming).

388) The obstinate person even accuses the angels. Angels whom Allah considers infallible (Ma'sum) and about whom He says: »لا یعصون اللّه ما امرهم« (They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them) (At-Tahrim, 6). They consider the reason for hostility toward Jibril to be his bringing heavy commands, and the reason for friendship toward Mika'il to be his bringing light commands. This is exactly like a mischievous child who considers the math teacher bad and the physical education teacher good, even though both are active in the field of education and training.

Ayah

مَن كَانَ عَدُوًّا لِّلَّهِ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتِهِۦ وَرُسُلِهِۦ وَجِبْرِيلَ وَمِيكَىٰلَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَدُوٌّ لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۹۸)
[Say,] ‘Whoever is an enemy of Allah, His angels and His apostles and Gabriel and Michael, [let him know that] Allah is the enemy of the faithless.’ (98)

Messages

1- Faith and allegiance are necessary towards all sacred entities. «للّه وملائكته ورسله»

2- Enmity towards the Prophets and the Saints (Awliyā') is disbelief (kufr) and enmity towards Allah. «فانّ اللّه عدّوٌ للكافرین»

3- Not all angels are of the same rank and level. «ملائكته... جبریل ومیكال» (He has mentioned the names of two specific angels: Jibril and Mika'il.)

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ءَايَـٰتٍۭ بَيِّنَـٰتٍ ۖ وَمَا يَكْفُرُ بِهَآ إِلَّا ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (۹۹)
We have certainly sent down manifest signs to you, and no one denies them except transgressors. (99)

Key Points

Man, by following desires and committing sin, exits the orbit of truth and inclines toward disbelief (kufr). The Quran states: The ultimate end of those who commit sin—if they do not repent and make amends—is denial and disbelief. [389] Ibn Suriya, whose story appeared two verses prior, after the Prophet answered his questions, had no excuse except to say: Since Gabriel brings revelation to you and not Michael, we will not believe in you. But since his answer was addressed in the previous verse, he said, in order to deceive the Jewish common folk: He did not present a clear proof to us. The verse was revealed so that the excuses of scholars like Ibn Suriya should not distress you. People should know that the Children of Israel are making excuses, otherwise, We have sent down clear and firm signs and proofs so that no one should doubt your prophethood. With these proofs, only those who are transgressors (fasiq) and who have exited the orbit of truth due to excessive desires and sins will disbelieve.

389) ثمّ كان عاقبة الّذین اساؤا السُؤاى ان كذّبوا بایات اللَّه... (Ar-Rum, 10).

Messages

1- In the face of unjust weakening (of authority), appropriate strengthening must take place. Since the Jewish scholars unjustly weakened the Prophet of Islam and claimed He had no clear proof, Allah strengthened Him. «انزلنا الیك آیات بیّنات»

2- Transgression (fisq) and sin are the grounds for disbelief (kufr). «ما یكفر بها الا الفاسقون»

Ayahs

أَوَكُلَّمَا عَـٰهَدُوا۟ عَهْدًا نَّبَذَهُۥ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱٠٠)
Is it not that whenever they made a covenant, a part of them would cast it away? Rather, the majority of them do not have faith. (100)
وَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ مُصَدِّقٌ لِّمَا مَعَهُمْ نَبَذَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ كِتَـٰبَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَآءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ كَأَنَّهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۱٠۱)
And when there came to them an apostle from Allah, confirming that which is with them, a part of those who were given the Book cast the Book of Allah behind their back, as if they did not know [that it is Allah’s Book]. (101)

Key Points

The Jewish scholars, before the Prophet's mission (Bi'that), used to give glad tidings to the people about his appearance and invitation, and they would recount his signs and characteristics. Based on the signs that were with the Jewish scholars, they recognized Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله just as they recognized their own sons, but after his mission, they sought to deny and conceal those signs.

Messages

1- In dealing with opponents, fairness must be observed. In the previous Ayah, He said: most of them do not believe, so that the right of the minority is preserved. In this Ayah too, He says: a group of them are like this, so that not everyone is viewed the same way. «نبذ فریق منهم»

2- Knowledge that is not acted upon is like ignorance. Regarding the scholars who ignored their knowledge and concealed the truths, He says: «كانّهم لایعلمون»

Ayah

وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ مَا تَتْلُوا۟ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينُ عَلَىٰ مُلْكِ سُلَيْمَـٰنَ ۖ وَمَا كَفَرَ سُلَيْمَـٰنُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يُعَلِّمُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ ٱلسِّحْرَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَى ٱلْمَلَكَيْنِ بِبَابِلَ هَـٰرُوتَ وَمَـٰرُوتَ ۚ وَمَا يُعَلِّمَانِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ حَتَّىٰ يَقُولَآ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ فِتْنَةٌ فَلَا تَكْفُرْ ۖ فَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ مِنْهُمَا مَا يُفَرِّقُونَ بِهِۦ بَيْنَ ٱلْمَرْءِ وَزَوْجِهِۦ ۚ وَمَا هُم بِضَآرِّينَ بِهِۦ مِنْ أَحَدٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ مَا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلَا يَنفَعُهُمْ ۚ وَلَقَدْ عَلِمُوا۟ لَمَنِ ٱشْتَرَىٰهُ مَا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلَـٰقٍ ۚ وَلَبِئْسَ مَا شَرَوْا۟ بِهِۦٓ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (۱٠۲)
And they followed what the devils pursued during Solomon’s reign —and Solomon was not faithless but it was the devils who were faithless—teaching the people magic and what was sent down to the two angels at Babylon, Harut and Marut, who would not teach anyone without telling [him], ‘We are only a test, so do not be faithless.’ But they would learn from those two that with which they would cause a split between man and his wife—though they could not harm anyone with it except with Allah’s leave. They would learn that which would harm them and bring them no benefit; though they certainly knew that anyone who buys it has no share in the Hereafter. Surely, evil is that for which they sold their souls, had they known! (102)

Key Points

What is understood from the narrations is that Prophet Solomon (Sulayman) عليه السلام ordered the collection and preservation of the sorcerers' papers to prevent deviation or misuse. But after his death, a group gained access to those writings and began teaching and spreading sorcery [391] among the people. They even introduced the miracles of Prophet Solomon as sorcery and denied his prophethood. And some of the Children of Israel, instead of following the Torah, resorted to magic and sorcery. This practice became so famous and widespread that the Jews during the time of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله also considered Solomon a skilled sorcerer and did not recognize him as a prophet! This Ayah explains the sin and deviation of abandoning the Torah and following the sorcery-teaching devils (Shayatin), which the Jews followed instead of the Torah—following what the devils recited to the people during Solomon's time. To justify their actions, they also attributed sorcery to Solomon, whereas Solomon was a man of God and his work was a miracle, and the devils were opposed to him and their work was sorcery.

In addition to what has been mentioned, the Jews also acquired sorcery from another source. This was by Allah placing two angels named "Harut" and "Marut" in human form among the people in the city of "Babylon" [392] to teach them the method of nullifying and neutralizing magic and sorcery. However, for this task, it was necessary to first familiarize them with sorcery. Because unless a person knows the compositional formula of something, he cannot neutralize it. Therefore, the angels first taught the method of sorcery and then the method of its nullification, and before teaching, they stipulated to their students that they should not misuse it, saying: This work is a source of trial and testing, lest you yourselves become entangled while trying to combat and neutralize it. But the Jews misused that teaching and exploited it for improper purposes, even using it to separate a man from his wife.

The Quran reminds us here of a point that deserves careful attention and reflection. It states: The sorcerer, despite possessing the knowledge of sorcery, is not outside the sphere of Divine power and cannot perform anything or harm anyone without the Will of Allah. But since Allah has placed an effect in everything, sorcery and magic can also have negative effects, among which is its influence on marital and family relations.

391) Sorcery (Sihr) refers to a subtle and precise art that diverts matters from their normal course. Sometimes sorcery is used in the sense of deception, or presenting falsehood in the form and guise of truth. 392) "Babylon" was the name of a region near the present-day Najaf and Karbala.

Messages

  1. Even under a righteous government and the rule of a Divine leader, not all people are reformed; some groups deviate. «واتبعوا ماتتلوا الشیاطین على ملك سلیمان»
  2. The devils (Shayatin) do not remain idle even during Solomon's rule and continuously inspire evil suggestions. «ما تتلواالشیاطین على ملك سلیمان»
  3. Governance is not incompatible with prophethood. Some prophets held governance. «ملك سلیمان»
  4. Sorcerers, in order to make their work appear sacred and justified, introduced Solomon as a sorcerer as well. «وما كفر سلیمان»
  5. Allah defends the status of the Prophets against the accusations of others. «و ما كفر سلیمان»
  6. Teaching, learning, and practicing sorcery are equivalent to disbelief (Kufr). [393] «كفروا یُعلّمون النّاس السحر»
  7. To counter harmful sciences and deviant beliefs, it is necessary for the righteous to be aware of them. «ما انزل على الملكین»
  8. In the context of teaching, the instructor must warn against the negative applications of the knowledge. «وما یُعلّمان... حتّى یقولا... فلا تكفر»
  9. Man is placed between two types of teaching: both satanic whispers; «یعلّمون الناس السحر» and Divine inspirations. «وما یعلّمان»
  10. Angels can also become teachers for humans. «ما یعلّمان ...حتّى یقولا»
  11. Sometimes knowledge and learning are a means of trial. «انّما نحن فتنة»
  12. Causing separation between a husband and wife is a satanic act and amounts to disbelief (Kufr). «فلا تكفر فیتعلّمون منهما ما یفرّقون به بین المرء...»
  13. Knowledge and education are not always beneficial; sometimes they are harmful and detrimental. «یتعلّمون ما یضرّهم و لاینفعهم»
  14. Sorcery and magic are real and effective in human life. «یفرّقون به...»
  15. The sorcerer performs actions, but all effects and influences are under the supervision of Allah. One can be saved from conspiracies by seeking refuge in Allah, seeking protection (Isti'adhah), reliance (Tawakkul), prayer (Du'a), and charity (Sadaqah). «و ماهم بضارین»
  16. Sorcerers might achieve money and fame, but they will have no share in the Hereafter. «ماله فى الاخرة من خلاق»
  17. Whoever resorts to causing separation and sorcery loses his humanity. «لبئس ما شروا به انفسهم»

393) It is also mentioned in narrations that the sorcerer is a disbeliever (Kafir) and his punishment is execution.

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱتَّقَوْا۟ لَمَثُوبَةٌ مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ ۖ لَّوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (۱٠۳)
Had they been faithful and Godwary, the reward from Allah would have been better, had they known! (103)

Key Points

Taqwa (piety/God-consciousness) is not only about abstaining from evils, but also about vigilance and preservation regarding good deeds. For example, in the phrase «اتّقواالنار» (Fear the Fire), it means protection and self-preservation from the Fire, and in the phrase «اتّقواللّه» (Fear Allah), it means vigilance regarding Divine commands and prohibitions. Just as in the Ayah «اتّقوا اللَّه و... الارحام» (Fear Allah and... the wombs/kinship), it means to maintain preservation regarding family and relatives.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in response to a question about Taqwa, said: Taqwa is like being vigilant when passing through a thorny, prickly, and weed-filled area.

Messages

1- Faith alone is not sufficient; Taqwa (piety) and vigilance are necessary. «امنوا و اتقوا» (Believe and be pious).

2- Divine rewards have no limitations. «لمثوبة» (a reward) is indefinite (nakerah) and a sign of infinity.

3- Divine rewards are certain. This is indicated by the letter Lām (ل) in «لَمثوبة».

4- Divine rewards are better than everything. Nothing follows the word khayr (خير - better), which indicates absolute superiority, not relative superiority.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَقُولُوا۟ رَٰعِنَا وَقُولُوا۟ ٱنظُرْنَا وَٱسْمَعُوا۟ ۗ وَلِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱٠٤)
O you who have faith! Do not say Ra‘ina, but say Unzurna, and listen! And there is a painful punishment for the faithless. (104)

Key Points

Some Muslims, in order to properly understand the Prophet’s words, requested that he speak slowly (with deliberation) and consider their situation. They expressed this request using the word "راعنا," meaning, "Be mindful of us." However, since this expression was considered a type of insult in the Jewish custom, [394] the Ayah was revealed commanding them to say "انظرنا" instead of "راعنا," so that the enemy would not misuse the term. This is the first Ayah from the beginning of the Quran that starts with the address: "یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا" (O you who have believed). From this point onward, there are more than eighty instances that use this same address.

394) "راعنا" is derived from the root ra'y (رعى), meaning to grant respite or consideration. But the Jews derived the word "راعنا" from the root al-ru'ūnah (الرعونة), which means foolishness or stupidity.

Messages

1- Pay attention to the reflection (implication) of your words. "لا تقولوا راعنا..." Individuals might speak with good intentions, but they must also consider the repercussions of their words.

2- The enemy monitors all our actions and even our words, and seeks to exploit every opportunity possible to strike a blow. "لا تقولوا راعنا..."

3- Islam pays attention to the selection of appropriate vocabulary, measured expression, and the method of presenting and conveying the subject matter. "وقولوا انظرنا"

4- When speaking with elders and teachers, verbal etiquette must be observed. "لا تقولوا راعنا و قولوا..."

5- If we advise others to observe etiquette, we must first observe etiquette ourselves when speaking to people. "یا ایها الذین آمنوا" is a respectful address.

6- If something is prohibited, a suitable alternative must be introduced. "لاتقولوا راعنا وقولوا انظرنا"

Ayah

مَّا يَوَدُّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَلَا ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ أَن يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْكُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَخْتَصُّ بِرَحْمَتِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو ٱلْفَضْلِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۱٠٥)
Neither the faithless from among the People of the Book nor the idolaters like that any good be showered on you from your Lord; but Allah singles out for His mercy whomever He wishes, and Allah is dispenser of a mighty grace. (105)

Key Points

This Ayah unveils the malice and enmity of the polytheists and disbelievers, including the Jews and Christians, towards the believers. They are unwilling to see Muslims possess a great Prophet and a heavenly Book, desiring to convey the call of Monotheism (Tawḥīd) to the entire world, fight against all racial and regional discriminations, combat all the superstitions of the polytheists and the distortions of the People of the Book, and prevent the deception of the masses by their leaders. Allah says in this Ayah: Allah's grace and mercy are specifically granted according to His will to whomever He desires, and it has nothing to do with the wishes of others, whether they like it or not!

Messages

1- Recognize the inner intention and intrinsic spirit of the enemy, and never incline towards them. [395] «ما یودّ...»

2- Bestowing good is among the attributes of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «خیر من ربكم»

3- Rely upon Allah (Tawakkul) and do not fear the malice and envy of the enemy. «واللّه یختص»

4- The envy of the envious has no effect whatsoever on Allah's will to grant grace. «من یشاء»

5- Allah's grace, mercy, and guidance encompass all nations and peoples and are not exclusive to the Children of Israel or a specific group. «من یشاء واللَّه ذو الفضل العظیم»

395) They never wish the slightest good to reach the Muslims. Conversely, they take pleasure in their return to disbelief and regression, in their compromise, silence, and weakness towards the disbelievers, and in their falling into hardship. »ودّوالو تكفرون كما كفروا فتكونون سواء« The disbelievers wish that you would disbelieve as they have disbelieved, so that you would be alike. (Surah An-Nisā', 89). »ودّوالو تدهن فیدهنون« The disbelievers wish that you, O Prophet, would compromise with them. (Surah Al-Qalam, 9). »ودّوا ما عنتّم« The disbelievers wish that you Muslims would fall into distress. (Surah Āl ‘Imrān, 118). »ودّت طائفة من اهل الكتاب لو یضلّونكم« A group from the People of the Book wish to lead you astray. (Surah Āl ‘Imrān, 69).

Ayah

۞ مَا نَنسَخْ مِنْ ءَايَةٍ أَوْ نُنسِهَا نَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ مِّنْهَآ أَوْ مِثْلِهَآ ۗ أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱٠٦)
For any verse that We abrogate or cause to be forgotten, We bring another which is better than it, or similar to it. Do you not know that Allah has power over all things? (106)

Key Points

The word nansahā (ننسها) is derived from the root insā' (إنساء), meaning to postpone or to omit/delete. This means that the revelation of an Ayah is postponed and revealed after some time. This Ayah is in the position of responding to the negative propaganda of the Jews. They would ask why certain laws change in Islam. Why was the Qiblah changed from Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) to the Ka'bah? If the first (command) was correct, then what is the second command? And if the second command is correct, then your previous actions are invalid.
