The Quran answers these objections: We do not abrogate any ruling (hukm) or postpone it unless We substitute it with something better than it or similar to it. They are unaware of the educational, social, and political goals and effects of the rulings. Just as a physician prescribes medication for a patient in one stage, but when the patient's condition slightly improves, he changes the medication and treatment plan. Or, as a teacher changes the curriculum as the student progresses, Allah also changes the evolutionary programs for humanity in different and varying times and conditions. One of the manifestations (masādiq) of the Ayah is the continuation of the Imamate in society and the succession of each Imam in place of the previous Imam, just as we read in a hadith concerning this Ayah: Whenever an Imam passes away, another Imam succeeds Him. [396]

396) Nukhbat al-Tafāsīr.

Messages

1- Along with his inherent and constant needs, man sometimes finds himself in specific social, economic, political, and military conditions. In these cases, alongside fixed laws, regulations, and rulings, there must be variable instructions that are conveyed to the people through abrogation (naskh) or by the command of the Prophet and his successors. «ماننسخ من آیة»

2- The change in rulings is not evidence of the failure of the previous plan, but rather a sign of attention to new issues and a wise change. This is similar to changing a book or a teacher. «ماننسخ من آیة... نأت بخیر منها...»

3- Although the commands are variable and the prophets are numerous in appearance, Allah's grace is always consistent. «مثلها»

4- The establishment of rulings, as well as their change and postponement, is in the hands of Allah. «ننسخ، ننسها»

5- Something better must always be substituted, not something inferior. «نأت بخیر منها»

6- Islam never reaches a dead end. Some laws are subject to change. «ننسخ، ننسها»

7- Changing a law, besides requiring various contexts and the emergence of new interests (masālih), requires power. «ننسخ... ان اللَّه على كل شى قدیر»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ (۱٠۷)
Do you not know that to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth? And besides Allah, you do not have any guardian or helper. (107)

Key Points

Those who object and raise issues regarding the change of some rulings and commands do not pay attention to the absolute sovereignty of Allah. The sovereignty of Allah is permanent, inherent, and universal, whereas the sovereignty and governance of others besides God are limited, temporary, contractual, and non-inherent. The Children of Israel had such a wrong perception of Divine sovereignty, believing God to be constrained in exercising His authority, saying: «یداللّه مغلولة» [397], while the Hand of Allah is open in creation and origination, and likewise in establishing and enacting laws and changing and transforming them. [398] «بل یداه مبسوطتان»

397) Al-Ma'idah, 64. 398) Allah can create change in creation whenever He wills; for example, He can make water bitter: «لو نشاء لجعلناه اجاجاً» (Al-Waqi'ah, 70). Or He can dry up trees: «لو نشاء لجلناه حطاماً» (Al-Waqi'ah, 65). And above all, He can destroy all people and create another group: «ان یشاء یذهبكم و یأت بخلق جدید» (Ibrahim, 19). Just as He commanded a group of the Children of Israel: «كونوا قردةًخاسئین» (Al-Baqarah, 65), meaning, "Be despicable apes," and they were transformed from human form into the form of apes. The Children of Israel have seen this type of change and transformation many times throughout their history: the drying up of the sea for their passage, the staff of Prophet Moses عليه السلام turning into a serpent, the splitting of rocks and the flowing of water. How is it that they have seen all this with their own eyes, yet now they find fault with a slight change in rulings and commands?

Messages

1- The right of eternal and absolute sovereignty over the heavens and the earth belongs exclusively to Allah. «له ملك السموات والارض»

2- The changing of laws is exclusive to the God Who possesses the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. Therefore, there is no suspicion of any whim or fear in these changes. «ماننسخ... له ملك السموات...»

3- Satisfying others who are fault-finders and stubborn should not be a priority for you, because no one besides Allah will help or protect you. «ما لكم من دون اللَّه...»

Ayah

أَمْ تُرِيدُونَ أَن تَسْـَٔلُوا۟ رَسُولَكُمْ كَمَا سُئِلَ مُوسَىٰ مِن قَبْلُ ۗ وَمَن يَتَبَدَّلِ ٱلْكُفْرَ بِٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَوَآءَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ (۱٠۸)
Would you question your Apostle as Moses was questioned formerly? Whoever changes faith for unfaith certainly strays from the right way. (108)

Key Points

Considering the Ayah itself and what is derived from the occasions of revelation (Shān Nuzūl), some weak-in-faith Muslims and some polytheists (Mushrikūn) made irrelevant and illogical demands of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. For example, they demanded, "Bring us a letter from Allah!" or "Make rivers flow for us." Others, similar to the Children of Israel (Banī Isrā'īl), said: "Show us Allah openly so that we may see Him with our eyes and believe in Him!" Bringing a miracle (mu'jizah) and completing the proof (itmām al-hujjah) is necessary for the truthfulness of the Prophet's call, but fulfilling every request according to the whim and desire of every person who comes along is not correct. An engineer or a painter presents a few samples of his work to prove his skill, but it is not necessary for him to build a house or paint a picture for everyone. Recounting the difficulties and the history of the Prophets is meant to console the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, [showing] that if people make unreasonable questions and demands of you, the previous Prophets also faced similar demands.

Messages

1- Avoid undue questions and demands, as they sometimes pave the way for disbelief (kufr). «ام تریدون ان تسئلوا... و من یتبدل الكفر»

2- The dangers that threatened the followers of other religions also threaten Muslims. «تسئلوا رسولكم كما سئل موسى»

3- Learn lessons from the fate and outcome of others. «كما سئل موسى»

Ayah

وَدَّ كَثِيرٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَوْ يَرُدُّونَكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ إِيمَـٰنِكُمْ كُفَّارًا حَسَدًا مِّنْ عِندِ أَنفُسِهِم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۖ فَٱعْفُوا۟ وَٱصْفَحُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِىَ ٱللَّهُ بِأَمْرِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱٠۹)
Many of the People of the Book are eager to turn you into unbelievers after your faith, out of their inner envy, [and] after the truth had become manifest to them. Yet excuse [them] and forbear until Allah issues His edict. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (109)

Key Points

'Afw' (عفو) means forgiving the transgression, 'Safh' (صَفح) means overlooking the transgression. The enemy, who inwardly desires your disbelief (kufr), will practically refrain from no plot or scheme. Their methods of action include raising baseless questions, temptations (waswasah), casting doubts (shubuhāt), and so on, against which one must be vigilant.

Messages

1- Be mindful of the dispositions and plans of your enemies. «ودَّ... یرُدّونكم»

2- Fairness must be maintained even when dealing with the enemy. «ودّ كثیر» (The Ayah states: many of the People of the Book are like this, not all of them.)

3- The flame of envy is so dangerous that it does not subside even after knowledge and awareness have been attained. «حسداً... من بعد ما تبین لهم الحقّ»

4- By embracing Islam, you attain a greatness and honor that makes your enemies envious and jealous of you, and they wish for you to return to the ignorance, polytheism, and disunity of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). «ودّ كثیر... لو یردّونكم»

5- One should not immediately resort to violence against opponents; sometimes it is necessary to show forbearance towards them, even knowing their malice and envy. «فاعفوا»

6- The command to pardon the enemy is temporary, lasting until the Muslims do not become disheartened and the enemies do not become emboldened. «فاعفوا و اصفحوا حتّى»

7- Pardon is not a sign of weakness. Allah is capable of doing everything, and even today He can grant you victory over your enemies. «انّ اللّه... قدیر»

Ayah

وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ ۚ وَمَا تُقَدِّمُوا۟ لِأَنفُسِكُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ تَجِدُوهُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۱۱٠)
And maintain the prayer and give the zakat. Any good that you send ahead for your own souls, you shall find it with Allah. Indeed Allah watches what you do. (110)

Key Points

Following the first address in Ayah 104, "یا ایها الذین آمنوا" (O you who have believed), this Ayah carries the third command for the Muslims.

In the first command, Allah reminded them to observe etiquette when speaking with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, so that they would be careful in their speech not to give enemies and opponents an excuse or pretext for mockery. "لاتقولوا راعنا و قولوا اُنظرنا"

The second command was forgiveness and overlooking the malice and envy of the People of the Book until the issuance of a new command. "فاعفوا و اصفحوا"

The third command is the establishment of prayer (Salat) and the payment of Zakat. At a time when Muslims were targets of all kinds of malice and envy and had been commanded to forgive and overlook, it is necessary for them to strengthen their relationship with Allah through the establishment of prayer, and their connection with the deprived members of society through Zakat.

Messages

1- Usually, the command for prayer is mentioned alongside Zakat in the Quran. This means that the remembrance of God must be accompanied by attention to God's creation. "اقیموا الصلوة واتوا الزكوة"

2- A person's deeds enter the arena of the Day of Judgment before the person himself. "تقدّموا لانفسكم"

3- The quantity of good deeds is not important; everyone must perform whatever amount they are able to. "من خیر"

4- Good deeds are preserved for the Day of Judgment. "تجدوه"

5- Belief in Divine supervision and reward on the Day of Judgment is the strongest motivation for righteous action. "ما تقدموا... تجدوه عند اللَّه انّ اللَّه بما تعملون بصیر"

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ لَن يَدْخُلَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ إِلَّا مَن كَانَ هُودًا أَوْ نَصَـٰرَىٰ ۗ تِلْكَ أَمَانِيُّهُمْ ۗ قُلْ هَاتُوا۟ بُرْهَـٰنَكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱۱۱)
And they say, ‘No one will enter paradise except one who is a Jew or Christian.’ Those are their [false] hopes! Say, ‘Produce your evidence, should you be truthful.’ (111)

Messages

1- Religious arrogance caused the Jews and Christians to consider themselves a superior race and Paradise to be their exclusive domain. «الاّ مَن كان هوداً او نصارى»

2- Seeking privilege and self-superiority is a baseless wish and a vain delusion. «تلك امانیّهم»

3- Claims made without proof are condemned. «قل هاتوا برهانكم»

4- Every belief must be based on evidence. «قالوا... قل هاتوا برهانكم» The Quran presents its own statements with proof and also demands proof from its opponents.

Ayah

بَلَىٰ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُۥ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ فَلَهُۥٓ أَجْرُهُۥ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (۱۱۲)
Certainly, whoever submits his will to Allah and is virtuous, he shall have his reward from his Lord, and they will have no fear, nor shall they grieve. (112)

Key Points

The Ayah considers the reason for entering Paradise to be submission to the command of Allah and being a doer of good. Meaning, Paradise is not granted to anyone based on mere claims or slogans, but rather faith and righteous deeds are necessary.

Messages

1- For entering Paradise, instead of imagination and wishes, both submission to Allah and inner faith are necessary, as well as external righteous deeds. «اسلم وجهه لله و هومحسن»

2- Doing good must be the consistent practice of man, not in a seasonal or temporary manner. «هو محسن»

3- Granting reward is among the affairs of Lordship. «اجره عند ربه»

4- Whoever sincerely turns towards Allah, will both have a complete share (reward); «فله اجره عند ربه» and will also be safe from every kind of anxiety. «لا خوف علیهم»

Ayah

وَقَالَتِ ٱلْيَهُودُ لَيْسَتِ ٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ وَقَالَتِ ٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰ لَيْسَتِ ٱلْيَهُودُ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ وَهُمْ يَتْلُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ مِثْلَ قَوْلِهِمْ ۚ فَٱللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ (۱۱۳)
The Jews say, ‘The Christians stand on nothing,’ and the Christians say, ‘The Jews stand on nothing,’ though they follow the [same] Book. So said those who had no knowledge, [words] similar to what they say. Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ. (113)

Key Points

This Ayah is a renewed depiction of the prejudiced character of the People of the Book, where each group seeks to negate the other. The Jews introduce the Christians as being upon falsehood and having no standing before Allah, and conversely, the Christians also consider the Jews to be without status in the sight of Allah. Such behaviors stem from their prejudiced spirit, whereas if they paid attention to their own heavenly book, they would desist from these behaviors. Then [Allah] states: The polytheists and idolaters, despite not having a heavenly book, say the same things. That is, the adherents of false beliefs negate one another, but all these differences will end on the Day of Resurrection with the judgment of Allah the Almighty, and they will witness the truth.

Messages

1- Undue prejudice and baseless monopolization are forbidden. Humiliating and ignoring others is a sign of tyranny and self-centeredness. [399] «لیست النصارى على شى ء... لیست الیهود على شى ء»

2- If prejudice and selfishness exist, knowledge cannot guide either. The People of the Book were able to recite it, but due to their spirit of monopolization, the recitations were ineffective. «وهم یتلون الكتاب»

3- In an atmosphere mixed with prejudice, the knowledgeable and the ignorant think alike. The ignorant polytheists said the same thing that the reciters of the Torah and the Gospel said. «كذلك قال الذین لا یعلمون»

399) Every group boasts about what it possesses, and these undue boastings are the root of many conflicts (fitan). In Surah Al-Mu'minun, Ayah 53, there is another expression: »كلّ حزب بمالدیهم فرحون« Every group is joyful with what is with them.

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن مَّنَعَ مَسَـٰجِدَ ٱللَّهِ أَن يُذْكَرَ فِيهَا ٱسْمُهُۥ وَسَعَىٰ فِى خَرَابِهَآ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مَا كَانَ لَهُمْ أَن يَدْخُلُوهَآ إِلَّا خَآئِفِينَ ۚ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا خِزْىٌ وَلَهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱۱٤)
Who is a greater wrongdoer than those who deny access to the mosques of Allah lest His Name be celebrated therein, and try to ruin them? Such ones may not enter them, except in fear. There is disgrace for them in this world and a great punishment in the Hereafter. (114)

Key Points

Based on what is derived from the occasions of revelation (Shān Nuzūl) and some narrations, the Ayah was revealed concerning those who intended to destroy mosques. Throughout history, the destruction of mosques or the prevention of their flourishing has repeatedly occurred at the hands of misguided individuals and tyrants (Tāghūts). From the destruction of Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) and the burning of the Torah by Christians led by a person named "Fatlūs," to the Quraysh preventing Muslims from entering al-Masjid al-Harām, these are all signs of such efforts.

Today, on the one hand, we witness the destruction of mosques remaining from the early days of Islam near the tombs of the Imams of Baqī' علیهم السلام, under the pretext of fighting polytheism (Shirk). On the other hand, we see the ruin of historical mosques, such as the Babri Mosque in India, which indicates the antiquity of Muslims in the subcontinent. All of this speaks to the blasphemous spirit of tyrants and ignorant people who fear the remembrance and Name of Allah that resonates in the centers of Monotheism (Tawhīd).

This Ayah warns parents and elders who prevent their children from going to mosques.

If the destruction of a mosque is oppression, then the development (or maintenance) of a mosque is the most beneficial of deeds. [400]

400) Tafsīr Fakhr al-Rāzī.

Messages

1- Cultural oppression is the greatest form of oppression. «و من اظلم» (In the Quran, Aẓlam [most oppressive] is used for fabricating lies against Allah and closing the House of Allah, both of which have cultural aspects.)

2- The destruction of a mosque is not only done with a shovel and pickaxe; rather, any program that diminishes the flourishing of the mosque is an effort toward its destruction. «منع مساجد اللَّه»

3- Mosques are acceptable when the remembrance of Allah is kept alive within them, and God-pleasing matters and the rulings of Allah are recounted. «یذكر فیه اسمه»

4- The enemy does not fear the doors and walls of the mosque; their fear is from the Name of Allah being revived and the awakening of Muslims. «ان یذكر فیها اسمه»

5- Mosques are bastions of struggle; therefore, the enemy strives to destroy them. «سعى فى خرابها»

6- The mosque must be flourishing and rich in content, like a military command post. Just as a spy is terrified and anxious about infiltrating military centers, opponents and enemies must also be fearful and worried about penetrating and entering mosques. «ما كان لهم ان یدخلوها الاّ خائفین»

7- Those who rise up to fight against religious sanctities, besides incurring divine wrath on the Day of Judgment, will also suffer humiliation and disgrace in this world. [401] «سعى فى خرابها... لهم فى الدنیا خزى»

401) If striving to destroy a building that reminds people of Allah brings worldly disgrace and eternal punishment, then those who, through rumor, slander, and contempt, break the sanctity of men who remind society of Allah, will certainly suffer disgrace and misery in this very world.

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ ٱلْمَشْرِقُ وَٱلْمَغْرِبُ ۚ فَأَيْنَمَا تُوَلُّوا۟ فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَٰسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱۱٥)
To Allah belong the east and the west: so whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah! Allah is indeed all-bounteous, all-knowing. (115)

Key Points

After the change of the Qiblah (direction of prayer) for Muslims from Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) to the Ka'bah, the Jews raised questions or cast doubts regarding the reason for the change of the Qiblah. Although in the previous verses [402], Allah briefly and implicitly answered these objections, this verse also emphasizes the reality that the East and the West belong to Allah, and wherever you turn, Allah is there. If the Ka'bah has been designated as the Qiblah, it is for the manifestation of Muslim unity and the renewal of the memories of the sacrifice and anti-polytheism struggle of Abraham (عليه السلام). For this reason, it possesses sanctity and respect.

As stated in the legal treatises (Risalah) of the Maraji' (religious authorities), the Qiblah is not a condition for supererogatory prayers (Nawafil), and they can even be performed while walking or riding. Some narrations also refer to this point in connection with this verse. [403]

If, according to the previous verse, a group attempted to destroy the Mosque, you should not give up, for attention to Allah is not restricted to a specific direction.

Whether the direction of the Qiblah is toward the East or the West, it is a pedagogical and political matter; the essence and the goal are the remembrance of Allah and connection with Him. The Quran, in praise of a group of servants, states: «یذكرون اللّه قیاماً و قعوداً و على جنوبهم» [404] (They remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying on their sides). Or, in response to the clamor created over the change of the Qiblah, He says: «لیس البر ان تولّوا وجوهكم قِبل المشرق والمغرب ولكنّ البرّ من آمن باللّه» [405] meaning: O people! Righteousness is not that you turn your faces toward the East or the West during worship, but rather righteousness lies in your true faith in Allah and the performance of deeds pleasing to Him.

Although the previous verse spoke of the greatest injustice, namely the destruction of mosques or the prevention of access to mosques, and elsewhere He says: If it were not for Jihad, monasteries, churches, and mosques would have been destroyed. [406] However, the verse brings the glad tidings that you should not despair or feel hopelessness and helplessness; the entire world is a place of worship, and every part of existence is a Qiblah.

402) Baqarah, 106.

403) Imam Baqir (عليه السلام) says: »انزل اللَّه هذه الایة فى التطوع خاصّة« This verse was revealed specifically concerning supererogatory prayers. Tafsir Burhan; Tafsir Rahnamā.

404) Aal-i Imran 191.

405) Baqarah, 177.

406) Hajj, 40.

Messages

1- Any action that is commanded by Allah and possesses a divine color is the Face of Allah (Wajh Allah) and an act of worship. «اینما تولّوا فثَمّ وجه اللَّه»

2- Allah is present everywhere and oversees everything. «انّ اللَّه واسع علیم»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ وَلَدًا ۗ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ ۖ بَل لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ كُلٌّ لَّهُۥ قَـٰنِتُونَ (۱۱٦)
And they say, ‘Allah has taken a son.’ Immaculate is He! Rather, to Him belongs whatever there is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him, (116)

Key Points

The People of the Book and the polytheists, each in their own way, imagined a son for Allah. The Jews said: 'Uzayr is the son of God. [407] The Christians also introduced Jesus (عليه السلام) as the son of God [408], and the polytheists considered the angels to be the daughters of God. [409] This Ayah is a refutation of this wrong and misplaced delusion, and it declares the Essence of Allah to be far removed (transcendent) from such an attribution.

We should not compare Allah with ourselves. If a human being needs a child, it is due to the following reasons:

1- His life is limited, and he desires the survival of himself and his lineage.

2- His power is limited, and he needs an assistant and a helper.

3- He is in need of affection and emotion, and it is necessary for him to have a companion.

But Allah is transcendent (far removed) from all these shortcomings and needs. Rather, everything in the heavens and the earth is humble before Him.

407) وقالت الیهود عزیر ابن اللّه (Tawbah, 30).

408) وقالت النصارى المسیح ابن اللّه (Tawbah, 30).

409) یجعلون للّه البنات (Nahl, 57).

Messages

1- What shortcoming does the God before Whom all the heavens and the earth are submissive have, that He would need to compensate for it by taking a son? «بل له ما فى السموات»

2- Humility and submission are only fitting before the One to Whom all existence belongs, not before idols and tyrants who are incapable of creating even a single fly and possess no power to benefit or harm. «كلّ له قانتون»

Ayah

بَدِيعُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَإِذَا قَضَىٰٓ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ (۱۱۷)
the Originator of the heavens and the earth. When He decides on a matter, He just says to it, ‘Be!’ and it is. (117)

Key Points

He is not only the Owner of all beings, but He is also their Creator, and He creates them in an innovative manner (badi') and without any prior plan. So, what need does He have for a son?! Whenever He wills the existence of something, He says to it: Be! And it is created immediately. According to the expression of Hadrat Reza عليه السلام: Allah, in His work, does not even need to utter the word "Kun" (Be); His Will is the same as the act of creation. [410]

In these few verses, we have become acquainted with Divine power: «للّه المشرق والمغرب» (The East and the West belong to Allah). «له مافى السّموات والارض» (What is in the heavens and the earth belongs to Him). «كلٌ له قانتون» (All are humble and obedient to Him). «بدیع السموات والارض» (He is the Originator of the heavens and the earth). «كن فیكون» (By His command, everything comes into existence).

Just as Allah creates in an instant, in His capacity as the All-Dominant (Qahhariyyah), He can also destroy everything in an instant. As He states in Ayah 19 of Surah Ibrahim: «ان یشأ یذهبكم و یأت بخلق جدید» (If He wills, He can remove you and bring forth a new creation). Faith in such power, where the existence and non-existence of everything is in His hand, grants man extraordinary capability and prevents him from despair and hopelessness.

410) فارادةاللَّه الفعل لاغیرذلك یقول له كن فیكون بلالفظ ولانطق ولاهمّة ولاتفكّر« Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 109.

Messages

1- Allah's creation is always innovative and original. «بدیع»

2- Allah can create the entire universe in a single moment; «كن فیكون» although His wisdom dictates that the chain of causes should be involved and creation should occur gradually.

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ لَوْلَا يُكَلِّمُنَا ٱللَّهُ أَوْ تَأْتِينَآ ءَايَةٌ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّثْلَ قَوْلِهِمْ ۘ تَشَـٰبَهَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ قَدْ بَيَّنَّا ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (۱۱۸)
Those who have no knowledge say, ‘Why does not Allah speak to us, or come to us a sign?’ So said those who were before them, [words] similar to what they say. Alike are their hearts. We have certainly made the signs clear for a people who have certainty. (118)

Key Points

Yet another inappropriate demand from another group of disbelievers! Ignorant individuals say the following in response to the invitation of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: Why does Allah not speak directly to us? Why is an Ayah not revealed directly to us?

To prevent the potential negative effects of such vain and inappropriate words on other Muslims and to console the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, the Qur'an treats their question and demand as very ordinary, stating that these questions are nothing new, and the disbelievers before them also had such inappropriate expectations from previous Prophets.

Then He says: The way of thinking of both groups is similar, but We do not assign any validity or value to such requests. This is because We have sufficiently clarified Our Ayat (signs) for those who seek the truth.

Inappropriate expectations arise either due to a spirit of arrogance and self-importance, or due to ignorance and foolishness. The ignorant person does not know that the descent of the Angel of Revelation is not possible upon every heart, and the Wise One does not pour pure and pleasant syrup into every vessel. We read in the Qur'an: If you were pure-hearted and righteous in conduct, We would grant you Furqan (the criterion) and the power of discernment. [411]

411) ان تتّقوا اللّه یجعل لكم فرقاناً« انفال، ۲۹.

Messages

1- The essence lies in effort and merit, not in demanding and expecting. There are groups who, instead of striving and demonstrating merit, always raise their inappropriate expectations. «لولا یكلّمنا اللّه»

2- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to complete the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah) and state the evidence, not to respond to the desires and carnal inclinations of every person. «قد بیّنا الایات»

Ayah

إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا ۖ وَلَا تُسْـَٔلُ عَنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۱۱۹)
Indeed We have sent you with the truth, as a bearer of good news and as a warner, and you will not be questioned concerning the inmates of hell. (119)

Key Points

O noble Messenger! Do not pay attention to the unreasonable demands of the disbelievers. Each of them expects multiple sheets of verses to be revealed to them: «بل یرید كلّ امرء منهم ان یؤتى صحفاً منشّرةً» (412) While We have sent you to the people with the logic of truth so that, through glad tidings and warnings, you may acquaint them with their own felicity. If a group makes excuses and refuses to accept the truth, expecting revelation to descend upon them, you are not responsible for their Hellfire. You are only entrusted with glad tidings and warnings, and you are not responsible for the outcome, whether people accept or reject it. Glad tidings and warnings are signs of human free will. This meaning is repeatedly stated in the Quran: that which is incumbent upon Allah is the sending of an infallible Prophet with the logic of truth, accompanied by glad tidings and warnings. Now, it is up to the people themselves to freely accept the path of truth or stubbornly persist in obstinacy.

412) Al-Muddaththir, 52.

Messages

1- In the face of every demoralization from the enemy, there is a need for spiritual strengthening, consolation, and reassurance from Allah. «ارسلناك بالحقّ» (In the previous verse, the excuses of the disbelievers were mentioned; in this verse, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is confirmed and strengthened by the word «بالحقّ».)

2- Glad tidings and threats must be in balance, like the two pans of a scale; otherwise, they lead to arrogance or despair. «بشیراً ونذیراً»

Ayah

وَلَن تَرْضَىٰ عَنكَ ٱلْيَهُودُ وَلَا ٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰ حَتَّىٰ تَتَّبِعَ مِلَّتَهُمْ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ هُدَى ٱللَّهِ هُوَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ ۗ وَلَئِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَ أَهْوَآءَهُم بَعْدَ ٱلَّذِى جَآءَكَ مِنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ ۙ مَا لَكَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ (۱۲٠)
Never will the Jews be pleased with you, nor the Christians, unless you followed their creed. Say, ‘Indeed it is the guidance of Allah which is [true] guidance.’ And should you follow their desires after the knowledge that has come to you, you will not have against Allah any guardian or helper. (120)

Key Points

After the change of the Qibla, the distress of the Jews towards the Muslims increased, and perhaps some Muslims also wished the Qibla remained Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) so they could live in harmony and friendship with the Jews. They were unaware that the satisfaction of the People of the Book would not be achieved by maintaining that (Qibla), and their expectation was adherence to their entire religion, not just their Qibla.

Although this Ayah is addressed to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, it is also addressed to all Muslims throughout history: the Jews and Christians will never be satisfied with you unless you surrender to them unconditionally and abandon the divine principles and values. But you Muslims must decisively reject the advances of the stranger (the non-believer) and know that the only path to felicity is the path of revelation, not following the desires of this person or that.

Messages

1- The enemy is not satisfied with little. He will only be satisfied with your complete downfall, the obliteration of your school (of thought), and the disintegration of your goals. «لن ترضى عنك...»

2- If Muslims observe that the disbelievers are satisfied with their religion, they must doubt their own piety. Disbelief-pleasing religiosity is itself disbelief. «لن ترضى... حتّى تتّبع ملّتهم»

3- Except for divine revelation and guidance, all paths are deviations. «قل ان هدى اللّه هوالهدى»

4- The responsibility of the knowledgeable person is greater than that of the ignorant person. «بعد الذى جائك من العلم»

5- Relations with the People of the Book must not come at the cost of abandoning principles. Attracting others, yes; retreating from principles, never. «لئن اتّبعت اهوائهم...»

6- Following the inclinations and whims of people leads to the cessation of divine favors. Either the favor of Allah, or the whims of people. «ما لك من اللّه من ولىّ ولانصیر»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يَتْلُونَهُۥ حَقَّ تِلَاوَتِهِۦٓ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ ۗ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِهِۦ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (۱۲۱)
Those to whom We have given the Book follow it as it ought to be followed: they have faith in it. As for those who deny it—it is they who are the losers. (121)

Key Points

In contrast to the Jews and Christians who were criticized due to their obstinacy and rebellion, a group among them has been praised and appreciated by Allah. They are those who refer back to the heavenly Books, see the signs of the advent and mission of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله therein, and believe in him. It is stated in Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan: The meaning of "the Book" (الكتاب) in this Ayah is the Quran, and the addressees are the Muslims who fulfill the right of its recitation (Haqq al-Tilawah) and believe in the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. Tafsir Al-Mizan has also considered this meaning probable. A narration concerning the etiquette of reciting the Quran is cited in Tafsir Al-Mizan, which highlights eight points: 1- Reciting the verses rhythmically (Tartil). 2- Understanding the verses (Tafaqquh). 3- Acting upon the verses. 4- Hoping for the promises. 5- Fearing the warnings. 6- Taking lessons from the stories. 7- Fulfilling the Divine commands. 8- Abandoning the prohibitions. At the end of the narration, Imam Sadiq عليه السلام also said: The right of recitation (Haqq al-Tilawah) is not limited merely to memorizing the verses, studying the letters, recitation, and Tajwid (elocution). And based on narrations, those who truly fulfill the right of reciting the Quran are only the Infallible Imams. [413]

413) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 215.

Messages

1- Observing fairness is necessary, even when dealing with opponents. [414] «الّذین اتیناهم الكتاب یتلونه حقّ تلاوته»

2- The right of recitation (Haqq al-Tilawah) is not summarized merely in reciting with a beautiful voice and observing the rules of Tajwid (elocution); rather, faith and action are also necessary. «یتلونه حقّ تلاوته اؤلئك یؤمنون به»

3- Whoever deviates in choosing the school of thought (or path) is the true loser. «فاؤلئك هم الخاسرون»

414) In the verses where the Quran criticizes the Children of Israel, words such as "many" (كثیر), "most" (اكثر), and "a group" (فریق) are used so that the right of the pure-hearted and faithful individuals is not lost.

Ayahs

يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتِىَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَنِّى فَضَّلْتُكُمْ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱۲۲)
O Children of Israel, remember My blessing which I bestowed upon you, and that I gave you an advantage over all the nations. (122)
وَٱتَّقُوا۟ يَوْمًا لَّا تَجْزِى نَفْسٌ عَن نَّفْسٍ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يُقْبَلُ مِنْهَا عَدْلٌ وَلَا تَنفَعُهَا شَفَـٰعَةٌ وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ (۱۲۳)
Beware of the Day when no soul will compensate for another, neither will any ransom be accepted from it, nor will any intercession benefit it, nor will they be helped. (123)

Key Points

These two verses are similar to verses 47 and 48 of this very Surah, which were mentioned earlier, and we have discussed points there.

Ayah

۞ وَإِذِ ٱبْتَلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ رَبُّهُۥ بِكَلِمَـٰتٍ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ ۖ قَالَ إِنِّى جَاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِمَامًا ۖ قَالَ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِى ۖ قَالَ لَا يَنَالُ عَهْدِى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱۲٤)
When his Lord tested Abraham with certain words and he fulfilled them, He said, ‘I am making you the Imam of mankind.’ Said he, ‘And from among my descendants?’ He said, ‘My pledge does not extend to the unjust.’ (124)

Key Points

Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام) holds a special status and position among the prophets. The name of that great man is mentioned 69 times in 25 chapters of the Quran, and He is mentioned, like the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله), as an uswah (role model) and example for humanity. In some verses, that great man is counted among the righteous, the virtuous, the devout, the truthful, the patient, and the fulfillers (of covenants). In honoring this great Prophet, Allah has used specific expressions, and they have even said that one of the secrets of Hajj is the remembrance and preservation of the sacrifices and selflessness of Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام). That divine man struggled against deviation and polytheism in various arenas; His proof and argument against the star and moon worshipers, His practical struggle against the idolaters, His debate against Nimrod, and His success in other trials, made Him a role model and example, whom Allah appointed to the station of Imamate (leadership).

The issue of azmayish (trial) and imtihan (test) is raised about twenty times in the Quran and is one of the divine traditions (sunan). The goal of the test: Testing is not for Allah to become aware, because He knows everything beforehand; these tests are for the manifestation and emergence of hidden talents and the effort and action of human beings. If a person does not act, he will not deserve a reward. The tools of testing: The bitterness and sweetness of life are all instruments of examination. Sometimes individuals are tested with hardships and calamities, and others with prosperity, and inevitably all people, even the prophets, are subjected to examination. These examinations and tests are for the growth and development of people.

Ibrahim (عليه السلام), with every test He succeeded in, attained a station: First, He became Abdullah (Servant of Allah). Then He reached the station of Nabiullah (Prophet of Allah). After that, Rasulullah (Messenger of Allah), Khalilullah (Friend of Allah), and finally, He was appointed to the station of Imamate and leadership of the people. (415)

The meaning of al-kalimat (the words/commands) in the Ayah refers to the severe tests from which Prophet Ibrahim emerged victorious in all of them. Neither His uncle, nor His wife, nor His son could prevent Him from fulfilling His duty. Although Ibrahim was under the guardianship of His uncle Azar, He rose up to fight against his and his society's idolatry and became the great idol-breaker of history. And when He was commanded by God to settle His wife and son in the desert of Mecca and go to another place for propagation, He entrusted them to God without any attachment and left. Similarly, when the command came to sacrifice His son Ismail, He preferred the pleasure of God over His heart's desire and placed the knife upon His son's throat. But the call came that Our intention was not to kill Ismail, but to test Ibrahim.

Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام) also requested the station of Imamate for His descendants. The response came that this station is a divine covenant ('ahd) granted to whosoever is worthy. Among your descendants, those who possess the worthiness will attain this station. Just as the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) is from the lineage of Ibrahim (عليه السلام) and attained this station, but those who have committed the slightest sin or oppression (zulm) do not deserve this station.

Ibrahim (عليه السلام) is the only prophet whom polytheists, Jews, and Christians all consider themselves followers of and on His path. In this Ayah, while honoring Ibrahim (عليه السلام), it indirectly makes everyone understand that if they truly accept Him, they must abandon polytheism and be completely submissive to the divine commands, just as He was. This Ayah is one of the verses that forms the intellectual and doctrinal foundation for the Shi'a belief that the Imam must be infallible (ma'sum), and anyone upon whom the title of oppressor (zalim) applies will not attain the station of Imamate. (416)

This Ayah refers to the station of Imamate as a "covenant" ('ahd). Therefore, the Ayah: «اوفوا بعهدى اوف بعهدكم» (Fulfill My covenant, I will fulfill your covenant) means that if (417) you are loyal to and obey the Imam whom I have appointed, I will also fulfill the promise of help and assistance that I have given.

415) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 175. 416) In Tafsir Al-Manar, Vol. 1, p. 457, it is stated: Abu Hanifa, based on this Ayah, opposed the government of the time (Mansur Abbasi) and did not accept their judicial positions. It then says: The four Imams of the Sunnis opposed the governments of their time because they considered them oppressors. Of course, in historical sources and texts, things contrary to the claim of the author of Al-Manar are seen. 417) Baqarah 40.

Messages

  1. Prophets are also subjected to divine testing. «واذ ابتلى ابراهیم»
  2. Selection and testing are necessary for appointing individuals to high stations. «اذ ابتلى ابراهیم... بكلمات»
  3. The source of Imamate is not inheritance, but worthiness, which is proven by success in divine tests. «فاتمّهن»
  4. Positions and responsibilities must be entrusted to individuals gradually, after success in each stage. «فاتمّهن...»
  5. Imamate is an elevated station and one of the divine offices. The Imam must necessarily be appointed by Allah. «انّى جاعلك»
  6. Imamate is a divine covenant, and this covenant must always exist between God and the people. «عهدى»
  7. Among the most important conditions for leadership are justice and a good track record. Anyone who has a history of polytheism or oppression is unworthy of Imamate. (418) «لا ینال عهدى الظالمین»

418) In Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan, quoting from Amali Tusi, it is narrated that Ibn Mas'ud quoted the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) as saying that Allah said: »من سجد لصنم دونى، لا اجعله اماماً... انا و علىّ بن ابى طالب لم یسجد احدنا لصنم قطّ« (Whoever prostrates to an idol besides Me, I will not make him an Imam... And neither I nor Ali ibn Abi Talib ever prostrated to an idol.)

Ayah

وَإِذْ جَعَلْنَا ٱلْبَيْتَ مَثَابَةً لِّلنَّاسِ وَأَمْنًا وَٱتَّخِذُوا۟ مِن مَّقَامِ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ مُصَلًّى ۖ وَعَهِدْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ أَن طَهِّرَا بَيْتِىَ لِلطَّآئِفِينَ وَٱلْعَـٰكِفِينَ وَٱلرُّكَّعِ ٱلسُّجُودِ (۱۲٥)
And [remember] when We made the House a place of reward for mankind and a sanctuary, [declaring], ‘Take the venue of prayer from Abraham’s Station.’ We charged Abraham and Ishmael [with its upkeep, saying], ‘Purify My House for those who go around it, [for] those who make it a retreat and [for] those who bow and prostrate.’ (125)

Key Points

After referring to the exalted status of Abraham (عليه السلام) in the previous Ayah, [Allah] refers to his legacy, which is the Ka'bah, and says: We made the Ka'bah a mathābah for the people. The word mathābah (مثابة) comes from the root thawb (ثوب), meaning returning to the original state. The Ka'bah is the place of return and the meeting place for all people—a secure and sacred meeting place. The Station of Abraham (Maqām Ibrāhīm) is a place approximately 13 meters away from the Ka'bah, where it is obligatory for pilgrims, after performing the circumambulation (Tawāf) of the House of God, to offer two units (rak'ahs) of prayer behind it. A stone is located there upon which Prophet Abraham stood to raise the walls of the Ka'bah, and that stone is currently preserved in a casing. The meaning of ‘ahd (عهد) in this Ayah is the Divine command. ‘Ahadnā (عهدنا) means We commanded. Allah is not a body that requires a house. The word Baytī (بیتى) [My House] in this Ayah is similar to Shahr Allāh (شهراللّه) [The Month of Allah] regarding the month of Ramadan, where Allah attributes a specific place or time to Himself in order to grant it honor. And it is sufficient for the greatness of the Ka'bah that sometimes He says: Baytī (بیتى) [My House]. And sometimes He says: Rabb hādhā al-Bayt (ربّ هذا البیت) [Lord of this House].

Messages

1- The personality of Prophet Abraham is a universal personality. In the previous Ayah, He made Abraham an Imam for all people, and in this Ayah, He made the Ka'bah the meeting place for all people. «جعلنا البیت مثابة للنّاس»

2- Society needs an Imam and security. In the previous Ayah, He said: «انّى جاعلك للناس اماماً» (Indeed, I will make you an Imam for the people), and in this Ayah, He says: «واذ جعلنا البیت مثابةً للناس وأمناً» (And [mention] when We made the House a place of return for the people and [a place of] security).

3- He who is willing to submit himself, his wife, and his child in the way of Allah, all heads must bow down to Allah in the place where he stood. Muṣallā (مصلّى). (419)

4- Since the House belongs to Allah; Baytī (بیتى) [My House], its servant must also be among His chosen friends (Awliyā’). «طهّرابیتى» (Purify My House).

5- It is sufficient honor for the pilgrims of the House of Allah that Abraham and Ishmael were responsible for the purification and cleanliness of that House for their sake. «طهّرا بیتى للطائفین...» (Purify My House for those who perform Tawāf...).

6- Worship is linked to purity. «طهّرا... والركع السجود» (Purify... and those who bow and prostrate).

7- Worship and prayer are so important that even Abraham and Ishmael were commissioned to purify [the House] for the performance of its rites. «طهّرا بیتى لِ ... الرّكّع السجود» (Purify My House for... those who bow and prostrate).

8- The mosque of the Muslims must be clean and inviting. «طهّرا... الرّكّع السجود» (Purify... those who bow and prostrate).

(419) The content of a tradition mentioned in Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 122.

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْ هَـٰذَا بَلَدًا ءَامِنًا وَٱرْزُقْ أَهْلَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ مَنْ ءَامَنَ مِنْهُم بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۖ قَالَ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَأُمَتِّعُهُۥ قَلِيلًا ثُمَّ أَضْطَرُّهُۥٓ إِلَىٰ عَذَابِ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۱۲٦)
And when Abraham said, ‘My Lord, make this a secure town, and provide its people with fruits—such of them as have faith in Allah and the Last Day,’ He said, ‘As for him who is faithless, I will provide for him [too] for a short time, then I will shove him toward the punishment of the Fire and it is an evil destination.’ (126)

Key Points

In the previous verses, Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) requested the status of Imamate (leadership) for his descendants, but Allah replied: This status and rank will not reach the unjust individuals. In this verse, Prophet Abraham requested worldly provision (rizq) only for the believers, but Allah does not accept this limitation and does not refrain from granting material provision to the disbelievers (kuffar). Meaning, material provision is not important, therefore it is given to both the worthy and the unworthy, but spiritual, social, and leadership statuses are very important and are not entrusted to just anyone.

Messages

1- Prophets, in addition to guiding and directing people, also pay attention to the material needs of the people, such as security and livelihood, and strive and pray for them. «ربّ اجعل هذا بلداً امناً»

2- Enjoyment of blessings is delicious and pleasant when it occurs in a secure, calm, and anxiety-free environment. «امناً وارزق»

3- In prayer (du'a), let us not forget others. Instead of saying «وارزقنا» (and provide for us), He said: «وارزق اهله» (and provide for its people).

4- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is to provide sustenance (rizq) to all people, whether Muslim or disbeliever, and this tradition is not compromised even by the prayer of Abraham (عليه السلام). «ومن كفر فامتعه»

5- Enjoyment in this world is not a sign of Allah's favor upon a person. «ومن كفر فامتعه»

6- When helping fellow human beings, pay no attention to their school of thought (or belief system). «ومن كفر فامتّعه»

7- Material enjoyments, however great they may be, are little compared to the blessings of the Hereafter. «قلیلاً»

Ayah

وَإِذْ يَرْفَعُ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ ٱلْقَوَاعِدَ مِنَ ٱلْبَيْتِ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلُ رَبَّنَا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّآ ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۱۲۷)
As Abraham raised the foundations of the House with Ishmael, [they prayed]: ‘Our Lord, accept it from us! Indeed You are the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (127)

Key Points

It is understood from numerous verses and narrations that the House of Ka'bah existed since the time of Prophet Adam, and Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) renewed its construction. Just as he (Abraham) said when settling his wife and child in the land of Mecca: «ربّنا انّى اسكنتُ من ذرّیتى بوادٍ غیر ذى زَرع عند بیتك المحرّم» [420] O God, I have settled some of my family in this dry, barren land, beside Your Sacred House. Therefore, even during the infancy of Prophet Ishmael (عليه السلام), a trace of the Ka'bah existed. And in Surah Aal-i-Imran [421], the Ka'bah is also mentioned as the first House for mankind. In the verse under discussion, there is also mention of Abraham (عليه السلام) raising the foundations of the House, and this expression indicates that the basis (foundation) of the Ka'bah existed previously, and Abraham and Ishmael (عليهما السلام) raised it up. Imam Ali (عليه السلام) also states in Khutbah Qasi'ah [422]: Allah tests all people, from the time of Prophet Adam until eternity, with this very Ka'bah and its stones.

Abraham and Ishmael (عليهما السلام) were not equal in the work. We read in a narration: Abraham was doing the construction, and Ishmael was handing the stones to his father. Perhaps due to this difference, a separation was placed between the names of these two great figures: «یرفع ابراهیم القواعد من البیت و اسماعیل»

420) Ibrahim, 37. 421) Aal-i-Imran, 96. 422) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 192.

Messages

1- Let us not forget the founders of good deeds. «اذیرفع» (When they raised). «اذ» means remember the past.

2- In the path of Divine goals, construction work and labor are also acts of worship. Therefore, they ask Allah for its acceptance. «یرفع ابراهیم القواعد... ربنّا تقبّل منّا»

3- The work is not important; its acceptance is what matters. Even if we build the Ka'bah, if it is not accepted by Allah, it holds no value. «ربّنا تقبّل»

4- We should not consider our deeds worthy of mention before the Greatness of Allah. Prophet Abraham did not mention his work or construction; he only said: «ربّنا تقبّل منّا»

5- It is stated in narrations: Among the conditions for the acceptance and etiquette of supplication is the praise of Allah. Abraham (عليه السلام) accompanied his supplication with the praise of the Lord: «انّك انت السمیع العلیم»

Ayah

رَبَّنَا وَٱجْعَلْنَا مُسْلِمَيْنِ لَكَ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِنَآ أُمَّةً مُّسْلِمَةً لَّكَ وَأَرِنَا مَنَاسِكَنَا وَتُبْ عَلَيْنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱۲۸)
‘Our Lord, make us submissive to You, and [raise] from our progeny a nation submissive to You, and show us our rites [of worship], and turn to us clemently. Indeed You are the All-clement, the All-merciful.’ (128)

Key Points

Although Prophet Ibrahim and Ismail both displayed the highest degree of submission (taslim) to Allah in executing the command of sacrifice, yet despite all this, in this Ayah they ask Allah to make them submissive to His command. It is as if they are asking Allah for greater submission or the continuation of the spirit of submission. A person who is only submissive to Allah will neither submit to his idol-carving uncle, nor kneel before idols, nor follow the tyrants (Taghuts).

Messages

1- Do not be content with your current perfections; ask Allah for their completion and continuation. «ربنا و اجعلنا مسلمَین لك»

2- Attention to one's progeny and children is a wise and God-pleasing foresight, indicating broad vision and inner passion and love, and it has been mentioned repeatedly in the supplications of Prophet Ibrahim. «ومن ذریّتنا»

3- The path and method of servitude must be learned from Allah, otherwise man falls into various superstitions and deviations. «أرنا مناسكنا»

4- Unless the spirit of submission exists, the explanation of rulings will be useless. Here, Ibrahim عليه السلام first requests the spirit of submission from Allah, and then asks for the path and method of worship and servitude. «واجعلنا مسلمَین لك... ارنا مناسكنا»

5- The need for repentance (tawbah) and the return of Divine favors is valuable in every state and for every rank. «تب [423] علینا»

6- One of the manners of supplication (du'a) is the praise of the Lord, which is also evident in this Ayah. «التواب الرحیم»

423) We read in a Hadith: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله would seek forgiveness (Istighfar) seventy times every day, even though he was infallible and had no sin.

Ayah

رَبَّنَا وَٱبْعَثْ فِيهِمْ رَسُولًا مِّنْهُمْ يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتِكَ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۱۲۹)
‘Our Lord, raise amongst them an apostle from among them, who will recite to them Your signs and teach them the Book and wisdom and purify them. Indeed You are the All-mighty, the All-wise.’ (129)

Key Points

| This Ayah recounts another supplication (Du'a) made by Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام, which expresses the extraordinary greatness of his soul and depicts his inner fervor and sincerity. Although he himself was alive and one of the prominent Divine Prophets, he prayed for future generations, saying: O Lord! Raise among the people and my progeny a Prophet from among themselves. It is narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله that He said: «أنا دعوة ابى ابراهیم» (I am the result of the supplication of my father Ibrahim) who said: «ربّنا وابعث فیهم رسولاً منهم» (Our Lord, and raise up in them a Messenger from among them). [424] Although in the supplication of Ibrahim عليه السلام, teaching (Ta'lim) was mentioned before purification (Tazkiyah), Allah, in answering this prayer, placed purification first, to alert Ibrahim that purification is more valuable, and teaching is secondary. «یزكّیهم ویعلّمهم الكتاب والحكمة» (He purifies them and teaches them the Book and the Wisdom). [425]

424) Tafsir Safi and Al-Mizan. 425) Surah Al-Jumu'ah, 2.

Messages

1- The need for a Divine leader is one of the most fundamental needs of human society. The fact that a prayer was made for it centuries ago indicates its importance, value, and role. «یرفع ابراهیم... وابعث فیهم» (Ibrahim raises... and raise up in them). Indeed, raising the walls of the Ka'bah without the presence of an infallible and Divine leader would result in nothing more than an idol-house.

2- The goal of the Prophets' mission (Bi'thah) is the teaching and purification of people based on the Divine Book. «یتلوا... یعلّمهم... یزكّیهم» (He recites... He teaches them... He purifies them).

3- Knowledge and learning yield results only when accompanied by insight and wisdom (Hikmah), and coupled with purification (Tazkiyah) and piety (Taqwa). «یعلّمهم الكتاب والحكمة ویزكّیهم» (He teaches them the Book and the Wisdom and purifies them).

Ayah

وَمَن يَرْغَبُ عَن مِّلَّةِ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ إِلَّا مَن سَفِهَ نَفْسَهُۥ ۚ وَلَقَدِ ٱصْطَفَيْنَـٰهُ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱۳٠)
And who will [ever] forsake Abraham’s creed except one who debases himself? We certainly chose him in the [present] world, and in the Hereafter he will indeed be among the Righteous. (130)

Key Points

In the previous verses, aspects of the goals and personality of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) were discussed. This verse, considering the brilliance and purity of Ibrahim's character and his religion, asks: Who, except the ignorant and foolish (sufahā'), would turn away from Ibrahim and His goals?! His religion is so valuable that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is honored that his path is the path of Ibrahim. Ibrahim is the one who, in argument, confounded the disbeliever: «فبهت الذى كفر » (426) and, in courage, single-handedly broke all the idols: «فجعلهم جذاذاً» (427). The Quran introduces him as Halim (forbearing), and he is also a prime example and leader in patience and reliance (Tawakkul), to the extent that he was thrown into the fire without having any fear. In generosity, he roasted a fat calf for his guests. In submission, he left his child in the barren desert of Mecca, and when the child reached adolescence, by the command of Allah, he placed a knife upon his throat. Indeed, a person can only flee from the divine religions by deceiving his own intellect. «سفه نفسه» Ibrahim علیه السلام requested of Allah in a supplication: «ألحقنى بالصالحین» (428) Allah answered his prayer and states: Ibrahim is among the righteous in the Hereafter. Anyone who abandons the path of Ibrahim—who sought from God a center of security, an infallible leader, abundant provision, the success of Islam and submission, the acceptance of repentance, and happiness for mankind—and follows others, is nothing more than a fool and ignorant person!!

In the culture of the Quran, those who disregard the truth and show ingratitude for blessings are called safih (foolish). Just as the Children of Israel were called sufahā' (fools) due to the baseless excuses they made regarding the change of the Qibla: «سیقول السفهاء» Furthermore, concerning the verse «و لاتؤتوا السفهاء اموالكم» (And do not give the fools your property), it is narrated from the Imam علیه السلام that He said: Whoever drinks wine is a safih. Such a person turns to harmful and forbidden drinks instead of beneficial and lawful ones. Indeed, those who abandon the path of truth and go astray are sufahā', and the one who takes the correct step in choosing a leader and a school of thought is wise ('āqil). We read in a tradition: Intellect ('Aql) is that by which God is worshipped; otherwise, it is mere mischief (or devilry).

426) Baqarah, 258. 427) Anbiya, 58. 428) Shu'ara, 83.

Messages

1- A safih is one who disregards logic, school of thought, leader, and the path of truth, and sets off following his own desires or those of others. «سفه نفسه»

2- Adherence to religion is wisdom, and turning away from it is proof of lack of wisdom. «من یرغب... سفه نفسه»

Ayah

إِذْ قَالَ لَهُۥ رَبُّهُۥٓ أَسْلِمْ ۖ قَالَ أَسْلَمْتُ لِرَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱۳۱)
When his Lord said to him, ‘Submit,’ he said, ‘I submit to the Lord of all the worlds.’ (131)

Messages

1- Divine ranks and favors are not bestowed upon anyone without reason. If Allah chooses Abraham, it is because of his spirit of submission before God. «اسلم قال اسلمتُ»

2- The philosophy behind human submission to Allah is that He is the Lord of the Worlds (Rabb al-Alamin). «أسلمت لرب العالمین»

Ayah

وَوَصَّىٰ بِهَآ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ بَنِيهِ وَيَعْقُوبُ يَـٰبَنِىَّ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱصْطَفَىٰ لَكُمُ ٱلدِّينَ فَلَا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ (۱۳۲)
Abraham enjoined this [creed] upon his children, and [so did] Jacob, [saying], ‘My children! Allah has indeed chosen this religion for you; so do not die except as those who have surrendered themselves [to Allah]. (132)

Messages

1- We must be concerned with the soundness of the belief and faith of our generation and children, and not limit our wills solely to material aspects. «وصىّ... فلاتموتن الاّ وانتم مسلمون»

2- The path of truth is the path of Islam and submission to Allah. The prophets enjoined this very path. «وصىّ بها ابراهیم بنیه ویعقوب»

3- Allah has chosen the path of religion for us among all paths. «ان اللّه اصطفى لكم الدین»

4- A good end (حسن عاقبت) and dying as a Muslim is important. [429] «فلاتموتنّ الاّ وانتم مسلمون»

5- Although the time and place of death are not in our hands, we can prepare the ground for our good end through correct belief and action, supplication (du'a), and avoidance of sin and corrupt individuals. «فلاتموتنّ الاّ وانتم مسلمون»

429) Many individuals who were people of faith later apostatized and became disbelievers. »آمنوا ثم كفروا« Nisa, 137.

Ayah

أَمْ كُنتُمْ شُهَدَآءَ إِذْ حَضَرَ يَعْقُوبَ ٱلْمَوْتُ إِذْ قَالَ لِبَنِيهِ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِنۢ بَعْدِى قَالُوا۟ نَعْبُدُ إِلَـٰهَكَ وَإِلَـٰهَ ءَابَآئِكَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ إِلَـٰهًا وَٰحِدًا وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ مُسْلِمُونَ (۱۳۳)
Were you witnesses when death approached Jacob, when he said to his children, ‘What will you worship after me?’ They said, ‘We will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham, Ishmael, and Isaac, the One God, and to Him do we submit.’ (133)

Key Points

A group of Jews believed and claimed that Ya'qub (Jacob), at the time of his death, had commanded and advised his children to follow the religion that the Jews believed in, despite all its distortions, and that they adhered to their faith for this reason. Allah, in refuting this claim and belief, questions them: Were you present at Ya'qub's bedside at the time of his death that you speak in this manner? Rather, he asked his children for Islam (submission) and surrender to Allah, and the children promised him that they would adopt the worship of the One God and submission to Him.

In the Quran, the grandfather and the uncle are referred to as "father" (أب). [This is seen in] «ابائك ابراهیم واسماعیل» (your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael), because Abraham was the grandfather of Ya'qub's children, and Ishmael was their uncle.

Messages

1- Speech must be based on knowledge and awareness. «أم كنتم شهداء»

2- Death comes to everyone, even the Prophets. «حضر یعقوب الموت»

3- The most sensitive time of life is the moment of death, the most sensitive issue is the matter of religion and belief, and the most sensitive audience is the human generation (offspring). «اذ حضر یعقوب الموت اذ قال لبنیه ما تعبدون»

4- Admonition on the verge of death has profound effects. «اذ حضر یعقوب الموت اذ قال»

5- Fathers must pay attention to the religious future of their children. Even the children of the Prophets are exposed to the danger of irreligion. «ماتعبدون من بعدى»

6- All Prophets commanded the worship of and submission to Allah. «الهاًواحداً»

7- We should submit only to the command of Allah. «له مسلمون»

Ayah

تِلْكَ أُمَّةٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ ۖ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَلَكُم مَّا كَسَبْتُمْ ۖ وَلَا تُسْـَٔلُونَ عَمَّا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۳٤)
That was a nation that has passed: for it there will be what it has earned, and for you there will be what you have earned, and you will not be questioned about what they used to do. (134)

Key Points

One of the mistakes of the Children of Israel was that they greatly boasted and prided themselves on their ancestors and predecessors, assuming that even if they themselves were corrupt, they would be forgiven due to the merits of their forefathers. This Ayah warns them that they are responsible for their own deeds, just as their ancestors were responsible for theirs. In the book Ghurar al-Hikam, it is narrated from Imam Ali عليه السلام: "الشرف بالهمم العالیة لا بالرمم البالیة" (Honor and greatness are achieved through high and lofty aspirations, not through the decayed bones of ancestors and predecessors.) We see throughout history that individuals determine their future through their own actions. The wife of Pharaoh became an inhabitant of Paradise, while the wife of Lot, due to her misconduct, was destined for Hell. These are examples of the justice and righteous system of the Lord. In a Hadith narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, He said: O Children of Hashim, beware lest other people work for their Hereafter while you rely solely on your lineage.

Messages

1- Kinship ties will not be effective on the Day of Judgment. «لها ماكسبت ولكم ما كسبتم»

2- The future of every individual and every society depends on their own actions. «ولاتسئلون عمّاكانوا یعملون»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ كُونُوا۟ هُودًا أَوْ نَصَـٰرَىٰ تَهْتَدُوا۟ ۗ قُلْ بَلْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ حَنِيفًا ۖ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱۳٥)
They say, ‘Be either Jews or Christians, that you may be [rightly] guided.’ Say, ‘No, rather, [we will follow] the creed of Abraham, a Hanif, and he was not one of the polytheists.’ (135)

Key Points

The word "Yahūd" (Jews) is derived from "Hūd," meaning returning to Allah. In Majma' al-Bahrain, it is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that the title Yahūd was taken from Ayah 156 of Sūrat Al-A'rāf, where the Children of Israel said to Allah: «انّا هُدنا الیك» (Indeed, we have returned to You). And the word Naṣārā (Christians) is also taken from the words of Prophet Jesus عليه السلام, where He said: «مَن انصارى الى اللّه» (Who are my helpers toward Allah?). However, the word "Ḥanīf" is derived from "ḥanaf," meaning coming onto the straight path, just as "janaf" means being placed on a crooked path. In Ayah 113, we read that the Jews did not accept the Christians, and the Christians considered the Jews to be baseless, and each group imagined that only they were rightly guided. While both groups have fallen into doubt and deviated from the straight path, the path of truth is the path of Abraham عليه السلام, who never fell into doubt. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The Ḥanīf (upright) and truth-seeking religion is the very inclination toward truth and Islam. (430)

430) Tafsīr Burhān.

Messages

1- Undue monopolization is forbidden. The Jews and Christians, without having proof, each consider guidance to be solely within their own religion. «كونوا هوداً او نصارى تهتدوا»

2- Names and titles are not important; faith and action are what matter. Being a Jew or a Christian is merely a title; what is valuable is Tawḥīd (monotheism) and the worship of the One God. «بل ملة ابراهیم حنیفاً»

3- Respond to the negative propaganda of others. «قالوا... قل»

4- Abraham عليه السلام was never among the polytheists, whereas the Jews and Christians have fallen into polytheism (Shirk). «وما كان من المشركین»

Ayah

قُولُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْنَا وَمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَٱلْأَسْبَاطِ وَمَآ أُوتِىَ مُوسَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰ وَمَآ أُوتِىَ ٱلنَّبِيُّونَ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّنْهُمْ وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ مُسْلِمُونَ (۱۳٦)
Say, ‘We have faith in Allah and what has been sent down to us, and what was sent down to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes, and that which Moses and Jesus were given, and that which the prophets were given from their Lord; we make no distinction between any of them and to Him do we submit.’ (136)

Key Points

This Ayah commands Muslims to tell their opponents: We believe in Allah, all the true Prophets, and whatever has been revealed from Allah. Self-centeredness or racial prejudices do not cause us to accept some of them and reject others.

We believe in all the Divine Prophets, whether those who came before Abraham (عليه السلام), such as Adam, Seth, Noah, Hud, and Salih, or those who came after Abraham, such as Solomon, John, and Zechariah. We accept, by the decree of reason, every Prophet who possesses clear proof and a miracle.

Asbāt (اسباط) is the plural of Sibt (سبط), meaning grandson or descendant. It refers to the twelve tribes of the Children of Israel, who originated from the twelve sons of Jacob, and among whom there were Prophets.

The phrase «وما اوتى موسى وعیسى» (and what was given to Moses and Jesus) means we accept whatever was revealed to those two Prophets, not everything that is currently with you. Therefore, He did not say: «وما اوتیتم» (and what was given to you).

Consequently, if we stand against your invitation when you say: «كونوا هوداً او نصارى» (Be Jews or Christians) and consider salvation to lie in being Christian or Jewish, it is due to the deviations and superstitions you have introduced into Allah’s religion. Otherwise, we accept the true Torah and Gospel, in which the glad tidings of the advent and mission of the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) are mentioned.

Messages

1- Belief in all Prophets and heavenly Books is necessary. «آمنّا باللّه وما انزل...»

Disbelief in some Prophets is disbelief in all of them. A true Muslim is one who believes in all the Divine Prophets.

2- We believe in what was revealed to Moses and Jesus (علیهما السلام), not what exists today under the name of the Torah and the Gospel. «وما اوتى موسى وعیسى»

3- Practical submission is necessary alongside heartfelt faith. «آمنّا باللّه... نحن له مسلمون»

Ayah

فَإِنْ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِمِثْلِ مَآ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦ فَقَدِ ٱهْتَدَوا۟ ۖ وَّإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَإِنَّمَا هُمْ فِى شِقَاقٍ ۖ فَسَيَكْفِيكَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۱۳۷)
So if they believe in the like of what you believe in, then they are surely guided; but if they turn away, then [know that] they are only [steeped] in defiance. Allah will suffice you against them, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (137)

Key Points

If the People of the Book—the Jews and Christians—instead of stubbornness, self-centeredness, and reliance on racial and tribal issues, were to believe in all the prophets and heavenly books like you Muslims do, and abandon undue prejudices, they would certainly be guided. But if, as in the past, they consider only themselves to be right and deem others misguided, they will be in conflict with the truth and separated from the truth. Of course, you Muslims should be encouraged in your path and not fear their insults and conspiracies. Because Allah will repel their evil from you and will support and suffice you against them, for He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

Messages

1- Muslims are the model for the truly guided. «بمثل ما آمنتم» because they have faith and belief in all the prophets and heavenly books.

2- Allah has declared His support and sufficiency for the Muslims. «فسیكفیكهم اللّه»

3- The disbelief of the Jews and Christians stems from malice, obstinacy, and contention, not logic and reasoning. «وان تولّوا فانّما هم فى شقاق» [431]

431) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام considers shiqaq (contention and strife) to be disbelief (kufr). Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Rahnamā.

Ayah

صِبْغَةَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ صِبْغَةً ۖ وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ عَـٰبِدُونَ (۱۳۸)
‘The baptism of Allah, and who baptizes better than Allah? And Him do we worship.’ (138)

Key Points

Man must adopt a color (or characteristic) in his life, but among all colors, the Divine Color (the color of Allah) is the best. It is obvious that unless we abandon the color of race, tribe, and desires, we will not acquire the color of unity, brotherhood, and submission to the command of Allah. All colors fade and become colorless over time. But the Divine Color is eternal and lasting. «كل شى ء هالك الاّ وجهه» (432) And what color is better than the color of Allah, Whom we worship and serve. Let us note that the Jews wash their children with a special water, thereby giving them a religious color.

The best color is that which possesses purity and permanence, and attracts the eyes of the friends of Allah. It should be in harmony with nature (fitrah) and logic, its buyer should be Allah, and its price should be Paradise. All colors are erased; tribe, race, and lineage disappear sooner or later, but what is eternal and lasting is that Divine Color (Ṣibghah), meaning sincerity and faith.

It is narrated in a hadith: A person was making a hole in the wall of his house. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام asked: Why are you doing this?! He replied: So that the smoke from the house stove can exit. The Imam said: You could have the intention of making it an opening for light to enter so that you can recognize the times for prayer. That is, if a hole is to be made in the wall, why should it only be for expelling smoke? Why not for the entry of light? (433) Yes, a Divine Color can be given to every action.

The path of Allah is one. If the Divine Color is absent, other colors confuse and bewilder man. And conforming to a group, most of whom are misguided, is itself a source of disgrace and humiliation on the Day of Judgment.

(432) قصص، 88.

(433) آیةاللَّه جوادى، تفسیر موضوعى، ج 5، ص 132.

Messages

1- Faith in Allah, the Prophets, the heavenly Books, and submission to Allah constitute the Ṣibghah (dye) and Divine Color. In the previous verses, He said: «قولوا آمنّا باللّه و...», and in this verse, He says: «صبغة اللّه»

2- The supreme and most beautiful of colors is the Divine Ṣibghah and Color. «ومن احسن من اللّه صبغة»

3- One must be a servant of Allah alone, not a servant of others. «و نحن له عابدون»

Ayah

قُلْ أَتُحَآجُّونَنَا فِى ٱللَّهِ وَهُوَ رَبُّنَا وَرَبُّكُمْ وَلَنَآ أَعْمَـٰلُنَا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمَـٰلُكُمْ وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ مُخْلِصُونَ (۱۳۹)
Say, ‘Will you argue with us concerning Allah, while He is our Lord and your Lord, and to us belong our deeds, and to you belong your deeds, and we worship Him dedicatedly?’ (139)

Key Points

This Ayah is a warning to the People of the Book (Ahl al-Kitab) to abandon their baseless claims. They used to say: We are closer to Allah, or His children, or His beloved ones. The Children of Israel assumed that all prophets must be from their lineage, and due to the antiquity of their religion and the Book of the Torah, they considered themselves superior. They believed divine favor was exclusive to them, and based on this assumption, they argued and debated with the Muslims.

Messages

1- Allah is the Master of all human beings. He is gracious to everyone and can bestow His special favor upon anyone, regardless of their lineage. «هو ربّنا وربّكم»

2- The felicity of every person depends on their deeds. Claims and slogans are ineffective. «لنا اعمالنا ولكم اعمالكم»

3- The criterion for proximity to Allah is sincere worship and declaring Him free from any partner. «نحن له مخلصون»

Ayah

أَمْ تَقُولُونَ إِنَّ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَٱلْأَسْبَاطَ كَانُوا۟ هُودًا أَوْ نَصَـٰرَىٰ ۗ قُلْ ءَأَنتُمْ أَعْلَمُ أَمِ ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن كَتَمَ شَهَـٰدَةً عِندَهُۥ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٤٠)
[Ask them,] ‘Do you say that Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes were Jews or Christians?’ Say, ‘Is it you who know better, or Allah?’ And who is a greater wrongdoer than someone who conceals a testimony that is with him from Allah? And Allah is not oblivious of what you do. (140)

Key Points

This verse is another address to the People of the Book, asking: Do you boast so much about being Jewish or Christian that you are even willing to attribute the greatest slanders to the best of Allah's saints (Awliyā')?! Do you claim that Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the other prophets from the lineage of Jacob (the "Asbāṭ"), were Jewish or Christian, despite the fact that they lived before the revelation of the Torah and the Gospel? Is it not an act of oppression that you conceal a reality of which you are aware, and hide this clear historical truth from the general public, making it seem as though all the prophets followed Judaism or Christianity? But know that Allah is not unaware of what you do.

Messages

1- Sometimes fanaticism reaches a point where a person is willing to distort or deny clear historical realities. «ام تقولون...»

2- The greatest injustices are cultural oppression and the concealment of truths. «ومن اظلم ممن كتم»

3- Allah's knowledge of concealment and oppression should serve as a warning for us. «و ما اللّه بغافل عمّا تعملون»

Ayah

تِلْكَ أُمَّةٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ ۖ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَلَكُم مَّا كَسَبْتُمْ ۖ وَلَا تُسْـَٔلُونَ عَمَّا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٤۱)
That was a nation that has passed: for it there will be what it has earned, and for you there will be what you have earned, and you will not be questioned about what they used to do. (141)

Messages

1- In attaining happiness, relying on ancestors and the history of fathers is not the solution. «لها ما كسبت ولكم ما كسبتم» Even if your father was virtuous, what benefit do you gain from the virtue of the father? 2- Every community is solely responsible for its own deeds. «لا تسئلون عمّا كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

۞ سَيَقُولُ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ مِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَا وَلَّىٰهُمْ عَن قِبْلَتِهِمُ ٱلَّتِى كَانُوا۟ عَلَيْهَا ۚ قُل لِّلَّهِ ٱلْمَشْرِقُ وَٱلْمَغْرِبُ ۚ يَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۱٤۲)
The foolish among the people will say, ‘What has turned them away from the qiblah they were following?’ Say, ‘To Allah belong the east and the west. He guides whomever He wishes to a straight path.’ (142)

Key Points

Since the polytheists of Mecca had turned the Ka'bah into an idol house, the Holy Prophet and the Muslims did not pray towards the Ka'bah during the thirteen years they spent in Mecca after the start of the mission (Bi'that), lest it be thought that they were showing respect to the idols. However, shortly after the migration (Hijra) to Medina, the Jews began to protest and belittled the Muslims, saying: You pray facing our Qibla, the Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem), therefore you are our followers and lack independence. The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was distressed by this insult and would look up at the sky while praying, as if awaiting the revelation until the command to change the Qibla was issued. The Prophet had prayed two units (rak'ats) of the noon prayer (Dhuhr) towards Bayt al-Maqdis when Gabriel was commanded to take the Prophet's arm and turn His face towards the Ka'bah. Consequently, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله prayed one prayer facing two Qiblas, and that location in Medina is now known as the Mosque of the Two Qiblas (Masjid al-Qiblatayn). (434)

After this incident, the Jews became upset and, according to their habit, began to find fault. They, who until yesterday were saying that Muslims lacked independence and were their followers, now asked why these people had turned away from the Qibla of the previous prophets. The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was commanded to reply to them: The East and the West belong to Allah, and God has no specific location. Yes, the change of Qibla is a sign of Divine wisdom, not of His weakness or ignorance. Just as in the cosmic order, the change of seasons is a sign of planning (تدبیر), not weakness or regret. Allah is present everywhere, and the East and West of the universe belong to Him. Therefore, the orientation of the body towards the Ka'bah is a symbol of the orientation of the heart towards God. How many people pray towards the Ka'bah, yet their Qibla is something else. As Khwaja Abdullah Ansari says:

The Qibla of kings is the crown and the lasso (power). The Qibla of the masters of the world is silver and gold. The Qibla of form-worshippers is water and clay (the body). The Qibla of those who know the meaning is the soul and the heart. The Qibla of the body-worshippers is sleep and food. The Qibla of man is cultivation through knowledge. The Qibla of the lover is eternal union. The Qibla of the Gnostic (Aarif) is the Perfection of the Majestic One.

434) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 136.

Messages

1- Individuals who constantly seek excuses to evade accepting the truth are foolish (safih). (435) «سیقول السفهاء»

2- Whoever considers Allah's hand to be tied in establishing or changing laws is foolish (safih) and light-headed. Protesting against the Wise and Knowing God is a sign of foolishness. «سیقول السفهاء»

3- Muslims must anticipate the conspiracies, rumors, and fault-finding questions of the enemy and prevent their influence and spread by responding to them. «سیقول... قل»

4- The act of questioning itself is not criticized; rather, the spirit of the questioner is criticized when he asks out of protest and fault-finding. «ما ولاّهم عن قبلتهم»

5- The sanctity and dignity of places and times depend on the favor of the Lord, not on anything else. «لِلّه المشرق و المغرب»

6- The change of the Qibla was one of the signs of the Prophet of Islam mentioned in the heavenly books, thus it became a means of guidance for some. «یهدى به من یشاء»

435) Just as in narrations, the person who sacrifices his Hereafter for the sake of the world is called insane (majnun), the followers of a corrupt leader are called poor (faqir), and the one who loses his religion is called plundered (gharat-zada).

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَـٰكُمْ أُمَّةً وَسَطًا لِّتَكُونُوا۟ شُهَدَآءَ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ وَيَكُونَ ٱلرَّسُولُ عَلَيْكُمْ شَهِيدًا ۗ وَمَا جَعَلْنَا ٱلْقِبْلَةَ ٱلَّتِى كُنتَ عَلَيْهَآ إِلَّا لِنَعْلَمَ مَن يَتَّبِعُ ٱلرَّسُولَ مِمَّن يَنقَلِبُ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ ۚ وَإِن كَانَتْ لَكَبِيرَةً إِلَّا عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ هَدَى ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيُضِيعَ إِيمَـٰنَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِٱلنَّاسِ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱٤۳)
Thus We have made you a middle nation that you may be witnesses to the people, and that the Apostle may be a witness to you. We did not appoint the qiblah you were following, but that We may ascertain those who follow the Apostle from those who turn back on their heels. It was indeed a hard thing except for those whom Allah has guided. And Allah would not let your prayers go to waste. Indeed Allah is most kind and merciful to mankind. (143)

Key Points
