Christians, during worship, would face the birthplace of Jesus (عليه السلام) in Jerusalem (Bayt al-Maqdis), which was located in the East relative to their place of residence. Jews would worship facing Jerusalem, which was located in the West relative to their place of residence. But the Ka'bah is situated in the middle relative to those two. The Quran, referring to this matter, states: Just as We placed the Ka'bah in the middle, We also made you Muslims the middle nation (Ummah).

Commentators have raised various discussions regarding this Ayah. In interpreting the "middle nation" (Ummat Wasat), some have said: the intent is a nation that is neither extremist nor negligent (neither excessive nor deficient), neither a proponent of determinism (Jabr) nor delegation (Tafwid), neither believing in the primacy of the individual nor the primacy of society. However, with a few premises, it becomes clear that the intent of the Ayah regarding the middle nation refers to specific individuals. Those premises are:

  1. The Quran has introduced the middle nation as witnesses over deeds, so they must testify concerning people's deeds on the Day of Judgment.
  2. Being a witness requires knowledge, awareness, and justice.
  3. Not all individuals of the nation possess awareness and justice.

For this reason, we read in narrations that the intent of the middle nation is the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام), who, firstly, have knowledge of all deeds. As the Quran states: «سیرى اللّه عملكم و رسوله والمؤمنون» [436] (Allah will see your deeds, and [so will] His Messenger and the believers), where the believers who see and gain knowledge of people's deeds are, according to narrations, the infallible Imams. And secondly, they possess justice. As Allah guaranteed their infallibility ('Ismah) and justice in the Ayah: «انّما یُرید اللّه لیذهب عنكم الرِّجس اهل البیت و یُطهّركم تطهیراً» [437] (Allah only intends to remove from you the impurity [of sin], O people of the [Prophet's] household, and to purify you with [complete] purification).

Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) says: How can a nation, some of whose members are not fit to testify regarding a few dates in the courts of this world, possess the fitness to testify over all people on the Day of Judgment?

In any case, the station of being a witness in the presence of Allah, especially concerning all the deeds of the people, is exclusive to certain individuals of the nation, who, according to the narrations, are the infallible Imams.

A question remains here: Why did Allah mention the name of the nation instead of the names of specific individuals? In response, we say: The Quran has even introduced a single person as a nation: «انّ ابراهیم كان امّة قانتاً للّه» [438] (Indeed, Abraham was a nation [Ummah], obedient to Allah).

The Philosophy of the Change of Qibla

Regarding the philosophy of the change of Qibla, multiple analyses have been presented; some have said that the initial selection of Jerusalem as the Qibla was intended to attract the attention and cooperation of the Jews, and when the Muslims gained power and capability, this action was no longer necessary. Others have said: Affection for Mecca and homeland caused the Qibla to change.

However, these analyses are illusory and contrary to reality. What the Quran explicitly states and emphasizes in this regard is that the change of Qibla was a divine test so that the true followers of the Prophet might be recognized. For those who do not have pure faith, immediately upon the change of a command, they begin to question and object: Why did this happen? What happens to our deeds according to the previous command? If the previous command was correct, then what is the new command? If the new instruction is correct, then what was the previous command?

Allah also gives glad tidings to those who are submissive to His commands that their past deeds will not be wasted, because they were in accordance with the instruction.

In this Ayah, the word Iman (faith) is used instead of the word Salat (prayer), which indicates the importance and status of prayer and its equivalence to the entirety of faith. «وما كان اللّه لِیضیع ایمانكم» (And never would Allah have caused your faith to be lost).

436) Tawbah, 105. 437) Ahzab, 33. 438) Nahl, 120.

Messages

  1. The friends of Allah (Awliya') supervise our deeds. «لتكونوا شهداء» Yes, some human beings possess such capacity that they can be observers of all deeds and aware of the motivations of people throughout history.
  2. There is a hierarchy on the Day of Judgment. The structure and organization of the court of the Day of Judgment are such that first the Imams testify concerning the deeds of the people, and then the Prophet is a witness over the Imams. «لتكونوا شهداء على الناس و یكون الرّسول علیكم شهیداً»
  3. Testing is a definite divine tradition, and Allah sometimes tests people by changing a command. «لنعلم من یتبع الرسول...»
  4. Following religious instructions leads to growth and perfection, while disobeying them is a form of regression. «ینقلب على عقبیه»
  5. We should not habituate ourselves to anything other than the command of Allah—such as a specific direction, color, time, place, or method—so that whenever a command is issued contrary to our expectation, habit, or preference, we do not seek to flee or recommend alternatives. «لنعلم من یتّبع الرّسول ممن ینقلب على عقبیه»
  6. The station of submission is difficult for ordinary people: «لكبیرة» (it is indeed great/difficult), and only those whom special divine guidance encompasses attain this station. «الاّ على الّذین هدى اللَّه» (except for those whom Allah has guided).
  7. Laws and regulations do not apply to previous times. If the command to change the Qibla was issued today, the previous prayers are valid, and from today onward, one must pray facing the other direction. «وما كان اللّه لیضیع ایمانكم»
  8. All cosmic (takwini) and legislative (tashri'i) changes and transformations stem from the mercy and compassion of Allah. «لرؤف رحیم»

Ayah

قَدْ نَرَىٰ تَقَلُّبَ وَجْهِكَ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ ۖ فَلَنُوَلِّيَنَّكَ قِبْلَةً تَرْضَىٰهَا ۚ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۚ وَحَيْثُ مَا كُنتُمْ فَوَلُّوا۟ وُجُوهَكُمْ شَطْرَهُۥ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ لَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٤٤)
We certainly see you turning your face about in the sky. We will surely turn you to a qiblah of your liking: so turn your face towards the Holy Mosque, and wherever you may be, turn your faces towards it! Indeed those who were given the Book surely know that it is the truth from their Lord. And Allah is not oblivious of what they do. (144)

Key Points

This Ayah indicates that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was awaiting and eager for the revelation concerning the change of the Qibla. This change of Qibla, from Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) to the Ka'bah, was a promise from Allah to His Prophet. However, when supplicating, the Prophet would look towards the sky without saying anything, until the Ayah was revealed, commanding the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and all Muslims to face the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) and the Ka'bah during worship.

Although the People of the Book (Ahl al-Kitab) and the Jews knew the truth of this change and had previously read in their scriptures that the Prophet of Islam would pray towards two Qiblas, they concealed this truth or played a destructive role by raising doubts and questions. Allah threatens them with the phrase: «و ما اللّه بغافل عمّا یعملون» (And Allah is not unaware of what they do). [439]

The satisfaction of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله with the Ka'bah being the Qibla had several reasons, including: A: The Ka'bah was the Qibla of Abraham عليه السلام. B: He would be relieved from the taunts, humiliation, and mockery of the Jews. C: The independence of the Muslims would be established. D: Attention would be directed towards the first foundation of Monotheism, namely the Ka'bah.

Since the issue of changing the Qibla was a significant event, the command to face the Ka'bah is given twice in the Ayah: once addressing the Prophet using the word; «وجهك» (your face), and once addressing the Muslims using the word; «وجوهكم» (your faces).

439) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The sky is the source of the descent of revelation and the focus of the Prophets' gaze. «نرى تقلّب وجهك فى السماء»

2- Allah is aware of the desires of human beings. «فلنولّینّك قبلة ترضاها»

3- The legislation and alteration of rulings come from Allah, and the Prophet does not issue commands on his own accord. «فولوّا...»

4- The Prophet's etiquette before Allah was such that He did not express the request for the Qibla change verbally, but only conveyed His anticipation through His gaze. «قدنرى تقلب وجهك»

5- Allah desires the satisfaction of His Messenger, and the satisfaction of Allah lies in the satisfaction of His Messenger. «قبلة ترضاها»

6- The Quran threatens and censures scholars who know the truth but still evade it. «لیعلمون انّه الحقّ... و ما اللّه بغافل عمّا یعملون»

7- Religious commands should not be made difficult for people. «شطر» means direction. That is, standing in the direction of the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) is sufficient, and it is not necessary to face the Ka'bah precisely. «فوَلّوا وجوهكم شطره»

Ayah

وَلَئِنْ أَتَيْتَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِكُلِّ ءَايَةٍ مَّا تَبِعُوا۟ قِبْلَتَكَ ۚ وَمَآ أَنتَ بِتَابِعٍ قِبْلَتَهُمْ ۚ وَمَا بَعْضُهُم بِتَابِعٍ قِبْلَةَ بَعْضٍ ۚ وَلَئِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَ أَهْوَآءَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَكَ مِنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ ۙ إِنَّكَ إِذًا لَّمِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٤٥)
Even if you bring those who were given the Book every [kind of] sign, they will not follow your qiblah. Nor shall you follow their qiblah, nor will any of them follow the qiblah of the other. And if you follow their desires, after the knowledge that has come to you, you will be one of the wrongdoers. (145)

Key Points

This Ayah exposes the obstinacy and stubbornness of the People of the Book and swears [440] that no matter how many signs and proofs come to them, they will not follow Islam and its Qiblah. This is because they have understood the truth, but knowingly refuse to accept it. The threats and warnings of the Quran directed at the Prophet are intended to prevent the emergence and growth of extremism (ghuluww) and the false attribution of divinity to the saints (awliya). Just as in other religions, people, through extremism, considered Jesus عليه السلام the son of God and the angels the daughters of God.

440) In the word "لئن" (la-in), the letter lām is for the answer to an oath (jawāb qasam).

Messages

1- Stubbornness and fanaticism prevent thought, reasoning, and truth-seeing. They are not only fanatical regarding Islam, but they also harbor obstinacy and stubbornness among themselves. «ماتبعوا قبلتك... ومابعضهم بتابع قبلة بعض»

2- One must not surrender to the clamor and uproar of the opponents, and decisiveness is necessary to disappoint the enemies. «وما انت بتابع»

3- The Jews and the Christians each had a specific Qiblah. «ومابعضهم بتابع قبلة بعض»

4- The deviation of scholars is very dangerous. «...من بعد ماجاءك من العلم»

5- Knowledge alone is not sufficient for guidance; the spirit of accepting the truth is necessary. The Jews possessed the Book, but despite their undue fanaticism, this knowledge was not helpful. «اهوائهم من بعد ماجاءك من العلم»

6- The Prophet does not have the right to change Divine law based on people's desires. «اهوائهم من بعد ماجاءك من العلم»

7- The religion of Islam originates from the source of true knowledge. «جاءك من العلم»

8- Laws worthy of acceptance and adherence are those that are purified from carnal desires and built upon knowledge. «لئن اتّبعت اهوائهم من بعد ماجاءك من العلم»

9- The law is the same for everyone. If, hypothetically, the Prophet were also to follow whims and desires, he would be subject to punishment. «انّك اذاً»

10- Despite the existence of Divine law, following the desires of the people is oppression (zulm). «اذاً من الظالمین»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يَعْرِفُونَهُۥ كَمَا يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْنَآءَهُمْ ۖ وَإِنَّ فَرِيقًا مِّنْهُمْ لَيَكْتُمُونَ ٱلْحَقَّ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (۱٤٦)
Those whom We have given the Book recognize him just as they recognize their sons, but a part of them indeed conceal the truth while they know. (146)

Key Points

This reality has been recounted several times in the Quran: that the People of the Book, due to the glad tidings in the Torah and the Gospel regarding the advent and prophethood of the Prophet of Islam, were awaiting Him, and the characteristics of the Prophet had been explained to them so clearly that they recognized Him just as they recognized their own sons. Yet, despite all this, a group among them concealed the truth. In another place, the Quran states that they had knowledge not only of the Prophet but also of the community He would establish and His companions: «محمّد رسول اللّه والّذین معه اشدّاء على الكفّار رحماء بینهم تراهم رُكّعاً سُجّداً یبتغون فضلاً من اللّه و رضواناً سیماهم فى وجوههم من اثَر السجود ذلك مثلهم فى التّوریة و مثلهم فى الانجیل» (441) Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, and those who are with Him are firm and severe against the disbelievers, but merciful among themselves. You see them bowing and prostrating, seeking favor from Allah and His pleasure. Their mark is on their faces from the trace of prostration. This is their description in the Torah, and their description in the Gospel. To emphasize the precise knowledge the People of the Book had of the Prophet, the Quran likens it to a father's recognition of his son, which is the clearest form of recognition. This is because:

  • It forms from the beginning of birth, and even before it.
  • It is the recognition of something that is awaited.
  • It is a recognition in which there is no doubt.

Concealing knowledge is the greatest sin. Regarding those who conceal, the Quran states: «یلعنهم اللّه و یلعنهم اللاعنون» (442) Allah and all the angels, humans, jinn, and every conscious being curse and condemn them forever. Indeed, concealing the truth is like denying one's own child, and how ignoble it is for a father to deny his child for the sake of worldly pleasures!

441) Al-Fath, 29. 442) Al-Baqarah, 159.

Messages

1- If the spirit of truth-seeking is absent, knowledge alone is insufficient. Despite their deep recognition of the Messenger of Allah, the Jews still did not accept Him. «یعرفون... لیكتمون» (They recognize... yet they conceal).

2- Observe fairness even regarding enemies. The Quran does not attribute concealment to all the People of the Book. «فریقاً منهم لیكتمون» (A group among them conceal).

Ayah

ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُمْتَرِينَ (۱٤۷)
This is the truth from your Lord; so do not be among the skeptics. (147)

Messages

1- The Truth (Al-Haqq) is only that which originates from His source of infinite knowledge. «الحقّ من ربّك» 2- Divine commands and decrees, when established through revelation (Wahy) or narrations (Riwayat), leave no room for dispute or controversy. «فلا تكوننّ من المُمترین» 3- A leader must possess decisiveness and certainty, especially when changing a law or breaking a tradition (Sunnah). «فلا تكوننّ من المُمترین»

Ayah

وَلِكُلٍّ وِجْهَةٌ هُوَ مُوَلِّيهَا ۖ فَٱسْتَبِقُوا۟ ٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ ۚ أَيْنَ مَا تَكُونُوا۟ يَأْتِ بِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱٤۸)
Everyone has a cynosure to which he turns; so take the lead in all good works. Wherever you may be, Allah will bring you all together. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (148)

Key Points

The word wajhah (وجهةٌ) has two meanings: qiblah (direction of prayer) and tariqah (way or method). If we take this word, like most commentators, to mean qiblah, the meaning of the Ayah is the same as what we have translated. But if we take the word wajhah (وجهةٌ) to mean way or method, the meaning of the Ayah is this: For every person or group, there is a path and a way to which they are drawn, and everyone follows a path. And this meaning is similar to Ayah 84 of Surah Al-Isra, where He said: «كلٌّ یعمل على شاكلته» Some commentators have considered the referent of «هو» (Huwa) in «هو مولّیها» (Huwa muwallīhā) to be Allah, in which case the meaning of the Ayah becomes: Everyone has a qiblah that Allah has determined for them.

Precedence in good deeds increases their value. In the Quran, the terms «سارعوا» (sāriʿū), «سابقوا» (sābiqū), and «فاستبقوا» (fastabiqū) are used regarding good deeds. And in praise of the Prophets, He says: «یسارعون فى الخیرات» [443] (They hasten in good deeds).

In some narrations concerning this Ayah, it is mentioned: At the time of the appearance (Zuhur) of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام, his devotees will gather in his presence from all corners and regions of the world in one place. [444]

443) Al-Anbiya, 90. 444) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 139.

Messages

1- Every nation has had a qiblah, and the issue of the qiblah is not exclusive to Muslims. «لكلّ وجهة»

2- Abandon futile discussions and let your attention and focus be on performing good deeds. «فاستبقوا الخیرات»

3- Instead of competing and striving for precedence in material affairs, one must strive to surpass others in good deeds. «فاستبقوا الخیرات»

4- On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will gather everyone together in one place, and this is an example of the manifestation of Divine power. «یأت بكم الله جمیعاً»

Ayah

وَمِنْ حَيْثُ خَرَجْتَ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٤۹)
Whencesoever you may go out, turn your face towards the Holy Mosque. Indeed it is the truth from your Lord, and Allah is not oblivious of what you do. (149)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the focus on the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) was related to the city of Medina, where the Muslims resided, but this verse states: even during travel, at the time of prayer, turn your faces towards the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram).

Ayah

وَمِنْ حَيْثُ خَرَجْتَ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۚ وَحَيْثُ مَا كُنتُمْ فَوَلُّوا۟ وُجُوهَكُمْ شَطْرَهُۥ لِئَلَّا يَكُونَ لِلنَّاسِ عَلَيْكُمْ حُجَّةٌ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ فَلَا تَخْشَوْهُمْ وَٱخْشَوْنِى وَلِأُتِمَّ نِعْمَتِى عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (۱٥٠)
And whencesoever you may go out, turn your face towards the Holy Mosque, and wherever you may be, turn your faces towards it, so that the people may have no allegation against you, neither those of them who are wrongdoers. So do not fear them, but fear Me, that I may complete My blessing on you and so that you may be guided. (150)

Key Points

The repetition of the subject of the change of the Qibla in multiple verses is a sign of its importance. Furthermore, in each of these verses, a new point is mentioned alongside this subject. For instance, in this verse, Allah, after commanding the turning towards the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram), states: This is so that people may not have any pretext against the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the Muslims. This is because the People of the Book knew that the Prophet of Islam would pray towards two Qiblas, and if this matter had not been realized, they would have objected that your Prophet lacks the characteristics mentioned in the previous heavenly books. Or they would have mocked and humiliated the Muslims, saying that you are followers of the Jews and do not have an independent Qibla. Of course, this objection was not only from the Jews; the polytheists also said: If Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله pays attention to the religion of Abraham, then why does He not pay attention to the House that Abraham built? However, the hypocrites did not remain silent and began their criticisms with the change of the Qibla, saying: The Prophet lacks consistency in judgment. In any case, Allah, through emphasis and repetition, prepares the Muslims for the acceptance of the matter and steadfastness, and reminds them that those who seek proof and evidence will not criticize you. However, the unjust individuals who conceal the truth will not cease their fault-finding, and you should not pay attention to them, nor fear them, but fear Allah alone.

The Qibla is a sign and manifestation of Monotheism (Tawhid). The Qibla is the emblem and sign of the Muslims. In Nahj al-Balagha, the Ka'bah is mentioned as an ‘alam (علم), meaning the flag and manifest sign of Islam. Idol worshippers and star worshippers would turn towards an idol, a star, or the moon during prayer, but Islam replaced that with turning towards the House of God and considered facing the Qibla a sign of turning towards God.

We read in narrations: The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله usually sat facing the Qibla and recommended: sleep and sit facing the Qibla. Even sitting facing the Qibla has been considered an act of worship.

The Qibla holds a special status and respect, and in some acts, turning towards it is obligatory. For example: obligatory prayers must be performed facing the Qibla. The slaughtering of an animal and the burial of the deceased must be towards the Qibla. Sitting facing the Qibla or having one's back to the Qibla in the restroom is forbidden (Haram).

In addition to what has been mentioned, the Qibla is a factor for the unity of Muslims. If we look at the Muslims from above the globe, we see that they turn towards the Qibla five times every day with a specific order and arrangement.

The Ka'bah has been the base and preparation ground for divine movements and revolutions. From Prophet Abraham and Prophet Muhammad, to Imam Husayn علیهم السلام, and in the future, Imam Mahdi علیه السلام, all have started and will start their movements from beside the Ka'bah. It is sufficient for the greatness of the Ka'bah that Muslims are known as the People of the Qibla (Ahl al-Qibla).

The Almighty Allah says regarding the incident of the change of the Qibla: The change of the Qibla is so that I may complete My favor upon you in the future. That is, your independence and turning towards the Ka'bah is the groundwork for the completion of the favor in the future. "لأتمّ نعمتى" (that I may complete My favor).

This incident occurred in the second year of the Hijra in Medina. In the eighth year of the Hijra, Allah also says regarding the conquest of Mecca: "ویتمّ نعمته علیك" (and that He may complete His favor upon you) [445]. Meaning: This victory is so that Allah may complete His favor upon you in the future.

As you observe, in both verses, the "completion of the favor" (Itmam Ni'mah) is expressed in the form of the imperfect verb (present/future tense), which indicates its occurrence in the future. But in Ayah 3 of Surah Al-Ma'idah, which was revealed upon returning from Hajjat al-Wida' (the Farewell Pilgrimage) in the tenth year of the Hijra, and after introducing the infallible leadership at Ghadir Khumm, He says: "الیوم اكملت لكم دینكم و اتممت علیكم نعمتى" (This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you).

It is noteworthy and requires attention that Allah describes the people turning towards the Qibla as the first step towards the completion of the favor, the conquest of Mecca as another step, and the appointment and introduction of the Leader as the final step in the completion of the favor.

It is necessary to recall that in the process of determining the Qibla, as well as appointing the Leader, He reminds them: "لاتخشوهم و اخشونى" (So fear them not, but fear Me). This is because in both cases, there is the possibility of resistance, opposition, fault-finding, and rumor-mongering from others.

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «یا على مثلك فى الامّة كمثل الكعبة نصبها اللّه للنّاس علماً» [446] O Ali! Your example among the Ummah is like the example of the Ka'bah, which Allah has established for the people as a clear sign and a flag, so that they do not lose their direction.

Likewise, in another Hadith, it is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that He said: «ونحن كعبة اللّه ونحن قبلة اللّه» [447] We are the Ka'bah of Allah and we are the Qibla of Allah.

445) Al-Fath, 2. 446) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 22, p. 483. 447) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 6, pp. 211 and 303.

Messages

  1. Muslims must avoid any action that gives the enemy a pretext. «لئلایكون للناس علیكم حجة»
  2. Rejecting the dominance of others and achieving independence is a value. «لئلاّ یكون للناس علیكم حجة»
  3. The change of the Qibla is a factor that neutralizes the baseless objections and fault-finding of the People of the Book, the polytheists, and the hypocrites. «لئلا یكون للناس علیكم حجة»
  4. External enemies are not the greatest danger to Muslims; lack of piety (Taqwa) is the main danger. «فلا تخشوهم واخشونى»
  5. The designation of a specific Qibla for Muslims is the groundwork for the completion of Allah's favor upon them. «ولاتّم نعمتى علیكم»
  6. The designation of the Qibla and praying towards the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) is the groundwork for the guidance of the believers. «لعلكم تهتدون»
  7. Guidance has stages. Although the addressees of the verse are Muslims who are already guided, the intention is to achieve higher stages of guidance. «لعلّكم تهتدون»

Ayah

كَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا فِيكُمْ رَسُولًا مِّنكُمْ يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا وَيُزَكِّيكُمْ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَيُعَلِّمُكُم مَّا لَمْ تَكُونُوا۟ تَعْلَمُونَ (۱٥۱)
Even as We sent to you an apostle from among yourselves, who recites to you Our signs and purifies you, and teaches you the Book and wisdom, and teaches you what you did not know. (151)

Key Points

The word "Tilāwah" (تلاوت) comes from the root "Tilw" (تِلو), meaning continuous or successive. It refers to a reading that is continuous, possesses proper and suitable order, and is endowed with a kind of sanctity. The word "Tazkiyah" (تزكیه) means growth, development, and purification. Teaching the Book (Ta'līm al-Kitāb) is the learning of heavenly verses and rulings, and teaching wisdom (Ta'līm al-Ḥikmah) is the granting of correct thought and insight. The prophethood (Bi'thah) of the Prophet of Islam is the result of the acceptance of the prayer of Prophet Ibrāhīm (Abraham), who asked Allah: «وابعث فیهم رسولاً منهم یتلوا علیهم آیاتك...» [448] O God! Send forth among them a messenger from among them who shall recite Your verses to them... The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله also used to say: «انا دعوة ابى ابراهیم» I am the fulfillment of the prayer of my father Ibrāhīm.

448) Al-Baqarah, 129.

Messages

1- A successful leader is one who is from the people themselves, among the people, and speaks the same language as the people. «ارسلنا فیكم رسولاً منكم»

2- Purification (Tazkiyah) precedes teaching (Ta'līm). «یزكّیكم و یعلّمكم»

3- Religious education is the main axis, and other forms of education follow in subsequent stages. First, «یعلّمكم الكتاب و الحكمة» and then «یعلّمكم ما لم تكونوا تعلمون»

4- Humanity requires revelation to recognize many truths. Instead of saying «ما لاتعلمون» (what you do not know), He said: «مالم تكونوا تعلمون» (what you did not know), reminding us that if the Prophets had not been sent, humanity would have had no way to gain awareness of matters such as its own future and the future of the world.

Ayah

فَٱذْكُرُونِىٓ أَذْكُرْكُمْ وَٱشْكُرُوا۟ لِى وَلَا تَكْفُرُونِ (۱٥۲)
Remember Me and I will remember you, and thank Me, and do not be ungrateful to Me. (152)

Key Points

In some verses, Allah says: «اذكروا نعمتى» (Remember My favors) [449]. But in this verse, He says: «اذكرونى» (Remember Me Myself). And this is due to the varying degrees of people's knowledge and recognition (of Him). Furthermore, the remembrance of Allah is the precursor to gratitude (Shukr), and thus it is placed before gratitude.

This verse demonstrates the ultimate kindness of Allah toward His servant. How greatly favored is the human being—whose characteristics include ignorance, poverty, mortality, and lowliness—when Allah, the All-Knowing, the Self-Sufficient, the Everlasting, and the Mighty, tells him: Remember Me, so that I may remember you. What value does our remembrance possess? Is it not true that our act of remembrance is itself a divine grace (Tawfiq) granted by Him?

Remembering God is not only by the tongue; one must remember God with the heart and soul. The true remembrance is the remembrance of God at the time of sin, leading to the abandonment of that sin. And the best type of remembering Allah is the prayer (Salat), concerning which He said: «اقم الصلوة لِذكرى» (Establish the prayer for My remembrance) [450].

Although gratitude (Shukr) is one instance of the remembrance of God (Dhikr), it is mentioned separately alongside Dhikr, indicating the importance of gratitude as a prominent manifestation of remembrance.

Allah engages in an exchange with humanity and leaves no human action unrewarded: «اذكرونى اذكركم» (Remember Me, I will remember you), «اوفوا بعهدى اوف بعهدكم» (Fulfill My covenant, I will fulfill your covenant) [451], «فافسحوا یفسح الله لكم» (Make room, Allah will make room for you) [452].

Imam Baqir عليه السلام, commenting on this verse, counted the Tasbihat of Lady Fatimah (Tasbihat al-Zahra) as one instance of the remembrance of God, which consists of 34 repetitions of اللَّه اكبر (Allahu Akbar), 33 repetitions of الحمدللَّه (Alhamdulillah), and 33 repetitions of سبحان اللَّه (Subhan Allah).

Obstacles [453] to the remembrance of Allah: A: Satan. «انساهم ذكر اللّه» [454] (Satan made them forget the remembrance of Allah). B: Accumulation and rivalry. «ألهیكم التَّكاثُر» [455] (The rivalry in accumulating wealth distracted you). C: Hopes and fantasies. «و یُلهِهم الاَمَل» [456] (Hope distracted them).

Effects of remembering God: * Remembering His favors is the key to knowing Him and being grateful to Him. * Remembering His endless power is the key to reliance (Tawakkul) upon Him. * Remembering His knowledge and awareness is the key to our modesty (Haya) and piety (Taqwa). * Remembering His kindnesses is the key to loving Him. * Remembering His justice is the key to fearing Him. * Remembering His assistance is the key to hope (Raja).

449) Al-Baqarah, 40. 450) Taha, 14. 451) Al-Baqarah, 40. 452) Al-Mujadilah, 11. 453) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 140. 454) Al-Mujadilah, 19. 455) Al-Takathur, 1. 456) Al-Hijr, 3.

Messages

1- Allah grants dignity to humanity and elevates the status of man to the point where He says: You remember Me, so that I may remember you. «اذكرونى اذكركم»

2- Those who are constantly mindful of Allah are included in His special favor. «اذكرونى اذكركم»

3- The remembrance of Allah and His favors is the basis for gratitude and thanks (Shukr). Just as heedlessness toward the remembrance of Allah leads to ingratitude (Kufran). «اذكرونى، اشكروا لى و لاتكفرون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱسْتَعِينُوا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ وَٱلصَّلَوٰةِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (۱٥۳)
O you who have faith! Take recourse in patience and prayer; indeed Allah is with the patient. (153)

Key Points

We read a similar Divine statement in this very Surah [457] where Allah told the Children of Israel: Seek help through patience (sabr) and prayer (salat) against adversities and hardships. In this Ayah, too, Muslims are commanded to seek assistance through patience and prayer when facing the difficult events of life. Indeed, similar ailments require similar remedies. Fundamentally, the limited human being, amidst numerous problems and unpleasant events, will be crushed and destroyed if he is not connected to the unlimited Divine power. And the person connected to Allah does not lose heart during events and hardships, nor do the events seem overwhelming to him. Whoever performs the prayer with presence of heart and attention ascends to the Mi'raj (ascension). The greater and higher his spiritual flight, the smaller the world, its problems, and even its joys become. A person is either living in blessing, in which case he must act upon the previous Ayah: «اذكرونى، اشكروا لى» (Remember Me, and be grateful to Me); or he is living in hardship, in which case he must act upon this Ayah: «استعینوا بالصبر» (Seek help through patience). Allah says: God is with the patient ones, and He does not say: He is with the prayer performers. This is because prayer also requires patience and steadfastness. [458] We read in narrations: Whenever a difficult task arose for Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, he would perform two units (rak'ats) of prayer. [459] Ibn Sina (Avicenna) also followed this practice. Patience is the mother of all perfections. Patience in war is the source of courage. Patience against sin is the means of piety (taqwa). Patience regarding the world is a sign of asceticism (zuhd). Patience in desire is the cause of chastity (iffah). Patience in worship leads to obedience (ta'ah), and patience regarding ambiguities (shubuhat) is the source of scrupulousness (wara'). [460]

457) Baqarah, 45. 458) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. 459) Tafsir Safi, Vol. 1, p. 111. 460) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan, Vol. 2, p. 258.

Messages

1- Faith will be more effective if accompanied by action, reliance (tawakkul), patience, and worship. [461] «الّذین آمنوا استعینوا بالصبرو...»

2- Prayer is a lever, not a burden. «استعینوا بالصبر والصلوة»

3- Patience and prayer are means of attracting Divine support. «استعینوا بالصبر والصلوة انّ اللّه مع الصابرین»

4- Although Allah is present with everyone and everywhere; «هو معكم اینما كنتم» [462] (He is with you wherever you are), yet Allah's companionship with the patient ones has a special meaning, which is His grace, affection, and assistance to the patient ones. «انّ اللّه مع الصابرین»

461) Tafsir Rahnamā.

462) Hadid, 4.

Ayah

وَلَا تَقُولُوا۟ لِمَن يُقْتَلُ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ أَمْوَٰتٌۢ ۚ بَلْ أَحْيَآءٌ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تَشْعُرُونَ (۱٥٤)
Do not call those who were slain in Allah’s way ‘dead.’ No, they are living, but you are not aware. (154)

Key Points

In the Battle of Badr, fourteen Muslims were martyred, six of whom were from the Muhajirun (Emigrants) and eight from the Ansar (Helpers). Some people used to say: So-and-so died. This Ayah was revealed and forbade them from this way of thinking. [463] Martyrs (Shuhada), not only are their names or the effects of their deeds alive and enduring, but they possess a real life in Barzakh (the intermediate realm). A life in which there is sustenance (rizq), joy, and glad tidings; a life in the proximity of Allah's Mercy, in which there is no grief or fear. The characteristics of this life will be discussed in the commentary on Surah Aal-i-Imran [464], Insha'Allah. Martyrdom (Shahadah) in the way of Allah includes being killed on the battlefield, assassination and attempted murder by enemies, and the killing of believers while defending their lives, honor, and property, in any place and at any time.

463) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 464) Aal-i-Imran, 168 to 170.

Messages

1- Limited materialistic perspectives must be completed and corrected through faith in Allah and His statements. «لاتقولوا»

2- Difficult duties require ideological support and compensation for losses. Indeed, one who knows that he is alive will embrace martyrdom. «لاتقولوا... اموات بل احیاء»

3- Martyrdom is valuable only when it is in the way of Allah. «فى سبیل اللَّه»

4- Striving in the way of religion is among the high values in the sight of Allah. «یقتل فى سبیل اللَّه»

5- The soul remains after death, even if the physical body disintegrates. «احیاء»

6- The source of many analyses is ignorance of the realities. «لاتقولوا, لاتشعرون»

Ayah

وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُم بِشَىْءٍ مِّنَ ٱلْخَوْفِ وَٱلْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ ٱلْأَمْوَٰلِ وَٱلْأَنفُسِ وَٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (۱٥٥)
We will surely test you with a measure of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth, lives, and fruits; and give good news to the patient (155)

Key Points

Allah the Almighty tests all human beings, but the test and trial are not the same for everyone. The entire universe is a stage for testing, and all people, even the Prophets, are subjected to examination. We must know that Allah's tests are not for the removal of ambiguity, but rather for the flourishing of talents and the nurturing of human beings. The means of divine testing include all bitter and sweet events, such as fear and hunger, financial and life losses, and scarcity of produce. Fear of the enemy, economic siege, war and Jihad, and sending children and loved ones to the battlefields are among these trials. To succeed in the trial of fear of the enemy, reliance (Tawakkul) and remembrance of Allah are needed, and to combat shortages, patience (Sabr) is needed, the path for which was shown in the two previous verses: «واستعینوا بالصبر والصلوة». It is not necessary for all people to be tested with all issues. Rather, it is possible that: A: Everyone is tested with something specific. B: A person may pass one test with honor but bring about disgrace in another trial. C: An individual's test may also be a means of testing others. Sometimes the decrease in wealth and produce, or the emergence of fear and other problems, is due to divine testing, but at other times, these matters are the punishment for the actions of the people themselves. Humans sometimes commit sins for which Allah afflicts them with certain hardships. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «انّ اللَّه یبتلى عباده عند الاعمال السیّئة بنقص الّثمرات و حبس البركات و اغلاق خزائن الخیرات لیتوب تائب ویتذكّر متذكّر» (465) Indeed, Allah afflicts His servants, due to their corrupt deeds, with scarcity of produce, withholding of blessings, and the closing of the sources of goodness, so that perhaps they may be reminded and repent. Of course, this very warning is also a test, just as He grants a blessing due to faith, which is a means of testing: «لاسقیناهم مآء غدقاً لِنَفتنهم فیه» (466) Indeed, We gave them pleasant water to drink so that We might test them thereby. Allah has bestowed several favors upon the patient (Sabirun), including: 1- Love. «واللَّه یحبّ الصابرین» (467) 2- Help/Victory. «ان اللَّه مع الصابرین» (468) 3- Paradise. «یجزون الغرفة بما صبروا» (469) 4- Reward without measure. «انما یوفّى الصّابرون اجرهم بغیر حساب» (470) 5 - Glad tidings. «بشّر الصابرین» The path to victory in divine tests involves several things: A: Patience and resistance. B: Attention to the transient nature of events and problems. C: Attention to the history of those who came before, and how they overcame difficulties. D: Attention to the fact that all our problems are in the sight and view of Allah, and everything is accounted for. When his son was struck by an arrow and martyred in his arms, Imam Hussain عليه السلام said: «هَوِّن علىّ ما نزل بى انّه بعین اللّه» (471) This difficult event seems easy for me because Allah is watching.

(465) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 143. (466) Al-Jinn, 16. (467) Aal-i Imran, 146. (468) Al-Baqarah, 153. (469) Al-Furqan, 75. (470) Az-Zumar, 10. (471) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 45, p. 46.

Messages

1- Testing and trial are a definite divine program and tradition. «ولَنبلوَنّكم»

2- Hardships are a cause for resistance and growth. Many human qualities, such as patience (Sabr), contentment (Rida), submission (Taslim), satisfaction (Qana'ah), piety (Taqwa), forbearance (Hilm), and self-sacrifice (Ithar), develop in the shadow of encountering poverty and distress. «وبشّر الصابرین»

3- The subject of the glad tidings is not specified in the Ayah so that it may encompass all types of divine glad tidings. «بشّر الصابرین»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَصَـٰبَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّآ إِلَيْهِ رَٰجِعُونَ (۱٥٦)
—those who, when an affliction visits them, say, ‘Indeed we belong to Allah and to Him do we indeed return.’ (156)

Key Points

The patient ones (Sābirūn), instead of losing heart and seeking refuge in others, seek refuge only in Allah. This is because, from their perspective, the entire world is a classroom and a testing ground where we must grow. The world is not a place to stay permanently; it is neither a dormitory nor a pleasure resort, and its hardships and difficulties are not signs of Allah's lack of affection. Adversities are meant to make the ground beneath our feet hot so that we move faster and quicker; therefore, there is sweetness even in bitterness, because they lead to the blossoming of talents and the attainment of divine rewards. The calamities that come from Him are the disposition of the true Owner, Allah, over His possession. If a person knows that Allah is Wise (Hakīm) and Merciful (Rahīm), and that he himself was a servant who was previously nothing: «لم یك شیئاً» (472), and even after certain stages was not a thing worthy of mention: «لم یكن شیئاً مذكورا» (473), he will accept that he must be at His disposal. He has guided me from the inanimate to the plant, from the plant to the animal, and from the rank of animality to humanity, and He has ordained these events for my growth and elevation. Just as we grind a grain of wheat under pressure and then turn it into bread in the fire of the oven to elevate its existential stages. The motto of the patient ones is «انّا لِلّه و انّا الیه راجعون» (Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return). We read in a Hadith: Whenever you face a calamity, say the phrase «انّا لِلّه و انّا الیه راجعون» (474). Saying «انّا لِلّه» and remembering Allah during adversities has numerous effects: 1. It prevents a person from uttering blasphemous words and complaints. 2. It provides comfort, solace, and self-admonition to the person. 3. It prevents satanic whispers. 4. It is an expression of true beliefs. 5. It serves as a lesson and an example for others.

People fall into several categories when facing problems and calamities: 1. A group screams and shouts. «اذا مسّه الشّر جزوعاً» (475) 2. A group is tolerant and patient. «وبشّر الصابرین» 3. A group, besides being patient, is grateful. «اللّهم لك الحَمد حَمد الشّاكرین لَك على مُصابهم» (476)

These reactions are signs of each person's understanding of the philosophy behind calamities and hardships. Just as a child becomes restless from eating pungent onion, and a teenager tolerates it, but an adult pays money to buy and eat the onion.

472) Maryam, 67. 473) Insān, 1. 474) Durr al-Manthūr, Vol. 1, p. 377. 475) Ma‘ārij, 20. 476) The last phrase of Ziyārat ‘Āshūrā.

Messages

1- The root of patience is faith in Allah, the Resurrection (Ma'ād), and hope for receiving reward. «الصّابرین الّذین... قالوا انّا للّه و انّا الیه راجعون»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَٰتٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُهْتَدُونَ (۱٥۷)
It is they who receive the blessings of their Lord and [His] mercy, and it is they who are the [rightly] guided. (157)

Key Points

The word "Salawat" (صلوات) derives from the root "Salw" (صلو), meaning entry into blessing and mercy, contrary to the word "Sali" (صلى), which means entry into wrath and anger. For example: «تصلى ناراً حامیة» (He shall enter a blazing Fire). [477] Allah Himself sends blessings and Salawat upon the believers who have shown patience and resilience in difficulties: «صلوات من ربهم» (blessings from their Lord). But regarding affluent believers who pay the Zakat (alms) on their wealth, He commands the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله to send blessings upon them: «صلّ علیهم» (Send blessings upon them). [478]

477) Al-Ghashiyah, 4. 478) At-Tawbah, 103.

Messages

1- Allah immerses the patient ones in His special mercy. «علیهم صلوات» (upon them are blessings).

2- Allah's encouragement of the patient teaches us that those who sacrifice, the patient, and the strivers (Mujahidin) must enjoy special dignity and respect in society. «علیهم صلوات من ربّهم...» (upon them are blessings from their Lord...).

3- Encouragement is among the attributes of Lordship (Rububiyyah) and a necessity for nurturing (Tarbiyah). «صلوات من ربهم» (blessings from their Lord).

4- The guidance of the patient is definite and certain. Although in the Quran, the guidance of many people is merely a hope: «لعّلهم یهتدون» (that they may be guided), regarding the patient, their guidance is considered certain. «اولئك هم المهتدون» (it is they who are the guided ones).

5- Guidance has stages. Although those who say «انّاللّه وانّاالیه راجعون» (Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return) are believers and guided, they attain the higher stage after patience and receiving divine blessings (Salawat) and mercy. «اولئك هم المهتدون» (it is they who are the guided ones).

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ ٱلصَّفَا وَٱلْمَرْوَةَ مِن شَعَآئِرِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَمَنْ حَجَّ ٱلْبَيْتَ أَوِ ٱعْتَمَرَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِ أَن يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِمَا ۚ وَمَن تَطَوَّعَ خَيْرًا فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَاكِرٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱٥۸)
Indeed Safa and Marwah are among Allah’s sacraments. So whoever makes hajj to the House, or performs the ‘umrah, there is no sin upon him to circuit between them. Should anyone do good of his own accord, then Allah is indeed appreciative, all-knowing. (158)

Key Points

Sha'a'ir (شعائر) is the plural of Sha'irah (شعیرة), referring to signs designated for specific rituals, and Sha'a'ir Allah (شعائراللَّه) are the signs that God has designated for worship. [479] Safa and Marwah are the names of two hills beside the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram), situated opposite each other at an approximate distance of 428 meters. Today, they have been converted into a covered street. Pilgrims to the House of God are obligated to traverse the distance between these two hills seven times. This act commemorates the memory of Hajar, the devoted wife of Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام), who, in search of a sip of water for her child Isma'il, traversed the distance between these two hills seven times in anxiety and worry, without any helper or companion. Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: There is no place on earth better than the area between these two hills, because every arrogant person must traverse the distance between the two hills there, bareheaded, barefoot, and shrouded (in the Ihram garments), without any distinction or mark, sometimes running and sometimes walking. In addition to this, Mount Safa holds memories of the Prophet of Islam’s invitation, showing how He called the disbelievers of Mecca to Monotheism while they refused to listen. [480]

During the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), the polytheists had installed an idol named Usaf (اُساف) atop Mount Safa and another idol named Na'ilah (نائله) atop Mount Marwah. During the Sa'y (ritual traversing), they would touch these two idols with their hands for blessing. For this reason, Muslims felt aversion towards the Sa'y between Safa and Marwah and thought that, given the history of idols being placed atop the two hills, they should not perform the Sa'y. The above Ayah was revealed, stating that Safa and Marwah are among the Divine Symbols (Sha'a'ir), and if ignorant people have polluted them, this is not a reason for Muslims to abandon them. [481]

Hajj and Umrah are sometimes performed together, like the Adhan and Iqamah (calls to prayer), and sometimes the rituals of Umrah are performed separately and alone. Five acts are obligatory in Umrah: 1. Ihram (consecration). 2. Tawaf (circumambulation). 3. Tawaf Prayer. 4. Sa'y between Safa and Marwah. 5. Shortening the hair or nails. Hajj has several other acts in addition to these, which are mentioned in their proper place. In any case, the Sa'y between Safa and Marwah is relevant both in Umrah and in Hajj.

Seeing Safa and Marwah is being present on the stage of history, so that affirmation replaces imagination, and objectivity replaces subjectivity. Safa and Marwah is a class in theology (knowing God), showing how His Will gathers all these human beings, despite all their differences, in one garment and one direction. It is a class in prophetology (knowing the Prophet), showing how Ibrahim, that Divine Prophet, left his wife and child alone in that desert to carry out the command of Allah. And it is also a class in anthropology (knowing man), teaching how a person can perform deeds in moments whose effects remain forever.

The Sa'y of Safa and Marwah teaches that if we all move together, we will see the Divine Spirit in our society. The Sa'y of Safa and Marwah teaches that we must cast aside arrogance and move alongside others. The Sa'y of Safa and Marwah teaches us that women and children also have a share in reviving the Name of Allah.

In the previous verses, the discussion was about affliction and trial; in this Ayah, an example of the trial faced by Hajar, her child, his father, and other Muslims is presented. The effect of sincere, Godly work and devotion progresses to the point that all Prophets and Saints are obligated to traverse this distance seven times, imitating Hajar, the wife of Ibrahim, with an anxious expression and running (harwalah) just as she did. And this command is Allah’s gratitude for the hardships endured by Hajar. «فانّ اللّه شاكر علیم» (for Allah is Appreciative, Knowing).

Although the Sa'y of Safa and Marwah is obligatory, He says: «فلاجناح علیه» (there is no blame upon him). This tone is due to the previous concern regarding the site being a place for idols and a route for polytheists.

Tawaf does not necessarily mean circular rotation; rather, any movement in which a person returns to the initial position, whether circular or longitudinal, is called Tawaf. Therefore, the word Tawaf is used both for the Tawaf around the Ka'bah, which is a circular movement: «و لیَطّوَّفوا بالبیت العتیق» (and let them circumambulate the Ancient House) [482], and it is also used regarding the longitudinal movement between Safa and Marwah.

479) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 480) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 145. 481) Tafsir Tibyan, Vol. 1, p. 44. 482) Hajj, 29.

Messages

  1. Allah’s special attention to certain places, times, or individuals causes them to become Divine Symbols (Sha'a'ir Allah). «انّ الصفا و المروة من شعائر اللَّه» (Indeed, Safa and Marwah are among the symbols of Allah).
  2. Intention gives value to deeds. The polytheists performed the Sa'y to touch the idols, but Islam says: Perform the Sa'y for the pleasure of Allah. [483] «فلاجناح علیه ان یطّوف بهما» (there is no blame upon him for traversing between them).
  3. If centers of truth are polluted by a group with superstitions, one should not abandon them; rather, one must purify those centers by being present there and cut off the hand of the deviant group from them. «فلا جناح علیه ان یطّوف بهما»
  4. Worship must be loving and voluntary. «فمن تطوّع» (And whoever volunteers good).
  5. Allah is Appreciative (Shakir) of the worship of His servants. «فان اللَّه شاكر» (for Allah is Appreciative). This expression is the greatest kindness from Him towards His servants.

483) Many actions have the same appearance but different motivations. Similar to Yusuf (Joseph) and Zulaikha running towards the closed doors: Yusuf runs so as not to be polluted, but Zulaikha runs so that he may be polluted. «فاستبقا» (So they raced).

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ مَآ أَنزَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَٱلْهُدَىٰ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا بَيَّنَّـٰهُ لِلنَّاسِ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ۙ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَلْعَنُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَيَلْعَنُهُمُ ٱللَّـٰعِنُونَ (۱٥۹)
Indeed those who conceal what We have sent down of manifest proofs and guidance, after We have clarified it in the Book for mankind—they shall be cursed by Allah and cursed by the cursers, (159)

Key Points

Although the context of the Ayah concerns the scholars of the Jews and Christians who did not disclose the truths of the Torah and the Gospel to the people, the phrase «یكتمون» (they conceal), which denotes continuity, includes all concealers throughout history. Just as the curse of the Lord will continue forever. Concealing the truth can take various forms: sometimes through silence and failure to express the truth, sometimes through justification (rationalization), and sometimes by distracting people with minor issues and making them oblivious to the main matters. In certain cases, such as the secrets of the believers or the faults of religious brethren, concealment (kutmān) is obligatory or recommended (mustahabb). The sin of concealment is mostly committed by the scholars. It is also mentioned in Ayah 187 of Sūrat Āl ‘Imrān that Allah took a covenant from the People of the Book to make the truths clear to the people and not to conceal them: «واذا اخذ اللّه میثاق الّذین اوتواالكتاب لتبیّننّه للنّاس ولاتكتمونه» It is stated in narrations: On the Day of Resurrection, a bridle will be placed upon the mouths of those who concealed the truth.

Messages

1- Cultural oppression is the worst form of oppression, which brings the curse of the Creator and the creation. «یكتمون... یلعنهم اللّه و یلعنهم اللاعنون»

2- Concealing the truth is forbidden, whether it is the concealment of miracles and proofs of truthfulness; «البیّنات» (the clear signs), or the concealment of guidance and instructions. «الهُدى» (the guidance). (484)

3- Concealing the truth is one of the greatest sins, as it prevents people from being guided and leaves generations in misguidance. «یلعنهم اللَّه»

4- Concealing the truth is an injustice against the religion of Allah and against the people's right to be guided. Therefore, Allah and the people curse those who conceal the truth. «یلعنهم اللَّه ویلعنهم اللاعنون»

5- The imprecation and curse of the people are effective, and the leverage of public hatred must be used for enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (nahy ‘anil munkar). «یلعنهم اللاعنون»

(484) Tafsīr al-Mīzān.

Ayah

إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟ وَأَصْلَحُوا۟ وَبَيَّنُوا۟ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَأَنَا ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱٦٠)
except such as repent, make amends, and clarify—those I shall pardon, and I am the All-clement, the All-merciful. (160)

Key Points

For the sin of concealment (kutmān), just like other sins, the path of repentance (tawbah) and return is open. However, true repentance is achieved through heartfelt remorse, reforming one's actions, and disclosing the concealed matters. The repentance of one who has not prayed is to make up (qaḍā) his prayers. The repentance of one who has wasted people's property is that he must return the equivalent amount to its owner. In this case too, the one who has betrayed the world of knowledge, thought, and generations by concealing the truths can only compensate for the past by clarifying and recounting those truths. Allah said to Satan: «انّ علیك لعنتى» (485) My curse is upon you. And in the following Ayah, He says: «علیهم لعنة اللَّه...» Thus, those who conceal (the truth) and Satan are in the same rank.

485) Ṣād, 78.

Messages

1- Allah has provided the possibility of repentance and return for wrongdoers under all circumstances. «یلعنهم اللّه... الاّ الّذین تابوا»

2- Concealing religious truths is corruption, because the repentant person has been commanded to reform and compensate. «تابوا واصلحوا»

3- The repentance for every sin is commensurate with it. The repentance for concealment is the clarification of truths. «تابوا... وبیّنوا»

4- Just as the curse of Allah encompassed those who conceal (the truth) in the context of threat and reproach, the words «اَنَا» (I), «توّاب» (Acceptor of Repentance), and «رحیم» (Merciful) are used in the context of kindness to convey: I Myself, with My special kindness, return to you. «یلعنهم اللَّه... انَا التوّاب الرّحیم»

5- Threatening the sinner and giving glad tidings to the righteous are two fundamental pillars for the education of the individual and society. «یلعنهم اللّه... انَا التوّاب الرّحیم»

6- The return of Allah's grace to the repentant is permanent, certain, and accompanied by affection. «اتوب علیهم و انَا التوّاب الرّحیم»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَمَاتُوا۟ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةُ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ (۱٦۱)
Indeed those who turn faithless and die while they are faithless—it is they on whom shall be the curse of Allah, the angels and all mankind. (161)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ لَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ (۱٦۲)
They will remain in it [forever] and their punishment shall not be lightened, nor will they be granted any respite. (162)

Key Points

The previous Ayah stated that if those who conceal the truth repent and reveal the facts, they will be covered by divine grace. In this Ayah, He says: But if the disbelievers do not repent and die while in a state of disbelief, the same curse of Allah, the angels, and all people will still afflict them.

Question: The Ayah mentions the curse of all people upon the disbelievers, but it is obvious that some people are themselves disbelievers or friends of disbelievers. So, what is the meaning of the curse of all people in the Ayah?

Answer: The curse is relevant both in this world and the Hereafter. Those who are friends of the disbelievers or are themselves disbelievers in this world will be cursed in the Hereafter. «كلّما دخلت امّة لعنتْ اُختها» [486]

One of the requests and prayers of the friends of Allah (Awliya) is to die as a Muslim. Prophet Joseph (عليه السلام) asks Allah to let him die as a Muslim: «تَوفّنى مُسلِماً» [487]. Prophets Abraham (عليه السلام) and Jacob (عليه السلام) advised their children: «فلاتموتنّ الاّ وانتم مسلمون» [488], meaning, do not die except while you are Muslims, that is, submitted to the Lord.

486) A'raf, 38.

487) Yusuf, 101.

488) Baqarah, 132.

Messages

1- Insistence on disbelief and dying while in a state of disbelief leads to man's eternal distance from divine mercy. «ماتوا و هم كفّار... علیهم لعنة اللّه»

2- What is important is the end of a person's life—whether he dies with faith or without faith. «ماتوا و هم كفّار»

3- Mitigation or postponement of punishment relates to this world. In the Hereafter, there is neither mitigation nor postponement. «لا یخفّف... لا هم ینظرون»

Ayahs

وَإِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۖ لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱٦۳)
Your god is the One God; there is no god except Him, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful. (163)
إِنَّ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَـٰفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَٱلْفُلْكِ ٱلَّتِى تَجْرِى فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ بِمَا يَنفَعُ ٱلنَّاسَ وَمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مِن مَّآءٍ فَأَحْيَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا وَبَثَّ فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ دَآبَّةٍ وَتَصْرِيفِ ٱلرِّيَـٰحِ وَٱلسَّحَابِ ٱلْمُسَخَّرِ بَيْنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (۱٦٤)
Indeed in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the alternation of night and day, and the ships that sail at sea with profit to men, and the water that Allah sends down from the sky—with which He revives the earth after its death, and scatters therein every kind of animal—and the changing of the winds, and the clouds disposed between the sky and the earth, there are surely signs for a people who exercise their reason. (164)

Key Points

The harmony among the elements of nature, the components of existence, and the laws governing them, all demonstrate the sovereignty, power, and will of the One God. The creation of the heavens and their constant expansion [489]—to which the hand of man has so far only reached a part of the first heaven—and their firmness [490], and the seven layers [491], and the governing system [492], and the proportions and connections between each one, and their being without pillars [493], and their protection [494], and the movements of the stars in their orbits and the distance of each one from the other, all are signs of the power of the One, Wise God.

Sa'di says:

Creation is all the planning of the Lord of the heart; He has no heart, who does not acknowledge the Lord.

Mountains, seas, and trees, all are in glorification; Not every listener understands these secrets.

The intellect is astonished by the golden cluster of grapes; Understanding is incapable before the ruby seed of the pomegranate.

Pure and flawless is the God Who, by His mighty decree, Subjugates the moon and the sun, and the night and the day.

The word Riyāḥ (ریاح) is the plural of Rīḥ (ریح), meaning wind, but wherever the word Rīḥ appears in the Qur'an, it is associated with wrath and punishment, such as: «ریح صرصر» [495]. But wherever the word Riyāḥ appears, it is associated with rain and Divine grace. We read in a tradition: Whenever a wind blew, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله would say: «الّلهم اجعلها ریاحاً و لا تجعلها ریحاً» (O God! Make this wind Riyāḥ of mercy, not Rīḥ of punishment.) [496]

۴۸۹) و انّا لموسعون« ذاریات، ۴۷.

۴۹۰) سبعاًشداداً« نبأ، ۱۲.

۴۹۱) سبع سموات طباقاً« ملك، ۳.

۴۹۲) اوحى فى كلّ سماء امرها« فصّلت، ۱۲.

۴۹۳) بغیر عمد ترونها« رعد، ۲.

۴۹۴) سقفاً محفوظاً« انبیا، ۳۲.

۴۹۵) الحاقه، ۶.

۴۹۶) تفسیر صفوةالتفاسیر، ج ۱، ص ۱۱۳.

Messages

  1. Knowing nature is one of the paths to knowing God, which encompasses knowing His power, His wisdom, and His oneness. «الهكم اله واحد... فى خلق السموات... لایات»

  2. Allah has no peer or likeness and is not composed of parts. «اله واحد»

  3. Both nature and the industry made by human hands originate from Him. «خلق السموات و الارض... والفلك»

  4. Every creature in the universe is an Ayah (sign) from the book of Allah in nature. «لایات»

The green leaves of the trees, in the eyes of the wise, Every leaf is a volume, of the knowledge of the Creator.

  1. Only the wise take lessons in knowing God from observing existence. «ان فى خلق السموات... لایات لقوم یعقلون»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَتَّخِذُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَندَادًا يُحِبُّونَهُمْ كَحُبِّ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَشَدُّ حُبًّا لِّلَّهِ ۗ وَلَوْ يَرَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ إِذْ يَرَوْنَ ٱلْعَذَابَ أَنَّ ٱلْقُوَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعَذَابِ (۱٦٥)
Among the people are those who set up compeers besides Allah, loving them as if loving Allah—but the faithful have a more ardent love for Allah—though the wrongdoers will see, when they sight the punishment, that power, altogether, belongs to Allah, and that Allah is severe in punishment. (165)

Key Points

| Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: The meaning of "دون اللَّه" (besides Allah) and "انداد" (rivals/equals) in this Ayah is not idols, but rather oppressive and misguided leaders whom people love just as they love Allah. [497] Furthermore, from a grammatical perspective, the word "هم" (them) in "یحبّونهم" (they love them) is used for humans, not objects. The root of love (mahabbah) is the love of perfection and the love of beauty. Believers see all perfections and beauties in Allah, and thus express the greatest love for Him. The love and affection of the believers are based on the worthiness and merit of the Beloved, and it never turns cold or fades away. But the love of the polytheists is based on imagination, ignorance, imitation, and vain desires.

497) كافى، ج 1، ص 374.

Messages

1- Worship and love for anyone other than Allah are forbidden. «یتّخذ من دون اللَّه انداداً یحبّونهم»

2- Emotions must be connected to beliefs. «الّذین آمنوا اشدّ حُبّاً للّه»

3- Some people will not realize the futility and vanity of their path and thought until the veils are lifted and they witness the Day of Judgment. «ولو یرى الّذین»

4- Power is effective in attracting followers. «انّ القوّة للّه جمیعاً» Therefore, do not be attracted to anyone other than Him.

Ayah

إِذْ تَبَرَّأَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتُّبِعُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوا۟ وَرَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ وَتَقَطَّعَتْ بِهِمُ ٱلْأَسْبَابُ (۱٦٦)
When those who were followed will disown the followers, and they will sight the punishment while all their means of recourse will be cut off, (166)

Key Points

| Let us be mindful of who our leader is and whose love and affection we hold in our hearts. We must know that the Taghut (tyrants) and those besides Allah want us for themselves so that, through our power and devotion, they may achieve their worldly desires and aspirations. But on the Day of Resurrection, they will abandon everyone and express aversion and disgust towards us.

Messages

1- Any love or affection that lacks a foundation in intellect and innate nature (fitrah) will sooner or later turn cold or lead to enmity. «یحبّونهم كحبّ اللّه... تبرّء الّذین اتّبعوا»

2- Love is the basis for obedience/following. «یحبونهم... اتبعوا»

3- Foresight is essential for intellect. We should love and be devoted to someone who possesses power and can support us on the day of danger. «اذ تبرّء الّذین اتّبعوا»

4- The criterion for the genuineness or lack of foundation of affections and loves is seeing the punishment on dangerous days. «رأوا العذاب»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوا۟ لَوْ أَنَّ لَنَا كَرَّةً فَنَتَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُمْ كَمَا تَبَرَّءُوا۟ مِنَّا ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُرِيهِمُ ٱللَّهُ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ حَسَرَٰتٍ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ وَمَا هُم بِخَـٰرِجِينَ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ (۱٦۷)
and when the followers will say, ‘Had there been another turn for us, we would disown them as they disown us [now]!’ Thus shall Allah show them their deeds as regrets for themselves, and they shall not leave the Fire. (167)

Key Points

According to narrations (Riwāyāt), there are multiple scenes and stations (Mawāqif) on the Day of Judgment. In some of them, the seal of silence is placed upon the lips, and they merely look at each other with regretful glances and weep. In some instances, they seek help from one another, and in another scene, they wail and curse each other. In this Ayah, we read that the followers of disbelief (Kufr) will also deeply regret their support and affection for their leaders, but the matter will be over, and the only thing left is regret in their hearts, which they express verbally: If we were to return again, we would never follow them. Those who are so disloyal that they disown us on this Day—if we were to return to the world, we would also disown them. They feel regret, but what good is regret?

In several verses, the word khulūd (eternity/perpetuity) is used regarding punishment. Some interpret khulūd as a long duration, but the phrase "و ماهم بخارجین من النّار" (and they will never leave the Fire) in this Ayah implies that khulūd means eternity, not merely a long duration.

In narrations, examples of the regret of sinners on the Day of Judgment are seen, including: those who leave vast wealth for their heirs but do no good deeds themselves during their lifetime. On that Day, they will observe that if the heir performed a good deed using the inheritance, it is recorded in the heir's scroll (of deeds), and if the heir performed a bad deed, the deceased is considered a partner in the heir's crime. [498] Another example of those who regret are those who performed many acts of worship but did not accept the leadership and Wilāyah (guardianship) of ‘Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib علیهما السلام. [499]

Man possesses free will (Ikhtiyār). If this were not the case, regret, remorse, and the ability to make a new decision would not exist for him. Regret and remorse are signs that we could have done something else. And the new decision is the key that man, through will and choice, can select any path he deems appropriate.

498) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 151. 499) Bihār, Vol. 27, p. 184.

Messages

1- Let us abandon the tyrants (Ṭāghūts). Let us abandon them in this world before they abandon us on the Day of Judgment. "فنتبرّأ منهم" (so we would disown them). 2- On the Day of Judgment, man's eye will become truth-seeing, and he will regret his deeds. "یریهم اللّه اعمالهم حسرات" (Allah will show them their deeds as regrets). 3- The wish to return exists, but there is no return. "لو أنّ لنا كرّة..." (If only we had a return...) and "وماهم بخارجین" (and they will never leave).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ كُلُوا۟ مِمَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ حَلَـٰلًا طَيِّبًا وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ خُطُوَٰتِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ (۱٦۸)
O mankind! Eat of what is lawful and pure in the earth, and do not follow in Satan’s steps. Indeed, he is your manifest enemy. (168)

Key Points

Islam has always enjoined people to benefit from the pure and lawful blessings of Allah, and it combats all forms of monasticism (rahbaniyyah) and undue asceticism (zuhd). Therefore, it considers both unhealthy foods to be from Satan—«انّما الخمر... رجس من عمل الشّیطان» (500)—and undue abstention from food to be a step of Satan—«كلوا... و لا تتّبعوا خطوات الشیطان». This is because some historical accounts state that certain Arab tribes had, without reason, forbidden themselves from consuming parts of their crops and animals, and sometimes they attributed these prohibitions to Allah. The Ayah was revealed to clarify this ambiguity. Islam pays full attention to the material life of man, and foremost among these are nutritional needs, concerning which dozens of Ayahs and hundreds of narrations (Hadith) have been reported. One of the duties of the prophets (Anbiya) is also to clarify the lawful (halal) and unlawful (haram) foods and drinks for the people. Usually, the Quran states a condition alongside the permission to consume. For example, here it says: «كلوا... حلالاً طیباً». It also says: «كلوا و اشربوا... و لا تعثوا فى الارض مفسدین» (501)—Eat and drink... but do not commit corruption on the earth. In another Ayah, it says: «كلوا واشربوا ولاتسرفوا» (502)—Eat and drink, but do not commit extravagance (israf). And elsewhere it says: «كلوا و اطعموا» (503)—Eat and feed others. In Tafsir Burhan, it is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that a person named Tariq had decided to separate from his wife and live a monastic life. The Imam said: «انّ هذا من خطوات الشیطان» (This is from the steps of Satan).

500) Ma'idah, 90. 501) Baqarah, 60. 502) A'raf, 31. 503) Hajj, 28.

Messages

1- The fundamental condition for consumption is two things: being lawful (halal), and being pure and pleasing (tayyib). «كلوا حلالاً طیّباً»

2- Islam opposes certain forms of self-mortification (riyadhat). «كلوا»

3- Benefiting from forbidden (muharramat) and impure things is following Satan. «كلوا... حلالا طیباً ولا تتبعوا خطوات الشیطان»

4- The natural needs of humanity are a ground for deviation and the dominance of Satan. While fulfilling these needs, attention must be paid to the potential pitfalls. «كلوا... ولاتتبعوا خطوات...»

5- Satan deviates man step by step. One must be cautious from the very first step. «خطوات الشیطان»

6- Satan enters through various ways to deviate people. «خطوات»

7- Compelling people to use forbidden things and preventing them from enjoying lawful blessings is a manifestation of Satan's manifest enmity. «كلوا... حلالا طیباً... انّه لكم عدو مبین»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا يَأْمُرُكُم بِٱلسُّوٓءِ وَٱلْفَحْشَآءِ وَأَن تَقُولُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۱٦۹)
He only prompts you to [commit] evil and indecent acts, and that you attribute to Allah what you do not know. (169)

Key Points

It is stated in Tafsir Ruh al-Bayan that Satan passes through stages in his temptation (waswasah): First, he invites to disbelief (kufr). If he is unsuccessful, he invites to innovation (bid'ah). If he is unsuccessful, he invites to major sins (kabirah). If he is unsuccessful, he invites to minor sins (saghira). If he is unsuccessful, he invites to permissible acts (mubah) instead of acts of worship ('ibadat). And if he is still unsuccessful, he invites to acts of worship that are lower in rank, so that the person remains behind from the higher stage.

The command of Satan is merely his temptations (waswasah). It is not that he removes free will from man, forcing him to commit sin.

The effect of Satan's command is a sign of our weakness. Whenever a person becomes weak, he accepts Satan's temptations as the command of a master; «انمّا سلطانه على الّذین یتولّونه» (504) Otherwise, the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah) are at a stage where Satan has no influence over them. «انّ عبادى لیس لك علیهم سلطان» (505)

504) An-Nahl, 100. 505) Al-Hijr, 42.

Messages

1- The sign of Satan's enmity is temptation towards committing immorality (fahsha) and fabricating lies against Allah. «عدوّ مبین انّما یأمركم»

2- Satan both commands sin and shows the way to justify it. The command to evil (su') and immorality (fahsha) is the instruction to sin, and the command to fabricate lies against Allah is the instruction to justify the sin. «یأمركم... وان تقولوا»

3- Expressing opinions regarding religious rulings (Ahkam) and issuing fatwas without knowledge is forbidden (haram). «ان تقولوا على اللَّه ما لا تعلمون»

4- Even in a state of doubt or uncertainty, one must not attribute anything to Allah, let alone in cases where we know that statement or word is not from Him. Therefore, precision must be exercised in interpreting the Quran and explaining religious rulings. «وان تقولوا على اللَّه ما لاتعلمون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱتَّبِعُوا۟ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ قَالُوا۟ بَلْ نَتَّبِعُ مَآ أَلْفَيْنَا عَلَيْهِ ءَابَآءَنَآ ۗ أَوَلَوْ كَانَ ءَابَآؤُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَهْتَدُونَ (۱۷٠)
When they are told, ‘Follow what Allah has sent down,’ they say, ‘No, We will follow what we have found our fathers following.’ What, even if their fathers neither exercised their reason nor were guided?! (170)

Key Points

The previous Ayah warned us to avoid following the footsteps and commands of Satan. This Ayah explains one of the manifestations of Satan's path, which is blind imitation. Rational following and obedience are not prohibited. The imitation criticized by the Quran is the imitation of those who neither possessed intellect themselves nor accepted the guidance of the Prophets. Divine guidance exists in every age and time. The fact that the Quran states that their forefathers were not receptive to guidance implies that Divine guidance existed at all times, but they refused to accept it. Imam Ali عليه السلام states in Nahj al-Balaghah: «بلى لاتخلوا الارض من قائم للّه ظاهراً او خائفاً» [506] The earth is never devoid of a heavenly leader, whether manifest or hidden, inviting people to the path of Allah.

506) Nahj al-Balaghah, Short Sayings 147.

Messages

1- Regression and backward movement are forbidden. Following the tradition and path of the forefathers is unacceptable if it is not accompanied by reasoning and intellect. «الفینا علیه ابائنا»

2- Racial and tribal prejudices are among the grounds for rejecting the truth. «بل نتّبع... ابائنا»

3- The customs and beliefs of the forefathers influence future generations. «ما الفینا علیه ابائنا»

4- The path of truth is attained through intellect and revelation. «لایعقلون شیئاً و لایهتدون»

5- The transmission of experience and knowledge is valuable, but the transmission of superstitions from the past generation to the future generation is anti-value. «اباؤهم لایعقلون»

6- Intellect guides us to follow revelation. «اتّبعوا ما انزلنا... او لوكان اباؤهم لا یعقلون»

Ayah

وَمَثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ كَمَثَلِ ٱلَّذِى يَنْعِقُ بِمَا لَا يَسْمَعُ إِلَّا دُعَآءً وَنِدَآءً ۚ صُمٌّۢ بُكْمٌ عُمْىٌ فَهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ (۱۷۱)
The parable of the faithless is that of someone who shouts after that which does not hear [anything] except a call and cry: deaf, dumb, and blind, they do not exercise their reason. (171)

Key Points

"Du'a" (دعا) means calling from nearby, while "Nida'" (نداء) is for calling from afar. In this Ayah, there are two similes: one is the comparison of the caller to the truth (the Prophet) to a shepherd, and the other is the comparison of the disbelievers to animals who understand nothing from the shepherd's speech except a shout. Meaning, O Prophet! Your example in inviting this faithless people toward the truth and breaking the barriers of blind imitation is like someone who calls sheep and animals to save them from danger, but they do not comprehend the message. This is because the eyes and ears of their hearts are sealed, and they are, in reality, deaf, dumb, and blind.

Messages

1- The value of the eye, ear, and tongue lies in their being a prelude to intellect (reasoning); otherwise, animals also have eyes and ears. «صم بكم عمى فهم لایعقلون»

2- The path to knowledge is asking, listening, and seeing, accompanied by contemplation. «صم بكم عمى فهم لایعقلون»

3- Indifferent individuals who ignore the call to truth possess five contemptible characteristics: they are like animals, they are blind, they are deaf, they are dumb, and they are senseless (lacking intellect). «مثل الّذین كفروا... فهم لایعقلون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ وَٱشْكُرُوا۟ لِلَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ (۱۷۲)
O you who have faith! Eat of the good things We have provided you, and thank Allah, if it is Him that you worship. (172)

Key Points

It is narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله that Allah says: I create people, but they worship others besides Me. I provide sustenance for them, but they thank someone else. [507]

Whenever the Quran intends to prohibit man from an action, it first presents the permissible ways (of doing it) and then states the prohibitions. Since it intends to forbid and prohibit people from certain foods in the subsequent verses, in this verse, it has reiterated the lawful ways.

Gratitude (Shukr) has stages. Sometimes it is with the tongue, and sometimes it is through action. True gratitude is that blessings are utilized in the way that Allah has decreed.

The primary purpose for the creation of natural blessings is the believers. Three verses prior, He said: O people! Eat of what is on the earth. In this verse, He says: O believers! Eat of the pleasant blessings which I have provided for you. Perhaps there is a secret in this difference of expression, which is that the main goal is providing sustenance to the believers, but other people also benefit alongside them.

Just as the gardener's main goal in irrigation is the growth of flowers and fruit trees, even though weeds and thorns also benefit.

A specific expectation is held for everyone. The expectation from ordinary people is that after eating, they do not pursue sin, the whispers of Satan, and corruption; «یا ایّها النّاس كلوا... ولا تَتّبعوا خُطوات الشّیطان» [508] But the expectation from the people of faith is that after eating, they pursue righteous deeds and gratitude. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا كلوا... واشكروا» and «یا ایّها الرّسل كلوا... واعملوا صالحاً» [509]

507) Tafsir Safi. 508) Baqarah, 168. 509) Mu'minun, 51.

Messages

1- Self-purification, asceticism (zuhd), and piety (taqwa) do not contradict benefiting from good things (tayyibat). «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا كلوا من طیبات»

2- In the school of Islam, material things are a prelude to spiritual things. «كلوا، واشكروا، تعبدون»

3- Islam pays attention to hygiene in nutrition. «طیّبات ما رزقناكم»

4- Do not have expectations before securing people's livelihood and showing them affection. «كلوا... واشكروا»

5- Gratitude is a sign of monotheism and pure worship of Allah. If a person considers sustenance to be the result of his economic thinking, effort, management, capital, and credit, he will not assign a share to Allah so that he may thank Him. «واشكروا للّه ان كنتم ایّاه تعبدون»

6- Gratitude is a clear example of worship. «واشكروا للّه... تعبدون»

7- Thanking Allah is obligatory (wajib). «كلوا... واشكروا لِلّه»

8- One who worships Allah does not, on his own accord, prohibit what Allah has deemed lawful. «كلوا... ان كنتم ایّاه تعبدون»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَيْتَةَ وَٱلدَّمَ وَلَحْمَ ٱلْخِنزِيرِ وَمَآ أُهِلَّ بِهِۦ لِغَيْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَمَنِ ٱضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلَا عَادٍ فَلَآ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۷۳)
He has forbidden you only carrion, blood, the flesh of the swine, and that which has been offered to other than Allah. But should someone be compelled, without being rebellious or transgressive, there shall be no sin upon him. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (173)

Key Points
