Following the previous Ayah, which stated: "Do not forbid upon yourselves the lawful things of Allah on your own accord," Allah has only forbidden carrion (dead meat), blood, the flesh of swine (pork), and any animal over which the name of other than Allah is invoked during slaughter, similar to the time of Jahiliyyah (Ignorance). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The flesh of carrion causes bodily weakness, cuts off progeny, and leads to sudden death, and consuming blood causes hard-heartedness and cruelty of the heart. [510] According to health theories, pork is the carrier of two types of worms: tapeworm and trichina. Furthermore, in some countries, the consumption of pork has been deemed forbidden, and the Gospel of Christ has also likened sinners to swine. A person who has no food to save his life may use forbidden food due to necessity (idtirar), provided that it is not done out of rebellion, disobedience, or transgression. That is, he should consume only the amount necessary to save himself from death, not seeking pleasure. This permission is due to the grace and kindness of Allah. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If a person deliberately refuses to eat forbidden foods while in a state of necessity and dies, he has died a disbeliever. [511] Considering that forbidden foods are more than the four items mentioned in the Ayah, the word "انّما" (innama - only/verily) does not imply absolute intellectual restriction, but rather serves as a contrast to the prohibitions of Jahiliyyah. The rule of necessity is not exclusive to foodstuffs; it mitigates the law whenever it arises in any matter. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked what a patient should do regarding prayer if a doctor has ordered him rest and sleep. The Imam recited this Ayah and said: He should perform the prayer while lying down. [512] The rulings of Islam are based on underlying benefits (masalih). In the previous Ayah, eating was permissible because the foods were pure (tayyib), and in this Ayah, things that are not pure are forbidden. Divine prohibitions are not solely for medical and hygienic reasons, such as the prohibition of carrion and blood. Rather, sometimes the reason for the prohibition relates to ideological, intellectual, and educational matters. An example is the prohibition of the meat of an animal over which the name of other than Allah has been invoked, which is for the sake of eliminating polytheism (shirk). Just as sometimes we avoid a person's food due to his lack of hygiene, sometimes our avoidance is based on turning away, protesting, or disassociating from him.

510) Wasā’il, Vol. 16, p. 310. 511) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 130. 512) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 145.

Messages

1- Islam has paid full attention to the issue of food and nutrition and has repeatedly warned about harmful and forbidden foods. [513] "انّما حرّم علیكم المیتة..." (Verily, He has forbidden upon you carrion...)

2- Prohibitions belong only to Allah, not to others. "انّما حرّم علیكم" (Verily, He has forbidden upon you)

3- Attention to Allah and invoking His Name is necessary when slaughtering animals, so that none of our actions fall outside the sphere of Monotheism (Tawhid), and so that we combat the manifestations of polytheism and idolatry. "وما اهلّ به لغیر اللَّه" (and that over which the name of other than Allah has been invoked)

4- Necessity changes the ruling only when it is imposed upon a person, not when the person makes himself necessary. The word "اُضطُرّ" (was compelled) is in the passive voice, not the active. "فمن اضطرّ" (But whoever is compelled)

5- Islam is a comprehensive religion that faces no dead ends. Every obligation can be lifted in times of necessity. "فمن اضطُرّ... فلا اثم علیه" (But whoever is compelled... there is no sin upon him)

6- Legislators must take special circumstances into account when enacting laws. "فمن اضطرّ... فلا اِثم علیه" (But whoever is compelled... there is no sin upon him)

7- Do not misuse the conditions of necessity. "فمن اضطُرّ غیرباغ ولا عاد" (But whoever is compelled, neither desiring [it] nor transgressing)

513) The prohibition of pork, carrion, and blood is repeated four times in the Quran: the Ayah under discussion, and Surahs Al-Ma'idah, 3; Al-An'am, 145; and An-Nahl, 115.

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَيَشْتَرُونَ بِهِۦ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۙ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِى بُطُونِهِمْ إِلَّا ٱلنَّارَ وَلَا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ وَلَا يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱۷٤)
Indeed those who conceal what Allah has sent down of the Book and sell it for a paltry gain—they do not take in, into their bellies, [anything] except fire, and Allah shall not speak to them on the Day of Resurrection, nor shall He purify them, and there is a painful punishment for them. (174)

Key Points

The scholars of the Jews and Christians, before the coming of the Prophet of Islam, used to promise the people the arrival of that Prophet and would describe the signs mentioned in the Torah and the Gospel. But as soon as the Prophet of Islam was commissioned (as a messenger), and they saw that acknowledging His prophethood was equivalent to losing their status, wealth, and so on, they concealed the truth so that they might remain in their positions for a few more days and consume gifts and presents. However, this is a meager price they receive in exchange for their great sin, and these earnings are nothing but fire that they consume. Just as in another Ayah, consuming the wealth of orphans is described as consuming fire. (514)

On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will not speak to these concealers out of affection, even though on that Day, the believers will converse with God. Of course, this conversation will be either through the creation of waves in the atmosphere, or through inspiration, or by the language of the heart, and all the righteous will become Kalim Allah (one who speaks with Allah) on that Day.

In the previous Ayah, following the prohibition of pork and carrion, mention of forgiveness was made. But in this Ayah, which speaks of the prohibition of concealing the truth and sins related to intellectual and cultural matters, the tone of the Ayah has become much harsher, and no mention of forgiveness is made. Furthermore, these threats become even more severe in the next Ayah.

Concealing the truth was not limited only to the prophethood of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. Those who conceal the truth regarding the true successor of the Prophet will also suffer this punishment. Those who erase the event of Ghadir Khumm from their books of Tafsir (exegesis) and history, distort and justify the meaning of the Ayat (verses), and send people to others instead of the infallible Imam, have committed the concealment of truth.

(514) Nisa, 10.

Messages

1- Worldliness is the greatest danger for scholars and learned people. «یكتمون، یشترون»

2- Selling religion, regardless of the price, is a loss, because the truths and knowledge contained in the heavenly books are more valuable than all profits. «ثمناً قلیلاً»

3- Forbidden consumables are embodied in the form of fire. «ما یأكلون... الاّ النار»

4- Things possess an inner, celestial (Malakuti) aspect, which will be embodied in that form on the Day of Resurrection. «فى بطونهم الاّ النّار»

5- Punishment must be commensurate with the crime. Those who blocked the path for people to hear the word of God in this world will be deprived of the pleasure of hearing the word of God in the Hereafter. «لا یكلّمهم اللَّه»

6- The punishments of the Day of Resurrection are both physical and spiritual. «لایكلّمهم اللَّه»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱشْتَرَوُا۟ ٱلضَّلَـٰلَةَ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ وَٱلْعَذَابَ بِٱلْمَغْفِرَةِ ۚ فَمَآ أَصْبَرَهُمْ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ (۱۷٥)
They are the ones who bought error for guidance, and punishment for pardon: how patient of them to face the Fire! (175)

Key Points

For the sin of concealment (of truth), eight consecutive threats have been mentioned: five threats in the previous Ayah, two threats in this Ayah, and one threat in the next Ayah. Perhaps no other sin has been followed by so many consecutive threats. The motives for concealing the truth are numerous. Among them, one can point to arrogance, undue religious fanaticism, maintaining status, weakness of character, lack of courage, narrow-mindedness, the influence of strangers, and preserving position and wealth. The repentance for the sin of concealment is not merely seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) and weeping, but rather declaring the truths. Of course, in cases where someone's life is in danger, or where corruption and danger are involved, concealment is permissible. Examples include concealing one's own sins or the sins of others to prevent the spread of evil deeds.

Messages

1- Concealing the truths is a specific sin of the scholars. «اولئك الّذین»

2- In the declaration of the heavenly books lies Divine guidance and forgiveness, and in their concealment lies misguidance and punishment. «اشتروا الضلالة بالهدى و العذاب بالمغفرة»

3- Selling one's religion and concealing the truth leads to the severest punishments. The phrase «فما اصبرهم على النار» (How patient they are with the Fire!) is used in the Quran only regarding this group.

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ نَزَّلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَفِى شِقَاقٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (۱۷٦)
That is so because Allah has sent down the Book with the truth, and those who differ about the Book are surely in extreme defiance. (176)

Key Points

All these threats and promises of punishment mentioned for those who conceal religious truths are because Allah has revealed the Divine Book so clearly, plainly, and accompanied by proofs and arguments that no room for doubt or ambiguity remains for anyone. However, despite this, a group of people, in order to protect their personal interests, resort to justification and distortion, creating disagreement in the understanding of the Divine Book, so that, as the saying goes, they muddy the water and catch fish from it. Allah says in describing them: This group lives in schism and separation. One of the ways to conceal the truth is to create disagreement. In the previous three verses, all threats were directed at those who conceal the truth (the concealers). In this verse, instead of saying: the concealers are in schism, He said: those who disagree concerning the Book are in schism. This indicates that the concealers are the same as those who create disagreement. This is because it is in an environment polluted by disagreement that one can confuse people and hide the truths.

Messages

1- The source of religious and sectarian disagreements is the scholars, not the Divine Books. «نزل الكتاب بالحقّ... اختلفوا فى الكتاب»

2- Concealing truths leads to schism, division, separation, and dispersion. «شقاق بعید»

Ayah

۞ لَّيْسَ ٱلْبِرَّ أَن تُوَلُّوا۟ وُجُوهَكُمْ قِبَلَ ٱلْمَشْرِقِ وَٱلْمَغْرِبِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلْبِرَّ مَنْ ءَامَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ وَٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ وَءَاتَى ٱلْمَالَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِۦ ذَوِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينَ وَٱبْنَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَٱلسَّآئِلِينَ وَفِى ٱلرِّقَابِ وَأَقَامَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتَى ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَٱلْمُوفُونَ بِعَهْدِهِمْ إِذَا عَـٰهَدُوا۟ ۖ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ فِى ٱلْبَأْسَآءِ وَٱلضَّرَّآءِ وَحِينَ ٱلْبَأْسِ ۗ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا۟ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ (۱۷۷)
Piety is not to turn your faces to the east or the west; rather, piety is [personified by] those who have faith in Allah and the Last Day, the angels, the Book, and the prophets, and who give their wealth, for the love of Him, to relatives, orphans, the needy, the traveller and the beggar, and for [the freeing of] the slaves, and maintain the prayer and give the zakat, and those who fulfill their covenants, when they pledge themselves, and those who are patient in stress and distress, and in the heat of battle. They are the ones who are true [to their covenant], and it is they who are the Godwary. (177)

Key Points

The word birr (برّ) means goodness, but anyone who is extremely righteous is called barr (برّ). Meaning, his existence is entirely goodness. Ba'sa' (بأساء) comes from bu's (بؤس), meaning poverty and hardship that is imposed upon a person from outside. Dharra' (ضرّاء) is pain and illness that pressures a person from within. And hīn al-ba's (حین البأس) is the time of war and struggle (Jihad).

After the incident of the change of the Qibla, which we read about in Ayah 144, the topic of the day revolved around the Qibla and its change. This Ayah states: Why have you resorted to contentious debates instead of focusing on the substance of religion, which is faith in Allah and the Hereafter and performing good deeds?

This Ayah is the most comprehensive Ayah in the Quran, because it addresses important principles of belief, action, and ethics. In Tafsir al-Mizan, it is narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله that He said: Whoever acts upon this Ayah, his faith is complete.

This Ayah has expressed fifteen good qualities in three sections: faith, action, and ethics.

In the section on faith, it refers to the issue of belief in Allah, the angels, the prophets, the Day of Judgment, and the heavenly Books.

In the section on action, it refers to devotional issues like prayer (salat), economic issues like charity (zakat), social issues like freeing slaves, military issues like patience on the battlefield and in war, and spiritual and psychological issues like patience in the face of difficulties.

In the section on ethics, it refers to fulfilling covenants, detaching oneself from material possessions, and showing compassion to the poor.

Faith in Allah causes submission to the truth and non-surrender to the tyrants (taghuts). Faith in the Day of Judgment leads to broad vision and high aspiration. Faith in the existence of angels is a sign of faith in the supernatural structure. Faith in the Prophets is faith in revelation and the flow of guidance throughout history, and proof that man has not been left aimless and without a program in this world.

Spending (in charity) expresses cooperation and philanthropy, and prayer is a direct connection with Allah, and zakat is planning for solving the problems of the deprived. Fulfillment of covenant strengthens relationships, and patience is the factor that tempers human beings. [515]

The phrase ātā al-māla ‘alā ḥubbih (آتى المال على حبّه) has been interpreted in three ways: A: Giving wealth to others despite the love one has for it. B: Giving wealth based on the love of Allah. C: Giving wealth based on the affection one has for the poor person.

Patience is the mother of all perfections, and the Quran considers patience to be the path to Paradise: «اولئك یجزون الغُرفة بما صبروا» [516] "Those will be rewarded with the high station for what they patiently endured." Just as the angels say to the people of Paradise: «سلامٌ علیكم بما صبرتم» [517] "Peace be upon you for what you patiently endured." Similarly, concerning the divine leaders, He says: «جعلنامنهم ائمّةیهدون بامرنا لمّا صبروا» [518] "And We made from among them leaders guiding by Our command when they were patient."

Similar to this Ayah, the following narrations have also been cited to introduce religious knowledge: * «لیس العاقل من یعرف الخیر من الشرّ و لكن العاقل من یعرف خیر الشّرین» [519] The wise person is not he who knows good from evil, but the wise person is he who chooses the better of two evils. * «لیس العلم بكثرة التعلّم انّما هو نور یقذفه اللّه فى قلب من یرید» [520] Knowledge is not by much learning; rather, it is a light that Allah casts into the heart of whomever He wills. * «لیس البرّ فى حُسن الزّى و لكن فى السكینة و الوقار» [521] Greatness is not in fine attire, but rather in tranquility and dignity. * «لیس السخى... الذى ینفق ماله فى غیر حقّه و لكنه الّذى یؤدّى الى اللّه ما فرض علیه» [522] The generous person is not... the one who spends his wealth unjustly, but rather the one who renders to Allah what He has obligated upon him. * «لیس العبادة كثرة الصیام و الصلاة و انّما العبادة كثرة التفكّر فى امر اللّه» [523] Worship is not the abundance of fasting and prayer; rather, worship is the abundance of contemplation concerning the command of Allah. * «اشدّ من یَتم الیتیم یتیمٌ انقطع عن امامه» [524] More orphaned than the orphan is the one who is cut off from his Imam.

For reaching the perfection of piety, both obligatory and non-obligatory spending are necessary. Some people help the needy but do not pay their obligatory rights, and others, by paying Khums and Zakat, become indifferent to the poor and deprived. This Ayah considers the true believer to be one who pays both the obligatory rights and performs voluntary charity. For this reason, we read in narrations: In the wealth of the rich, there is a right for the deprived besides Zakat. [525] And whoever sleeps full while his neighbor is hungry does not have faith in Allah and the Day of Judgment. [526]

515) Tafsir Maraghi, under the Ayah. 516) Al-Furqan, 75. 517) Ar-Ra'd, 24. 518) As-Sajdah, 24. 519) Bihar, Vol. 78, p. 6. 520) Bihar, Vol. 1, p. 225. 521) Bihar, Vol. 1, p. 106. 522) Bihar, Vol. 71, p. 352. 523) Bihar, Vol. 71, p. 325. 524) Bihar, Vol. 108, p. 171. 525) Tafsir Qurtubi. 526) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 665.

Messages

  1. Do not abandon the main goals by focusing on the appearance instead of the substance of religion. «لیس البرّ... ولكن البرّ»
  2. One of the duties of the Prophets and heavenly Books is to change the culture of the people. «لیس البرّ... و لكن البر»
  3. Understanding concepts is not important; acting upon them is. The word birr means goodness, but knowing it is not important; the one who actualizes this concept is valuable. «لكن البرّ من آمن»
  4. Faith precedes action. «من آمن باللَّه... آتى المال»
  5. Faith in all Prophets and Angels is necessary. «آمن باللَّه... والملائكة والكتاب والنبیّین»
  6. Connection with Allah is mentioned alongside connection with people and social cooperation during incidents and hardships. «آمن باللَّه و الیوم الاخر... وآتى المال على حبه»
  7. All good deeds are formed under the shadow of faith in Allah. «آمن باللّه... آتى المال»
  8. The goal of Islam in spending is not only to feed the hungry, but also for the owner of the wealth to detach himself from it. «على حبّه»
  9. The righteous spend their wealth and possessions willingly and lovingly in the way of Allah. «آتى المال على حبّه»
  10. Spend upon the poor, orphans, and relatives with your own hand. «آتى المال على حبّه»
  11. In spending, needy relatives have priority over other groups of the destitute. «ذوى القربى و الیتامى...»
  12. Do not turn away the beggar, even if he is not poor, destitute, a traveler, or a relative. The word al-sā'ilīn (السائلین) is mentioned independently. «والمساكین و... السائلین»
  13. Faith, prayer, and Zakat are not complete without participation in Jihad. «وحین البأس... اولئك الّذین صدقوا»
  14. Claimants of faith are many, but true believers who act upon the entire substance of religion are a small group. «اولئك الّذین صدقوا»
  15. The sign of sincerity is acting upon religious duties and social commitments. «اولئك الّذین صدقوا»
  16. The pious person is one whose actions confirm his beliefs. «آتى المال... اولئك الّذین صدقوا اولئك هم المتقون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْقِصَاصُ فِى ٱلْقَتْلَى ۖ ٱلْحُرُّ بِٱلْحُرِّ وَٱلْعَبْدُ بِٱلْعَبْدِ وَٱلْأُنثَىٰ بِٱلْأُنثَىٰ ۚ فَمَنْ عُفِىَ لَهُۥ مِنْ أَخِيهِ شَىْءٌ فَٱتِّبَاعٌۢ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَأَدَآءٌ إِلَيْهِ بِإِحْسَـٰنٍ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ تَخْفِيفٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ ۗ فَمَنِ ٱعْتَدَىٰ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَلَهُۥ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱۷۸)
O you who have faith! Retribution is prescribed for you regarding the slain: freeman for freeman, slave for slave, and female for female. But if one is granted any extenuation by his brother, let the follow up [for the blood-money] be honourable, and let the payment to him be with kindness. That is a remission from your Lord and a mercy; and should anyone transgress after that, there shall be a painful punishment for him. (178)

Key Points

"Qisas" (retaliation) is derived from the root qass, meaning pursuit or tracking. Thus, a continuous and sequential narrative is called a qissah (story). The pursuit of murder until the punishment of the killer is called qisas.

In every society, murder occasionally occurs. A comprehensive and complete religion like Islam must present a just and logical plan and program in response to such incidents, one that can prevent the increase and repetition of such events, as well as prevent undue acts of revenge and potential misuse, ensuring that killers do not become emboldened and the blood of the oppressed is not wasted.

In the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) before Islam, sometimes an entire tribe would be devastated and long wars would erupt merely because one person was killed. Islam, through the plan presented in this Ayah, has considered both the protection of people's lives, the satisfaction of both parties, and the observance of limits and measure.

Of course, the law of Qisas is not a Divine decree that cannot be pardoned or overlooked; rather, it is a right for the guardians of the victim's blood, who can waive their right, either by taking diyah (blood money) or without it.

Question: Why is gender relevant in the law of Qisas? If the killer is male and the victim is female, is the man not subject to Qisas? Answer: The murder of a woman and a man is equal from the Divine, human, and otherworldly punishment perspective, but there is a difference in worldly punishment. This is because usually the man is the breadwinner of the house, and his murder causes an economic blow to the family. The law is based on the general type (norm), not on rare cases where a woman might be the breadwinner.

Messages

1- A religious government is necessary for the realization of religious rulings. The implementation of the law of Qisas is impossible without power and governance. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا...»

2- The principles of equality and justice are observed in the law of Qisas. «الحُرّ بالحُرّ و العبد بالعبد و الاُنثى بالاُنثى...»

3- Decisiveness and compassion are necessary side by side. «فمن عفى له من اخیه» (Then for him whom his brother has forgiven) implies that the guardians of the victim are the brother of the killer, and the killer does not step outside the bounds of Islam and brotherhood.

4- Islamic jurisprudence is intertwined with Islamic ethics. «كتب علیكم القصاص... فمن عفى»

5- Islam neither considers Qisas the only path, like Judaism, nor recognizes forgiveness as the best path, like Christianity; rather, it presents various paths such as Qisas, blood money (diyah), and pardon. «كتب علیكم القصاص... فمن عفى»

6- The permissibility of pardoning the killer and converting Qisas into taking blood money is for your training/refinement. «تخفیف من ربّكم»

7- Transgression of Divine limits and misuse thereof is forbidden. «فمن اعتدى علیه... فله عذاب الیم»

8- If you establish a remission (or leniency) in a law, prevent its misuse. «ذلك تخفیف... فمن اعتدى»

Ayah

وَلَكُمْ فِى ٱلْقِصَاصِ حَيَوٰةٌ يَـٰٓأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (۱۷۹)
There is life for you in retribution, O you who possess intellects! Maybe you will be Godwary! (179)

Key Points

Apparently, the Ayah is responding to a series of objections raised against the ruling of Qisas (retaliation), especially by those who pretend to be intellectuals. The Quran states: The ruling of Qisas ensures life and vitality for human society. Qisas is not a personal confrontation or revenge; rather, it ensures social security. In a society where the aggressor is not subjected to Qisas, justice and security vanish, and that society is seemingly lifeless and dead. Just as in medicine, agriculture, and animal husbandry, the prerequisite for the life and health of humans, plants, and animals is the elimination of microbes and pests. If we argue that the murderer should be released on the pretext that he experienced psychological excitement, there is no guarantee that this excuse will not be raised in other crimes, because all criminals do not commit crimes while in a state of mental and psychological health and tranquility. Based on this, all offenders must be free, and a healthy society would turn into a jungle where anyone could commit any act due to emotional and psychological upheavals. It should not be assumed that today's world is a world of emotion and philanthropy, and that the law of Qisas is a harsh law incompatible with the global culture of human rights. Islam, alongside the ruling of Qisas, has permitted forgiveness and the acceptance of blood money (Diyyah) so that action may be taken according to expediency. Justifications, such as utilizing criminals and murderers in prison for forced labor to benefit economic progress, are unacceptable. Because these programs do not guarantee public security. The fundamental priority is the dignity of humanity and a justice-nurturing society, not a dangerous world coupled with increased production, especially at the hands of criminals and murderers! Since the law of Qisas guarantees justice and security and is the secret to the life of society, the end of the Ayah states: «لعلّكم تتقون» (that you may become righteous), meaning the implementation of this law prevents the occurrence of other murders.

Life (Hayaat) has several types: 1- Natural life. Such as the revival of the earth in spring and after rain. «یحیى الارض» (527) 2- Spiritual life. Such as the call of the Prophet, which is the factor for reviving people. «دعاكم لما یحییكم» (528) 3- Barzakhi (intermediate) life, which the martyrs possess. «لا تقولوا لمن یقتل فى سبیل اللّه اموات بل احیاء» (529) 4- Hereafter life, which is for everyone. «یمیتكم ثم یحییكم» (530) 5 - Social life under the shadow of security and justice. Similar to this very Ayah: «لكم فى القصاص حیاة»

527) Rum, 50. 528) Anfal, 24. 529) Baqarah, 154. 530) Baqarah, 28.

Messages

1- The implementation of justice guarantees the life of society. «لكم فى القصاص حیاة»

2- Balance between compassion and severity is necessary. Allah, Who is the Most Compassionate and the Most Merciful, considers Qisas the secret to life. «لكم فى القصاص حیاة»

3- Eliminating a dangerous element is a rational principle. «یا اولى الالباب»

4- Do not judge hastily. You might not consider Qisas good upon superficial observation, but through wisdom and reflection, you realize that Qisas is the secret to life. «یا اولى الالباب»

5 - Piety (Taqwa) and avoidance of sin are the philosophy behind religious rulings, whether devotional or judicial. «لعلّك [۵۳۱] م تتقون»

6- Qisas is a cause for deterring people from repeating murder. «لعلكم تتقون»

531) In explaining the philosophy of fasting, He also states: »لعلكم تتّقون« (Baqarah, 183).

Ayah

كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ إِن تَرَكَ خَيْرًا ٱلْوَصِيَّةُ لِلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ وَٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۖ حَقًّا عَلَى ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۱۸٠)
Prescribed for you, when death approaches any of you and he leaves behind any property, is that he make a bequest for his parents and relatives, in an honourable manner—an obligation on the Godwary. (180)

Key Points

Some people think that making a will (وصیّت) is a precursor to dying soon, whereas it is a type of foresight. The reason it states: "make a will at the time of death" is because that moment is the last opportunity; otherwise, one can make a will many years before death arrives. Some have considered making a will obligatory (wajib), but the phrase "حقّاً على المتّقین" (a duty upon the pious) suggests that this act is recommended (mustahabb); otherwise, it would have said: "حقّاً على المؤمنین" (a duty upon the believers).

In the noble Ayah, the word "خیر" (goodness/blessing) is used instead of the word "مال" (wealth) to clarify that wealth and riches are a source of goodness and virtue. What is criticized in Islam is unlawful wealth (mal haram), excessive attachment to wealth, prioritizing wealth over personal virtues and social needs, or exploiting people to acquire it.

The will must be based on al-Ma'ruf (the recognized good), meaning it must be acceptable to reason, not driven by malice, revenge, or undue and excessive affection.

Since only some relatives benefit from inheritance, and that too in specified amounts, Islam recommends that if there are family members who are deprived of inheritance or whose share is small, one should use the will to increase their portion. However, if someone fails to observe justice in this will or commits an injustice, they have committed a major sin (532).

It is mentioned in narrative texts: A person who had young children donated all his wealth in the way of Allah. When the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله became aware, He asked, "What did you do with this deceased person?" They replied, "We buried him." He said, "If you had informed Me earlier, I would not have allowed him to be buried in the Muslim cemetery, because despite having so many children, he left no wealth for them and gave everything away in the way of Allah" (533).

Making a will is a delicate matter; if, God forbid, it is done carelessly and later becomes a source of discord and distress, all good deeds will be erased. It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that He said: Sometimes a person worships for sixty years, but because he does not prepare his will justly, he goes to Hell (534).

The will is an indication that a person's ownership continues even after death regarding a portion of his assets. The amount designated in the will is one-third of the wealth, and if a person makes a will for more than this amount, the permission of the heirs is required. To learn how to make a will, it is best to read the wills of the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah), the martyrs, and the scholars.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever initially undertakes to execute a person's will but then abandons it without excuse, no deed of His will be accepted, and all the angels between the heavens and the earth curse him. He is constantly under the wrath of Allah, and for every word of "Ya Rabb" (O Lord) that he utters, one curse is bestowed upon him, and the reward for all his previous deeds is registered in favor of the testator (535).

Effects and Blessings of the Will 1. The will is a sign of meticulousness and being accountable. 2. The will is a sign of respect for the rights of others. 3. The will is the execution of good deeds that were neglected and the continuation of righteous action after death. 4. The will is a way to fill economic gaps and adjust wealth distribution.

The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever dies having made a will, it is as if he has died a martyr (536).

A will made for parents and relatives is a factor in renewing affection and a type of appreciation. Therefore, parents are mentioned at the beginning of the Ayah concerning the will, so that, in addition to their share of inheritance, the will is arranged in a way that benefits them as well, which is itself an instance of benevolence (Ihsan).

Types of Wills 1. Obligatory (Wajib): Such as willing the fulfillment of Haqq Allah (rights of Allah), Haqq an-Nas (rights of people), making up missed prayers and other acts of worship, paying obligatory dues like Khums, Zakat, debts, and people's liabilities. 2. Recommended (Mustahabb): Such as willing charitable matters. 3. Permissible (Mubah): Such as advising children regarding the type of occupation, profession, clothing, or food. 4. Disliked (Makruh): Such as willing the construction of elaborate tombs. 5. Forbidden (Haram): Such as willing the establishment of centers of corruption or the publication of misleading books.

532) Safinat al-Bihar, entry "Wasi". 533) Safinat al-Bihar, entry "Wasi". 534) Nahj al-Fasahah, sentence 626. 535) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan. 536) Wasa'il, Vol. 13, p. 352.

Messages

  1. Although a person departs the world upon death, the record of his deeds remains open through actions such as the will. "اذا حضر احدكم الموت.... الوصیة"
  2. Wealth and riches, if spent in the right way, are goodness (khayr). "ان ترك خیر الوصیّة"
  3. In the will, a share must be designated for parents and close relatives, in addition to their inheritance. "الوصیّة للوالدین و الاقربین"
  4. The will must be based on the praiseworthy custom (urf) of society. "الوصیّة... بالمعروف"
  5. Neglecting the will is a type of impiety regarding the rights of others. "الوصیّة... حقّاً على المتّقین"

Ayah

فَمَنۢ بَدَّلَهُۥ بَعْدَ مَا سَمِعَهُۥ فَإِنَّمَآ إِثْمُهُۥ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يُبَدِّلُونَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱۸۱)
And should anyone alter it after hearing it, its sin shall indeed lie on those who alter it. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (181)

Key Points

This Ayah is also a warning to those who attempt to alter or change the will (testament) of others. If someone, after hearing and becoming aware of the subject of the will and its intended uses, undertakes to alter or change it, the sin of this alteration and change falls upon the one who committed this improper act, but the testator (the one who made the will) receives his reward. For example, if someone wills that aid be given to one hundred poor people, but instead of the one hundred poor, his assets are given to others who are unaware and consume the property, in this case, the testator who has passed away receives the reward for aiding the poor, and the unaware recipients are not sinful. In this situation, the sin rests solely upon the one who altered the will, and he must know that Allah is Hearing and Knowing and will give him the punishment for this betrayal in this world and the Hereafter. We read in a Hadith: Do not alter the will, even if it is in favor of a Jew or a Christian. [537]

537) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 159.

Messages

1- Altering a will by others is forbidden (Haram). «فمن بدّله... فانما اثمه»

2- The right of ownership is respected even after death, and no one has the right to alter the will. «فمن بدّله... فانّما اثمه»

3- Sin committed knowingly and maliciously is more dangerous. «بعد ما سمعه»

4- Witnesses must be taken when making a will. «بعد ما سمعه»

5- Testators receive their reward even if someone alters their will. «اثمه على الّذین یبدّلونه»

6- Belief that we are in the presence of Allah is the best factor for piety (Taqwa) and for avoiding the alteration and change of people's wills. «فمن بدّله... ان اللَّه سمیع علیم»

Ayah

فَمَنْ خَافَ مِن مُّوصٍ جَنَفًا أَوْ إِثْمًا فَأَصْلَحَ بَيْنَهُمْ فَلَآ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۸۲)
But should someone, fearing deviance or sin on the testator’s behalf, set things right between them, there is no sin upon him. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (182)

Key Points

The author of Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan has taken the word "janafan" (جنف) to mean an inclination towards unintentional deviation, and the word "ithm" (اثم) to mean intentional sin. What is forbidden in Islam is the alteration and change of valid wills. However, if the will causes discord (fitnah) or violates Sharia standards, changing it is permissible. For instance, if the testator wills more than one-third of his property, that amount can be reduced. If he recommends a sin, the will can be changed. Or if acting upon the will creates discord and corruption, changes can be made to the will under the supervision of the Islamic ruler. In any case, there is no deadlock in Islam, and since the path of all actions is reaching piety (Taqwa), all anti-Taqwa instances can be corrected based on Taqwa.

Messages

1- The principle of the more important and the important (al-ahamm wa al-muhimm) must be observed. Respecting the will is important, but removing discord (fitnah) and reforming the affairs of the Muslims is more important. «فمن خاف... فاصلح بینهم فلا اثم علیه»

2- Changing the will must be based on removing discord and reformation. «فمن خاف... فاصلح»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلصِّيَامُ كَمَا كُتِبَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (۱۸۳)
O you who have faith! Prescribed for you is fasting as it was prescribed for those who were before you, so that you may be Godwary. (183)

Key Points

Taqwa (piety/God-consciousness) means self-restraint from sin. Most sins originate from two roots: anger (ghadhab) and lust (shahwah). Fasting curbs the intensity of these two instincts, thereby reducing corruption and increasing taqwa. [538]

According to exegetes and scholars of Quranic sciences, verses that begin with the address "یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا" (O you who have believed) were revealed in Medina and are considered Medinan verses. The command for fasting, like the instruction for Jihad and the payment of Zakat, was issued in the second year of the Hijra.

The Effects and Blessings of Fasting: * Taqwa and the fear of God, both outwardly and inwardly, are the most important effects of fasting. * Fasting is the only hidden act of worship. People see Salat (prayer), Hajj (pilgrimage), Jihad, Zakat, and Khums, but fasting is not visible. * Fasting strengthens human will. A person who abstains from his own food, water, and spouse for a month can certainly control himself regarding the property and honor of others. * Fasting strengthens empathy. A person who tastes the flavor of hunger for a month becomes acquainted with pain and feels and understands the suffering of the hungry. * The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Fasting is half of patience. [539] * The fast of ordinary people is merely abstaining from food, water, and spouse. However, in the fast of the elite (Khawass), in addition to avoiding things that break the fast (mufattirat), avoiding sins is also necessary. And the fast of the elite of the elite (Khass al-Khass) is, besides avoiding mufattirat and refraining from sins, emptying the heart of everything but Allah. [540] * Fasting makes man resemble the angels, who are far removed from eating, drinking, and lust. [541] * The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever fasts the month of Ramadan for the sake of Allah, all his sins will be forgiven. [542] Just as it is mentioned in a Hadith Qudsi (Sacred Tradition) that Allah says: "الصوم لى و اَنَا اَجزى به" (Fasting is for Me, and I Myself will reward it). [543] * The importance of fasting is such that in narrations, the reward for many acts of worship has been considered equal to the reward for fasting. [544] * Although fasting was obligatory upon previous nations as well, the fast of the month of Ramadan was specific to the Prophets, and in the Islamic Ummah, the fast of Ramadan has been made obligatory upon everyone. [545] * It is narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله that He said: There is a Zakat (purification tax) for everything, and the Zakat of bodies is fasting. [546]

538) كافى، ج ۲، ص ۱۸. 539) تفسیر المنار، ذیل آیه. 540) تفسیر روح البیان. 541) صاحب جواهر، نقل از آیةاللَّه جوادى. 542) و ۴. تفسیر مراغى، ج ۲، ص ۶۹. 543) 544) كافى، ج ۲، ص ۱۰۰. 545) تفسیر نورالثقلین، ج ۱، ص ۱۳۶. 546) بحار، ج ۶۹، ص ۳۸۰ .

Messages

1- A beautiful address is a step toward making the message effective. "یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا" (O you who have believed).

We read in a narration from Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan: The pleasure of the address "یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا" eases the difficulty of fasting. If parents want their children to listen to their words, they must call them beautifully.

2- One of the methods of propagation is to make difficult commands appear easy. This verse states: The command for fasting is not exclusive to you Muslims; this law existed in previous nations as well. And acting upon a command that was for all nations is easier than a command that is only for one group. "كما كُتب على الّذین من قبلكم" (as it was decreed upon those before you).

3- The Quran has explained the philosophy behind certain rulings, such as fasting. This is because if people know the result of an action, they show greater enthusiasm in performing it. "لعلّكم تتّقون" (so that you may become righteous/pious).

Ayah

أَيَّامًا مَّعْدُودَٰتٍ ۚ فَمَن كَانَ مِنكُم مَّرِيضًا أَوْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ فَعِدَّةٌ مِّنْ أَيَّامٍ أُخَرَ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يُطِيقُونَهُۥ فِدْيَةٌ طَعَامُ مِسْكِينٍ ۖ فَمَن تَطَوَّعَ خَيْرًا فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّهُۥ ۚ وَأَن تَصُومُوا۟ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۱۸٤)
That for known days. But should any of you be sick or on a journey, let it be a [similar] number of other days. Those who find it straining shall be liable to atonement by feeding a needy person. Should anyone do good of his own accord, that is better for him, and to fast is better for you, should you know. (184)

Key Points

One of the meanings of the Bāb If'āl (Form IV) in the Arabic language is negation (or removal). For example, the word 'ujmah means speech impediment (or inarticulateness). When it goes into the Bāb If'āl and becomes i'jām, it means the removal of the speech impediment. In this verse, too, the word yuṭīqūnahu means the negation of strength and ability.

Submission to the command of Allah is a virtue. If He commands fasting, one must fast, and if He decrees breaking the fast (ifṭār), one must break the fast. It is mentioned in Majma' al-Bayān: A group of the Prophet's companions fasted even while traveling and were unwilling to break their fast. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله declared them sinners. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If someone fasts while traveling, I will not perform the funeral prayer over his body. It is also mentioned in Tafsir al-Qurṭubī that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, while traveling from Medina to Mecca during Ramadan, asked for water. He then held the vessel of water high so that the people could see, and then He drank from the water. In any case, if a traveler or a sick person fasts, his fast is void and he must make up for it (qaḍā’). [547] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Even if a mother is concerned about the milk for her child or the fetus, she must break her fast, and this is a sign of Allah's compassion. [548]

547) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 164. 548) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 182.

Messages

1- Islam has an appropriate law for every individual under every condition. In this verse, the ruling for travelers, the sick, and the elderly is stated. «فمن كان منكم مریضاً او على سفر»

2- Conditions should not entirely negate the overall philosophy, effects, and benefits of the ruling. If a sick person or a traveler cannot fast under certain conditions, he must make it up (qaḍā’) on other days so that he may benefit from the advantages of fasting. «فعدة من ایّام اُخر»

3- The intention to travel alone does not prevent fasting; being on the journey is necessary. «على سفر»

4- Making up the fast (qaḍā’) does not have a specific time. «فعدة من ایام اُخر»

5- Ability is a condition for religious obligation (taklīf). «على الّذین یطیقونه»

6- Feeding the poor is embedded within the core of the religious rulings. «طعام مسكین»

7- Acts of worship that lead to growth and proximity [to Allah] are those performed with interest and willingness. «فمن تطوّع»

8- Divine commands are structured such that the minimum is obligatory for everyone, and anything beyond that is left to human choice. In this verse, feeding one hungry person is obligatory, but more than that is left to the individual as a recommended act (mustaḥabb). «فمن تطوع خیراً فهم خیر له»

9- Performing Allah's commands has good effects that return to the person himself, not to Allah. «ان تصوموا خیر لكم»

Ayah

شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ فِيهِ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ هُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَـٰتٍ مِّنَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَٱلْفُرْقَانِ ۚ فَمَن شَهِدَ مِنكُمُ ٱلشَّهْرَ فَلْيَصُمْهُ ۖ وَمَن كَانَ مَرِيضًا أَوْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ فَعِدَّةٌ مِّنْ أَيَّامٍ أُخَرَ ۗ يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ بِكُمُ ٱلْيُسْرَ وَلَا يُرِيدُ بِكُمُ ٱلْعُسْرَ وَلِتُكْمِلُوا۟ ٱلْعِدَّةَ وَلِتُكَبِّرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ مَا هَدَىٰكُمْ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۱۸٥)
The month of Ramadan is one in which the Quran was sent down as guidance to mankind, with manifest proofs of guidance and the Criterion. So let those of you who witness it fast [in] it, and as for someone who is sick or on a journey, let it be a [similar] number of other days. Allah desires ease for you, and He does not desire hardship for you, and so that you may complete the number and magnify Allah for guiding you, and that you may give thanks. (185)

Key Points

"Ramadan" (رمضان) comes from the root "Ramad" (رَمض), meaning burning. However, it is a burning that does not produce smoke or ash. The reason for naming this month is that during the month of Ramadan, human sins are burned away.

The month of Ramadan is the month of the revelation of the Quran, and it is the only month whose name is mentioned in the Quran. Laylat al-Qadr (the Night of Decree) is also in this month. In Tafsir Burhan, it is narrated from the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that He said: All heavenly books were revealed in the month of Ramadan. The month of Ramadan is the best month of Allah. On the last Friday of the month of Sha'ban, the Prophet delivered a detailed sermon on the greatness of Ramadan, which is recorded in some commentaries and hadith collections. [549] Furthermore, in Sahifa Sajjadiyya, Imam Sajjad عليه السلام has a heart-rending supplication for bidding farewell to the month of Ramadan.

Islam is an easy religion, and its foundation is based on ease and lack of hardship. Whoever is sick or traveling should not fast and should make up for it (Qada). If performing Wudu (ablution) is difficult, Tayammum (dry ablution) replaces it. If standing during prayer is difficult, it permits praying while sitting. This rule is famous in jurisprudence as the "Rule of La Haraj" (No Hardship).

Ramadan: The Month of Allah's Feast

In Ramadan, believers are invited to the feast of Allah with the card: «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا كُتب علیكم الصّیام» (O you who have believed, fasting has been prescribed for you). This feast has several characteristics:

1- The Host is Allah, and He has personally invited the guests.

2- The means of reception are Laylat al-Qadr, the revelation of the Quran, the descent of the angels, the answering of prayers, the refinement of the soul, and distance from Hellfire.

3- The time of reception is the month of Ramadan, which, according to narrations, its beginning is mercy, its middle is forgiveness, and its end is reward.

4- The manner of reception is such that during Laylat al-Qadr, the needs of the guests for an entire year are provided, and the earth is adorned by the descent of the angels on the Night of Decree.

5- The food of this month is the food of the soul, which is necessary for spiritual growth, not the food of the body. The grace of the food of this feast is the verses of the Quran, such that reciting one verse of it in Ramadan is like reciting the entire Quran in other months.

This feast has no resemblance to worldly feasts. Allah, the All-Knowing, the Rich, the Creator, the Everlasting, the Mighty, and the Majestic, becomes the Host for ignorant, poor, mortal, created, and humble human beings, and He says: I will answer your prayers, and for every breath you take in the month of Ramadan, I will grant the reward of glorification (Tasbih). [550]

Etiquette of the Feast

In Wasā'il al-Shīʿah [551], concerning the ethics of the fasting person, we read in a detailed narration: The fasting person should avoid lying, sin, argumentation, envy, backbiting, opposing the truth, obscenity, blame and anger, taunting, oppression and harming people, negligence, associating with the corrupt, tale-bearing, and consuming unlawful earnings. He should pay special attention to prayer, patience, truthfulness, and the remembrance of the Day of Judgment.

The condition for attending this feast is not merely enduring hunger. It is stated in a hadith: The fast of the one who refuses to obey the heavenly leaders, or who is ill-mannered and unkind toward his spouse in family and personal matters, or who refrains from fulfilling her legitimate needs, or whose parents are displeased with him, is not accepted, and he has not fulfilled the conditions of this banquet.

Although fasting has medical benefits, such as the elimination and removal of excess bodily waste due to hunger, the early rising (Sahar), the refinement of the soul, and the answering of prayers in the month of Ramadan are something else entirely. The truly deprived person is the one who is denied all this goodness and blessing.

549) Bihar, Vol. 96, p. 356.

550) The sermon of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله on the last Friday of the month of Sha'ban.

551) Wasā'il, Vol. 7, p. 119.

Messages

1- The value of Ramadan lies in the revelation of the Quran. The value of human beings can also be measured by the extent to which the Quran has penetrated them. «الّذى اُنزل فیه القرآن»

2- Guidance has stages: one stage is general; «هدى للناس» (guidance for mankind), and one stage is specific. «وبیّنات من الهدى» (and clear proofs of guidance)

3- The obligation of fasting is after certainty regarding the sighting of the month of Ramadan. «فمن شهد منكم فلیصمه»

4- Making up the fast (Qada) is obligatory for the sick and the traveler. «فعدة من ایام اُخر»

5- The make-up fast (Qada) is not restricted to a specific time. «ایّام اُخر»

6- The rulings of Allah are based on ease and correspond to human capacity. «یرید بكم الیسر»

7- Hardship and difficulty remove obligations from a person. «لایرید بكم العسر»

8- The make-up fast (Qada) must be equal to the number of days for which one had an excuse. «لتكملوا العدة»

9- Guidance and the success (Tawfiq) to perform acts of worship come from Allah. Takbir (saying Allahu Akbar) is a sign of glorifying Allah and not paying attention to oneself or others. «لتكبّروا اللَّه على ما هداكم»

10- Fasting prepares the ground for human guidance and gratitude. «لتكبّروا اللَّه على ما هداكم و لعلّكم تشكرون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِى عَنِّى فَإِنِّى قَرِيبٌ ۖ أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ ٱلدَّاعِ إِذَا دَعَانِ ۖ فَلْيَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لِى وَلْيُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِى لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْشُدُونَ (۱۸٦)
When My servants ask you about Me, [tell them that] I am indeed nearmost. I answer the supplicant’s call when he calls Me. So let them respond to Me, and let them have faith in Me, so that they may fare rightly. (186)

Key Points

Some people used to ask the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: How should we call upon Allah? Is Allah close to us so that we should call upon Him softly, or is He far away so that we should call out loudly?! This Ayah was revealed in response to them.

The supplicant is so beloved by the Lord that in this Ayah, Allah uses the term "My own" (or "I Myself") seven times to show His favor toward him: If My servants ask you concerning Me, tell them: I Myself am near to them, and whenever they call upon Me, I Myself answer their prayers, so let them believe in Me and respond to My call. This loving relationship exists when a person wishes to commune privately with Allah.

Supplication (Du'a) is aligning and harmonizing oneself with the entire universe. According to the verses of the Quran, the entire universe is engaged in glorification (Tasbih) and devotion (Qunut); «كلّ له قانتون» (552) and all beings express their need before Him; «یَسئله مَن فى السموات والارض» (553). Therefore, we too should ask Him so that we are not an incongruous patch upon existence.

The Quran offers several recommendations regarding supplication, including:

1- Supplication and request must be sincere. «فادعوا اللّه مخلصین له الدین» (554)

2- It must be accompanied by fear and hope. «وادعوه خوفاً وطمعاً» (555)

3- It must be combined with love, desire, and fear. «یدعوننا رغباً و رهباً» (556)

4- It should be done humbly and in secret. «ادعوا ربكم تضرعاً و خفیة» (557)

5 - It should be accompanied by a hidden call and invocation. «اذ نادى ربّه نداء خفیاً» (558)

In Usul al-Kafi, hundreds of narrations have been cited regarding the importance, role, and etiquette of supplication, paying attention, persistence, mentioning needs during prayer, collective supplication, and belief in its acceptance.

Question (559): Why are our prayers sometimes not answered?

Answer: The non-acceptance of our prayers is due to our polytheism (Shirk) or our ignorance. We read in Tafsir al-Mizan that Allah says in this Ayah: «اجیب دعوة الداع اذا دعان» (I Myself answer the call of the caller when he calls upon Me) – meaning, the one who calls upon Me alone and sincerely asks Me for good. Therefore, if a prayer is not answered, it is either because we have not asked Allah for something good (Khayr), and it was actually harmful (Sharr) for us; or, if it truly was good, we did not request it from Allah sincerely and honestly, and it was accompanied by seeking help from others. Or, it may be that the acceptance of our request is not in our best interest (Maslahah), in which case, according to the narrations, a calamity is averted from us instead, or it is stored up for our future or our progeny, or it is compensated for in the Hereafter.

We read in Usul al-Kafi: The prayer of one who consumes unlawful food, or fails to enjoin good and forbid evil, or supplicates out of negligence and indifference, will not be answered.

The meaning of supplication is not the abandonment of work and effort, but rather reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) combined with effort. Hence, we read in a narration: The prayer of the idle person is not answered.

Perhaps the placement of the Ayah concerning supplication amidst the verses of fasting is due to the greater suitability that the Month of Allah (Ramadan) has for prayer.

Question: Given that Allah’s actions are governed by laws and based on fixed factors and traditions (Sunan), what role does supplication play?

Answer: Just as the ruling regarding the prayer and fasting of a traveler differs from that of a resident, the supplicant differs from the person heedless of Allah, and Allah’s tradition (Sunnah) is to favor the former, not the latter. Yes, supplication and conversation with Allah increase a person's capacity to receive Divine favors. Similarly, seeking intercession (Tawassul) and visiting the shrines of the friends of Allah change a person's condition. For instance, if a child goes to a party accompanied by his father, the affection he receives will be greater than if he went alone. Therefore, supplication, visitation, and Tawassul cause a change in conditions, not the disruption of definite Divine traditions.

Regarding Allah's closeness to man, Fayd Kashani قدس سره says:

I asked: Why is Your beautiful face hidden from us? He said: You yourself are the veil, otherwise My face is manifest. I asked: How long will this separation last? He said: As long as you exist (as an independent self). I said: This is the very breath (of life). He said: The word remains the same.

(552) Baqarah, 116.

(553) Ar-Rahman, 29.

(554) Ghafir, 14.

(555) A'raf, 56.

(556) Anbiya, 90.

(557) A'raf, 55.

(558) Maryam, 3.

(559) Kafi, Vol. 2, Kitab ad-Du'a.

Messages

1- Supplication is beneficial wherever and whenever it occurs, because Allah says: I am near. «فانّى قریب» (560)

2- Allah is near to us, but what about us? If His wrath sometimes overtakes us, it is due to our distance from Allah, which results from sins. «فانّى قریب»

3- Allah’s acceptance (of prayer) is continuous, not seasonal. «اُجیب» (I answer) is a sign of permanence.

4- Although Allah knows everything, supplication is our duty. «فلیستجیبوا لى»

5- Supplication reaches acceptance when it is accompanied by faith. «فلیؤمنوا بى»

6- Supplication is a means of growth and guidance. «لعلّهم یرشدون»

(560) Whatever has been mentioned regarding specific times or sacred places for supplication is for merit (Fadilah), not for restriction to those days or exclusivity to those places.

Ayah

أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ لَيْلَةَ ٱلصِّيَامِ ٱلرَّفَثُ إِلَىٰ نِسَآئِكُمْ ۚ هُنَّ لِبَاسٌ لَّكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ لِبَاسٌ لَّهُنَّ ۗ عَلِمَ ٱللَّهُ أَنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ تَخْتَانُونَ أَنفُسَكُمْ فَتَابَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَعَفَا عَنكُمْ ۖ فَٱلْـَٔـٰنَ بَـٰشِرُوهُنَّ وَٱبْتَغُوا۟ مَا كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۚ وَكُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمُ ٱلْخَيْطُ ٱلْأَبْيَضُ مِنَ ٱلْخَيْطِ ٱلْأَسْوَدِ مِنَ ٱلْفَجْرِ ۖ ثُمَّ أَتِمُّوا۟ ٱلصِّيَامَ إِلَى ٱلَّيْلِ ۚ وَلَا تُبَـٰشِرُوهُنَّ وَأَنتُمْ عَـٰكِفُونَ فِى ٱلْمَسَـٰجِدِ ۗ تِلْكَ حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ فَلَا تَقْرَبُوهَا ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ (۱۸۷)
You are permitted on the night of the fast to go into your wives: they are a garment for you, and you are a garment for them. Allah knew that you would betray yourselves, so He pardoned you and excused you. So now consort with them and seek what Allah has ordained for you, and eat and drink until the white streak becomes manifest to you from the dark streak at the crack of dawn. Then complete the fast until nightfall, and do not consort with them while you dwell in confinement in the mosques. These are Allah’s bounds, so do not approach them. Thus does Allah clarify His signs for mankind so that they may be Godwary. (187)

Key Points

In the early days of Islam, sexual intercourse with one's spouse was forbidden during the day and night of Ramadan. Furthermore, they could only break their fast at a specific time during the night, and if they fell asleep, they were not allowed to eat after waking up. Some Muslims engaged in sexual intercourse despite the prohibition, while others, despite the difficulty and hardship, refrained from eating at night after waking up. One of the companions of the Prophet, named Mut'im ibn Jubayr, fell asleep at the time of Iftar because his food was delayed. When He woke up, He said: "I no longer have the right to eat," and He fasted the next day without food. He then went to work digging the trench (around Medina). During the work, He fainted due to extreme weakness and lost consciousness. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was deeply affected, and this Ayah was revealed, stating that eating is permissible until the break of dawn, and sexual intercourse with spouses during the nights of Ramadan is not prohibited. Almighty Allah knows that you were betraying yourselves and that you did not and do not have the power of patience and self-restraint. For this reason, He made the obligation easy for you and pardoned your past actions.

I'tikaf (spiritual retreat) means cutting oneself off from the world and seeking refuge in Allah. The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, despite the difficulties and problems He faced, would sometimes perform I'tikaf in the mosque for several days, thereby purifying His soul and granting it greater tranquility. Since sexual intercourse is forbidden in the mosque even outside the state of I'tikaf, it seems that the intent of the word "عاكفون" (those who are devoted/staying) is simply residing in the mosque, not I'tikaf in the sense of a specific form of worship.

Many points and subtleties are hidden in the analogy of the spouse to a garment:

  • A garment must be suitable for a person in design, color, and material; likewise, a spouse must be compatible (kufw) with the person and suitable for their thoughts, culture, and personality.
  • A garment is a source of adornment and comfort; a spouse and children are likewise a source of adornment and comfort for the family.
  • A garment covers a person's flaws; similarly, both the man and the woman must cover each other's flaws and shortcomings.
  • A garment protects a person from cold and heat; the presence of a spouse also warms the family nucleus and saves life from coldness.
  • Being without a garment leads to disgrace; being without marriage and a spouse also leads to deviation and disgrace for a person.
  • Thick clothing is used in cold weather, and thin clothing in hot weather; similarly, each spouse must adjust their character and behavior according to the emotional needs of the other party; if the husband is angry, the wife should treat him gently, and if the wife is tired, the husband should be patient with her.
  • A person must protect their garment from contamination; similarly, each spouse must protect the other from becoming contaminated by sin.

Messages

1- Leniency and ease in religious rulings are characteristics of the religion of Islam. «اُحلّ لكم»

2- Observing politeness in speech is a characteristic of the Quran. "رفث" (Rafath) means speaking about marital issues, which is used in the Ayah as a euphemism for sexual intercourse.

3- Wherever you intend to close one path, leave a legitimate path open. Since Allah prohibits sexual intercourse during the day of Ramadan, He permits it at night. [561] «اُحلّ لكم لیلة الصیام الرفث»

4- Worship during the day and legitimate pleasure at night are signs of the comprehensiveness of the religion. Both the rulings of fasting and marital issues are mentioned in one Ayah. «احلّ لكم لیلة الصیام الرفث الى نسائكم»

5- Islam pays full attention to natural needs. «الرفث الى نسائكم»

6- The need of men and women for each other is mutual, and each needs the other to cover their natural needs. «هنّ لباس لكم و انتم لباس لهنّ»

7- Allah is aware of the details of human actions. «علم اللَّه انكم»

8- Man is prone to error, and the sexual instinct in him is strong. «كنتم تختانون انفسكم»

9- Abandoning the command of Allah is a betrayal and an injustice to ourselves. «تختانون انفسكم»

10- In sexual intercourse and seeking children, do not prescribe a duty for Allah regarding whether the child should be a boy or a girl. «و ابتغوا ما كتب اللَّه لكم»

11- Even sexual intercourse must be purposeful. «وابتغوا ما كتب اللَّه لكم»

12- The criteria for Islamic rulings are general, natural, and simple standards. Anyone, wherever they are, can discern the appearance of the white thread of dawn or the darkness of night. «یتبیّن لكم الخیط الابیض من الخیط الاسود»

13- We must not forget the role of timing in acts of worship. «اتموا الصیام الى اللیل»

14- I'tikaf is residing in the mosque, conditional upon fasting. «عاكفون فى المساجد»

15- Prevention of sin is necessary. The Quran says: Do not approach sin. Because approaching sin is the same as falling into it. «فلا تقربوها»

16- Piety (Taqwa) is the philosophy behind divine rulings. Fasting is for Taqwa, and legitimate sexual intercourse is also for Taqwa. [562] «لعلهم یتقون»

17- Acting upon divine commands is the foundation for growth and piety. «لعلهم یتقون»

Ayah

وَلَا تَأْكُلُوٓا۟ أَمْوَٰلَكُم بَيْنَكُم بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ وَتُدْلُوا۟ بِهَآ إِلَى ٱلْحُكَّامِ لِتَأْكُلُوا۟ فَرِيقًا مِّنْ أَمْوَٰلِ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلْإِثْمِ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۱۸۸)
Do not eat up your wealth among yourselves wrongfully, nor proffer it to the judges in order to eat up a part of the people’s wealth sinfully, while you know [that it is immoral to do so]. (188)

Key Points

Tudlu (تدلوا) means lowering a bucket (dalu) into a well. In this Ayah, giving a bribe to a judge is likened to this action. The intent of amwāl an-nās (اموال النّاس) includes both public and private property. Furthermore, bribery is forbidden even for obtaining the non-financial rights of the people.

Bribery is one of the major sins that leads to numerous social corruptions, including: the elimination of justice, the despair and hopelessness of the weak, the audacity and boldness of the powerful, the corruption and depravity of the ruler and the judge, and the destruction of public trust.

Given these negative effects and consequences, this act has been severely criticized in narrations. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله addressed Imam Ali عليه السلام, saying: «یا على ثمن المیتة والكلب والخمر و مهر الزانیة والرشوة فى الحكم» (563). O Ali! The income derived from selling carrion, dogs, wine, adultery, and bribery in judgment is the same and is forbidden (Haram).

Likewise, Ali عليه السلام, regarding the noble Ayah «اكّالون للسّحت» (eaters of forbidden gain) (564), said: Bribe-takers are those who solve people's problems and, in return, accept their gifts (565).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام considers bribery to be tantamount to disbelief in Allah (Kufr). And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله cursed the briber, the recipient, and the intermediary, and said: The scent of Paradise will not reach the owner of the bribe.

In another Hadith, we read: Any ruler who is indifferent to the distresses of the people, Allah conceals His favor from him. If he accepts a gift to carry out the people's affairs, he is in chains, and if he takes a bribe, he is a polytheist (Mushrik) (566).

Imam Ali عليه السلام considers the bribe-taker to be deprived of the right to authority (Wilayah) (567). And elsewhere, that Imam said: No group became afflicted with bribery except that they became afflicted with fear, anxiety, and worry! (568)

Some people give different names to bribery to justify their transgression, including: gift (Hadiyah), present (Tuhfah), service fee (Haqq al-Zahmah), congratulatory money (Chashm Rowshani), and so on.

A person named "Ash'ath ibn Qays" brought a sweet dish (Halwa) to the door of Ali عليه السلام as a gift, hoping that the Imam might rule in his favor in court. The Imam said: By Allah! If the seven climes were granted to me so that I might unjustly take a barley husk from the mouth of an ant, I would not do it (569).

A person said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: I am in charge and responsible for a certain task, and people bring me gifts. How is this? The Prophet replied: What is wrong with our officials speaking of gifts! If they had stayed at home, would anyone have given them a gift?! (570)

(563) Bihar, Vol. 77, p. 54. (564) Ma'idah, 42. (565) Bihar, Vol. 104, p. 273. (566) Wasā'il, Vol. 12, p. 63. (567) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 131. (568) Nahj al-Fasahah, H. 2693. (569) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 224. (570) Nahj al-Fasahah, H. 539.

Messages

1- Ownership must be acquired through legitimate means, such as: acquisition (Hiyāzah), trade, agriculture, industry, inheritance, gift, and the like. However, possession through falsehood (Bāṭil) and bribery does not establish ownership. «لا تاكلوا اموال الناس بالباطل»

2- Society is considered a single body. «بینكم، اموالكم»

3- Bribery is forbidden (Haram), and a person has no right to offer a bribe to seize the property of others. «تدلوا بها الى الحكام لتاكوا فریقاً من اموال الناس»

4- Islam recognizes people as the owners of their property. «اموال الناس»

5- Deliberate transgressions are dangerous. «وانتم تعلمون»

Ayah

۞ يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْأَهِلَّةِ ۖ قُلْ هِىَ مَوَٰقِيتُ لِلنَّاسِ وَٱلْحَجِّ ۗ وَلَيْسَ ٱلْبِرُّ بِأَن تَأْتُوا۟ ٱلْبُيُوتَ مِن ظُهُورِهَا وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلْبِرَّ مَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰ ۗ وَأْتُوا۟ ٱلْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوَٰبِهَا ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (۱۸۹)
They question you concerning the new moons. Say, ‘They are timekeeping signs for the people and [for the sake of] hajj.’ It is not piety that you enter houses from their rear; rather, piety is [personified by] one who is Godwary, and enter houses from their doors and be wary of Allah, so that you may be felicitous. (189)

Key Points

The word Mawāqīt (مواقیت) is the plural of Mīqāt (میقات), which refers to a specific time or place designated for the performance of a certain act. This āyah, while answering the question of a group of people who asked the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله about the crescent moon, points to several matters which we will examine together: O Prophet! Tell those who ask about the benefit of the moon's various phases: The changes that occur in the moon are a means for people to keep time and serve as a universal and natural calendar. One of the advantages of Islam is that it designs its programs using natural, universal, and free scales. For example, to determine the volume of Karr water (a specific measure), it uses the span of a hand (wajb) as the scale, or to determine the times of prayer, it refers to the sunrise, sunset, and zenith of the sun. And for the beginning and end of the month of Ramadan or the days of Hajj, it has made the crescent moon the criterion. The various shapes of the moon also serve as a type of universal calendar available to everyone. The need for a calendar and dating system is essential for social life, and the best universal means for this calendar is that people, whether literate or illiterate, anywhere in the world, can determine the days of the month with a single glance and in an instant.

Then He says: Righteousness is not in entering the house from the back or through a detour instead of using the door while in the state of Ihrām (consecration). This belief that one must enter houses from the back while in Ihrām is nothing more than a superstition. Every task has a way, and one must enter through its natural channel and path. To perform any task, the appropriate time, the appropriate method, and the appropriate leader are necessary. Just as the time is determined by Allah through the moon and the crescent, leadership must also be appointed by Allah. Therefore, the meaning of the āyah is this: Use the crescent moon for timekeeping, and in the method of performing tasks, refer to the divine instructions and the leaders, and do not take detours. Happiness and felicity have a path that must be entered through its proper way, and fear Allah in choosing the time, the method, and the leader, so that you may succeed.

Imam Sādiq عليه السلام said: The intent of the āyah "وَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوَابِهَا" (And enter houses through their doors) is that every task should be entered through its proper way. (571) And the infallible Imams علیهم السلام said: We, the Family of Muhammad, are the door of Allah. And the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: I am the city of knowledge, and Ali is its gate. (572)

In this āyah, the phrase "وَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوَابِهَا" is placed between two mentions of Taqwā (piety). Perhaps this is a sign that recognizing the proper procedure and the entry and exit points in affairs requires extraordinary piety, and those who have little piety will still go astray.

Perhaps the meaning of the āyah is this: Tell the people who ask you about the crescent moon an answer commensurate with their understanding—that this crescent is a way to know time and organize religious affairs. But if you want to understand the nature of the moon's changes, you must enter through its proper way, study, and learn. The Quran expressed this point in the framework that righteousness and goodness are not about taking detours; rather, every task must be entered through its proper way, and the way to understand atmospheric changes is through education in that field.

(571) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 190. (572) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān, Vol. 2, p. 509.

Messages

1- The movement of the celestial bodies and the moon has a precise schedule and timing that can be effective in the schedule of worship and the organization of people's lives. (573) "قُلْ هِيَ مَوَاقِيتُ لِلنَّاسِ"

2- If inanimate objects and celestial bodies have order and are oriented toward organizing human affairs, it is not fair that we should be disorganized, unaccountable, and unrestrained beings. "قُلْ هِيَ مَوَاقِيتُ لِلنَّاسِ"

3- When answering questions, provide an answer that the questioner understands and that meets his need. "قُلْ هِيَ مَوَاقِيتُ لِلنَّاسِ"

4- Islam combats superstitious and ignorant customs. "لَيْسَ الْبِرُّ بِأَنْ تَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ ظُهُورِهَا"

5- Righteousness (Birr) and goodness should not be derived from superstitions, customs, or the ways of ancestors; rather, the way to recognize righteousness is within the framework of divine command (Wahy), the infallible leaders علیهم السلام, and logical methods. "لَيْسَ الْبِرُّ... وَلَكِنَّ الْبِرَّ"

6- Whenever you close a path, show the correct path. "لَيْسَ الْبِرُّ... وَلَكِنَّ الْبِرَّ"

7- The value of religious concepts is realized when they manifest in individuals. Instead of saying: Righteousness is piety, He said: Righteousness is the pious person. "وَلَكِنَّ الْبِرَّ مَنِ اتَّقَىٰ"

8- Taking a detour is not piety. If we disobey the way of the wise and the saints of Allah and call it piety and self-purification, we have deviated. "وَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوَابِهَا وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ"

9- Employing incorrect and illogical methods to perform tasks is contrary to piety. "وَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوَابِهَا وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ"

10- Preserving time and observing order in tasks and worship are among the manifestations of piety (Taqwā) and success (Falāh). "مَوَاقِيتُ لِلنَّاسِ... لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ"

11- Felicity (salvation/success) is the ultimate stage of perfection. Many instructions are aimed at achieving piety, but in this āyah, piety is the prerequisite for felicity. "اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ"

(573) Imam Sādiq عليه السلام, in interpreting this āyah, said: Whenever you see the crescent of the month of Ramadan, fast, and at the end, as soon as you see the crescent of the month of Shawwāl, celebrate Eid al-Fitr. Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 176.

Ayah

وَقَـٰتِلُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِينَ يُقَـٰتِلُونَكُمْ وَلَا تَعْتَدُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ (۱۹٠)
Fight in the way of Allah those who fight you, but do not transgress. Indeed Allah does not like transgressors. (190)

Key Points

This Ayah, while affirming the necessity of defense and confrontation against the aggression of others, reminds us that even on the battlefield, one must not transgress the divine limits and boundaries, and must not attack the sick, women, children, and the elderly who are not involved in fighting you. Do not take up arms before inviting them to Islam, do not initiate the war, and observe human regulations and emotions even during warfare.

Some characteristics lose their value under different circumstances; for example, compassion is a virtue, but compassion shown to a sharp-toothed leopard is tyranny against the sheep. Or knowledge is a virtue, but sometimes not knowing becomes a virtue. For example, on the night when Hadrat Ali عليه السلام slept in the bed of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in place of the Prophet, and the Prophet of God migrated, the value of this sacrifice lies in Hadrat Ali not knowing whether he would be killed or not. If he knew that they would not kill him, ordinary people would also be willing to perform such an act. In any case, generosity and bravery might become low-value, high-value, or worthless under certain conditions, but the only characteristic that never loses its value is justice, which is a virtue in every time and place, and towards every individual, whether friend or foe.

In Islam, stages have been defined for dealing with the enemy: 1- Indifference. «دع أذاهم» (574) 2- Turning away. «فاعرض عنهم» (575) 3- Severity. «واغلظ علیهم» (576) 4- War. «فقاتلوا الّذین یقاتلونكم» (577)

The war of the Muslims is for the defense of themselves and the religion of Allah, but the enemy's goal in war is to extinguish the light of God: «لیطفؤا نور اللَّه بافواههم» (578) and to force the Muslims into submission. «لن ترضى عنك الیهود و لا النصارى حتّى تتّبع ملتهم» (579)

574) Ahzab, 48. 575) Nisa, 63. 576) Tawbah, 73. 577) Baqarah, 190. 578) Saff, 8. 579) Baqarah, 120.

Messages

1- Defense and retaliation are among human rights. If someone fights us, we fight him too. «قاتلوا... الذین یقاتلونكم»

2- The goal of war in Islam is not to seize land and water, or colonization and revenge; rather, the goal is the defense of truth by eliminating corrupt elements, liberating thoughts, and saving humanity from superstitions and delusions. «فى سبیل اللَّه»

3- Even in war, justice and right must be observed. «قاتلوا... لاتعتدوا» The Quran has repeatedly commanded, using the phrase «لاتعتدوا» (do not transgress), that boundaries and limits must not be crossed when executing any command.

4- Oppression and tyranny sever the bond of divine love. «ان اللَّه لا یحبّ المعتدین»

5- Mere presence on the front line does not guarantee proximity to Allah; rather, popularity with the Lord is achieved when we are just in war and observe rights. «لا تعتدوا ان اللَّه لا یحبّ المعتدین»

6- In war, the goal must be Allah alone, not desires, prejudices, spoils, hypocrisy, or seeking fame. «فى سبیل اللَّه»

7- Even when defending your natural right, you must keep Allah in mind. Although they started the war against you and you must defend yourselves, it must still be «فى سبیل اللَّه» (in the way of Allah).

Ayah

وَٱقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ ثَقِفْتُمُوهُمْ وَأَخْرِجُوهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ أَخْرَجُوكُمْ ۚ وَٱلْفِتْنَةُ أَشَدُّ مِنَ ٱلْقَتْلِ ۚ وَلَا تُقَـٰتِلُوهُمْ عِندَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ حَتَّىٰ يُقَـٰتِلُوكُمْ فِيهِ ۖ فَإِن قَـٰتَلُوكُمْ فَٱقْتُلُوهُمْ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱۹۱)
And kill them wherever you confront them, and expel them from where they expelled you, for persecution is graver than killing. But do not fight them near the Holy Mosque unless they fight you therein; but if they fight you, kill them; such is the requital of the faithless. (191)

Key Points

This Ayah issues the command to kill and expel the polytheists from Mecca, stating the reason that they have tortured and displaced you for years, and torture is harder and more severe than killing. Therefore, you should not show weakness in fighting and warring against them. Question: Why is torture harder than killing? Answer: In killing, a person is separated from this world but reaches the Hereafter. However, in torture, a person neither reaches the Hereafter nor enjoys the benefits of this world.

Messages

1- Retaliation and severity against the enemy are necessary in certain cases. «واقتلوهم»

2- Just defense is not limited only to the battlefield and war. «و اقتلوهم حیث ثقفتموهم»

3- The right to one's homeland is among the human rights accepted by religions. «اخرجوكم»

4- The instigator of discord (Fitnah) is like a combatant (Muharib) and must be punished in the severest manner. «واقتلوهم... اخرجوهم... والفتنة اشد من القتل»

5- The Sanctuary (Haram) and the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) are sacred, but the blood of Muslims is more sacred; and here, the issue of the more important and the important (Ahamm wa Muhimm) is raised. «لا تقاتلوهم عند المسجد الحرام حتّى یقاتلوكم»

6- Just as you should not initiate the war itself, you should also not initiate the violation of sacred things. «حتى یقاتلوكم»

Ayah

فَإِنِ ٱنتَهَوْا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۹۲)
But if they desist, then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. (192)

Key Points

| Regarding what the polytheists must cease doing in order to be forgiven, there are two possibilities: A: War and sedition (or strife). Due to the preceding verses which spoke of war. B: Disbelief (Kufr). Due to the reception of Divine forgiveness, which is exclusive to the believers.

Messages

| 1- Muslims must accept the genuine ceasefire of their enemies. «فان انتهوا» 2- Islam has left the path of return open, even for the disbelievers (Kuffar). «فان انتهوا...» 3- If the disbelievers cease sedition and war, or if they embrace faith, do not reproach them for their previous deeds. «فان اللّه غفور رحیم» 4- Man must himself create the conditions for receiving Divine mercy. «فان انتهوا فان اللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

وَقَـٰتِلُوهُمْ حَتَّىٰ لَا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ وَيَكُونَ ٱلدِّينُ لِلَّهِ ۖ فَإِنِ ٱنتَهَوْا۟ فَلَا عُدْوَٰنَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱۹۳)
Fight them until persecution is no more, and religion becomes [exclusively] for Allah. Then if they desist, there shall be no reprisal except against the wrongdoers. (193)

Key Points

The goal of war in Islam is not territorial expansion, acquisition of spoils, or revenge.
