Rather, the purpose of war and struggle (Jihad) in Islam is the removal of the foundation of polytheism (Shirk) and disbelief (Kufr). Fitnah has been used to mean polytheism, disbelief, trial (testing), and torture. In this Ayah, it states: If the polytheists cease fitnah and fighting (qital), then there will be no further enmity or war against them. This is because military engagement is only against the oppressors and aggressors. The expression ‘udwān (aggression/enmity) is due to their behavior; otherwise, Islam is not characterized by aggression. It is like when we say: the response to evil is evil. Although responding to evil with evil is justice, we call it evil because of the nature of their action.

Messages

1- The goal of war in Islam is the removal of polytheism and fitnah, and the establishment of God's religion and law. «حتى لا تكون فتنة»

2- Purification precedes reconstruction. First, fitnah must be removed so that the Divine religion can be established afterward. First, disbelief in Taghut (tyrants/false deities), then faith in Allah. «حتى لاتكون فتنة و یكون الدین للّه»

3- The path of return and repentance is not closed to anyone under any circumstances. Even a staunch enemy, if he changes his course, Allah will grant him forgiveness. «فان انتهوا فلا عدوان...»

4- In the previous Ayah, Allah's forgiveness regarding the sins of the disbelievers was mentioned: «فانّ اللّه غفور رحیم». In this Ayah, He instructs the people that if the enemy ceases fighting, they too should refrain from war. «فلاعدوان»

Ayah

ٱلشَّهْرُ ٱلْحَرَامُ بِٱلشَّهْرِ ٱلْحَرَامِ وَٱلْحُرُمَـٰتُ قِصَاصٌ ۚ فَمَنِ ٱعْتَدَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَٱعْتَدُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ بِمِثْلِ مَا ٱعْتَدَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۱۹٤)
A sacred month for a sacred month, and all sanctities require retribution. So should anyone aggress against you, assail him in the manner he assailed you, and be wary of Allah, and know that Allah is with the Godwary. (194)

Key Points

Before Islam, among the Arabs, four months each year held special sanctity during which fighting was forbidden and prohibited. Three of these months were consecutive: Dhul-Qa'dah, Dhul-Hijjah, and Muharram, and the fourth was the separate month of Rajab. It has even been said that one of the reasons for naming the month of Dhul-Qa'dah is that during this month, cessation (qu'ūd) from fighting is necessary. Islam accepted this tradition and declared a ceasefire for four months each year. However, the enemy is always lying in wait to exploit opportunities, and they might think to themselves that since Muslims are obligated to observe the ceasefire during these months, they should attack them. This Ayah states: If your opponents attack you during these four months, you must also stand against them and fight them in those same months, setting the sacred month against the sacred month. This is because the sanctity of Muslim blood and the preservation of the Islamic system are greater than the sanctity of these months, and whoever violates sanctities must be subject to retribution (qisas). Then, as a general rule, He states: Whoever transgresses against you, retaliate against him to the same extent. And observe piety (taqwa) and do not exact retribution beyond what is necessary, and know that Allah is with the pious (muttaqin). The author of Majma‘ al-Bayan says regarding this Ayah: In the case of usurpation (ghasb), one can also seek restitution (taqass) by citing this Ayah, meaning taking restitution from the usurper's property.

Messages

1- Not all times are equal; the sanctity of certain months must be preserved. «الشهر الحرام»

2- There is no deadlock in Islam. The preservation of Islam and the lives of Muslims takes precedence over preserving the sanctity of places and times. «فمن اعتدى علیكم فاعتدوا علیه»

3- Islam is not a religion of aggression and transgression, but neither does it accept the aggression and transgression of others. «فمن اعتدى علیكم فاعتدوا علیه»

4- Be just, even when dealing with enemies. «فاعتدوا علیه بمثل ما اعتدى علیكم»

5- The legislative system must be such that it does not lead to the despair of Muslims or the boldness of the disbelievers; the principle of reciprocity (tit-for-tat) is for this reason. «فاعتدوا... بمثل ما اعتدى»

6- Observing piety (taqwa), even during war, is among the educational principles of Islam. «اتّقوا اللّه»

7- If you do not cross the boundary of justice in war, you will also benefit from Divine assistance. And do not imagine that justice and piety will delay your victory. «بمثل ما اعتدى... واتقوا... و اعلموا ان اللَّه مع المتّقین»

Ayah

وَأَنفِقُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تُلْقُوا۟ بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إِلَى ٱلتَّهْلُكَةِ ۛ وَأَحْسِنُوٓا۟ ۛ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۹٥)
Spend in the way of Allah, and do not cast yourselves with your own hands into destruction; and be virtuous. Indeed Allah loves the virtuous. (195)

Key Points

Five consecutive verses called Muslims to defend the religion and confront the disbelievers; this verse concludes that sequence with charity (infaq) and benevolence (ihsan). Suicide and self-harm are definitively forbidden (haram), which is inferred from the verse. However, seeking martyrdom in cases where the foundation of Islam is in danger is not casting oneself into destruction (tahlukah). This verse, alongside the recommendation for charity, restrains Muslims from any form of extravagance or negligence (excess or deficiency). Because if the wealthy are stingy and do not think of the deprived, class differences will expand day by day, becoming a factor in the explosion and destruction of society. Similarly, if moderation in charity is not observed, and a person gives away everything he possesses, he has brought himself and his family to ruin by his own hands. The Quran also recommends moderation in charity to Muslims in other verses, saying to His Prophet: «ولا تجعل یدك مغلولة الى عنقك و لا تبسطها كل البسط» (580) O Prophet! Do not keep your hand tied up so that you give nothing, nor stretch it out completely so that you give away everything you possess at once. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام also states regarding this verse: Your charity should not be to the extent that your own hands become empty and you are led to misery. (581) Man desires to be loved; therefore, the Quran utilizes this innate nature and says: Do good, for the doers of good are loved by Allah.

580) Isra, 29. 581) Bihar, Vol. 93, p. 168.

Messages

1- Economics is the backbone of every movement. «انفقوا...» Jihad is also impossible without financial support and sacrificing certain resources. If people do not utilize their wealth during an enemy attack and in defense of the truth, they will certainly face defeat.

2- Insure yourselves and your wealth through charity. «انفقوا فى سبیل اللّه و لاتلقوا بایدیكم الى التهلكة»

3- In Islam, the front lines, war, and financial aid must all have a divine color and be for the pleasure of Allah. «فى سبیل اللَّه»

4- Becoming beloved by Allah is the best encouragement for benevolence (ihsan). «ان اللّه یحبّ المحسنین»

Ayah

وَأَتِمُّوا۟ ٱلْحَجَّ وَٱلْعُمْرَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ فَإِنْ أُحْصِرْتُمْ فَمَا ٱسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ ٱلْهَدْىِ ۖ وَلَا تَحْلِقُوا۟ رُءُوسَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ ٱلْهَدْىُ مَحِلَّهُۥ ۚ فَمَن كَانَ مِنكُم مَّرِيضًا أَوْ بِهِۦٓ أَذًى مِّن رَّأْسِهِۦ فَفِدْيَةٌ مِّن صِيَامٍ أَوْ صَدَقَةٍ أَوْ نُسُكٍ ۚ فَإِذَآ أَمِنتُمْ فَمَن تَمَتَّعَ بِٱلْعُمْرَةِ إِلَى ٱلْحَجِّ فَمَا ٱسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ ٱلْهَدْىِ ۚ فَمَن لَّمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ ثَلَـٰثَةِ أَيَّامٍ فِى ٱلْحَجِّ وَسَبْعَةٍ إِذَا رَجَعْتُمْ ۗ تِلْكَ عَشَرَةٌ كَامِلَةٌ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَن لَّمْ يَكُنْ أَهْلُهُۥ حَاضِرِى ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (۱۹٦)
Complete the hajj and the ‘umrah for Allah’s sake, and if you are prevented, then [make] such [sacrificial] offering as is feasible. And do not shave your heads until the offering reaches its [assigned] place. But should any of you be sick, or have a hurt in his head, let the atonement be by fasting, or charity, or sacrifice. And when you have security—for those who enjoy [release from the restrictions] by virtue of their ‘umrah until the hajj—let the offering be such as is feasible. As for someone who cannot afford [the offering], let him fast three days during the hajj and seven when you return; that is [a period of] ten complete [days]. That is for someone whose family does not dwell by the Holy Mosque. And be wary of Allah, and know that Allah is severe in retribution. (196)

Key Points

This Ayah contains the general principles and some aspects of the rulings regarding Hajj and Umrah, but their details are found in the narrations (Riwāyāt) and the legal opinions (Fatāwā) of the scholars. "Hajj" refers to rituals whose founder was Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) عليه السلام. It was customary among the Arabs since his time, and by the command of Allah, this program was also ratified in Islam and will continue until the Day of Judgment. "Umrah" means visitation (Ziyārah). Anyone who enters Mecca must go to visit the Ka'bah and perform its circumambulation (Tawāf) while wearing the garments of Iḥrām. Hajj and Umrah are two similar acts, like the Adhān and the Iqāmah, which have slight differences. "Hady" (هَدى) is the name of the sacrificial animal for Hajj. This Ayah mentions Umrah al-Tamattu' alongside Hajj al-Tamattu': «فمن تمتّع بالعمرة الى الحج» (Whoever performs Umrah [to be followed] by Hajj). However, one of the Caliphs prohibited it based on his own Ijtihād (independent reasoning). Of course, this Ijtihād stands in opposition to the explicit command of Allah. The ruling for emergency situations is limited to the time of necessity. Therefore, contrary to normal circumstances where fasting is not permissible during travel, in this journey, if the sacrificial animal is not slaughtered, fasting becomes obligatory. This fasting must take place on the seventh, eighth, and ninth days of the month, because the tenth day is Eid al-Adha (the Festival of Sacrifice), and fasting on that day is forbidden (Ḥarām). The Infallible Imams (علیهم السلام) stated in the interpretation of the phrase «اتمّوا الحج» (Complete the Hajj) that a complete Hajj is one in which there is no foul language, sin, or dispute, and forbidden acts are avoided. (582) And that a person is granted the success (Tawfīq) to meet the Imam of his time. (583)

582) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 181, and Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 193. 583) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 3, p. 183.

Messages

  1. Religious duties must be performed fully and completely. Once the rituals of Hajj have begun, they cannot be left incomplete or unfinished. «اتمّوا» (Complete them).
  2. In the rituals of Hajj, the intention of seeking closeness (Qurbah) and worship is necessary. Hajj cannot be performed with the motivation of tourism or sightseeing. «اتمّوا... للّه» (Complete them... for Allah).
  3. The rulings of Islam are not intended to impose hardship upon people. Completing Hajj and Umrah is not obligatory for someone who is sick internally or fears external danger. «فان اُحصرتم فما استیسر من الهدى» (But if you are prevented, then [send] what is easy of the sacrificial animal).
  4. Location plays a role in acts of worship. «یبلغ الهدى محلّه» (The sacrificial animal reaches its place).
  5. Illness causes a reduction (Takhfīf) in rulings, not their cancellation. Therefore, in Hajj, those who are excused must compensate for their duties through fasting, charity (Ṣadaqah), or sacrifice. «فمن كان منكم مریضاً او... ففدیة من صیام او صدقة او نسك» (And whoever among you is ill or... then [his] compensation is by fasting or charity or sacrifice).
  6. The criterion for the obligation of sacrifice in Hajj is that it be easy and simple. «فما استیسر من الهدى فمن لم یجد فصیام ثلاثة ایام...» (Then [send] what is easy of the sacrificial animal. But whoever cannot find [one] - then fasting for three days...).
  7. The rule of Umrah and Hajj al-Tamattu' is only for pilgrims who are not residents of Mecca; the Hajj of the residents of Mecca is of a different type. «اتموا الحج و العمرة... ذلك لمن لم یكن اهله حاضرى المسجد الحرام» (Complete the Hajj and Umrah... That is for him whose family is not in attendance at the Sacred Mosque).
  8. Attention to Divine punishment prepares the ground for piety (Taqwā), and piety prepares the ground for fulfilling duties. «واتمّوا الحج و العمرة... واتّقوا اللَّه واعلموا انّ اللَّه شدید العقاب» (And complete the Hajj and Umrah... And fear Allah and know that Allah is severe in penalty).
  9. Attention to piety is relevant in the performance of all Divine commands. «اتمّوا الحج... و اتّقوا اللّه» (Complete the Hajj... and fear Allah).
  10. The rituals of Hajj were performed differently before Islam. Therefore, the Quran emphasizes observing these changes. «انّ اللّه شدید العقاب» (Indeed, Allah is severe in penalty).
  11. There is a severe penalty for those who alter the rulings of Hajj. «شدید العقاب» (Severe in penalty).

Ayah

ٱلْحَجُّ أَشْهُرٌ مَّعْلُومَـٰتٌ ۚ فَمَن فَرَضَ فِيهِنَّ ٱلْحَجَّ فَلَا رَفَثَ وَلَا فُسُوقَ وَلَا جِدَالَ فِى ٱلْحَجِّ ۗ وَمَا تَفْعَلُوا۟ مِنْ خَيْرٍ يَعْلَمْهُ ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَتَزَوَّدُوا۟ فَإِنَّ خَيْرَ ٱلزَّادِ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ ۚ وَٱتَّقُونِ يَـٰٓأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۱۹۷)
The hajj [season] is in months well-known; so whoever decides on hajj [pilgrimage] therein, [should know that] there is to be no sexual contact, vicious talk, or disputing during the hajj. And whatever good you do, Allah knows it. And take provision, for indeed the best provision is Godwariness. So be wary of Me, O you who possess intellects! (197)

Key Points

The word "rafath" (رفث) means sexual intercourse, and "fusuq" (فسوق) means lying, insulting, quarreling (jidal), and saying "No, by Allah" (لا واللَّه) and "Yes, by Allah" (بلى واللَّه).

The time mentioned in the Ayah refers to both Hajj and Umrah together. Umrah al-Tamattu' is performed during the three months of Shawwal, Dhu al-Qa'dah, and Dhu al-Hijjah, but the time for Hajj is the month of Dhu al-Hijjah.

Messages

1- Time plays an important role in Islamic acts of worship. «الحج اشهر معلومات»

2- The pure environment and atmosphere of Hajj and Mecca must not be polluted by committing sins and causing bitterness. «لا رفث و لا فسوق و لا جدال فى الحج»

3- Belief and certainty in Allah's awareness is the key to vitality and hope in performing duties. «ما تفعلوا من خیر یعلمه اللَّه»

4- Provisions are necessary for every journey; the best provision for the Hajj journey is piety (Taqwa). «خیر الزّاد التّقوى»

5- Piety is a sign of wisdom. «واتّقون یا اولى الالباب»

Ayah

لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَبْتَغُوا۟ فَضْلًا مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۚ فَإِذَآ أَفَضْتُم مِّنْ عَرَفَـٰتٍ فَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ عِندَ ٱلْمَشْعَرِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۖ وَٱذْكُرُوهُ كَمَا هَدَىٰكُمْ وَإِن كُنتُم مِّن قَبْلِهِۦ لَمِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ (۱۹۸)
There is no sin upon you in seeking your Lord’s bounty [during the hajj season]. Then when you stream out of ‘Arafat remember Allah at the Holy Mash‘ar, and remember Him as He has guided you, and earlier you were indeed among the astray. (198)

Key Points

Arafat is the name of a land 20 kilometers from Mecca where it is obligatory for the pilgrims of the House of Allah to remain from noon on the 9th of Dhul Hijjah until sunset. If they leave before sunset, they must pay a penalty of one camel. The word 'Arafat' comes from ma'rifah (knowledge/recognition), meaning the place of recognition. There, Adam and Eve (peace be upon them) recognized each other and confessed their sin. According to Imam Sadiq and Imam Baqir (peace be upon them), Gabriel told Prophet Abraham in this place: Know and learn your duties. [584] We read in a hadith that Imam Mahdi (peace be upon him) is present in Arafat every year. The land of Arafat holds memories of the lamentations and tears of the friends of Allah. We read in narrations that on the sunset of Arafah, Allah forgives all the sins of the pilgrims to His House. And specific supplications for the Day of Arafah have been narrated from Imam Husayn and Imam Sajjad (peace be upon them). [585] One of the signs of the comprehensiveness of Islam is that alongside the devotional acts of Hajj, it also pays attention to material life and earning a livelihood. Just as in Surah Al-Jumu'ah, alongside the command to participate in Friday prayer, He says: After the prayer is finished, go forth to seek a livelihood. [586] Yes, Hajj is economics, even while being worship. Imam Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: One of the effects of Hajj is the formation of a great gathering of Muslims from different parts of the world, the transfer of various experiences, and the groundwork for commercial and economic exchanges. [587]

584) Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 207. 585) For more details, refer to the book Hajj written by the author. 586) Al-Jumu'ah, 10. 587) Wasā’il, Vol. 8, p. 9.

Messages

  1. Fighting against rigidity (or stagnation) is among the programs of the Quran. A group thought that religion was a one-dimensional matter and Hajj was a dry form of worship; Islam rejects this notion. «لیس علیكم جناح»
  2. Lawful income is Divine favor. The Quran uses the word fadl (favor) instead of the word kasb (earning) to say that lawful income is Divine favor. «فضلا من ربّكم»
  3. Although effort and seeking are from you; tabtaghū (you seek), the provision (rizq) is the favor of Allah. «فضلاً من ربّكم»
  4. Providing provision is among the affairs of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «فضلاً من ربّكم»
  5. In providing provision, the Hand of Allah is open. The word fadl is indefinite (nākirah). «فضلاً من ربّكم»
  6. The performance of acts of worship must be according to instruction. «واذكروه كما هداكم»
  7. Paying attention to Divine success (tawfīq) on the one hand, and one's past mistakes and sins on the other, increases a person's love and attachment to the favors of Allah. «هداكم و ان كنتم من قبله لمن الضالین»
  8. Do not forget your past history; it was He who guided you. «ان كنتم من قبله لمن الضّالین»

Ayah

ثُمَّ أَفِيضُوا۟ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفَاضَ ٱلنَّاسُ وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۹۹)
Then stream out from where the people stream out, and plead to Allah for forgiveness; indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (199)

Key Points

In this Ayah, while commanding collective movement alongside the people, it reminds the people of Mecca not to imagine that because they are the people of the Sanctuary (Haram) or belong to the Quraysh tribe or similar groups, their acts of worship should differ from others. They must set aside this arrogance and be like the general populace. Fundamentally, one of the effects and philosophies of Hajj is the removal of superficial privileges. There, a person gives up his shoes, hat, clothing, spouse, and dwelling, becoming a drop that joins the ocean. In this Ayah, the word "إفاضة" (Ifāḍah) is used twice, and once in the previous Ayah, meaning collective movement. This repetition and insistence indicate that collective movement in performing acts of worship is valuable. Seeking forgiveness (Istighfār) and the remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah are the best deeds in 'Arafāt and Mash'ar. Although the primary condition in these lands is the halt (wuqūf), and no other action is obligatory, the Qur'an in these verses invites us to the remembrance of God, the recollection of our past full of errors and mistakes, and the seeking of forgiveness for our past. How can one sit indifferently in a land where the friends of Allah (Awliyā') have shed tears?

Messages

1- Seeking privilege, under any name or title, is forbidden. «أَفِيضُوا مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفَاضَ النَّاسُ»

2- Collective movement is valuable. «أَفِيضُوا مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفَاضَ النَّاسُ»

3- Never despair; whatever the past may hold, seeking forgiveness (Istighfār) is the solution, for He is Forgiving (Ghafūr) and Merciful (Rahīm). «وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ»

Ayah

فَإِذَا قَضَيْتُم مَّنَـٰسِكَكُمْ فَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَذِكْرِكُمْ ءَابَآءَكُمْ أَوْ أَشَدَّ ذِكْرًا ۗ فَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَمَا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلَـٰقٍ (۲٠٠)
And when you finish your rites, then remember Allah as you would remember your fathers, or with a more ardent remembrance. Among the people there are those who say, ‘Our Lord, give us in this world,’ but for such there is no share in the Hereafter. (200)

Key Points

A group of the people of Mecca, after the completion of the Hajj rituals, would gather in a place and boast and take pride in their fathers and ancestors by mentioning them. The Quran commands that instead of boasting about fathers and ancestors, remember Allah [588] and speak of His blessings and successes, and be more serious in this endeavor. The remembrance of anyone or anything is a sign of its dominance over human thought. Whoever remembers their ancestors and takes pride in them has accepted their thought and culture, and this remembrance can impose the culture of Jahiliyyah (Ignorance) upon society. For this reason, Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) said: I will burn the golden and valuable calf of the Samiri and scatter its ashes into the sea. Because looking at it revives the culture and thought of polytheism (shirk) in man.

588) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan, Vol. 2, p. 529; Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 198.

Messages

1- Directing the use of free time is one of the duties of the educator. «فاذا قضیتم...فاذكروا اللَّه»

2- The remembrance of Allah must be abundant in terms of quantity; «واذكروا اللّه كثیراً» and loving and sincere in terms of quality. «اشدّ ذكراً»

3- Let us not be superficial. The only prayer of some groups, even in the best times and places, is to attain a short material life. «ربّنا اتنا فى الدنیا وما له فى الاخرة من خلاق»

Ayah

وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ (۲٠۱)
And among them there are those who say, ‘Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter, and save us from the punishment of the Fire.’ (201)

Key Points

One way to know people is by becoming familiar with their wishes and prayers (supplications). In the previous Ayah, the request of the first group from Allah was related to the world (dunya) and they paid no attention to its good or evil, but in this Ayah, the request of the second group from Allah is hasanah (goodness) in this world and the Hereafter. From the perspective of the first group, the world is desirable in itself, but from the perspective of the second group, a world is valuable only if it is hasanah (good) and leads to the Hereafter. Narrations have cited examples and instances for the goodness of this world and the Hereafter (589), but hasanah is not limited to just a few examples. In the Glorious Qur'an, the word dunya (world) has been used 115 times, and the word akhirah (Hereafter) has been used 115 times, and this is not a coincidence. Of course, this does not mean that the importance of the world and the Hereafter are both equal. In supplications, general goals should be presented, and the determination of specific instances should be left to Allah. We ask Allah for hasanah (goodness) and felicity, but as for what constitutes our growth and righteousness, we leave that to Allah. Because due to our scientific limitations, lack of awareness of the future, and our own spiritual dimensions, we cannot determine the specific instances. For this reason, it is recommended that you do not ask Allah for the means to an action, but ask for the action itself. Because Allah might realize the action for us through another means that does not occur to our minds or thoughts. For example, ask Allah for the success (Tawfiq) of performing Hajj, but do not say: O God, give me wealth so that I may go to Mecca. Because Allah sometimes arranges the means in a way we had not thought of.

589) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 199.

Messages

1- The world and the Hereafter are not contradictory, provided that a person seeks hasanah (goodness) and virtue. «فى الدنیا حسنة و فى الاخرة حسنة»

2- Not all prosperity is blameworthy; rather, a good life is also desirable. «ربّنا اتنا فى الدنیا حسنة»

3- Hell (Dozakh) has a separate consideration. Although they ask Allah for the goodness of the Hereafter, they pray separately for salvation from the Fire (590). «قِنا عذاب النار»

590) In supplications, there is a strange insistence and repetition for salvation from the Fire. For example, in Du'a al-Mujir, we read approximately seventy times: »اجِرنا مِن النار یا ربّ« O Lord, save us from the Fire of Hell. Or in Du'a Jawshan Kabir, which has one hundred sections, at the end of each section we say: »خَلّصنا من النّار یا ربّ«

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ نَصِيبٌ مِّمَّا كَسَبُوا۟ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۲٠۲)
Such shall partake of what they have earned, and Allah is swift at reckoning. (202)

Key Points

The term "bahra" (share/portion) is called "naṣīb." It is as if a share has been installed (naṣb) for the human being. The portion of the believers is not solely due to the supplication of "Rabbanā ātinā" (Our Lord, grant us), but also due to their effort and acquisition (kasb). And this warns us that the favors of Allah reach those who, alongside supplication (du'ā), also exert effort and strive.

Messages

1- Man's portion in the Hereafter is only from some of his deeds, not all of them. «ممّا كسبوا» (from what they earned). How many deeds a person performs, yet due to lacking the intention of seeking closeness (qaṣd-e qurbat) and pure intention (niyyat-e khāliṣ), he will not benefit from them in the Hereafter.

2- Without acquisition (kasb) and effort, there is no portion. «لهم نصیب ممّا كسبوا» (They shall have a portion of what they earned).

3- Swift recompense is an advantage. [591] «سریع الحساب» (Swift in reckoning).

591) Imam Ali عليه السلام says: Allah settles the account of all creation at once, just as He provides sustenance to all people simultaneously. Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān, Vol. 2, p. 531.

Ayah

۞ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فِىٓ أَيَّامٍ مَّعْدُودَٰتٍ ۚ فَمَن تَعَجَّلَ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ فَلَآ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَمَن تَأَخَّرَ فَلَآ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ لِمَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰ ۗ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (۲٠۳)
Remember Allah in the appointed days. Then whoever hastens off in a couple of days, there is no sin upon him, and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him—that for one who has been Godwary—and be wary of Allah and know that toward Him you will be mustered. (203)

Key Points

In a hadith mentioned in Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, it is recommended that after fifteen prayers, starting with the Dhuhr prayer on the day of Eid and ending with the Fajr prayer on the thirteenth day, this du'a (supplication) should be recited: «اللَّه اكبر، اللَّه اكبر، لااله الااللّه واللَّه اكبر، وللّه الحمد، اللَّه اكبر على ما هَدانا، اللَّه اكبر على ما رزقنا من بهیمة الانعام» Allah is Greater, Allah is Greater, and there is no god but Him, Allah is Greater, and praise and thanks belong exclusively to Him, Allah is Greater because He guided us, Allah is Greater because He provided us sustenance from the meat of the livestock. This Ayah states: There is no sin upon the person who hastens to leave the land of Mina and departs after noon on the twelfth day and goes to Mecca, just as there is no sin upon the person who remains in Mina until the night of the thirteenth.

Messages

1- Time has an effect on supplication (du'a). «فى أیّام معدودات»

2- Allah, under the shadow of His grace, does not restrict the path for people. «فمن تعجّل...ومن تأخّر»

3- The foundation of the deed is piety (taqwa). «لمن اتّقى» According to narrations, the meaning of taqwa here is avoiding forbidden acts while in the state of Ihrām.

4- Hastening the departure from the land of Mina on the 12th of Dhu al-Hijjah or delaying it until the 13th must be accompanied by piety (taqwa). «لمن اتّقى» If the haste is to show cleverness or for a corrupt purpose, or if staying longer in that land is for the sake of showing off and fame, the deed becomes invalid.

5- A sacred deed and a sacred land are not sufficient by themselves; the human being must also be sacred and pious. The repetition of the word taqwa side-by-side for the pilgrims of the House of Allah, especially in the land of Mina, is a sign of Satan's penetration everywhere.

6- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the strongest factor for piety (taqwa). «واتّقوا اللَّه... انّكم الیه تحشرون»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يُعْجِبُكَ قَوْلُهُۥ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَيُشْهِدُ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ مَا فِى قَلْبِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَلَدُّ ٱلْخِصَامِ (۲٠٤)
Among the people is he whose talk about worldly life impresses you, and he holds Allah witness to what is in his heart, though he is the staunchest of enemies. (204)

Key Points

The word "لُدّ" (ludd) means intense hostility, and the word "اَلَدّ" (aladd) is used for someone who possesses the most severe hostilities. The word "خصام" (khiṣām) is either the plural of "خصم" (khaṣm) or a verbal noun meaning hostility. The hypocrites, using comparative, statistical, expert, empirical tricks, and seemingly scholarly guesses, relying on social issues, and through rumors, threats, temptation, and propaganda, only have the power to maneuver in this material life. However, in the field of spirituality and in paying attention to unseen Divine aid and the Will of Allah, their feet are lame, and in the Hereafter, their tongues will be sealed, and they will not even have permission to apologize.

Messages

1- Do not be deceived by the eloquent and enchanting words of the hypocrites. «یعجبك قوله»

2- Do not trust every oath. «یشهد اللَّه على ما فى قلبه»

3- False swearing is one of the ways the hypocrites infiltrate. «یشهد اللَّه على ما فى قلبه» Enemies always use sacred things against sacred things and religion against religion, betraying the Messenger of Allah by swearing in the Name of God.

4- The subjects of the hypocrites' speech relate to worldly matters in order to influence others. «یعجبك قوله فى الحیوةالدنیا»

5- Allah informs His Prophet about the unseen and the inner thoughts of others. «هو الدّ الخصام»

6- Those who know they are liars try to present themselves as sincere and devoted by swearing oaths and beautifying their words. «یشهد اللَّه»

Ayah

وَإِذَا تَوَلَّىٰ سَعَىٰ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لِيُفْسِدَ فِيهَا وَيُهْلِكَ ٱلْحَرْثَ وَٱلنَّسْلَ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْفَسَادَ (۲٠٥)
If he were to wield authority, he would try to cause corruption in the land and to ruin the crop and the stock, and Allah does not like corruption. (205)

Key Points

The word tawallā («تولّى») can be interpreted in two ways: one is in the sense of attaining power and governance, which is used in the translation [of the Ayah]. The other is in the sense of turning away (or aversion). In this case, the meaning of the Ayah would be: Whenever he turns away from the truth and Divine guidance and leaves your presence, he goes seeking mischief and corruption.

In the previous Ayah, the seemingly good speech of the hypocrites was discussed, and in this Ayah, their corruptive behavior is explained.

The Quran states: «الّذین ان مكنّاهم فى الارض أقاموا الصلوة واتوا الزّكوة وأمروا بالمعروف ونَهوا عن المنكر» [592] If the righteous take control of the government, they establish prayer (salat), pay charity (zakat), enjoin good (amr bil-ma'ruf), and forbid evil (nahy 'anil-munkar). Through this, they maintain their relationship with Allah and the deprived members of society. However, if the unworthy attains power and takes charge of the people, due to the storm of internal desires, ignorance, and external pressures, he sacrifices everything in society for his own sake.

According to Al-Mizan; history testifies to how many apparent Muslims, ruling over people in the name of Islam and feigning faith, destroyed the crops (harth) and the progeny (nasl).

Those who cause the destruction of the economy and the new generation through the fabrication of laws, cultural invasion, propaganda, and narcotics are examples of this Ayah and are considered mufsid fil-ard (corruptors on earth).

The destruction of harth means the eradication of agriculture, and the destruction of nasl is the deviation of the future generation.

Maraghi and Fakhr Razi, in their commentaries, have suggested that the intent of harth («حرث») might be women, based on the verse «نساؤكم حرث لكم» [593], and the intent of nasl («نسل») is children. Meaning, the tyrants destroy the family structure and the upbringing of children.

592) Hajj, 41. 593) Baqarah, 223.

Messages

1- If the unworthy attains power, he drags everything into corruption. «اذا تولّى سعى»

2- The greatest danger is the destruction of the economy and the culture of the nation (Ummah). «یهلك الحرث و النسل»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُ ٱتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ أَخَذَتْهُ ٱلْعِزَّةُ بِٱلْإِثْمِ ۚ فَحَسْبُهُۥ جَهَنَّمُ ۚ وَلَبِئْسَ ٱلْمِهَادُ (۲٠٦)
And when he is told, ‘Be wary of Allah,’ conceit seizes him sinfully; so let hell suffice him, and it is surely an evil resting place! (206)

Messages

1- The arrogant do not listen to the admonition of others. «اذا قیل له اتّق اللَّه اخذته العزّة»

It is mentioned in history that Abdul Malik Marwan, one of the Caliphs of Banu Umayyah, said atop the pulpit in the city of Medina: By Allah, whoever commands me to piety, I will strike his neck.

2- [594] A ruler must be receptive to advice. «اذا قیل... اخذته العزّة»

3- Sin is the source of self-admiration, arrogance, and haughtiness. «اخذته العزّة بالاثم»

4- Drawing attention to Hellfire is a pride-shattering warning. «فحسبه جهنّم»

594) Tafsir Ahsan al-Hadith, Vol. 1, p. 376.

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَشْرِى نَفْسَهُ ٱبْتِغَآءَ مَرْضَاتِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ رَءُوفٌۢ بِٱلْعِبَادِ (۲٠۷)
And among the people is he who sells his soul seeking the pleasure of Allah, and Allah is most kind to [His] servants. (207)

Key Points

Ibn Abi al-Hadid, who is one of the scholars of the seventh century from the Ahl al-Sunnah (Sunnis), states in his Sharh Nahj al-Balagha: All exegetes have said that this Ayah was revealed concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام, who slept in the bed of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله on the Night of the Migration (Laylat al-Mabit). This matter is so widely transmitted (mutawatir) that none denies it except a disbeliever or a madman. [595] The polytheists of Mecca decided to select one person from every tribe to kill the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and eliminate him collectively so that Banu Hashim would not rise up to avenge his blood, thereby relieving themselves of the Prophet's call. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was informed of their plot, and Ali عليه السلام slept in His bed so that the Prophet could safely leave Mecca, and this Ayah was revealed concerning his status. [596] As soon as Ali عليه السلام slept in the Prophet's place on that dangerous night, Allah commanded His two angels, Gabriel and Michael: Which of you is willing to sacrifice himself for the other? Neither of them was willing. Allah said: Now observe how Ali ibn Abi Talib عليهما السلام is willing to sacrifice his life for the Messenger of Allah. Sometimes, in order to revive a good deed (ma'ruf) or eliminate an evil deed (munkar), one must surrender his life. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The person who is killed in the path of enjoining good and forbidding evil is one of the instances (misdaqs) of this Ayah. [597]

595) Sharh Nahj al-Balagha, Vol. 13, p. 262. 596) Many Sunni scholars have acknowledged this virtue of Ali عليه السلام, whom Allamah Amini (may Allah have mercy on him) has named in Al-Ghadir (Vol. 2, p. 48), among them Imam Ahmad Hanbal, in his Musnad (Vol. 1, p. 348). The author of Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan writes: In the book Ghayat al-Maram, twenty narrations are cited, nine from Sunni scholars and eleven from Shia scholars, all of whom consider this Ayah to be about the sacrifice of Ali عليه السلام. The story of Ali عليه السلام sleeping in the bed of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله is also narrated in Tarikh al-Tabari (Vol. 2, p. 373), which is a writing from the third century, just as Tarikh al-Ya'qubi (Vol. 2, p. 39) explicitly recounts the matter. In Sharh Nahj al-Balagha by Ibn Abi al-Hadid (Vol. 4, p. 73), it is mentioned: Mu'awiyah paid four thousand dirhams to have people say that this Ayah was revealed concerning Ibn Muljam! Tafsir-e Nemooneh, Vol. 2, p. 49. 597) Tafsir Safi, Vol. 1, p. 241.

Messages

1- The unworthy person's speech is beautiful and causes wonder, but the believer's action causes the world to wonder. «من النّاس مَن یعجبك قوله», «من النّاس مَن یشرى نفسه»

2- We must keep the memory of the sacrificers alive. «من الناس من یشرى»

3- Becoming a shield (or sacrificing oneself) for the friends of Allah is a value. «من الناس من یشرى»

4- The greatest profit is for a person to sell his best commodity—which is his life—to his Creator, not for the sake of Paradise or salvation from Hellfire, but solely to attain His pleasure. «ابتغاء مرضات اللّه»

5- In the path of Allah, welcoming danger and readiness for self-sacrifice is equivalent to encountering danger. Considering the occasion of the revelation of the Ayah, it praises the one who exposed his life to danger, even though no incident occurred. «من یشرى نفسه...»

6- The compassion (Ra'fah) of Allah is the best reward. Allah has designated a reward for every deed, but in this Ayah, He says: Allah is Compassionate (Ra'uf). «اللّه رئوف بالعباد»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ فِى ٱلسِّلْمِ كَآفَّةً وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ خُطُوَٰتِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ (۲٠۸)
O you who have faith! Enter into submission, all together, and do not follow in Satan’s steps; he is indeed your manifest enemy. (208)

Key Points

The Quran has proposed establishing peaceful relations with the following groups: 1- With harmless polytheists. «لم یقاتلوكم فى الدین ولم یخرجوكم من دیاركم ان تبرّوا و تقسطوا الیهم» Be kind to those [598] who do not wage war against you in religion and do not drive you out of your homes. 2- With the People of the Book. «قل تعالَوا اِلى كلمة سواء بیننا وبینكم اَلاّ نعبد الاّ اللَّه» [599] Say to the People of the Book: Come, let us unite upon what we and you agree upon. 3- With other Muslims. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا ادخلوا فى السِّلم كافّة» A few verses prior [600], concerning the great political and devotional gathering of Hajj, He said: There should be no transgression (fisq) or dispute (jidal) there. Thus, it is possible to form a society without transgression and dispute, and how much better it is that we always remain this way and avoid the satanic steps that cause division. Numerous narrations have considered entering into as-Silm (peace/submission) as entering under the leadership of the infallible (Ma'sum). [601] As it is stated in the narrations: «ولایة على بن ابى طالب حصنى» (The Wilayah of Ali ibn Abi Talib is My fortress). It is obvious that entering the fortress and stronghold is entering into Silm, safety, and tranquility. And what tranquility is greater than when a person feels that his leader is divine, infallible, knowledgeable, compassionate, and the foremost among people in all values and perfections.

598) Al-Mumtahanah, 8. 599) Aal-i-Imran, 64. 600) Al-Baqarah, 197. 601) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 205.

Messages

1- Entering the sphere of Silm (peace), safety, and submission is impossible except under the shadow of faith. «یاایّها الّذین آمنوا»

2- Set aside personal preferences and submit only to the law of Allah. «ادخلوا فى السّلم كافّة»

3- Establishing peace is the duty of all Muslims. «كافّة»

4- The whispers of Satan do not compel man to sin; rather, man has the power to confront Satan, and for this reason, he is forbidden from obeying him. «لا تتّبعوا»

5- Satan misleads man step by step. «خطوات»

6- The ways Satan misleads are numerous. «خطوات»

7- Satan is the enemy of peace and unity, and all calls for division are satanic loudspeakers. «ادخلوا فى السّلم كافّة و لا تتّبعوا خطوات الشیطان»

Ayah

فَإِن زَلَلْتُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَتْكُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتُ فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۲٠۹)
And should you stumble after the manifest proofs that have come to you, know that Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (209)

Messages

1- Allah has completed the conclusive argument (Hujjah). «جاءتكم البینات»

2- Those who knowingly commit transgression should await punishment. «زللتم من بعد ما جاءتكم البینات... عزیز حكیم»

3- Your lack of submission does not affect the Glory (Might) of Allah. «عزیز»

If the entirety of creation becomes disbelievers, no dust shall settle upon His Majesty.

4- The punishment inflicted by Allah is based on wisdom. «حكیم»

Ayah

هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّآ أَن يَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِى ظُلَلٍ مِّنَ ٱلْغَمَامِ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَقُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ ۚ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ (۲۱٠)
Do they await anything but that Allah [’s command] should come to them in the shades of the clouds, with the angels, and the matter be decided [once for all]? To Allah all matters are returned. (210)

Key Points

The invitation of the prophets is through argumentation (reasoning) and miracles, but sometimes fault-finding people had other expectations. Allah has power over everything, but He says: If, after your expectation is met, you still persist in stubbornness, Divine wrath will encompass you, and there will be no further respite for apology or repentance. We see examples of this in the heavenly table (Mā'idah) that they requested from Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام), or the she-camel that they requested from Prophet Salih (عليه السلام). Whenever the type of miracle occurs at the request of the people, absolute obedience is mandatory, otherwise, absolute wrath is certain. Of course, this applies to cases where the expectation itself is not impossible, such as seeing God. Otherwise, if the expectation itself is inappropriate, they will be given a negative response. Allah's system of creation and nurturing does not revolve around your inappropriate expectations. You might expect Allah and the angels to appear physically above your heads amidst the clouds and speak directly to you, but such a thing is not possible.

Messages

1- Despite clear proofs of the Prophet's mission, inappropriate expectations are forbidden. «هل ینظرون»

2- The expectation of seeing Allah and the angels is an excuse to evade accepting the truth. «ینظرون... یأتیهم اللّه و الملائكة»

3- The source and return of all things is towards Allah. Why are you waiting to see Him Himself? Do you not see His signs? «هل ینظرون... و الى اللّه ترجع الامور»

Ayah

سَلْ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ كَمْ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُم مِّنْ ءَايَةٍۭ بَيِّنَةٍ ۗ وَمَن يُبَدِّلْ نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَتْهُ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (۲۱۱)
Ask the Children of Israel how many a manifest sign We had given them. Whoever changes Allah’s blessing after it has come to him, indeed Allah is severe in retribution. (211)

Key Points

According to narrations, the history of the Children of Israel is the closest history to Muslims regarding the occurrence of various events. Allah bestowed upon them a leader like Moses, His miracles and favors, and saved them from the captivity of Pharaoh. He sent heavenly laws for the management of their lives and also provided them with a good life materially. However, their ingratitude and the changing of blessings reached a point where, instead of following Aaron, they followed the Samiri, and instead of worshipping Allah, they turned to calf-worship. Until Allah, Who had said concerning them, «فضّلتكم على العالمین» [602], said, as a result of such behaviors: «باؤوا بغضب» [603]. It is one of the fixed Divine traditions that any nation or people, whether Muslim or non-Muslim, if they change or alter the Divine blessings, they will suffer Divine wrath. Just as today, the developed world, instead of making beneficial use of technology and industry, has dragged the world into fire and corruption.

602) Baqarah, 47. 603) Baqarah, 61.

Messages

1- Studying history and its lessons is necessary. «سل بنى اسرائیل»

2- Blessings bring responsibility, and negligence regarding them brings punishment. «مَن یبدّل... شدید العقاب»

Ayah

زُيِّنَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۘ وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (۲۱۲)
Worldly life has been glamorized for the faithless, and they ridicule the faithful. But those who are Godwary shall be above them on the Day of Resurrection, and Allah provides for whomever He wishes without any reckoning. (212)

Key Points

This Ayah serves both as a warning to the disbelievers (kuffar)—that they should not become preoccupied and intoxicated by the glitter of this world, nor should they mock the believers—for there is a Resurrection (Qiyamah) where the scenes will be reversed. It also provides solace to the believers, encouraging them not to weaken due to the mockery of the disbelievers, but to remain hopeful for the future.

Messages

1- Disbelief (kufr) makes the world appealing in the eyes of man. «زیّن للّذین كفروا»

Indeed, the world appeals to those who have no belief in the boundless favors and blessings of the Resurrection, but in the eyes of the believer, how can the world compare to Paradise?

2- Worldliness becomes a factor leading to the humiliation and mockery of others. «زیّن للّذین كفروا... یسخرون»

3- Mocking the poor is the constant method of the people of the world. «یسخرون» (604)

4- Superiority in the Hereafter belongs exclusively to those believers whose piety has not been severed by the harsh words of the disbelievers. «والّذین اتّقوا فوقهم»

5- Provision without measure (bighayri hisab) is a sign of His grace, not that Allah does not keep account of it or that He does not distribute it wisely. «یرزق... بغیر حساب»

604) The imperfect verb (fi'l mudari') is a sign of continuity and permanence.

Ayah

كَانَ ٱلنَّاسُ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً فَبَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنذِرِينَ وَأَنزَلَ مَعَهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ ٱلنَّاسِ فِيمَا ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَا ٱخْتَلَفَ فِيهِ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوهُ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتُ بَغْيًۢا بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ فَهَدَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لِمَا ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فِيهِ مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۲۱۳)
Mankind were a single community; then Allah sent the prophets as bearers of good news and warners, and He sent down with them the Book with the truth, that it may judge between the people concerning that about which they differed, and none differed in it except those who had been given it, after the manifest proofs had come to them, out of envy among themselves. Then Allah guided those who had faith to the truth of what they differed in, by His will, and Allah guides whomever He wishes to a straight path. (213)

Key Points

What is understood from this Ayah is that people initially had a simple and basic life, but gradually, due to the multitude of individuals and the increase in tastes (preferences), disagreements and conflicts arose. In some narrations, this time is applied to the era before Prophet Nuh (Noah) عليه السلام, during which people lived a simple and limited life and were in a state of unawareness. [605] Then, individuals disagreed over the utilization of natural resources and because everyone wanted to employ others and benefit from their interests, leading to the emergence of societies and classes. The necessity of social life is the existence of a firm and just law that can resolve disagreements. Allah sent the Prophets to resolve disagreements and remove conflicts, and He revealed the heavenly Books. However, a group knowingly and out of obstinacy resisted the warnings and glad tidings of the Prophets, and they themselves became the creators of further disagreements. But Allah, through His grace, guided the individuals who were receptive and ready for guidance, and He left those who resisted due to envy or oppression to their own devices so that they might face their punishment. The Quran's criticism is directed at obstinate disagreement where a person is unwilling to accept the judgment of the Prophets and the law of Allah. Otherwise, a disagreement that ultimately submits to justice is acceptable, like the difference between the two pans of a scale which, in their opposing movements, seek to achieve justice. «فهدى اللّه... لما اختلفوا فیه»

605) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 209.

Messages

1- Human laws and civilizations are incapable of resolving disagreements, because every group and individual pursues their own desires. The resolution of disagreements must occur through Divine law and the judgment of the Prophets—a law that is immune from distortion and error, and a judgment that is infallible from caprice and selfish motives. «فبعث اللَّه النبیّین... معهم الكتاب»

2- The best way to resolve disagreement is strengthening faith in the Resurrection (Ma'ad). The Prophets use glad tidings for the prevention of disagreements and warnings for their cure. «مبشّرین ومنذرین... لیحكم بین النّاس»

3- The general outlines of the Prophets' goals are one. «النبیّین مبشرین و منذرین»

4- The judgment of the Prophets is within the framework of heavenly laws. «انزل معهم الكتاب لیحكم»

5- One of the duties of the Prophets is to create unity and eliminate division. «لیحكم... فیما اختلفوا»

6- The judgment of all Prophets is of one kind. He said: «لیحكم» (to judge, singular), and He did not say: «لیحكموا» (to judge, plural).

7- The worst type of disagreement is the knowing disagreement that arises due to envy and oppression. «ما اختلف فیه... من بعد ما جائتهم البیّنات بغیاً بینهم»

8- Disagreements also arise among the believers, but they are guided under the light of Allah's guardianship (Wilayah). «فهدى اللّه... لما اختلفوا فیه»

9- Faith is the prerequisite for benefiting from special Divine guidance. «فهدى اللَّه الذین آمنوا»

10- Although the guidance of all individuals is according to the Will of Allah, His Will is based on wisdom. «یهدى من یشاء»

11- The Straight Path (Sirat al-Mustaqim) is the ender of disagreements. «اختلفوا... یهدى... صراطمستقیم»

Ayah

أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَن تَدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِكُم مَّثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ خَلَوْا۟ مِن قَبْلِكُم ۖ مَّسَّتْهُمُ ٱلْبَأْسَآءُ وَٱلضَّرَّآءُ وَزُلْزِلُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَقُولَ ٱلرَّسُولُ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ مَتَىٰ نَصْرُ ٱللَّهِ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ نَصْرَ ٱللَّهِ قَرِيبٌ (۲۱٤)
Do you suppose that you will enter paradise though there has not yet come to you the like of [what befell] those who went before you? Stress and distress befell them and they were convulsed until the apostle and the faithful who were with him said, ‘When will Allah’s help [come]?’ Behold! Allah’s help is indeed near! (214)

Key Points

Bāsā’ (بأساء) refers to hardships imposed on a person from outside, such as war and theft. And Ḍarrā’ (ضرّاء) refers to internal hardships, such as illness, wounds, and injuries. This Ayah is a reminder of the endurance of hardships by past believers, and it serves as a warning and consolation for Muslims. Difficult events are a divine test, and problems such as bitter financial and physical calamities are all for the purpose of building up human beings.

Every affliction carries a gift with it Every bitterness is followed by purity. Beneath every suffering, there is a valuable treasure. If you see a thorn, open your eyes and behold the rose.

In a narration from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, we read: Paradise is enveloped by hardships. (606) And in another narration, it is stated: For the believers afflicted in this world, there is such a reward that others will say: If only these hardships, and even greater hardships, had been for us. (607) Yes, problems are a factor for growth, proximity (to Allah), and the path to entering Paradise.

Without enduring suffering, treasure cannot be attained. O brother, only he who works receives the reward.

The delay in the acceptance of prayer should not cause doubt. Allah has promised many times that: «انّهم لهم المنصورون» (Our servants shall surely be helped) (608) and «كتب اللّه لاغلبنّ انا ورسلى» (Allah has decreed: I and My Messengers shall surely prevail) (609). Allah has written that He and His Messengers are victorious. In any case, Allah has promised victory, and these promises might be delayed for certain reasons, but this delay should not cause doubt.

606) «انّ الجنّة حفّت بالمكاره و انّ النّار حفّت بالشهوات». Tafsir Kashif, Vol. 1, p. 319. 607) Tafsir Manhaj al-Sadiqin, Vol. 1, p. 552. 608) Saffat, 172. 609) Mujadilah, 21.

Messages

1- Expecting to enter Paradise merely because of having faith and without enduring hardship is an incorrect expectation. «ام حسبتم ان تدخلوا الجنّة...»

2- There is no change in the divine traditions. All nations, communities, and individuals must pass through the crucible of trial. «ولمّا یأتكم مثل الّذین خلَوا»

3- Paying attention to the problems of others is a factor for solace. We should take lessons and examples from history. «مستهم البأساء والضرّاء»

4- We must be prepared; the divine trials are so severe that they sometimes cause even the Prophets to become anxious. «یقول الرسول والذین آمنوا معه متى نصر اللَّه»

5- During hardships, the Prophets were not separate from the people. «یقول الرسول و الذین آمنوا معه»

6- One of the conditions for the acceptance of prayer is distress (necessity) and cutting off hope from all but Allah. «متى نصراللَّه... انّ نصر اللّه قریب»

7- The solace for all problems is the remembrance of Allah's help. «الا انّ نصر اللّه قریب»

8- Resistance (steadfastness) is the prerequisite for receiving divine help. «مسّتهم... انّ نصر اللّه قریب»

Ayah

يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ مَاذَا يُنفِقُونَ ۖ قُلْ مَآ أَنفَقْتُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ فَلِلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ وَٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ ۗ وَمَا تَفْعَلُوا۟ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِهِۦ عَلِيمٌ (۲۱٥)
They ask you as to what they should spend. Say, ‘Let whatever wealth you spend be for the parents, relatives, orphans, the needy, and the traveller.’ Allah indeed knows whatever good you do. (215)

Key Points

The word khayr (goodness/benefit) is used twice in this Ayah: once for spending goodness; «انفقتم من خیر» (what you spend of goodness), and another for doing good deeds; «تفعلوا من خیر» (whatever good you do), indicating that even those without money can achieve goodness through their actions and deeds.

We read in a Hadith: «لاصدقة و ذو رحم محتاج» [610] (There is no charity when a relative is needy). If relatives are in need, the turn for charity does not reach others.

In spending (infaq), observing priorities (the more important over the important), having complete awareness, and considering emotional standards are necessary.

Spending (infaq) is of five types: [611] 1- Obligatory spending (Wajib Infaq). Such as Zakat, Khums, expiations (kaffarat), ransom (fidyah), and the living expenses (nafaqah) which are the responsibility of the man. 2- Recommended spending (Mustahabb Infaq). Such as helping the poor, orphans, and giving gifts to friends. 3- Forbidden spending (Haram Infaq). Such as spending with usurped wealth or spending in the path of sin. 4- Disliked spending (Makruh Infaq). Such as spending on others when there are deserving relatives. 5 - Permissible spending (Mubah Infaq). Such as spending on others for the expansion of life. This is because spending to alleviate poverty is either obligatory or recommended.

610) Bihar, Vol. 77, p. 59. 611) Tafsir Mawahib al-Rahman, Vol. 3, p. 264.

Messages

1- The type of spending, its recipient, and its method must be learned from the school of revelation. «یسئلونك» (They ask you).

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام, in the Supplication of Noble Moralities (Du'a Makarim al-Akhlaq), asks Allah to guide him in spending: «وأصب بى سبیل الهدایة للبرّ فیما انفق منه» (And make me follow the path of guidance to righteousness in what I spend of it).

2- The questions of the people provide the context for the descent of the answer from Allah. «یسئلونك... قل» (They ask you... Say).

3- Spending includes small and large deeds. «من خیر» (of goodness).

4- Worldly wealth and possessions, in the hands of believers, are a source of goodness. «من خیر» (of goodness).

5- Spending must be from good and desirable things. «من خیر» (of goodness).

6- Spending is not limited to financial matters; it encompasses every benefit and advantage. «من خیر» (of goodness).

7- The best recipients of spending are parents and relatives. «فللوالدین و الاقربین...» (then for the parents and the nearest relatives...).

8- Spending on relatives strengthens family ties, and spending on others compensates for the financial and emotional deficiencies of the needy and eliminates class differences. «فللوالدین والاقربین والیتامى والمساكین وابن السبیل» (then for the parents and the nearest relatives and the orphans and the needy and the traveler).

9- A wise person is one who, in answering, points to something the questioner is unaware of. In the Ayah, the type of spending was asked about, but Allah specified the recipients. «ماذا ینفقون... فللوالدین...» (What should they spend... then for the parents...).

10- Islam pays special attention to the deprived class. «وابن السبیل» (and the traveler/wayfarer).

11- A righteous deed is never wasted, whether it is done openly or secretly, whether it is little or much. «فانّ اللّه به علیم» (for Allah is All-Knowing of it).

12- Belief in Allah's awareness of our deeds is the best encouragement. «فانّ اللَّه به علیم» (for Allah is All-Knowing of it).

Ayah

كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْقِتَالُ وَهُوَ كُرْهٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَعَسَىٰٓ أَن تَكْرَهُوا۟ شَيْـًٔا وَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَعَسَىٰٓ أَن تُحِبُّوا۟ شَيْـًٔا وَهُوَ شَرٌّ لَّكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۲۱٦)
Warfare has been prescribed for you, though it is repulsive to you. Yet it may be that you dislike something, which is good for you, and it may be that you love something, which is bad for you, and Allah knows and you do not know. (216)

Key Points

The word kurh (كُره) refers to a hardship that a person feels internally, such as the fear of war. And karh (كَره) refers to a hardship imposed upon a person externally, such as the compulsory decree in the Ayah: «اتینا طَوعاً او كَرهاً قالَتا اَتینا طائعین» [612] (We said to the heavens and the earth: Come willingly or unwillingly. They said: We come willingly.) The previous Ayah related to giving wealth, and this Ayah relates to giving life (i.e., fighting). Aversion to war is either due to a spirit of comfort-seeking, or due to humanitarianism and compassion for others, or due to an imbalance of power and the enemy's superiority. The Quran states: You do not know the effects and results of Jihad, and you only pay attention to its material and apparent losses, while Allah is aware of the secrets and consequences of actions, both immediate and future, in their various dimensions.

War, despite all its hardships, also has positive effects and benefits. Including: A: Combat capability increases. B: The enemy does not become emboldened. C: The spirit of commitment and sacrifice emerges among members of society. D: The power and honor (Izzah) of Islam and Muslims are established in the world. E: Divine assistance pours forth. F: The spirit of seeking help from Allah develops. G: Divine reward and recompense are attained. H: The spirit of innovation, invention, and initiative emerges.

Jihad in Other Religions Contrary to the propaganda the world promotes for peace and against violence, the issue of Jihad (struggle) in the way of religion has existed in all heavenly religions and is an absolute necessity.

In the Torah, we read: * Strike the inhabitants of the city with the edge of the sword and gather their possessions. [613] * Utterly destroy all of them and show no pity. [614] * When you go out to battle and see many enemies, do not be afraid. [615] * Let every man slay his brother, and every man his friend, and every man his neighbor, so that it may be an atonement for calf-worship. [616] * Moses moved a multitude toward war, and a significant battle took place. [617]

In the Gospel of Matthew, it is stated: * Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth; I have not come to bring peace, but a sword. [618] * Whoever finds his life (and does not come to war) will lose it, and whoever loses his life for My sake will find it. [619]

In the Gospel of Luke, we read: * Bring here those enemies of Mine, who did not want Me to reign over them, and slay them before Me. [620] * He who has no sword, let him sell his garment and buy one. [621]

In the Quran, too, in Surahs Al ‘Imran, [622] Al-Ma’idah, [623] and Al-Baqarah, [624] mention has been made of the existence of war and Jihad in past religions.

612) Fussilat, 11. 613) Torah, Deuteronomy, Chapter 13, Verse 15. 614) Torah, Deuteronomy, Chapter 7, Verse 3. 615) Torah, Deuteronomy, Chapter 20, Verse 1. 616) Torah, Exodus, Verse 26. 617) Torah, Numbers, Chapter 31, Verse 34. 618) Gospel of Matthew, Chapter 10, Verse 7. 619) Gospel of Matthew, Chapter 10, Verse 39. 620) Gospel of Luke, Chapter 19, Verse 28. 621) Gospel of Luke, Chapter 22, Verse 36. 622) Al ‘Imran, 147. 623) Al-Ma’idah, 24. 624) Al-Baqarah, 246.

Messages

1- The criterion for good and evil is not ease or hardship, nor personal desires; rather, the criterion is real interests, and we should not rely on our own preconceptions. «عسى ان تكرهوا شیئاً وهو خیر لكم»

2- Psychological aversion and affection are not signs of real good and evil. «تكرهوا... و هو خیر لكم... تحبوا... و هو شرّ لكم»

3- War and Jihad in the way of religion are sources of good. «كُتب علیكم القتال... و هو خیر لكم»

4- We must submit to the command of Allah, Who instructs us based on infinite knowledge, even if we do not know the reason for it. «واللّه یعلم و انتم لا تعلمون»

Ayah

يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلشَّهْرِ ٱلْحَرَامِ قِتَالٍ فِيهِ ۖ قُلْ قِتَالٌ فِيهِ كَبِيرٌ ۖ وَصَدٌّ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَكُفْرٌۢ بِهِۦ وَٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ وَإِخْرَاجُ أَهْلِهِۦ مِنْهُ أَكْبَرُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَٱلْفِتْنَةُ أَكْبَرُ مِنَ ٱلْقَتْلِ ۗ وَلَا يَزَالُونَ يُقَـٰتِلُونَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَرُدُّوكُمْ عَن دِينِكُمْ إِنِ ٱسْتَطَـٰعُوا۟ ۚ وَمَن يَرْتَدِدْ مِنكُمْ عَن دِينِهِۦ فَيَمُتْ وَهُوَ كَافِرٌ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۲۱۷)
They ask you concerning warfare in the holy month. Say, ‘It is an outrageous thing to fight in it, but to keep [people] from Allah’s way, and to be unfaithful to Him, and [to keep people from] the Holy Mosque, and to expel its people from it are more outrageous with Allah. And persecution is graver than killing. They will not cease fighting you until they turn you away from your religion, if they can. And whoever of you turns away from his religion and dies faithless—they are the ones whose works have failed in this world and the Hereafter. They shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they shall remain in it [forever]. (217)

Key Points

The meaning of the word fitnah (فتنة) in the Ayah is polytheism (shirk), torture, trial, and tribulation. It is mentioned in both Shia and Sunni exegeses: The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله sent a person named Abdullah ibn Jahsh along with eight men, carrying a letter, to gather information about the disbelievers (kuffar) and polytheists (mushrikin). However, after the encounter, a conflict arose, and the leader of the opposing group was killed, two of their men were taken captive, and their property was confiscated. This incident occurred on the first of the month of Rajab, which is one of the Sacred Months [625], while this group had assumed it was the end of Jumada al-Thani, not the first of Rajab. In any case, this fighting mistakenly took place on the first of Rajab, and the disbelievers used this issue as a pretext, spreading the rumor that Muslims do not respect the Sacred Months. In response to this enemy propaganda, the Quran replies that although the fighting occurred in a Sacred Month, this conflict was carried out mistakenly by the Muslims, whereas you polytheists have deliberately committed sins whose punishment is far greater than fighting in a Sacred Month. Therefore, having a history of all those crimes, disgraces, and the commission of major sins, you should not use this mistaken action as a pretext.

The nullification (habt) of the apostate's deeds in this world is that he is deprived of the benefits of Islam. This is because his punishment includes separation from his Muslim wife, deprivation of inheritance, severance of ties with Muslims, removal from the protection of Islam, and ultimately, execution. And the nullification of his deeds in the Hereafter is the erasure of all his good works. «حبطت اعمالهم فى الدنیا والاخرة»

625) Among the Abrahamic traditions prevalent among the Arabs before Islam was the respect shown to four months: Rajab, Dhu al-Qa'dah, Dhu al-Hijjah, and Muharram. During these months, they would not engage in any war or fighting. Islam also ratified this tradition and prohibited fighting and conflict during these months.

Messages

1- Provide appropriate answers to doubts/misconceptions. «یسئلونك... قل»

2- Al-Masjid al-Haram (the Sacred Mosque) and its people hold special respect. «المسجد الحرام واخراج اهله منه اكبر»

3- The determination of the severity of sins belongs to Allah. «اخراج اهله منه اكبر، الفتنة اكبر...»

4- Removing security is more important (worse) than killing. «والفتنة اكبر من القتل»

5- It is necessary to know the desires, goals, and efforts of your enemy. «ولایزالون یقاتلونكم حتّى یردّوكم عن دینكم»

6- The enemy's attack does not justify your disbelief (kufr) or apostasy. «ولایزالون یقاتلونكم... ومن یرتدد منكم...»

7- Enemies are always lying in wait; they desire your apostasy, disbelief, and turning away from your religion, and they will not be satisfied with anything less. They are not seeking temporary victory, but rather wish to destroy your culture and school of thought. «حتى یردّوكم عن دینكم»

8- A good outcome (husn al-aqibah) is the criterion of value. How many Muslims have died as disbelievers. «فیمت و هو كافر»

9- Apostasy has worldly and otherworldly consequences. «مَن یرتدد منكم... حبطت اعمالهم فى الدنیا و الاخرة»

10- Apostasy causes the nullification (habt) of good deeds. «من یرتدد... حبطت اعمالهم»

11- The value of faith and belief is such that if faith is lost, righteous deeds are also nullified. «مَن یرتدد منكم... حبطت اعمالهم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱلَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا۟ وَجَـٰهَدُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَرْجُونَ رَحْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۲۱۸)
Indeed those who are faithful and those who have migrated and waged jihad in the way of Allah—it is they who expect Allah’s mercy, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (218)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Ayah, which follows the previous Ayah, we read: The believers who performed Hijra (migration) and Jihad (struggle), but mistook the crescent of Rajab for the month of Jumada al-Ukhra and engaged in fighting, should not be distressed. Because their mistake is pardonable, and they did not intend to commit a sin.

Hope without action is nothing more than a wish. As we read in the last Ayah of Surah Al-Kahf: «من كان یرجوا لقاء ربّه فلیعمل عملاً صالحاً و لایُشرك بعبادة ربّه احداً» (Whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord, let him work righteousness and associate none with the worship of his Lord.) Whoever hopes for the meeting with the Lord and attaining the station of proximity (Qurb), must therefore perform sincere action. Yes, Rajā’ (hope) is that hope for which a person prepares the prerequisites for its attainment.

To be hopeful of Divine favors, Islam has provided several grounds: 1. Despair and hopelessness are among the major sins. [626] 2. Allah says: I am near the expectation (or thought) of My believing servant; if he thinks well of Me, I grant him goodness. [627] 3. Prophets and angels pray for the believers and seek forgiveness for them. [628] 4. The Divine reward is manifold. [629] 5. The path of repentance (Tawbah) is open. [630] 6. The afflictions of the believer are considered expiation for his sins. [631] 7. Allah accepts repentance and transforms evil deeds (Sayyi’āt) into good deeds (Hasanāt). [632]

626) Zumar, 53. 627) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 72. 628) Ghāfir, 7; Ibrāhīm, 41. 629) Baqarah, 261. 630) Nisā’, 18. 631) Bihār, Vol. 81, p. 176. 632) Furqān, 70.

Messages

  1. The rank of faith (Iman) precedes action. «آمنوا... هاجروا وجاهدوا»

  2. Hope for the Lord’s favor is conditional upon faith, migration, and struggle. «آمنوا... هاجروا وجاهدوا... اولئك یرجون»

  3. If the fundamentals of thoughts and actions are correct, minor mistakes are pardonable. «آمنوا... هاجروا... یرجون»

  4. We should hope for the favor of Allah, not for our own good deeds. Because the danger of an evil end (Sū’ al-‘Āqibah), the nullification of deeds (Habt al-‘Amal), and the non-acceptance of obedience lies in wait until the end of life. «یرجون رحمت اللّه»

  5. Since the servant hopes for the mercy of Allah, Allah also declares: He is Forgiving and Merciful. «یرجون رحمت اللّه و اللّه غفور رحیم»

  6. The value of migration and struggle is that it must be in the way of Allah and for Him. «فى سبیل اللّه»

  7. Even sincere believers and migrants need His mercy and forgiveness. «آمنوا، هاجروا... یرجون رحمت اللّه»

  8. The mistakes of migrants and strivers in the way of Allah must be overlooked. «واللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

۞ يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْخَمْرِ وَٱلْمَيْسِرِ ۖ قُلْ فِيهِمَآ إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنَـٰفِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَإِثْمُهُمَآ أَكْبَرُ مِن نَّفْعِهِمَا ۗ وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ مَاذَا يُنفِقُونَ قُلِ ٱلْعَفْوَ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۲۱۹)
They ask you concerning wine and gambling. Say, ‘There is a great sin in both of them, and some profits for the people, but their sinfulness outweighs their profit.’ And they ask you as to what they should spend. Say, ‘All that is surplus.’ Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you so that you may reflect (219)

Key Points

According to Raghib in Mufradat, the word ithm (اثم) refers to an action that makes a person slow and weak in performing good deeds. The first question posed by the people concerns the ruling on khamr (intoxicants) and maysir (gambling). The word khamr (خمر) means covering. Therefore, the cloth with which women cover their heads and observe hijab is called khimar (خمار). Since intoxicants remove the power of discernment from the intellect and essentially cover it, they are called khamr. Similarly, the word maysir (مَیسر) comes from yusr (یسر), meaning easy. It is as if, in gambling, the parties intend to easily seize each other's wealth. This Ayah, in response to their question, states: Drinking intoxicants and gambling are great sins, although they may have some benefits. Just as some people acquire wealth through grape cultivation or selling intoxicants, and certain groups do so by establishing gambling houses. The negative effects and consequences of intoxicants and gambling have been detailed in scientific and educational books. Here we present a list of what has been mentioned in Tafsir Namuneh regarding the harms of intoxicants and gambling: 1- Shortening of life span. 2- Negative consequences for children, especially if intercourse occurs while intoxicated. 3- Spread of moral corruption and rising crime statistics, such as theft, assault and battery, sexual offenses, and increased risks and accidents in driving. It is quoted from a scholar that if governments close half of the liquor stores, we will close half of the hospitals and asylums. Gambling is also among the causes of excitement, nervous diseases, strokes (cerebral and cardiac), increased heart rate, loss of appetite, paleness, and so on. Furthermore, researchers have linked thirty percent of crime statistics to gambling. Moreover, gambling plays a destructive role in economic prosperity and eliminates the enthusiasm for productive work. In some non-Islamic countries, gambling was also declared prohibited and illegal in certain years. For example, England banned gambling in 1853, the Soviet Union in 1854, and Germany in 1873.

The second question posed by the people concerns infaq (spending/charity); they ask what they should spend. The Ayah answers: Al-Afw (عفو)! In linguistics, al-afw (عفو), besides meaning forgiveness and pardon, also means the middle ground, the surplus amount, and the best part of the wealth. Each of these meanings is compatible with the Ayah, and it is possible that the intent of al-afw includes all these meanings. That is, if you wish to spend, observe moderation and do not spend all your wealth at once lest you become needy yourself, and also, when spending, give from the best of your possessions. As the Quran states elsewhere: «لن تنالوا البرّ حتّى تنفقوا ممّا تحبّون» (633) You will not attain righteousness unless you spend out of what you love.

It is mentioned in the commentaries that the prohibition of intoxicants was gradual. This is because the Arabs were addicted to intoxicants, and thus the verses gradually prepared them to accept the prohibition. First, this Ayah was revealed: «تتّخذون منه سكراً و رزقاً حسناً» (634) From grapes, you derive both intoxicating drink and good provision. That is, intoxicants are not good provision (rizq hasan). Then this Ayah was revealed: «فیهما اِثم كبیر و منافع للناس و اِثمهما اكبر» The harm of intoxicants and gambling for people is greater than their benefit. And then this Ayah was revealed: «لاتقربوا الصلوة و انتم سُكارى» (635) You should not be intoxicated when performing the prayer. Finally, the permanent and public prohibition was stated: «انّما الخمر و المیسر... رجس من عمل الشیطان» (636) Intoxicants and gambling... are foul and among the works of Satan.

633) Al ‘Imran, 92. 634) An-Nahl, 67. 635) An-Nisa, 43. 636) Al-Ma’idah, 90.

Messages

1- Intoxicants and gambling are both factors leading to the corruption of body and soul and causes of heedlessness. Therefore, they are mentioned together in the Quran. «الخمر والمیسر»

2- Guard thought and security. By prohibiting intoxicants, the intellect and thought are guarded, and by prohibiting gambling, spiritual and economic peace and health are guarded. «فیهما اثم كبیر... و اثمهما اكبر»

3- Be fair in your dealings. See the faults of others alongside their merits. The Ayah does not overlook the benefits of intoxicants and gambling but presents the matter in a way that revives the human capacity for reasoning and reflection. «فیهما اثم كبیر ومنافع للناس واثمهما اكبر»

4- Sometimes there may be benefits in prohibited things. «منافع للناس»

5- When legislating, attention must be paid to the issue of the more important and the important. «منافع للناس و اثمهما اكبر»

6- Divine rulings are based on benefits and harms. «اثمهما اكبر»

7- Familiarity with the philosophy behind the rulings is a step toward accepting them. «اثمهما اكبر»

8- Sins include major and minor categories. «اثمهما اكبر»

Ayah

فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۗ وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ ۖ قُلْ إِصْلَاحٌ لَّهُمْ خَيْرٌ ۖ وَإِن تُخَالِطُوهُمْ فَإِخْوَٰنُكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ ٱلْمُفْسِدَ مِنَ ٱلْمُصْلِحِ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَأَعْنَتَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۲۲٠)
about the world and the Hereafter. And they ask you concerning the orphans. Say, ‘It is better to set right their affairs, and if you intermingle with them, they are of course your brothers: Allah knows those who cause corruption from those who set things right, and had Allah wished He would have put you to hardship.’ Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (220)

Key Points

The phrase «فى الدنیا والاخرة» (in this world and the Hereafter) either relates to the issue of spending (infaq) mentioned in the previous Ayah—meaning your spending should be for the comfort of this world and the Hereafter. Do not spend all your wealth, causing hardship for yourself in this world, nor be stingy, leading to your deprivation of reward in the Hereafter. [637] Or it relates to contemplation (tafakkur), which was alluded to at the end of the previous Ayah. That is, man must contemplate the affairs of this world and the Hereafter, and acquire something for his Hereafter from this very world. He should contemplate the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad), the secrets of existence, the creations, and the governing laws. As far as he is able, he should accept matters through intellect and thought, although awareness of all secrets is not a prerequisite for accepting the school of thought (the religion). [638]

It is mentioned in the commentaries (Tafasir): When Ayah 10 of Surah An-Nisa' was revealed, stating that consuming the property of the orphan is like consuming fire, people who had orphans in their homes became distressed. Some expelled the orphans from their homes, and others separated the orphan's dishes and food, causing difficulties for both the orphan and the homeowner. They came before the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and asked about the treatment of orphans. The Prophet replied: Improving the affairs of the orphans is better than abandoning them. You should not abandon the orphans and shirk the responsibility of managing them merely because their property mixes with your own property. They are your religious brothers, and the mixing of their property with your life is permissible, provided the goal is not to waste or misuse their wealth. And know that Allah distinguishes the corruptor (mufsid) from the reformer (muslih).

637) Translation by the late Elahi Qomsha'i. 638) Tafsir al-Mizan, concerning the Ayah.

Messages

  1. Do not view this world and the Hereafter superficially; contemplate them. «یتفكّرون فى الدنیا و الاخرة»

  2. Abandoning the affairs of the orphans is not advisable; rather, interacting with them with good intention and the eye of brotherhood is advisable. «قل اصلاح لهم خیر»

  3. Any kind of improvement (islah) in the condition of the orphans is valuable. The word «اصلاح» (improvement/reform) is used absolutely to include all reforms, whether financial, academic, practical, educational, or religious. «اصلاح لهم خیر»

  4. Orphans are neither slaves nor our children; rather, they are our younger brothers and part of our own community. «فاخوانكم»

  5. Neither excess nor negligence: do not consume the property of the orphans in the name of reform, nor abandon them out of fear of corruption, for Allah knows the reformer (muslih) from the corruptor (mufsid). «واللّه یعلم المفسد من المصلح»

  6. If we know that Allah knows our thoughts and intentions, we will not commit corruption or wrongdoing. «واللّه یعلم المفسد»

  7. There is no obligation in Islam that is beyond one's capacity. «لو شاء اللّه لاعنتكم»

  8. The leniency of the commandments is a wise action. «لو شاء اللّه لاعنتكم انّ اللّه عزیز حكیم»

Ayah

وَلَا تَنكِحُوا۟ ٱلْمُشْرِكَـٰتِ حَتَّىٰ يُؤْمِنَّ ۚ وَلَأَمَةٌ مُّؤْمِنَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِّن مُّشْرِكَةٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ ۗ وَلَا تُنكِحُوا۟ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ حَتَّىٰ يُؤْمِنُوا۟ ۚ وَلَعَبْدٌ مُّؤْمِنٌ خَيْرٌ مِّن مُّشْرِكٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكُمْ ۗ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى ٱلنَّارِ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ يَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلْجَنَّةِ وَٱلْمَغْفِرَةِ بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۖ وَيُبَيِّنُ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (۲۲۱)
Do not marry idolatresses until they embrace faith. A faithful slave girl is better than an idolatress, though she should impress you.
