And do not marry [your daughters] to idolaters until they embrace faith. A faithful slave is better than an idolater, though he should impress you. Those invite [others] to the Fire, but Allah invites to paradise and pardon, by His will, and He clarifies His signs for the people so that they may take admonition. (221)

Key Points

Marriage to non-believing spouses can have a negative impact on the upbringing of children and the future generation of humanity, just as they might act as spies for foreigners. Although the Ayah concerns marriage to non-Muslims, any type of association that puts a person's faith at risk is forbidden.

Messages

  1. Muslims do not have the right to family ties with disbelievers (kuffar). «ولا تنكحوا»

  2. Averting danger is more important than gaining benefit. In marriage to a polytheist (mushrik), two possibilities exist: one, that the polytheist embraces faith; the other, that the believer becomes a polytheist. Since the danger of the believer becoming a polytheist is greater, Islam has prohibited marriage before faith. «لا تنكحوا المشركات حتّى یؤمنّ»

  3. In choosing a spouse, faith is fundamental, and marriage to disbelievers is forbidden. «و لأمة مؤمنة خیر»

  4. In marriage, do not be deceived by the beauty, wealth, or status of others. «و لو اعجبتكم... و لو اعجبكم»

  5. Status, wealth, and beauty do not take the place of faith. «ولواعجبكم»

  6. Value the faithful among the weak and the deprived, and marry them. «لامة مؤمنة... لعبد مؤمن خیر»

  7. Marriage can be a means of inviting others to the path (of Islam). «حتى یؤمنوا»

  8. Instincts must be controlled in the path of the faith. «حتى یؤمنّ... حتّى یؤمنوا»

  9. Faith is the source of value, even if found in a male or female bondservant, and polytheism (shirk) is the symbol of downfall, even if found in a free person. «لامة مؤمنة خیر من مشركة»

  10. The father has the right of guardianship (wilayah) over his daughter's marriage. Regarding taking a son-in-law, the address is directed to the father, telling him not to give his daughter to polytheists. «لا تنكحوا»

  11. First faith, then marriage. Do not think that perhaps they will embrace faith after marriage. «حتى یؤمنّ... حتّى یؤمنوا»

  12. Prevent the infiltration of polytheistic elements into the fabric of Muslim life, and pay attention to the various dangers of such marriages. «اولئك یدعون الى النار»

  13. A bad spouse paves the way for Hell. «یدعون الى النار»

  14. Acting upon divine commands is the path to reaching Paradise. «واللّه یدعو الى الجنّة»

  15. Forgiveness (Maghfirah) is through divine success (Tawfiq) and His permission (Idhn). This is contrary to Christianity, which considers forgiveness to be in the hands of their religious leaders. «و المغفرة باذنه»

  16. Humanity perceives realities instinctively (fitrah). The declaration of rulings is only for remembrance. «لعلّهم یتذكّرون»

Ayah

وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْمَحِيضِ ۖ قُلْ هُوَ أَذًى فَٱعْتَزِلُوا۟ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فِى ٱلْمَحِيضِ ۖ وَلَا تَقْرَبُوهُنَّ حَتَّىٰ يَطْهُرْنَ ۖ فَإِذَا تَطَهَّرْنَ فَأْتُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلتَّوَّٰبِينَ وَيُحِبُّ ٱلْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ (۲۲۲)
They ask you concerning [intercourse during] menses. Say, ‘It is hurtful.’ So keep away from wives during the menses, and do not approach them till they are clean. And when they become clean, go into them as Allah has commanded you. Indeed Allah loves the penitent and He loves those who keep clean. (222)

Key Points

Regarding interaction with women during menstruation (Hayd), the Jews are extremely strict, while the Christians (Nasara) are indifferent. We read in the Torah: Whoever touches a woman during menstruation is unclean until evening. That woman's bed is unclean, and wherever she sits is unclean. Whoever touches her bed must wash his clothes. And whoever touches anything she sat on must wash himself and is unclean until evening. And if a man lies with her, he is unclean for seven days. And if she sees blood for more days than usual, she is unclean. [639] In contrast to this strict ruling of the Torah, the Christians even permit sexual intercourse during menstruation. However, Islam has chosen the path of moderation and balance, and during the days of Hayd, it has only prohibited sexual intercourse, while any kind of socializing or sitting and rising with women is completely permissible.

Islam is a comprehensive religion and has an answer for every question raised in human life. In the previous verses, the phrase "یسئلونك" (They ask you) was repeatedly used to narrate the people's questions to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and state His answers. Sometimes the question is about war, sometimes about gambling and wine, and sometimes about spending (in charity). And in this verse, the question is about matters concerning women. This is a sign of the comprehensiveness of a school of thought, which has rulings even regarding sexual intercourse, stating: "من حیث امركم اللّه" (from where Allah has commanded you), meaning that Allah has a command regarding all issues, whether minor or major, material or spiritual.

Physically, a woman is not prepared for sexual intercourse during menstruation. The malaise and imbalance that occur for the woman make her listless regarding intercourse. Furthermore, during this period, when the woman's uterus is discharging blood, it is not ready to accept sperm. According to scientists, the transfer of blood contaminants through the man's passage might render him permanently sterile. It is also mentioned in narrations: If a child is conceived during this period, he will face physical and spiritual problems. [640]

639) Torah, Book of Leviticus, verses nineteen through twenty-nine. 640) Wasā’il, Vol. 2, p. 568. Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 178.

Messages

1- Prophets are the reference point and answerer of people's questions. "یسئلونك عن المحیض"

2- In asking questions, shyness is meaningless. "یسئلونك عن المحیض"

3- Stating the reason and philosophy behind rulings is effective in their acceptance. In this verse, the reason for the prohibition of intercourse is stated first, and then the command not to approach is given. "هو اذىً فاعتزلوا النساء"

4- Divine rulings are based on preserving benefits and repelling harms. "هو اذًى فاعتزلوا النساء فى المحیض"

5- Menstruation is a difficult period for the woman. "هو اذىً"

6- Seeking pleasure from one's spouse also has limits and boundaries. "فاعتزلوا النساء فى المحیض"

7- The husband must be considerate of his wife. Now that she is in discomfort, you should not seek gratification. "فاعتزلوا النساء فى المحیض"

8- Sexual intercourse must be accompanied by the observance of hygiene. "حتى یطهرن"

9- In intercourse with one's spouse, Divine rulings must be observed. "فاذا تطهّرن فاتوهنّ من حیث امركم اللّه"

10- If intercourse occurred and you repented, Allah will forgive you. "انّ اللّه یحبّ التّوابین"

11- Repentance and turning back from error is the path to being loved by Allah. "یحبّ التوّابین"

12- We should express our affection and love towards those who regret (their actions) and repent. "انّ اللّه یحبّ التوابین"

Ayah

نِسَآؤُكُمْ حَرْثٌ لَّكُمْ فَأْتُوا۟ حَرْثَكُمْ أَنَّىٰ شِئْتُمْ ۖ وَقَدِّمُوا۟ لِأَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّكُم مُّلَـٰقُوهُ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۲۲۳)
Your women are a tillage for you, so come to your tillage whenever you like, and send ahead for your souls, and be Godwary, and know that you will encounter Him; and give good news to the faithful. (223)

Key Points

In this Ayah, women are likened to a farm or cultivated field that nurtures the seed of the man within themselves, and after 9 months, presents the flower of the child to the garden of humanity. Just as humanity perishes without cultivation and farming, human society also vanishes without the existence of women. Women are not tools for lust, but rather the secret to the survival of the human race. Therefore, it is necessary that during sexual intercourse, the human goal should be the production of a pure generation that is a reserve for the Day of Judgment, and that a sacred goal is pursued even in such moments and states. The phrase «واتّقوا اللّه» (and fear Allah) warns against seeking sexual gratification through unauthorized means and encourages acting in a way that ensures honor on the Day of Judgment, delivering offspring and a generation who will be the embodiment of righteous deeds and goodness on that Day.

Messages

1- A suitable spouse is like a suitable farm, and the health of the seed is a condition for better production. «نسائكم حرث لكم»

2- Both the seed and the land play an important role in production. Women and men also have a fundamental role in the survival of the future generation. «حرث لكم»

3- Alongside seasonal prohibitions, there are long-term benefits. In the previous Ayah, He said: «فاعتزلوا» (keep away); in this Ayah, He says: «فأتواحرثكم» (go into your tillage).

4- In intercourse, the man must go to the woman. «فاتوا حرثكم»

5- Instincts must also take a divine direction. If the goal of intercourse is pure children who are a reserve for the Day of Judgment, then it takes on a divine color. «قدّموا لانفسكم»

It is mentioned in Sahih Bukhari: The friends of Allah intend during intercourse to have children who will be strivers (in the way of Allah).

6- If you deliver a pure generation, you yourselves will benefit. «قدّموا لانفسكم»

7- Woman is neither a commodity nor a source of darkness; rather, she is the founder of civilizations, the investor in history, and the sender of gifts for the Hereafter. «نساؤكم حرث لكم، قدّموا لانفسكم»

8- Procreation is the main goal of marriage. «قدّموا لانفسكم»

9- Observe piety in sexual matters. «فاتوا حرثكم... واتقوا اللّه»

10- Carnal desires must be controlled by piety. «أنّى شئتم... واتقوا اللّه»

11- Belief in the Hereafter is the best means of achieving piety. «اتّقوا اللَّه واعلموا انّكم ملاقوه»

Ayah

وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ عُرْضَةً لِّأَيْمَـٰنِكُمْ أَن تَبَرُّوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ وَتُصْلِحُوا۟ بَيْنَ ٱلنَّاسِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲۲٤)
Do not make Allah an obstacle, through your oaths, to being pious and Godwary, and to bringing about concord between people. And Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (224)

Key Points

In several commentaries, including Majma' al-Bayan and Ruh al-Bayan, regarding the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Ayah, it is stated that a dispute arose between the son-in-law and the daughter of one of the companions of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, named Abdullah ibn Rawahah. He swore an oath that he would not interfere at all to reconcile their affairs. The Ayah was revealed and prohibited such oaths. The word 'aymān' (أیمان) is the plural of 'yamīn' (یمین), meaning 'oath' or 'swearing'. And the word 'urḍah' (عُرضة) means 'to expose' or 'to make subject to'. Do not make an oath a means to justify or escape your responsibility, and do not use Allah and His sacred Name on every occasion. This act is itself a form of audacity and disrespect. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "لَا تَحْلِفُوا بِاللَّهِ صَادِقِينَ وَ لَا كَاذِبِينَ" [641] Do not swear by Allah, neither truthfully nor falsely.

641) Bihar, Vol. 76, p. 47.

Messages

1- Respect for the Name of Allah and sacred things must be maintained. «وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا اللَّهَ عُرْضَةً لِأَيْمَانِكُمْ»

2- An oath should not become an obstacle to performing good deeds and reconciling people. «لَا تَجْعَلُوا اللَّهَ عُرْضَةً لِأَيْمَانِكُمْ»

3- High values and good deeds should not be abandoned due to baseless excuses. «أَن تَبَرُّوا»

4- Be aware that Allah hears all utterances and knows what is unspoken. Be accountable for every word and thought. «سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ»

Ayah

لَّا يُؤَاخِذُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِٱللَّغْوِ فِىٓ أَيْمَـٰنِكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن يُؤَاخِذُكُم بِمَا كَسَبَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ (۲۲٥)
Allah will not take you to task for what is unconsidered in your oaths, but He will take you to task for what your hearts have incurred, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-forbearing. (225)

Key Points

Oaths taken out of inattention, or during anger, or without thought and intention (will), or due to excessive talking, haste, or a slip of the tongue, have no legal validity. Responsibility (liability) exists only for oaths taken consciously, in a normal state, in the sacred Name of Allah, for the purpose of performing a beneficial act. Such oaths are legally binding (obligatory to fulfill), and breaking them is forbidden (Haram). The expiation (Kaffarah) for breaking them consists of: feeding ten poor people, or clothing them, or freeing a slave. If none of these options are possible, then fasting for three days. [642]

642) Ma'idah, 89.

Messages

1- Human responsibility is dependent upon his will and choice. Allah forgives slips that occur from a person under abnormal circumstances. «لا یؤاخذكم اللّه باللغو»

2- Motivation and intention (Niyyah) are the criteria for reward and punishment. «كسبت قلوبكم»

3- One of the manifestations of Divine forbearance (Hilim) and forgiveness (Maghfirah) is overlooking a person's unintentional errors. «لا یؤاخذكم اللّه... غفور رحیم»

Ayah

لِّلَّذِينَ يُؤْلُونَ مِن نِّسَآئِهِمْ تَرَبُّصُ أَرْبَعَةِ أَشْهُرٍ ۖ فَإِن فَآءُو فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۲۲٦)
For those who forswear their wives shall be a waiting [period]of four months. And if they recant, Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. (226)

Key Points

The word īlāʾ (ایلاء) means swearing an oath to abandon sexual intercourse. Some men in the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah) used to do this to put their wives in a difficult situation, thereby placing them under psychological and instinctual pressure. They were neither useful spouses themselves nor did they free their wives by divorcing them. This Ayah gives men a four-month opportunity to clarify the status of their wives: either they retract their oath and live with their wives, or they officially divorce them. Of course, this four-month period is not out of respect for the wrongful oath, but rather because, even under normal circumstances, no man has the right to abstain from intercourse for more than four months.

Messages

1- Islam is the protector of the oppressed. Throughout history, women have been subjected to oppression and the violation of their rights, and the Quran has repeatedly supported them. «للذین یؤلون من نسائهم»

2- Combating the foundations of superstitious and ignorant (Jāhilī) traditions is among the fundamental duties of the Prophets. «للذین یؤلون»

3- Man sometimes uses the most sacred names as a pretext for the worst actions. «یؤلون»

4- Attention to the rights and the psychological and instinctual needs of women is a fundamental principle. «تربّص اربعة اشهر»

5- A period of opportunity is necessary for individuals to return (to reason) and make a wise decision. «اربعة اشهر»

6- Instead of inviting people to divorce, they should be encouraged to continue their life together. Men and women should know that returning to married life is the key to receiving Allah's forgiveness and mercy. «فان فاءُو فانّ اللّه عفور رحیم»

Ayah

وَإِنْ عَزَمُوا۟ ٱلطَّلَـٰقَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲۲۷)
But if they resolve on divorce, Allah is indeed all-hearing, all-knowing. (227)

Key Points

This short Ayah, while considering divorce permissible, also contains a warning and a threat in its meaning: if men decide upon divorce, the promise of forgiveness and mercy is no longer applicable, and Allah is Hearing and Knowing regarding their deeds. He knows whether lust compelled the husband to misuse the law of divorce, or whether the family situation truly necessitated their separation.

Messages

1- Islam accepts divorce, despite all its bitterness and ugliness, but it does not accept leaving the wife in a state of uncertainty. «و ان عزموا الطلاق»

2- The decision for divorce rests with the man. «عزموا»

3- In divorce, serious intention is necessary. «عزموا»

4- Avoid lust and decisions that ruin a woman's life. «فانّ اللّه سمیع علیم»

Ayah

وَٱلْمُطَلَّقَـٰتُ يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنفُسِهِنَّ ثَلَـٰثَةَ قُرُوٓءٍ ۚ وَلَا يَحِلُّ لَهُنَّ أَن يَكْتُمْنَ مَا خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ فِىٓ أَرْحَامِهِنَّ إِن كُنَّ يُؤْمِنَّ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۚ وَبُعُولَتُهُنَّ أَحَقُّ بِرَدِّهِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ إِنْ أَرَادُوٓا۟ إِصْلَـٰحًا ۚ وَلَهُنَّ مِثْلُ ٱلَّذِى عَلَيْهِنَّ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۚ وَلِلرِّجَالِ عَلَيْهِنَّ دَرَجَةٌ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۲۲۸)
Divorced women shall wait by themselves for three periods of purity [after menses], and it is not lawful for them to conceal what Allah has created in their wombs if they believe in Allah and the Last Day; and their husbands have a greater right to restore them during this [duration], if they desire reconcilement. The wives have rights similar to the obligations upon them, in accordance with honourable norms; and men have a degree above them, and Allah is all-mighty and all-wise. (228)

Key Points

The term qur' (قُرء) is used both to mean purification (being clean) and to mean menstruation. However, if it is pluralized as qurū' (قُروء), it means purification, and if it is pluralized as aqrā' (اَقراء), it means menstruation. The waiting period (‘iddah) guarantees the preservation of family rights. This is because during this period, there is an opportunity for reflection and returning to the initial life, and emotions subside. Furthermore, it becomes clear whether the woman is pregnant or not, so that if she decides to marry another man, the lineage of the subsequent man is not confused with that of her previous husband. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Regarding the issue of menstruation, purification, and pregnancy, the woman's own testimony is sufficient, and no witness, oath, or other evidence is required. However, it is forbidden for women to state anything contrary to the truth. They must state their condition openly, for if they conceal it, they do not believe in Allah and the Day of Judgment. The husband's return (rujū‘) to his wife during the ‘iddah period is not subject to any special formalities, unlike divorce (talāq), which requires many conditions and formalities. Of course, this period is specific to a group of women, but the divorce of pregnant women, menopausal women, or women with whom sexual intercourse has not taken place, has specific rulings that will follow.

Messages

1- A woman does not have the right to marry another immediately upon divorce. «و المطلقات یتربّصن»

2- In divorce, children must not be deprived of their rights. «ما خلق اللّه فى ارحامهنّ»

3- The best guarantee for the implementation of laws is faith in Allah. «لا یحلّ لهنّ... ان كُنّ یؤمنّ»

4- The priority for continuing the life together belongs to the first husband. «بعولتهنّ احقّ بردّهنّ»

5- Good intention in returning (rujū‘) is a principle. The husband must not return merely for sexual matters and then become indifferent again. «ان ارادوا اصلاحاً»

6- Responsibility brings rights. Whoever takes on a duty also acquires a right. For example, parents have a duty toward their children and therefore also have a right over their children. «ولهنّ مثل الّذى علیهنّ»

7- Justice must be observed in dealing with women. «و لهنّ مثل الّذى علیهنّ»

8- During the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah), laws were definitively detrimental to women, and Islam compensated for those detriments. «لهنّ مثل الذى علیهنّ» The word ‘alayhinna (علیهن) implies detriment/duty.

9- Discrimination is forbidden under equal conditions, but differentiation due to differences in fulfilling duties, talents, or needs is permissible. «وللرّجال علیهنّ درجة»

10- Men do not have the right to misuse their power, as the ultimate power belongs to Allah. «عزیز حكیم»

11- Observing the ‘iddah is among the wise divine programs. «واللّه عزیز حكیم»

Ayah

ٱلطَّلَـٰقُ مَرَّتَانِ ۖ فَإِمْسَاكٌۢ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ تَسْرِيحٌۢ بِإِحْسَـٰنٍ ۗ وَلَا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَن تَأْخُذُوا۟ مِمَّآ ءَاتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ شَيْـًٔا إِلَّآ أَن يَخَافَآ أَلَّا يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا فِيمَا ٱفْتَدَتْ بِهِۦ ۗ تِلْكَ حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ فَلَا تَعْتَدُوهَا ۚ وَمَن يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۲۲۹)
[Revocable] divorce may be only twice; then [let there be] either an honourable retention, or a kindly release. It is not lawful for you to take back anything from what you have given them, unless the couple fear that they may not maintain Allah’s bounds; so if you fear they would not maintain Allah’s bounds, there is no sin upon them in what she may give to secure her own release. These are Allah’s bounds, so do not transgress them, and whoever transgresses the bounds of Allah—it is they who are the wrongdoers. (229)

Key Points

During the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), divorcing a wife and returning to her (revocation/Ruju') was a common practice without limitation. However, Islam permitted a maximum of three divorces and two revocations (Ruju') in order to preserve the sanctity of the woman and the family. [643] In Islam, divorce is a detested matter and has been named the worst of permissible acts (Halal), but sometimes disagreement reaches a point where it is impossible for the two parties to continue life together. In this Ayah, in addition to revocable divorce (Talaq Raj'i), which is initiated by the man, the concept of Khul' divorce, which is a proposal for divorce initiated by the woman, is also presented. This means that the woman sacrifices her dowry (Mahr) or something else in exchange for her freedom and obtains the divorce. The multiplicity of divorces is based on the multiplicity of revocations and the continuation of the marriage. If someone says in a single session, "I have divorced three times," in reality, only one divorce has occurred, because he has only severed one marriage bond. Therefore, based on the jurisprudence (Fiqh) of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, multiple divorces must occur in multiple stages and cannot take place all at once. This is because it is not expedient for family life to be permanently dissolved in a single session and with one decision. [644]

643) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 644) It was with attention to this very issue that Sheikh Mahmoud Shaltut, the Grand Mufti of Egypt and head of Al-Azhar University, said: "Regarding the issue of divorce, I prefer Shi'a jurisprudence (Fiqh)."

Messages

1- When severing a relationship with others, the final decision should not be taken all at once; rather, the door must be left open for reflection and return. «الطلاق مرّتان»

2- Harming or causing damage to one's spouse is forbidden. The man should not revoke the divorce (Ruju') with the intention of taking advantage of her or inflicting psychological harm, only to abandon her afterward. «فامساك بمعروف»

3- In marriage, one must avoid personal and unfamiliar tastes and submit to ordinary and customary life. «امساك بمعروف»

4- Compensate for the bitterness of separation with the sweetness of a gift and kindness (Ihsan). «او تسریح باحسان»

5- Divorce should not be a cause for malice, violence, or revenge; rather, if divorce is necessary, goodness and kindness (Ihsan) are essential conditions. «أو تسریح باحسان»

6- The principle in life is maintaining the spouse, not abandoning her. The word «امساك» (retention) always precedes the word «تسریح» (release). «فامساك بمعروف او تسریح باحسان»

7- Rights and rulings (Ahkam) in Islam are not separate from ethics and emotion. The words «احسان» (kindness) and «معروف» (customary goodness) are signs of this fact. «فامساك بمعروف او تسریح باحسان»

8- Observing rights is necessary. The man does not have the right to take back anything from the woman's dowry (Mahr), which is her definite right. «لا یَحلّ لكم أن تأخذوا ممّا آتَیتموهنّ شیئاً»

9- The desires of the man and the woman must be within the framework of the limits set by Allah. «الاّ ان یخافا اَلاّ یقیما حدود اللّه»

10- The opinion of the husband and wife alone regarding divorce is not sufficient; rather, others must also have no hope for the continuation of their life together. «یخافا... فان خفتم» Sometimes the husband and wife say: "We do not have moral compatibility," but this statement arises from their excitement. Therefore, the Quran, alongside the fear of the couple «یخافا», brings in the involvement of the people so that they too perceive this incompatibility. «خفتم»

11- In decision-making, the main focus must be the establishment of the limits set by Allah and the fulfillment of duties. If the limits set by Allah are observed, the marriage should remain; otherwise, divorce should be considered. «فان خفتم ألاّ یقیما حدود اللّه»

12- The provision for Khul' divorce is so that the woman can extricate herself from a deadlock. «افتدت به»

13- Islam does not agree with the continuation of life through imposition, coercion, or compulsion. If the woman is under so much pressure that she is willing to waive her dowry (Mahr) to be released, Islam has opened the way for her. However, the man is not obligated to accept the Khul' divorce. «فلا جناح علیهما فیما افتدت به»

14- Breaking the Divine law is oppression (Dhulm). «و من یتعد حدوداللّه فاولئك هم الظّالمون»

Ayah

فَإِن طَلَّقَهَا فَلَا تَحِلُّ لَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدُ حَتَّىٰ تَنكِحَ زَوْجًا غَيْرَهُۥ ۗ فَإِن طَلَّقَهَا فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَآ أَن يَتَرَاجَعَآ إِن ظَنَّآ أَن يُقِيمَا حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ يُبَيِّنُهَا لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (۲۳٠)
And if he divorces her, she will not be lawful for him thereafter until she marries a husband other than him, and if he divorces her, there is no sin upon them to remarry if they think that they can maintain Allah’s bounds. These are Allah’s bounds, which He clarifies for a people who have knowledge. (230)

Key Points

In Islam, some regulations are designed to prevent hasty decisions. For example: A: Whoever intends to divorce his wife, the wife must be in a state of purity, and the divorce must be in the presence of two just witnesses. He must pay the wife's expenses for a period, and the wife can present herself to the man by adorning herself during this period. Each of these conditions can prevent hasty divorces. B: People used to crowd the Prophet for private conversations. Allah commanded that whoever has a private conversation with the Prophet must give some charity (sadaqah) and then converse. This law was to prevent the raising of unnecessary questions. [645] In any case, the condition of marrying another husband and his divorcing her, in order for her to become the wife of the first husband again, is another example of the "speed bump" laws in Islam.

645) Al-Mujadilah, 12.

Messages

1- Men should not misuse their authority and must know that their authority regarding divorce is limited. It is narrated from Imam Reza علیه السلام that He said: Do not take divorce lightly and do not harm women. [646] «فلا تحلّ له»

2- The hope for life based on divine rulings is a condition for marriage. «ان ظنّا ان یقیما حدود اللّه»

3- Muslims should know that the limits of Allah are not only in prayer (salat), charity (zakat), Hajj, and Jihad; observing family matters is also among the limits of Allah. «تلك حدود اللّه»

4- Not everyone knows the secrets and wisdom behind the divine regulations. Ordinary people see the outward appearance of the commands, while the knowledgeable see their spirit and depth. «لقوم یعلمون»

646) Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih, Vol. 3, p. 502.

Ayah

وَإِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَبَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ سَرِّحُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ ۚ وَلَا تُمْسِكُوهُنَّ ضِرَارًا لِّتَعْتَدُوا۟ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُۥ ۚ وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوٓا۟ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ هُزُوًا ۚ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَآ أَنزَلَ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَٱلْحِكْمَةِ يَعِظُكُم بِهِۦ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۲۳۱)
When you divorce women and they complete their term [of waiting], then either retain them honourably or release them honourably, and do not retain them maliciously in order that you may transgress; and whoever does that certainly wrongs himself. Do not take the signs of Allah in derision, and remember Allah’s blessing upon you, and what He has sent down to you of the Book and wisdom, to advise you therewith. Be wary of Allah and know that Allah has knowledge of all things. (231)

Key Points

Wherever the potential for deviation, oppression, and danger is greater, the warnings and recommendations must also be greater. Although in the two preceding verses He stated: «فامساك بمعروف او تسریح باحسان» (to retain [them] with kindness or release [them] with goodness), He states again in this verse: «فامسكوهنّ بمعروف او سرّحوهنّ بمعروف» (then retain them with kindness or release them with kindness). This is because the psychological state of the man and woman at the time of separation is not normal, and the potential for revenge and causing harm is very wide, and the danger of oppression and transgression is abundant. In the oppression and transgression against the rights of the woman, the man himself also suffers. The man and the woman are part of one body in the system of creation, and oppression against one member is oppression against all members. Whoever oppresses the woman has gone forth to meet divine punishment, and thus has also oppressed himself.

Messages

1- Islam desires a life accompanied by purity and affection; retaining a wife with the intention of harming her is forbidden (Haram). «ولاتمسكوهنّ ضراراً لتعتدوا»

2- Those who mistreat their wives are transgressors. [647] «لتعتدوا»

3- Oppression against the woman is oppression against oneself. «فقد ظلم نفسه»

4- Marriage is one of the great signs of Allah [648] and this sacred law should not be mocked through unnecessary divorces or by retaining a wife with the intention of harming her. «ولا تتخذوا آیات اللّه هزواً»

5- Instead of grudges, weaknesses, and bitterness, remember the blessings. Do not consider minor family problems significant compared to the millions of divine blessings. «واذكروا نعمت اللّه علیكم»

6- To improve marital relations, attention to the rulings and admonitions of the heavenly Book and piety (Taqwa) is necessary. «واذكروا... ما انزل علیكم من الكتاب و الحكمة یعظكم به واتقوا اللّه»

7- Whoever knows himself to be in the presence of God refrains from sin. «واتقوا اللّه واعلموا ان اللّه بكل شى ء علیم»

8- If your goal in divorce is oppression of the wife and misuse, you must know that Allah is aware. «انّ اللّه بكل شى ء علیم»

647) Those who transgress the limits and rights of others do not receive the love and affection of Allah. »انّ اللّه لایحّب المعتدین« [Al-Baqarah, 190].

648) و من ایاته ان خلق لكم من أنفسكم أزواجاً لتسكنوا الیها و جعل بینكم مودّة و رحمةً (And among His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquility in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy.) It is among the divine signs that He created for you, from yourselves, spouses so that they may be a means of tranquility for you, and He placed affection and mercy between you and them. [Ar-Rum, 21].

Ayah

وَإِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَبَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَلَا تَعْضُلُوهُنَّ أَن يَنكِحْنَ أَزْوَٰجَهُنَّ إِذَا تَرَٰضَوْا۟ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ يُوعَظُ بِهِۦ مَن كَانَ مِنكُمْ يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۗ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَزْكَىٰ لَكُمْ وَأَطْهَرُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۲۳۲)
When you divorce women and they complete their term, do not hinder them from [re]marrying their husbands, when they honourably reach mutual consent. Herewith are advised those of you who believe in Allah and the Last Day. That will be more decent and purer for you, and Allah knows and you do not know. (232)

Key Points

If divorced women agree and marry their former husbands, it has a great effect on the purity and purification of the individual and society. «ذلكم ازكى لكم واطهر» This is because, in this type of marriage, their secrets remain hidden, the children return to the embrace of their real parents and guardians, and since the husband and wife have tasted the bitter experience of divorce, they are concerned about separating again.

Messages

1- The opinion of the divorced woman regarding the choice of a husband is respected, and permission from others is not required for remarriage. «فلاتعضلوهنّ»

2- Do not be pessimistic forever just because of one divorce. It is possible that after the divorce, both parties regret it and are ready for a renewed life in a suitable manner. «ینكحهنّ ازواجهنّ»

3- The main condition for marriage is the mutual consent of both parties. «تراضوا بینهم»

4- Agreement on marriage must be based on rational and just principles. «تراضوا بینهم بالمعروف»

5- Those who obstruct and create problems in the path of others' legitimate marriage should doubt their faith in Allah and the Day of Judgment. «لا تعضلوهنّ... ذلك یوعظ به من كان منكم یؤمن باللّه والیوم الاخر»

6- Do not prevent natural and legitimate attractions. It is possible that a man and woman separated by divorce still have sweet memories of each other and recall those memories occasionally. If they do not reunite naturally and legitimately, there is a danger of deviation. «ینكحهنّ ازواجهنّ... ذلكم ازكى لكم و اطهر»

7- To maintain the health of society, divorced women should not remain without a spouse. «ینكحن... ذلكم ازكى لكم و اطهر»

8- The blessings of remarriage and returning to the first spouse, as well as the harms of divorce and being without a spouse, are beyond human comprehension. «واللّه یعلم وانتم لا تعلمون»

Ayah

۞ وَٱلْوَٰلِدَٰتُ يُرْضِعْنَ أَوْلَـٰدَهُنَّ حَوْلَيْنِ كَامِلَيْنِ ۖ لِمَنْ أَرَادَ أَن يُتِمَّ ٱلرَّضَاعَةَ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱلْمَوْلُودِ لَهُۥ رِزْقُهُنَّ وَكِسْوَتُهُنَّ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۚ لَا تُكَلَّفُ نَفْسٌ إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۚ لَا تُضَآرَّ وَٰلِدَةٌۢ بِوَلَدِهَا وَلَا مَوْلُودٌ لَّهُۥ بِوَلَدِهِۦ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱلْوَارِثِ مِثْلُ ذَٰلِكَ ۗ فَإِنْ أَرَادَا فِصَالًا عَن تَرَاضٍ مِّنْهُمَا وَتَشَاوُرٍ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا ۗ وَإِنْ أَرَدتُّمْ أَن تَسْتَرْضِعُوٓا۟ أَوْلَـٰدَكُمْ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذَا سَلَّمْتُم مَّآ ءَاتَيْتُم بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۗ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۲۳۳)
Mothers shall suckle their children for two full years—that for such as desire to complete the suckling—and on the father shall be their maintenance and clothing, in accordance with honourable norms. No soul is to be tasked except according to its capacity: neither the mother shall be made to suffer harm on her child’s account, nor the father on account of his child, and on the [father’s] heir devolve [duties and rights] similar to that. And if the couple desire to wean with mutual consent and consultation, there will be no sin upon them. And if you want to have your children wet-nursed, there will be no sin upon you so long as you pay what you give in accordance with honourable norms, and be wary of Allah and know that Allah watches what you do. (233)

Key Points

Regarding the father, mother, and child, the words "أب" (Ab) and "اُمّ" (Umm) are not used in this Ayah; rather, "والد" (Walid) and "والده" (Walidah) are used. This is because the words "أب" and "اُمّ" include the uncle, the teacher, and the father-in-law. Just as the wives of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله are the Ummahat al-Mu'minin (Mothers of the Believers), not their Walidat (biological mothers). Since the preceding verses related to divorce and the separation of husband and wife, it is necessary that the status of infants and newborns also be clarified, so that they do not become victims of parental disputes. Attention to the mother's emotions, the importance of mother's milk, the natural need of the infant for milk, and the duration thereof, are addressed in this Ayah.

Messages

1- Islam is a comprehensive religion. It even has a program for the proper feeding of the infant with mother's milk. «والوالدات یرضعن»

2- Even a divorced mother has priority over others in suckling her infant. «والوالدات یرضعن»

3- The duration of complete suckling is two years. «حولین كاملین»

4- The financial and material rights of the mother and the wet-nurse must be paid in return for suckling the child. «وعلى المولود له رزقهن و كسوتهن... اذا سلّمتم ما آتیتم»

5- The amount of expenditure must be based on recognized custom and according to one's ability. «بالمعروف لا تكلّف نفس الاّ وسعها»

6- Responsibility is based on human power and capacity. «لا تكلّف نفس الاّ وسعها»

7- The child should not be a cause of harm to the parents. «لا تضارّ والدة بولدها»

8- The mother's livelihood must be secured during the suckling period, even if the child's father has passed away. «وعلى الوارث مثل ذلك»

9- Weaning the child requires consultation and agreement between the parents. «و ان ارادا فصالا عن تراض منهما و تشاور»

10- The husband and wife must consult regarding the affairs of the newborn. «تشاور»

11- In suckling, the mother comes first, then the wet-nurse. «والوالدات یرضعن... ان اردتم أن تسترضعوا اولادكم»

12- Observing piety (taqwa) in the feeding of children is the responsibility of the parents. «...واتقوا اللّه»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُتَوَفَّوْنَ مِنكُمْ وَيَذَرُونَ أَزْوَٰجًا يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنفُسِهِنَّ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَعَشْرًا ۖ فَإِذَا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا فَعَلْنَ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِنَّ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۲۳٤)
As for those of you who die leaving wives, they shall wait by themselves four months and ten days, and when they complete their term, there will be no sin upon you in respect of what they may do with themselves in accordance with honourable norms. And Allah is well aware of what you do. (234)

Key Points

In Tafsir Al-Mizan, a report is given regarding the beliefs of various nations and peoples concerning women who lose their husbands. There we read: Some peoples believed that the wife should be burned alive after the husband's death. And others said that she should be buried alive under the earth along with her husband. Some peoples considered the wife's remarriage permanently forbidden. And some did not permit it sooner than one year or nine months. Among some peoples, it was common that if the man made a will, his wife must not marry until the period specified in the will, and some also permitted remarriage from the very moment of death. Amidst these deviant beliefs, in which excess and deficiency are evident, Islam presented its view based on observing the ‘iddah (waiting period) and then allowing marriage to a desired man within the framework of appropriateness and ma'ruf (known good conduct). During the four months and ten days of the ‘iddah of mourning (for death), it becomes clear whether the woman is pregnant or not. Furthermore, if the woman marries another man immediately after the death of her husband, the feelings and emotions of the previous husband's relatives are hurt. Of course, this period is for non-pregnant women; pregnant women must observe the ‘iddah until the time of delivery. The Quran has used the word “ma’ruf” (known good conduct/appropriate) in most cases related to marriage. This indicates that the basis of every decision must be rational and religiously acceptable.

Messages

1- Death is not annihilation, but the complete retrieval of the soul from the body. The word “tawaffa” (توفّى) means complete taking. «یتوفّون منكم»

2- A woman does not have the right to marry another immediately upon the death of her husband. «یتربّصن بانفسهنّ»

3- The husband's relatives do not have the right to interfere in the decisions of widowed women. «لا جناح علیكم فیما فعلن»

4- Belief that all our actions and decisions are under the supervision of Allah is the best guarantee for piety (taqwa). «واللّه بما تعملون خبیر»

Ayah

وَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا عَرَّضْتُم بِهِۦ مِنْ خِطْبَةِ ٱلنِّسَآءِ أَوْ أَكْنَنتُمْ فِىٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ عَلِمَ ٱللَّهُ أَنَّكُمْ سَتَذْكُرُونَهُنَّ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تُوَاعِدُوهُنَّ سِرًّا إِلَّآ أَن تَقُولُوا۟ قَوْلًا مَّعْرُوفًا ۚ وَلَا تَعْزِمُوا۟ عُقْدَةَ ٱلنِّكَاحِ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ أَجَلَهُۥ ۚ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِىٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ فَٱحْذَرُوهُ ۚ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ (۲۳٥)
There is no sin upon you in what you may hint in proposing to [recently widowed] women, or what you may secretly cherish within your hearts. Allah knows that you will be thinking of them, but do not make troth with them secretly, unless you say honourable words, and do not resolve on a marriage tie until the prescribed term is complete. Know that Allah knows what is in your hearts, so beware of Him; and know that Allah is all-forgiving, all-forbearing. (235)

Key Points

‘Arraḍtum (عرّضتم) means speech accompanied by allusion and insinuation, and khiṭbah (خطبة) means proposal/engagement. In this Ayah, expressing the desire for marriage during the waiting period ('iddah) is considered permissible, provided that it is not explicit but rather through insinuation. The "good word" (qawlun ma'rūf) in these instances is that the manner of expressing the desire for proposal should be appropriate to social etiquette and the grief experienced by the bereaved woman. For example, one might say: May Allah have mercy on your husband and grant you patience, but others who will love you like your former husband will be found.

Messages

1- Islam is a natural religion (dīn-e fiṭrī), and humans are naturally inclined toward marriage. Therefore, Islam permits this desire to be expressed, even during the 'iddah period, through insinuation so that the feelings of the relatives are not hurt. «لا جناح علیكم فیما عرّضتم»

2- Pay attention to instincts and psychological excitements, and instead of suppressing instincts, guide and warn the youth. «علم اللّه انّكم ستذكرونهنّ ولكن لاتواعدوهنّ سرّاً»

3- Paying attention to the time and circumstances when presenting proposals is a fundamental principle. Proposing explicitly to a grieving woman during her 'iddah period is impolite, tasteless, and a form of audacity. «عرّضتم... تقولوا قولا معروفا»

4- Whoever knows that Allah is aware of what is within him will practice piety (taqwa). «یعلم ما فى انفسكم فاحذروه»

5- The recommendation for piety (taqwa) is intended in all circumstances, especially concerning marital issues. «فاحذروه»

6- Allah compensates for your lack of patience through His forbearance (Ḥilm). You are so hasty that you approach a bereaved woman, even during her 'iddah period, but Allah, through His forbearance, has not prohibited this action entirely. «انّ اللّه غفور حلیم»

7- Following a warning, also provide the opportunity for repentance/return. «فاحذروه... غفور حلیم»

Ayah

لَّا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِن طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ مَا لَمْ تَمَسُّوهُنَّ أَوْ تَفْرِضُوا۟ لَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً ۚ وَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ عَلَى ٱلْمُوسِعِ قَدَرُهُۥ وَعَلَى ٱلْمُقْتِرِ قَدَرُهُۥ مَتَـٰعًۢا بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۖ حَقًّا عَلَى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۲۳٦)
There is no sin upon you if you divorce women while you have not yet touched them or settled a dowry for them. Yet provide for them—the well-off according to his capacity, and the poorly-off according to his capacity—with a sustenance that is honourable, an obligation on the virtuous. (236)

Key Points

Some people thought that divorce before sexual intercourse or before determining the dowry (Mahr) was invalid. This Ayah, while correcting this notion, also reminds [them] of the matter of the gift.

Messages

1- Even granting a divorce must be done in an atmosphere of goodwill and kindness. «ان طلّقتم... متعوهنّ... بالمعروف حقّاً على المحسنین»

2- Modesty of speech is a value. Instead of explicitly mentioning marital relations, Allah says: «ما لم تمسّوهنّ»

3- Determining and paying the dowry (Mahr) is obligatory upon the man. «تفرضوا لهنّ فریضة»

4- Compensate the bitterness of divorce with a suitable gift. Although sexual intercourse has not occurred, divorce is considered a type of psychological pressure for the woman, which must be compensated with kindness and a worthy gift. «متّعوهنّ»

5- The marriage contract (Aqd) holds sanctity and respect. Even though intercourse has not occurred, the execution of the marriage formula grants the woman the right to receive a suitable gift upon separation. «متّعوهنّ»

6- Maintaining fairness and justice by both the man and the woman is intended. Just as the woman's distress must be compensated with a suitable gift, the man should also not be pressured because of the gift, and everyone must act according to his ability. «على الموسع قدره و على المقتر قدره»

7- The man's duty towards the family is according to his ability. «على الموسع قدره و على المقتر قدره»

8- Respecting the social standing of the wife is necessary. The word «معروف» (Ma'ruf) means that customary and social matters must be observed when giving the gift. «متاعا بالمعروف»

9- Avoid extremism and negligence in life matters. «بالمعروف»

10- A gift to the wife is among the signs of the virtuous. «متاعا بالمعروف حقّا على المحسنین»

11- A physical gift (commodity) is better than cash. The word «متاع» (Mata') instead of Dirham and Dinar is a sign that the gift should be a commodity. «متاعا بالمعروف»

12- The gift that the husband gives to the wife should not be considered an imposition, a ransom, or charity; it is a type of right the woman has over the man. «حقّاً على المحسنین»

13- Ethics are the foundation of divine rulings. «حقّاً على المحسنین»

Ayah

وَإِن طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَمَسُّوهُنَّ وَقَدْ فَرَضْتُمْ لَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً فَنِصْفُ مَا فَرَضْتُمْ إِلَّآ أَن يَعْفُونَ أَوْ يَعْفُوَا۟ ٱلَّذِى بِيَدِهِۦ عُقْدَةُ ٱلنِّكَاحِ ۚ وَأَن تَعْفُوٓا۟ أَقْرَبُ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ ۚ وَلَا تَنسَوُا۟ ٱلْفَضْلَ بَيْنَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۲۳۷)
And if you divorce them before you touch them, and you have already settled a dowry for them, then [pay them] half of what you have settled, unless they forgo it, or someone in whose hand is the marriage tie forgoes it. And to forgo is nearer to Godwariness; so do not forget graciousness among yourselves. Indeed Allah watches what you do. (237)

Messages

1- When discussing marital issues within the family, observe modesty of speech. «تمسّوهن»

2- Stipulating and paying the dowry (mahr) is obligatory upon the man. «فریضة»

3- Forgiveness and pardoning are desirable from both the woman and the man. Either the woman forgives half the dowry, or the man gives the entire dowry. «یعفون... تعفوا»

4- The permission for a woman's marriage rests with her guardian. «بیده عقدة النكاح»

5- Those who show generosity (or pardon) are closer to piety (taqwa). «وان تعفوا اقرب للتّقوى»

6- Divorce must be accompanied by forgiveness and grace. «تعفوا... لاتنسوا الفضل»

7- During divorce, moral issues and human dignities must not be forgotten. «ولا تنسوا الفضل بینكم»

Ayah

حَـٰفِظُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلصَّلَوَٰتِ وَٱلصَّلَوٰةِ ٱلْوُسْطَىٰ وَقُومُوا۟ لِلَّهِ قَـٰنِتِينَ (۲۳۸)
Be watchful of your prayers, and [especially] the middle prayer, and stand in obedience to Allah; (238)

Key Points

Regarding the meaning of al-Salat al-Wusta (the middle prayer), there are several theories, but considering the occasion of the revelation (Shān Nuzūl) of the Ayah—that a group did not attend the Dhuhr (noon) prayer due to the heat—and considering the narrations (Riwāyāt) and exegeses (Tafāsīr), the intended meaning is the Dhuhr prayer itself. The attention given to prayer amidst the verses related to family matters indicates that instincts and the financial and legal demands of one's spouse should not distract you from prayer. The preservation (Hifz) of everything must be appropriate to its nature; preserving wealth is protecting it from the thief's grasp, preserving the body is protecting it from microbes, preserving the soul is protecting it from moral afflictions like greed, envy, and arrogance, and preserving one's child is protecting him from bad company. But what is the preservation of prayer? The preservation of prayer is knowing its secrets, performing it on time, performing it correctly, learning its rulings and etiquette, preserving the centers of worship, and maintaining mental focus during it. In a Hadith, we read: Prayer says to one group, "You wasted me, may Allah waste you," and it says to another group, "You preserved me, may Allah preserve you." [649]

649) Kāfī, vol. 3, p. 268.

Messages

1- Establishing prayer must be continuous. «حافظوا»

2- All people are responsible for preserving the prayer. «حافظوا»

3- Establish the prayer in congregation. «حافظوا», «قوموا»

4- Wherever there is a potential for negligence or heedlessness, a greater warning is necessary. «والصلوة الوسطى»

5- Establishing prayer requires attention, vitality, knowledge, and sincerity. «قوموا لِلّه قانتین»

6- Man should not be a discordant patch in existence. The Quran says about existence: «كل له قانتون» [650] (All are obedient to Him). Therefore, if we are not qānit (obedient/submissive), we will be a discordant patch in existence. «قوموا للّه قانتین»

7- The value of prayer lies in its humility (Khushū'). «قانتین»

650) Rūm, 26.

Ayah

فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ فَرِجَالًا أَوْ رُكْبَانًا ۖ فَإِذَآ أَمِنتُمْ فَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَمَا عَلَّمَكُم مَّا لَمْ تَكُونُوا۟ تَعْلَمُونَ (۲۳۹)
and should you fear [a danger], then [pray] on foot or mounted, and when you are safe remember Allah, as He taught you what you did not know. (239)

Key Points

In the Quran, sometimes the word dhikr (remembrance) is used instead of the word Salat (prayer), as He says regarding the Friday Prayer: «فاسعوا الى ذكر اللّه» (Hasten to the remembrance of Allah). Or Allah says to Moses عليه السلام: «اقم الصلوة لذكرى» (Establish the prayer for My remembrance). In this verse too, the intent of «فاذكروا اللّه» is prayer. Indeed, the philosophy and spirit of prayer is the remembrance of Allah. This verse refers to the Prayer of Fear (Salat al-Khawf), which is established during wartime conditions with specific rulings detailed in jurisprudence (Fiqh). We read in the narrations that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله prayed using gestures during the Battle of the Confederates (Ahzab), and Imam Ali عليه السلام commanded that during some battles, people should pray using signs and gestures. Imam Kadhim عليه السلام, in response to a person who asked: If a predatory animal attacks us and the time for prayer is short, what should we do? He replied: Pray in the condition you are in, even if it means facing away from the Qibla. [651]

651) Tafsir Namuneh, Vol. 2, p. 148 and Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 2, p. 239.

Messages

1- Prayer is never dropped (nullified) under any circumstances. «فان خفتم فرجالا او ركبانا»

2- There is no hardship or difficulty (Usr wa Haraj) in Islam. When it is not possible to stabilize the body, or stand facing the Qibla, or fulfill other conditions, those conditions are removed. «فرجالا او ركبانا»

3- Prayer is gratitude for blessings. «فاذكروااللّه كما علّمكم»

4- There are some matters that a person does not know but can acquire through experience. But there are other matters that a person does not know and cannot know, except through revelation. «ما لم تكونوا تعلمون» (what you did not know) and He did not say: «لا تعلمون» (you do not know).

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُتَوَفَّوْنَ مِنكُمْ وَيَذَرُونَ أَزْوَٰجًا وَصِيَّةً لِّأَزْوَٰجِهِم مَّتَـٰعًا إِلَى ٱلْحَوْلِ غَيْرَ إِخْرَاجٍ ۚ فَإِنْ خَرَجْنَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِى مَا فَعَلْنَ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِنَّ مِن مَّعْرُوفٍ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۲٤٠)
Those of you who die leaving wives shall bequeath for their wives providing for a year, without turning them out; but if they leave, there is no sin upon you in respect of what they may do with themselves in accordance with honourable norms. And Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (240)

Key Points

Some commentators believe that the revelation of this Ayah was before the Ayah of the waiting period (Iddah) [652] and before the Ayah of inheritance, and that with the revelation of those Ayahs, this Ayah was abrogated (Naskh) or, in other words, the period of acting upon it came to an end. However, others believe that the Ayahs of Iddah and inheritance address the necessary and obligatory amount—that a woman has a certain right to inheritance and that she is obligated to observe the waiting period for a certain duration. But this Ayah can be considered a recommended (Mustahabb) act alongside the obligatory duty. Meaning that, in addition to the duration of the Iddah, if the wife wishes to remain in the house for up to one year out of respect for her husband, the husband, out of respect for his wife, should pay her one year's expenses—in addition to her share of the inheritance—from his own one-third (Thulth), over which he has the right of bequest. [653]

652) Al-Baqarah, 234. 653) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Men should bequeath a portion of their wealth to their wives. «والذین یتوفون... وصیّة لازواجهم»

2- In a bequest, one's wife has priority over others. «وصیّة لازواجهم»

3- The future of widowed women must be secured. «متاعاً الى الحول»

4- Any decision made by the woman regarding remarriage must be rational, lawful, and based on ascertained benefit (Maslahah). «فعلن فى انفسهنّ من معروف»

5- The legislation of divine rulings is based on wisdom. «عزیز حكیم»

Ayahs

وَلِلْمُطَلَّقَـٰتِ مَتَـٰعٌۢ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۖ حَقًّا عَلَى ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۲٤۱)
For the divorced women there shall be a provision, in accordance with honourable norms—an obligation on the Godwary. (241)
كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (۲٤۲)
Thus does Allah clarify His signs to you so that you may exercise your reason. (242)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the method of paying the dowry (mahr) to divorced women, both before and after consummation, was explained. This verse either concerns those women whose dowry was not specified in the contract and who are divorced before consummation, or it is an emotional and ethical recommendation for all cases of divorce, suggesting that in addition to paying the full or half dowry, a separate gift should be paid by the husband to the wife to compensate for her distress and heartbreak.

Of course, this is an ethical and recommendable (mustahabb) instruction that the pious (muttaqin) and the God-fearing ought to observe.

In any case, from the phrases «حقّاً على المحسنین» (a duty upon the doers of good) in Ayah 236, and «حقّاً على المتّقین» (a duty upon the God-fearing) here, and «لا تنسوا الفضل بینكم» (do not forget the favor among yourselves) in Ayah 237, one can deduce Islam's special attention to preserving the rights and feelings of divorced women.

Imam Hassan عليه السلام gave a slave girl as a gift to a woman whom he had divorced. [654]

654) Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 105.

Messages

1- Piety (taqwa) is necessary when dealing with divorced women. «وللمطلّقات... حقّاً على المتّقین»

2- Giving a gift, which serves the role of removing resentment and offering solace, is a sign of the God-fearing. «متاعاً بالمعروف حقّاً على المتّقین»

3- One must reflect upon divine rulings and commands in order to grasp their underlying benefits (masalih). «یبیّن اللّه لكم آیاته لعلكم تعقلون»

Ayah

۞ أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا۟ مِن دِيَـٰرِهِمْ وَهُمْ أُلُوفٌ حَذَرَ ٱلْمَوْتِ فَقَالَ لَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مُوتُوا۟ ثُمَّ أَحْيَـٰهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ (۲٤۳)
Have you not regarded those who left their homes in thousands, apprehensive of death, whereupon Allah said to them, ‘Die,’ then He revived them? Indeed Allah is gracious to mankind, but most people do not give thanks. (243)

Key Points

The context of revelation for this Ayah states that in one of the cities of Sham (Syria), which had a population of about seventy thousand households, the plague appeared and was rapidly destroying people one after another. Meanwhile, a group of people [655] who had the ability and means to migrate left the city hoping to escape death. The Lord destroyed them in that very desert with the same disease.

It is understood from some narrations that the original disease in that city was a form of punishment. Because when their leader and guide asked them to prepare themselves for struggle and Jihad and leave the city, they refused to go to the battlefield, using the excuse that the plague was widespread in the war zone. Allah afflicted them with the very thing they had used as an excuse to flee the war. In some exegeses and narrations [656] it is mentioned: When Hizqil the Prophet, one of the prophets of the Children of Israel, passed by that place, he asked Allah to revive them. Allah answered his prayer and revived them again, and they returned to life. Of course, these divine favors are a warning and a lesson for future generations so that humanity may understand and be grateful to Allah.

The author of Tafsir Al-Manar interpreted the death and life in the Ayah symbolically, considering it a metaphor for gaining and losing independence. The author of Tafsir Al-Mizan, while severely criticizing this way of thinking, states: One must believe in the apparent meaning of the verses, otherwise it would necessitate justifying or interpreting away all miracles and extraordinary events.

Since gratitude (shukr) is not merely verbal, but the reality of gratitude is using divine blessings appropriately, most people are not truly grateful. If the Ayah had said: «اكثرهم لایشكرون» (most of them are not grateful), the meaning would have been that most of those specific people were not grateful. However, the Quran describes the characteristic of most people throughout all ages, not just a specific group: «اكثر النّاس لایشكرون» (most people are not grateful).

Shi'a believe in Raj'ah (the Return) and the revival of a group of individuals before the Day of Judgment, and in addition to hundreds of narrations, they infer the possibility of its occurrence from verses like this Ayah.

655) According to the author of Tafsir Tibyan (Vol. 2, p. 282), their number was more than ten thousand, because Arabs refer to numbers greater than ten thousand as ulūf and less than that as ālāf. 656) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 198.

Messages

  1. Let us look at history and learn lessons from it. «ألَم تَرَ...»
  2. In recounting history, what is important are the factors of glory and downfall, not the names of individuals, tribes, or regions. «الّذین خَرجوا من دیارهم»
  3. Where the Will of Allah exists, escape is ineffective. «قال لهم اللّه موتوا»
  4. Allah has revived the dead many times in this very world. «ثم احیاهم»
  5. The ups and downs, deaths and mortalities, births, and subsequent revivals are all examples of divine favors and grace. «انّ اللّه لذو فضل»

Ayah

وَقَـٰتِلُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲٤٤)
Fight in the way of Allah, and know that Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (244)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that fleeing from battle is not a solution, and the wrath of Allah can encompass thousands of those who flee. This Ayah states: Now that death and life are in His hands, fight in His way, and know that your reward is preserved with Him. For He is aware of whatever befalls you.

Messages

1- Jihad (struggle) and fighting are valuable only when they are for Allah and in the way of Allah. War waged out of revenge, or to display power, exploit others, or expand territory, is not sacred; rather, it is anti-value. «قاتلوا فى سبیل اللّه»

2- Fighting in the way of Allah is gratitude for the blessing of life. The previous Ayah stated: Allah gave them life, but they were not grateful. And this Ayah states: Fight in the way of Allah. Therefore, Jihad is a type of gratitude. «لایشكرون... و قاتلوا»

3- Paying attention to the fact that we are in the presence of Allah is the strongest factor and motivation for fighting on the battlefield. «سمیع علیم»

4- Do not make excuses to flee from Jihad. Because Allah knows your intention and hears your excuses. «سمیع علیم»

Ayah

مَّن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يُقْرِضُ ٱللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا فَيُضَـٰعِفَهُۥ لَهُۥٓ أَضْعَافًا كَثِيرَةً ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَقْبِضُ وَيَبْصُۜطُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۲٤٥)
Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan that He may multiply it for him severalfold? Allah tightens and expands [the means of life], and to Him you shall be brought back. (245)

Key Points

Various commentaries [657] state that this Ayah, following the previous Ayah which encouraged people to engage in Jihad, urges believers towards spending (in charity) and lending (Qardh). Just as sincere effort and Jihad are necessary for the security and protection of society, material aid is also needed to provide for the deprived and to prepare the means for Jihad.

The topic of lending to Allah has been mentioned seven times in the Qur'an.

Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan [658] has stated conditions for Qardh al-Hasanah (a goodly loan). Among them are: 1- It must be from lawful wealth (Halal). 2- It must be from sound wealth. 3- It must be for a necessary expenditure. 4- It must be given without obligation (Minna). 5- It must be without ostentation (Riya). 6- It must be given secretly. 7- It must be paid with love and sacrifice. 8- It must be paid quickly. 9- The lender must be grateful to Allah for this success (Tawfiq). 10- The dignity (honor) of the borrower must be preserved.

The word "Qardh" (قرض) in the Arabic language means cutting, and the reason a loan is called Qardh is that a portion of wealth is cut off and given to others to be taken back later.

The word "Bast" (بسط) means expansion and spaciousness, and "Bisat" refers to goods that are spread out on the ground.

Jihad is sometimes with one's life, which was discussed in the previous Ayah, and sometimes with one's wealth, which is discussed in this Ayah.

The word "Kathir" (كثیر) alongside the word "Adh'af" (اضعاف) is a sign of a very great reward. And the phrase "Yudha'ifuhu" (یضاعفه) instead of "Yadh'afuhu" (یضعفه) is a symbol of exaggeration and increase. [659]

The expression of lending to Allah indicates that the reward for Qardh al-Hasanah is guaranteed by Allah.

Instead of commanding lending, He asks who will lend to Allah, so that people do not feel reluctance or compulsion, but rather lend to others with willingness, desire, and encouragement.

Since man has the instinct for seeking benefit, Allah uses this instinct to motivate man, saying: «فيضاعفه أضعافاً»

Instead of saying "lend to the people," He said, "lend to Allah," so that the poor feel that Allah has put Himself in their place, and they do not feel weakness or humiliation.

Although our existence and everything we possess belongs to Allah, Allah sometimes introduces Himself as the buyer and sometimes as the borrower to encourage us to do this deed.

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The armies of the heavens and the earth belong to Allah, so the Ayah of lending is for your test. [660]

Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: Among the instances of lending to Allah is financial aid to the infallible Imam. [661]

Although Qabdh (constriction) and Bast (expansion) are attributed to Allah in the Ayah, we read in narrations: Whoever is generous towards people, Allah grants him expansion (Bast), and whoever is stingy towards people, Allah constricts (Qabdh) things for him.

In narrations, the reward for Qardh al-Hasanah is stated to be eighteen times, while the reward for Sadaqah (charity) is ten times. [662] The reason for this difference is explained as follows: loans are taken by those in need, but gifts and donations are sometimes given to those who are not needy. [663]

According to narrations: Whoever is able to lend and does not, Allah forbids the scent of Paradise upon him. [664]

When this Ayah was revealed: «من جاء بالحسنة فله خير منها» [665] (Whoever brings a good deed will have better than it as a reward), the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله requested an increase from Allah. The Ayah was revealed: «فله عشر أمثالها» [666] (He will have ten times its like). He requested again, and the Ayah of Qardh al-Hasanah was revealed with the phrase «أضعافاً كثيرة». The Prophet realized that the "Kathir" (much) that Allah decrees is beyond counting. [667]

Allah's reward for lenders is both in this world and the Hereafter. This is because alongside «أضعافاً كثيرة», He says: «وإليه يرجعون» (And to Him they will be returned). It is as if the reckoning of the Day of Judgment is separate from the worldly rewards.

Misinterpretation and misunderstanding must be prevented. If, at the beginning of the Ayah, Allah asks people for a loan in an emotional tone, immediately following it He says: «واللّه يقبض و يبسط» (And Allah constricts and expands), lest a group, like the Jews, imagine that Allah is poor and say: «إنّ اللّه فقير و نحن أغنياء» [668] (Indeed, Allah is poor, and we are rich). They should also know that Allah's borrowing is for the growth of man, not because of His need.

The hypocrites used to say: Do not spend on the Muslims so that they disperse from around the Messenger of Allah. The Qur'an replied to them: What belief do they hold? Do they not know that the treasures of the heavens and the earth are in the hand of Allah! [669]

657) Tafsir Kabir, Kashif, and Namuneh. 658) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, under Ayah 11 of Surah Al-Hadid. 659) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. 660) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 183. 661) Tafsir Ruh al-Bayan, Vol. 1, p. 381. 662) Bihar, Vol. 74, p. 311. 663) Bihar, Vol. 100, p. 138. 664) Bihar, Vol. 100, p. 138. 665) An-Naml, 89. 666) Al-An'am, 160. 667) Tafsir Al-Mizan, Vol. 2, p. 310. 668) Aal-i Imran, 181. 669) Al-Munafiqun, 7.

Messages

1- Helping Allah's creation is helping Allah. «يُقْرِضُ اللَّهَ» instead of "lending to the people."

2- Encouragement is necessary to motivate people towards good deeds. «فَيُضَاعِفَهُ أَضْعَافًا كَثِيرَةً»

3- If we know that expansion and constriction are in the hand of Allah, we will spend easily. «وَاللَّهُ يَقْبِضُ وَيَبْسُطُ»

4- If we know that we will return to Him and that we will receive back whatever we have given, we will spend easily. «إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلْمَلَإِ مِنۢ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مُوسَىٰٓ إِذْ قَالُوا۟ لِنَبِىٍّ لَّهُمُ ٱبْعَثْ لَنَا مَلِكًا نُّقَـٰتِلْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ هَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ إِن كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْقِتَالُ أَلَّا تُقَـٰتِلُوا۟ ۖ قَالُوا۟ وَمَا لَنَآ أَلَّا نُقَـٰتِلَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَقَدْ أُخْرِجْنَا مِن دِيَـٰرِنَا وَأَبْنَآئِنَا ۖ فَلَمَّا كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقِتَالُ تَوَلَّوْا۟ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۲٤٦)
Have you not regarded the elite of the Israelites after Moses, when they said to their prophet, ‘Appoint for us a king that we may fight in the way of Allah.’ He said, ‘May it not be that you will not fight if fighting were prescribed for you?’ They said, ‘Why should we not fight in the way of Allah, when we have been expelled from our homes and [separated from] our children?’ So when fighting was prescribed for them, they turned back except a few of them, and Allah knows well the wrongdoers. (246)

Key Points

"Mala'" (مَلأ) refers to the dignitaries and nobles whose awe fills the eyes and the hearts (chests). The Children of Israel, after Prophet Moses عليه السلام, due to breaking the law and seeking luxury, once again fell under the domination and pressure of the tyrants (Taghut), losing their freedom and their land. To escape displacement and to get out from under the yoke of the tyrants, they decided to fight and asked their Prophet Samuel (Ashmu'il) to select a commander and a leader for them so that they could fight the tyrants under his leadership. However, despite all these claims, when the command was issued, all of them, except for a few, turned their backs on the battlefield and disobeyed that order.

Messages

1- Muslims must carefully observe the history of the Children of Israel and take heed. «ألم تَر» (Have you not seen?)

2- The presence of a Divine leader prevents dispersion, displacement, and submission to oppression. «من بعد موسى» (after Moses)

3- Some prophets had a mission only for a group of people, not for all mankind. «اذ قالوا لنبىّ لهم» (when they said to a prophet of theirs) [670]

4- The military commander must be chosen and appointed by the heavenly leader. «ابعث لنا» (Appoint for us). Therefore, religion is not separate from politics.

5- A successful war requires a competent commander. «ابعث لنا ملكا» (Appoint for us a king/commander).

6- To be saved from the tyrants (Taghuts), one must seek refuge in the Divine Prophets. «قالوالنبى لهم» (They said to a prophet of theirs).

7- It is necessary to obtain commitment from those who propose reformative suggestions. «هل عسیتم» (Is it possible for you/Might you).

8- The leader must be forward-looking and anticipate the possibility of people breaking their covenant and fleeing responsibility. «هل عسیتم»

9- Jihad (struggle) to remove oppression and defend the homeland is Jihad in the way of Allah. «ومالنا الاّ نقاتل فى سبیل اللّه و قد اخرجنا من دیارنا وابنائنا» (And why should we not fight in the way of Allah when we have been expelled from our homes and our children?)

10- The emergence of problems causes awakening and movement toward liberation. «اخرجنا» (We have been expelled).

11- The field of struggle is the factor for evaluating pretentious individuals. «توّلوا» (They turned away).

It is good when the touchstone of experience is brought forth, so that whoever has deceit within him may be disgraced.

12- Covenant breakers and those who give slogans without action are oppressors (Zalimin). «علیم بالظالمین» (Knower of the oppressors).

13- Allah is All-Knowing, so why all this pretense?! «واللّه علیمٌ» (And Allah is All-Knowing).

(670) If He had said: "Nabi'ihim" (their prophet), the indication that he was a specific prophet would have been less strong; but since He said: "li-Nabiyyin lahum" (to a prophet of theirs), this indication is intensified.

Ayah

وَقَالَ لَهُمْ نَبِيُّهُمْ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَدْ بَعَثَ لَكُمْ طَالُوتَ مَلِكًا ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ عَلَيْنَا وَنَحْنُ أَحَقُّ بِٱلْمُلْكِ مِنْهُ وَلَمْ يُؤْتَ سَعَةً مِّنَ ٱلْمَالِ ۚ قَالَ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱصْطَفَىٰهُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَزَادَهُۥ بَسْطَةً فِى ٱلْعِلْمِ وَٱلْجِسْمِ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ يُؤْتِى مُلْكَهُۥ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ وَٰسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲٤۷)
Their prophet said to them, ‘Allah has appointed Saul as king for you.’ They said, ‘How can he have kingship over us, when we have a greater right to kingship than him, as he has not been given ample wealth?’ He said, ‘Indeed Allah has chosen him over you, and enhanced him vastly in knowledge and physique, and Allah gives His kingdom to whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-knowing.’ (247)

Key Points

The word "Wāsiʿ" (واسع) means that Allah is Self-Sufficient (Ghani) and is not incapable of any kind of bestowal. Although the Children of Israel asked their Prophet for a military commander to lead the war: «مَلكاً نقاتل فى سبیل اللّه» (a king whom we may fight in the way of Allah), since they made excuses and said, "What business does Talut have with kingship?" it becomes clear that he was appointed not only as their military commander but also as their king (Sultan). Although this oppressed group of the Children of Israel had requested their Prophet to appoint a commander to free them from the clutches of the oppressors, when their Prophet appointed Talut—who was a young shepherd, poor, and unknown—as their commander and ruler, they said: How can he be our commander when he has neither fame nor wealth? And we are more deserving than him because of the wealth and possessions we have! When the Prophet heard their excuses regarding Talut's poverty and lack of means, he said: Undoubtedly, Allah has chosen him over you, and He possesses the scientific ability, physical power, and necessary strength for commanding the war. Allah grants commandership to whomever He wills based on their merit and hidden talents. Indeed, if His gaze falls upon a stone, it turns into a jewel; if it falls upon a thorn, it becomes a flower; and if He looks upon a poor person, he becomes a commander. Does a shepherd whose sheep get lost deserve commandership? It is mentioned in narrations: Talut was a shepherd who had come from the outskirts toward the city in search of his lost sheep. He came into the presence of the Prophet, and Allah revealed to the Prophet that this very shepherd was worthy of commandership, and that he should introduce him to the people. Perhaps the intent of the phrase «بسطة فى العلم» (abundance in knowledge) is the comprehensiveness of sciences. Meaning, he possessed extensive information in many fields of knowledge.

Messages

1- Prophets choose their successors based on revelation. «ان اللّه قد بعث»

2- A leader appointed by Allah is for the benefit of the people. «بعث لكم»

3- If you wish to achieve freedom and salvation, you must accept the Divine leader. «انّ اللّه قد بعث لكم طالوت ملكا»

4- Religion is not separate from politics. Prophets directly intervened in military matters and appointed and dismissed military commanders. «انّ اللّه قد بعث لكم»

5- Do not overlook the hidden talents in unknown and poor individuals. «قد بعث لكم»

6- The sign of true faith is submission to Allah and the Messenger, but the Children of Israel were not submissive due to a false assumption. «انّى یكون له الملك علینا»

7- One of the Divine tests is how we interact with the Divine leader. «قالوا انّى یكون...»

8- Self-superiority is condemned. (671) «نحن احقّ بالملك منه»

9- Name, status, and urban residency are not signs of superiority. This verse criticizes those who consider wealth and status as the criterion for superiority. «لم یؤت سعة من المال»

10- Divine selection is based on merit, not extravagance or futility. «ان اللّه اصطفاه علیكم وزاده بسطة فى العلم والجسم»

11- Scientific power and physical ability are two necessary conditions for commanding an army. «بسطة فى العلم و الجسم»

12- Scientific ability is more important than physical ability. «زاده بسطة فى العلم و الجسم» The word 'knowledge' (علم) came first, followed by 'body' (جسم).

13- Being the most knowledgeable (A'lam) is a fundamental principle in selection. «بسطة فى العلم»

14- A leader and manager must justify their actions to their subordinates, explain the reason for their work and selection, and resolve ambiguities, rather than saying, "It is my right to do whatever I wish." The Prophet stated the reason for choosing Talut as his scientific and physical ability. «زاده بسطة فى العلم و الجسم»

15- Neither poverty and obscurity prevent Divine grace, nor do wealth and fame cause it. «واللّه واسعٌ علیم»

16- The source of most people's objections is narrow-mindedness and short-sightedness, whereas Allah's knowledge is infinite and unlimited. «واسع علیم»

(671) Just as Satan said: »انا خیرٌ منه« (I am better than him). Al-A'raf, 12.

Ayah

وَقَالَ لَهُمْ نَبِيُّهُمْ إِنَّ ءَايَةَ مُلْكِهِۦٓ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمُ ٱلتَّابُوتُ فِيهِ سَكِينَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَبَقِيَّةٌ مِّمَّا تَرَكَ ءَالُ مُوسَىٰ وَءَالُ هَـٰرُونَ تَحْمِلُهُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۲٤۸)
Their prophet said to them, ‘Indeed the sign of his kingship shall be that the Ark will come to you, bearing tranquillity from your Lord and the relics left behind by the House of Moses and the House of Aaron, borne by the angels. There is indeed a sign in that for you, should you be faithful.’ (248)

Key Points

In order for the Jews to gain confidence and certainty in the leadership of Talut (Saul), their prophet told them: the sacred Ark of the Children of Israel will be returned to them, as a sign of Talut's selection by Allah. The Ark (Tābūt) referred to the wooden chest in which the mother of Moses placed her infant and, by God's command, cast it into the Nile River. Pharaoh's agents retrieved the chest and took the infant along with it to Pharaoh. That chest remained in Pharaoh's court. When Moses (peace be upon him) attained prophethood, he placed the Tablets of the Torah inside it, and upon his passing, he also placed his armor and other relics inside it and entrusted it to his successor, "Yusha' ibn Nun" (Joshua son of Nun). This chest, which had become a sacred chest known as the Ark of the Covenant, was among the Children of Israel, and they carried it at the forefront of their armies during battles. However, gradually its sanctity was violated, and it was lost. This stolen chest was returned to them by Allah, through the angels, during the leadership of Talut, so that it might be a source of tranquility and reassurance for them. [672]

672) Information about this chest is mentioned in the Torah, Book of Exodus, Chapter 37.

Messages

1- The source of tranquility is Allah, even if it comes through means (objects). «فیه سكینة من ربكم»

2- Man always needs tranquility, especially on the verge of entering the battlefield of Jihad. «فیه سكینة من ربكم» [673]

3- A divine leader requires a divine sign. Since Talut was chosen by Allah, a divine sign was necessary to confirm him. «تحمله الملائكة»

4- If a chest that was in contact with the body of Moses and the Tablets of the Torah is sacred and brings tranquility, then considering sacred the chest placed over the grave of the Divine Saints is not contrary to the Quran. This is because in all those chests found in the holy shrines, there is «بقیّة ممّا ترك آل محمّد صلى الله علیه وآله» (a remnant of what the family of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله left behind). «بقیة ممّا ترك آل موسى و آل هارون»

5- Preserving the relics of the Prophets is valuable and is a source of reassurance and blessing. «بقیةممّاترك»

673) The word "Sakīnah" (tranquility) in this Ayah, considering the preceding verses where the Children of Israel were preparing for Jihad and defense, indicates the importance and necessity of mental peace on the front lines.

Ayah

فَلَمَّا فَصَلَ طَالُوتُ بِٱلْجُنُودِ قَالَ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍ فَمَن شَرِبَ مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ مِنِّى وَمَن لَّمْ يَطْعَمْهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنِّىٓ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱغْتَرَفَ غُرْفَةًۢ بِيَدِهِۦ ۚ فَشَرِبُوا۟ مِنْهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ ۚ فَلَمَّا جَاوَزَهُۥ هُوَ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ قَالُوا۟ لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا ٱلْيَوْمَ بِجَالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِۦ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ ٱللَّهِ كَم مِّن فِئَةٍ قَلِيلَةٍ غَلَبَتْ فِئَةً كَثِيرَةًۢ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (۲٤۹)
As Saul set out with the troops, he said, ‘Allah will test you with a stream: anyone who drinks from it will not belong to me, but those who do not drink from it will belong to me, barring someone who draws a scoop with his hand.’ But they drank from it, [all] except a few of them. So when he crossed it along with the faithful who were with him, they said, ‘We have no strength today against Goliath and his troops.’ Those who were certain that they would encounter Allah said, ‘How many a small party has overcome a larger party by Allah’s will! And Allah is with the patient.’ (249)

Key Points

The word junūd (جنود) is the plural of jund (جُند), meaning army/host. The presence of several armies after groups of people turned away indicates the large size of their population. This Ayah reports the failure of a large group of the Children of Israel who did not pass the test of drinking water and enduring thirst. However, this remaining group had another test, which was a spiritual and moral test. When they faced the massive army of Goliath (Jalut), they said: We do not have the ability to confront this armed host. But those who believed in the Day of Judgment said: Victory is in the hands of Allah. How often a small group, by the permission of Allah, overcomes large armies.
