Victory in one stage of the test is not enough. Individuals succeed in some stages but fail in another stage. In this story, a group did not accept the leader because of poverty, a group failed the test of the stomach (appetite), and a group lost heart when confronting the enemy.

In the beginning, Talut did not attribute the group to himself and did not say: junūduh (his armies), but rather said: bil-junūd (with the armies). Although the armies were numerous and had accepted his command, he had not yet tested the extent of their obedience. But after the test, He attributes them to Himself: fa-innahu minnī (he is of me).

Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: The individuals who remained loyal to Talut until the end were three hundred and thirteen people. (674)

The status of the two groups is clear: whoever does not drink is definitely one of us, and whoever drinks is definitely an outsider. But those who drank with a handful are neither one of us nor an outsider. They should neither be made desperate nor included in the favors (of the loyal). Therefore, for man ightarafa ghurfatan bi-yadih (whoever takes a scoop with his hand), neither the word minnī (is of me) nor laysa minnī (is not of me) was used.

Those whose hands reached the wealth of the people or government funds, but who passed by it with dignity and asceticism, and whose greed and avarice did not corrupt them, are the Party of Allah (Hizbullah). And those who secure their backs by hoarding wealth are not true believers.

«فمن شرب منه فلیس منّى و من لم یطعمه فانّه منّى»

674) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 251.

Messages

  1. One of the duties of a military commander is to separate the combat forces from the spectators. «فصل طالوت بالجنود»
  2. One of the ways of testing is seasonal prohibitions. Comfort-seeking and the inability to endure seasonal and temporary pressures and hardships are incompatible with soldiery. «انّ اللّه مبتلیكم بنهر»
  3. Before confronting and engaging the enemy, one must practice resistance. «مبتلیكم بنهر»
  4. A leader must reject unproductive forces. «فمن شرب منه فلیس منّى»
  5. Submission to the commander is the secret to success. «من لم یطعمه فانّه منّى»
  6. The consideration of essential need is separate from the consideration of complete comfort. «الاّ من اغترف غرفةً بیده»
  7. Those who emerge victorious from the field of trial are few. «الا قلیلاً»
  8. Being revolutionary is not important; remaining revolutionary is important. In this incident, there were many who chanted slogans, but those who succeeded in the tests were few. «الاّقلیلاًمنهم»
  9. Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and the divine promises creates the ability to stand firm against hardships and difficulties. «ملاقوا اللّه»
  10. Faith has degrees. Although a group was with Talut, believed in him, and refrained from drinking the water; «آمنوا معه» not every believer is a fighter. Only believers who have faith in meeting Allah (Liqā’ Allāh) are fighters. «یظنّون انّهم ملاقوا اللّه»
  11. The abundance of the enemy's numbers and equipment is nothing compared to the Will of Allah. «كم من فئة قلیلة غلبت فئة كثیرة باذن اللّه»
  12. Qualitative forces are superior to quantitative forces. «كم من فئة قلیلة غلبت...»
  13. The sincere believer knows that victory comes from Allah. «باذن اللّه»
  14. If Muslims are patient and steadfast, they should not fear the large numbers of the enemy, because Allah is with the patient. «واللّه مع الصابرین»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا بَرَزُوا۟ لِجَالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِۦ قَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَٱنصُرْنَا عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۲٥٠)
So when they marched out for [encounter with] Goliath and his troops, they said, ‘Our Lord, pour patience upon us, make our feet steady, and assist us against the faithless lot.’ (250)

Messages

1- Always, and especially on the battlefronts, prayer must accompany action, not replace action. «برزوا لجالوت... ربّنا افرغ...»

2- Patience (steadfastness) and victory are inseparable. Victory comes as a result of patience. «صبراً... وانصرنا»

3- We should begin our supplications with «ربّنا». «ربّنا افرغ...»

4- During hardships, man is in great need of patience. «افرغ» means abundant descent (pouring forth). The word «صبراً» is also presented in the indefinite form (nakerah), which is a sign of great patience.

5- Passing tests and achieving success in them should not make you arrogant; rather, continue to seek help from Allah. «ثبت اقدامنا»

6- Man's duty is movement and effort, but victory is in the hands of Allah. «وانصرنا»

7- Victory in war is valuable only when the goal of the fighters is the supremacy of truth over falsehood, not merely the supremacy of one group over another. «وانصرنا على القوم الكافرین»

Ayah

فَهَزَمُوهُم بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ وَقَتَلَ دَاوُۥدُ جَالُوتَ وَءَاتَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُلْكَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَعَلَّمَهُۥ مِمَّا يَشَآءُ ۗ وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ ٱللَّهِ ٱلنَّاسَ بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍ لَّفَسَدَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۲٥۱)
Thus they routed them with Allah’s will, and David killed Goliath, and Allah gave him kingdom and wisdom and taught him whatever He liked. Were it not for Allah’s repelling the people by means of one another, the earth would surely have been corrupted; but Allah is gracious to the world’s people. (251)

Key Points

Based on narrations, David (عليه السلام) attained the station of prophethood as a result of this very courage and bravery, and his son Solomon (عليه السلام) also became one of Allah's prophets. We read in narrations that the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) said: Allah wards off calamity from one hundred thousand neighboring houses because of one righteous Muslim. Then that حضرت recited this Ayah: «فلولا دفع اللّه الناس ...» (675) Considering the previous verses, it becomes clear that the factors for victory are several: 1- A capable and worthy leader. «زاده بَسطةً فى العلم والجسم» 2- Believing followers. «قال الّذین یظنّون انّهم ملاقوا اللّه» 3- Reliance (on Allah). «كم مِن فئة قلیلة غَلبت فئة كثیرة باذن اللّه» 4- Patience and steadfastness. «ربّنا أفرغ علینا صبراً و ثبّت اقدامنا» 5 - Having a divine motivation. «وانصُرنا على القوم الكافرین»

675) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 1, p. 764.

Messages

1- Although the effort and struggle were yours, the defeat of the enemy is by the hand of Allah. «فهزموهم باذن اللّه»

2- The sincere supplications of the fighters and mujahidin are answered. «وانصرنا على القوم الكافرین. فهزموهم باذن اللّه»

3- Unless there is talent, merit, sacrifice, and a brilliant record, no one receives special divine favor. «وقتل داود جالوت واتاه اللّه الملك و الحكمة»

4- In the battlefield and jihad, the sharp edge of the attack should be directed toward the enemy's command. «قتل داود جالوت»

5- Keep alive the names of those who showed valor in war. «قتل داود»

6- The destruction of the leader of disbelief is equivalent to the scattering of his army. «فهزموهم... قتل داود جالوت»

7- If defense is not mounted against the aggressor and the oppressor, corruption and ruin will engulf the earth. «لولا دفع اللّه الناس... لفسدت الارض»

8- War against the corruptors is a necessity. If the harmful element is not eliminated, other elements will be dragged into ruin. «لولا دفع اللّه الناس... لفسدت الارض»

9- The Will and Favor of Allah are realized through natural means. «دفع اللّه... بعضهم ببعض»

10- The ruling of jihad and defense against the aggressor is a divine favor. «اللّه ذوفضل على العالمین»

Ayah

تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱللَّهِ نَتْلُوهَا عَلَيْكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ وَإِنَّكَ لَمِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۲٥۲)
These are the signs of Allah, which We recite for you in truth, and you are indeed one of the apostles. (252)

Key Points

This Ayah marks the end of the second Juz' of the Quran and states that the stories and events mentioned in the preceding verses are a source of admonition and learning.

The story of the death of thousands of people in a single moment and their subsequent revival through the prayer of the Prophet, the granting of the position of leadership to an unknown shepherd youth, yet one who was worthy and knowledgeable, the victory of a small group over a massive group of the enemy, the bravery and courage of a young man in battle and the granting of the status of prophethood to him—all of these are manifestations of the Divine signs (Ayat) and indications of the truthfulness of the speech and the mission (Risalah) of the Prophet of Islam.

Ayah

۞ تِلْكَ ٱلرُّسُلُ فَضَّلْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۘ مِّنْهُم مَّن كَلَّمَ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَرَفَعَ بَعْضَهُمْ دَرَجَـٰتٍ ۚ وَءَاتَيْنَا عِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَأَيَّدْنَـٰهُ بِرُوحِ ٱلْقُدُسِ ۗ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مَا ٱقْتَتَلَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتُ وَلَـٰكِنِ ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فَمِنْهُم مَّنْ ءَامَنَ وَمِنْهُم مَّن كَفَرَ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مَا ٱقْتَتَلُوا۟ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَفْعَلُ مَا يُرِيدُ (۲٥۳)
These are the apostles, some of whom We gave an advantage over others: of them are those to whom Allah spoke and some of them He raised in rank, and We gave Jesus, son of Mary, manifest proofs and strengthened him with the Holy Spirit. Had Allah wished, those who succeeded them would not have fought one another after the manifest proofs had come to them. But they differed. So there were among them those who had faith and there were among them those who were faithless, and had Allah wished, they would not have fought one another; but Allah does whatever He desires. (253)

Key Points

Although according to narrations (Ahadith), the number of Prophets was 124 thousand, the names of only 25 of them are mentioned in the Quran, and the names of others are not mentioned: «منهم من قصصنا و منهم من لم نقصص» [676] The status and ranks (degrees) of the Prophets are not equal, and each possesses a unique position and distinct advantages. For example, the advantages of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله are the finality (of prophethood), being unlettered (Ummi), and the non-distortion of His Book; the advantages of Ibrahim عليه السلام are being an Ummah (nation) unto himself and having blessed progeny; the advantages of Nuh عليه السلام are long life, steadfastness, and receiving a special salutation from Allah. In this Ayah, the advantages of Musa and 'Isa علیهما السلام are stated: that Musa was addressed by the speech of Allah (Kallim Allah), and 'Isa was supported by the Holy Spirit (Ruh al-Qudus). The word "درجات" (degrees/ranks) is an indication of multiple superiorities. Fakhr Razi, in his Tafsir Kabir under this Ayah, has mentioned about twenty advantages for the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله. Among them: the miracle of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله, the Holy Quran, is superior to all other miracles. Another is that the Prophet of Islam is a witness over all the Prophets, as He says: «فكيف اذا جئنا من كلّ امّة بشهيد و جئنا بك على هؤلاء شهيداً» [677]

676) Ghafir, 78. 677) Nisa, 41.

Messages

1- The Prophets possess a very high status. «تلك» (That/Those) refers to distance and high status.

2- The Prophets are not all of the same rank. Unity in purpose does not prevent certain special perfections and specific superiorities. «فضّلنا بعضهم على بعض»

3- Honoring the past Prophets is necessary. Musa and 'Isa علیهما السلام are honored in the Ayah. «من كلّم اللّه... عيسى بن مريم»

4- The tradition and law of Allah is to grant freedom to humanity. He could compel people to follow the path of truth, but true growth occurs under the shadow of freedom. In this Ayah, we read twice that if Allah had willed, no disagreement or conflict would have arisen among the people. «و لو شاء اللّه»

5- The root of most disagreements lies in desires, envy, and self-conceit, not in ignorance or lack of awareness. «من بعد ما جائتهم البیّنات»

6- The path of the Prophets is accompanied by clear evidence. «بیّنات»

7- Doctrinal disagreements are among the factors leading to wars. Of course, religion is not the source of disagreement; rather, people disagree. «ولو شاء اللّه ما اقتتل... ولكن اختلفوا»

8- The difference in people's beliefs is proof of their free will. «فمنهم من امن و منهم من كفر»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَنفِقُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِىَ يَوْمٌ لَّا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ وَلَا خُلَّةٌ وَلَا شَفَـٰعَةٌ ۗ وَٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۲٥٤)
O you who have faith! Spend out of what We have provided you before there comes a day on which there will be no bargaining, neither friendship, nor intercession. And the faithless—they are the wrongdoers. (254)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah has employed several levers to encourage people towards spending (in charity/Infaq): A: What you possess, We gave it to you; it is not inherently yours. «رزقناكم» B: Give a portion of what you possess, not all of it. «ممّا» C: This spending (Infaq) is better for your Hereafter than any friendship. «یأتى یوم...»

Messages

1- Before giving a command, address people respectfully. «یا ایهاالذین امنوا»

2- Utilizing opportunities for good deeds is valuable. «من قبل ان یأتى یوم»

3- Depriving others today means being deprived tomorrow. If no spending (Infaq) or generosity takes place today, then on that Day, there will be no affection, friendship, or intercession. «انفقوا... یوم لابیع فیه...»

4- Remembering the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is a factor encouraging spending (Infaq). «انفقوا... یأتى یوم...»

5- One way to cultivate the spirit of generosity is to pay attention to the fact that man will be empty-handed on the Day of Judgment. «لا بیع ولا خلة ولا شفاعة»

6- Stinginess is a sign of ingratitude for blessings (Kufran al-Ni'mah) and disbelief in divine promises. «انفقوا... والكافرون»

7- Disbelief (Kufr) is a clear example of oppression (Dhulm). «الكافرون هم الظالمون»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ ٱلْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُۥٓ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۲٥٥)
Allah—there is no god except Him—is the Living One, the All-sustainer. Neither drowsiness befalls Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that may intercede with Him except with His permission? He knows what is before them and what is behind them, and they do not comprehend anything of His knowledge except what He wishes. His seat embraces the heavens and the earth and He is not wearied by their preservation, and He is the All-exalted, the All-supreme. (255)

Key Points

Due to the presence of the word "Kursi" (Throne) in the Ayah, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله named this verse "Ayat al-Kursi." It is mentioned in both Shi'a and Sunni narrations that this Ayah is considered the peak of the Qur'an and holds the greatest status among the verses. [678] Furthermore, there is great emphasis on reciting this Ayah. It is narrated from Ali عليه السلام that he said: After hearing the virtue of this Ayah, no night passed for me except that I recited Ayat al-Kursi. [679]

In this Ayah, the Name of Allah and His attributes are mentioned sixteen times. For this reason, Ayat al-Kursi is considered the slogan and message of Tawhid (Monotheism). Although the slogan of Tawhid has been presented many times in the Qur'an with various expressions, such as: «لااله الاّ اللّه» [680], «لااله الاّ هو» [681], «لااله الاّ انت» [682], «لااله الاّ انَا» [683], in none of them are the attributes of Allah mentioned alongside the slogan of Tawhid like they are in Ayat al-Kursi.

The attributes of Allah are of two types. Some attributes are the Essence of His Being and are inseparable from the Divine Essence, such as Knowledge, Power, and Life. But some attributes, which are called attributes of action (Sifat al-Fi'l), relate to the action of Allah, such as creating and forgiving. The source of these types of attributes is the Divine Will; He creates if He wills. However, regarding the attributes of the Essence, one cannot say that Allah knows if He wills and does not know if He does not will. If we wish to draw an analogy for the attributes of the Essence, we must say that Allah being Knowing and Powerful is similar to a human being being created (makhluq). The attribute of being created is never separated from man, just as the attribute of Knowledge is never separated from Allah.

The word Ilah (deity) is used for any object of worship, whether true or false: «أفرایت مَن اتّخذ الهه هواه» [684]. But the word Allah is the Name of the Sacred Divine Essence. Deities other than Allah are incapable of creating a fly: «لن یخلقوا ذباباً» [685]. And those who worship other than Allah fall from the heights of Tawhid into terrifying valleys: «ومن یشرك باللّه فكانّما خرّ من السماء فتخطفه الطّیر» [686].

«لااله الاّ اللّه» is the first page of every Muslim's identity document. The first slogan and invitation of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله was also with this phrase: «قولوا لااله الاّ اللّه تفلحوا» [687]. Just as He said: «من قال لااله الاّ الله مخلصاً دخل الجنّة واخلاصه بها ان تحجزه لااله الاالله عمّا حرمّ اللّه» Whoever sincerely proclaims the slogan of Tawhid enters Paradise, and the sign of his sincerity is that saying «لااله الاّ اللّه» keeps him away from the prohibitions of Allah. [688] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «قول لااله الاّاللّه ثمن الجنة» [689] Saying «لااله الاّ اللّه» is the price of Paradise. Just as Imam Reza عليه السلام narrated in a Hadith Qudsi: «كلمة لااله الاّاللّه حصنى» [690] The word of Tawhid is the strong fortress of Allah. Yes, Tawhid is the source of man's salvation and prosperity. Of course, just as they say, whoever steps into a university or a seminary becomes a scholar—meaning, on the condition that he studies all the necessary lessons. Saying «لااله الاّ اللّه» is saving, but only with its conditions, which will be pointed out in their proper place.

Belief in Tawhid makes all powers and attractions small and insignificant in the eyes of man. An example of the educational effects of Tawhid is that Muslims would not prostrate before kings and rulers. In the court of Negus, the King of Abyssinia, the Muslim refugees said: «لانسجد الاّ للّه» We do not prostrate except to Allah. [691] Dihyah al-Kalbi, another Muslim, did not prostrate in the palace of the Roman Emperor, and in response to the courtiers, he said: «لااسجد لغیراللّه» I do not prostrate for anyone other than Allah. [692] Yes, Tawhid elevates man to such a degree that even Paradise and Hell become secondary concerns for him. The infallible Imams علیهم السلام also strove to nurture such human beings. Ali عليه السلام said: «لاتكن عبد غیرك و قد جعلك اللّه حراً» [693], Do not be the slave of another, for Allah has created you free. It is necessary for Muslims to recite the Adhan (call to prayer) loudly every day and everywhere, proclaiming freedom from the servitude of false gods and tyrants. And they should familiarize the ears of their newborns with the resonance of Tawhid, «لااله الاّ اللّه», before any other sound.

«هوالحىّ» In the seventieth section of the Du'a Jawshan Kabir (The Great Armor Supplication), the Ever-Living nature of Allah is presented thus: «یا حیّاً قبل كلّ حىّ، یا حیّاً بعد كلّ حىّ، یا حىّ الذّى لیس كمثله حىٌّ، یا حىّ الذّى لایشاركه حىٌّ، یا حىّ الذّى لایحتاج الى حىّ، یا حىّ الذّى یمیت كلّ حىّ، یا حىّ الذّى یرزق كلّ حىّ، یا حیّا لم یرث الحیوة من حىّ، یا حىّ الذى یحیى الموتى، یا حىّ یا قیّوم» Yes, Allah is Living, before every living thing and after every living thing. In His being Living, He has no equal or partner, and to remain Living, He is not dependent on another. He causes the living to die and provides sustenance to all living things. He has not inherited life from another living thing, but rather, He grants life to the dead. He is Living and Ever-Abiding. The meaning of Life concerning the Essence of the Lord differs from others, and like His other Divine attributes, it is inseparable from His Essence, and annihilation has no way into it: «و توكّل على الحىّ الّذى لایموت» [694]. In His Life, He is not in need of nourishment, reproduction, absorption, or excretion, which are necessities for the life of creatures such as humans, animals, and plants. «یا حىّ الذى لیس كمثله حىّ»

«القیّوم» (Qayyum), derived from the root Qiyam (standing/subsisting), refers to one who stands on His own and upon whom others depend. [695] The word Qayyum has appeared three times in the Qur'an, and in all three instances, it is placed alongside the word Hayy (Living). His subsistence is from Himself, but the subsistence of all other creatures is dependent on His existence. The meaning of the Lord's Qayyumiyyah (Self-Subsistence and Sustainer) is His complete dominance, protection, and management over creation. His subsistence is permanent and comprehensive; He creates, provides sustenance, guides, and causes death, and He is not heedless for a moment. Every living creature needs a source of grace to continue its life, just like a lamp that needs to be connected to electricity to remain lit. All creatures must be nourished by Al-Hayy to become alive, and they must draw from Al-Qayyum to continue living. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «كلّ شى ء خاضع له و كلّ شى ء قائم به» Everything is submissive to Him, and everything subsists through Him. [696] It is narrated that during the Battle of Badr, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله repeatedly said in prostration: «یا حىّ یا قیّوم» [697].

«لاتأخذه سنةٌ و لانوم» Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «ما من حىّ الاّ و هو ینام خلا اللّه وحده» There is no living thing except that it sleeps, except Allah alone. [698] Allah is Hayy (Living) and has no need for sleep or slumber. [699] Sleep disconnects the living from themselves, let alone from others, but Allah does not sleep and is always the Sustainer (Qayyum) over everything.

«له ما فى السموات و ما فى الارض» The true Owner of everything is He, and man's ownership is, in reality, nothing more than a loan. Man's ownership is temporary and subject to limited conditions determined by the True Owner, namely Allah. Now that everyone is His possession, why should one possession worship another possession? Others are also servants just like us: «عباد امثالكم» [700]. Nature is the dominion of Allah, and the laws governing it are subject to Allah. If only humans would make better use of both His property (milk) and His dominion (mulk). If everything is from Allah and for Allah, then why the stinginess and greed? Has the Creator God abandoned us? «أیحسب الانسان ان یترك سدى» [701]. Imam Kadhim عليه السلام was passing by the house of a person named "Bishr" when He noticed loud noises and music of amusement coming from the house. He asked a maidservant who came out of the house: Who is the master of the house? Is he a servant (of God)?! She replied: No, Sir, he is not a servant, he is free. The Imam said: If he were a servant, he would not commit all this disobedience. When the maidservant entered the house, she relayed the Imam's words to the master. He was shaken and repented. [702] It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that he said: The first degree of piety (taqwa) and servitude to Allah is that man does not consider himself an owner. [703]

«من ذا الذى یشفع عنده» The polytheists accepted Allah: «لئن سئلتهم من خلق السموات و الارض لیقولنّ اللّه» [704], but they considered the idols to be intercessors: «ویقولون هؤلاء شفعاء» [705]. Ayat al-Kursi shatters this illusory refuge and asks: Who is it that can intercede without His permission? Not only does existence belong to Him, but the efficacy of existence is also by His permission. By your imagination and delusion, a creature does not become an intercessor for another. Intercession exists on the Day of Judgment, but it is regulated and by the permission of Allah. If an action originates from someone, it is by the permission and will of Allah, and His Qayyumiyyah (Sustaining power) is not compromised such that an action could take place in a corner away from His Will. Intercession is when a strong entity helps a weak entity. For example, in the system of creation, light, water, air, and earth help the seed of a plant until it reaches the stage of a tree. In the system of reward and punishment, too, the friends of Allah help a sinner to attain salvation. [706] But these aids are never a sign of Allah's weakness or His being influenced. Because it is He Himself who grants the status of intercession and permission for it to His friends. And it is He who created the system of creation in such a way that when a seed is placed on the path of growth, it grows with light, air, and soil. In any case, material factors and the help that man receives from creatures are under the light of His permission and within the framework of the laws He has established. For this reason, some individuals are deprived of intercession: «فما تنفعهم شفاعة الشافعین» [707]. Just as light, heat, water, and soil only cause a seed to grow if it possesses the capacity for growth. The concept of intercession is separate from worldly favoritism and baseless recommendations found in corrupt societies. Intercession is meant to prevent despair and hopelessness and to create a bond between people and the friends of Allah. Intercession is a reward that Allah grants to His friends, and benefiting from it on the Day of Judgment is a manifestation of man's benefit from the light of knowledge and guidance of the Prophets and the friends of Allah in this world. The intercessor is not an independent power against the power of Allah, but rather a reflection of Him, and the status of intercession is reserved for those whom He wills. Therefore, idolaters should not imagine that they will benefit from intercession with the slogan: «هؤلاء شفعائنا» [708]. It is necessary to recall this point: the worship of anyone other than Allah is shirk (polytheism), but calling upon or invoking someone other than Allah is not always shirk. The phrases: «یدعوك» [709], «یدعوكم» [710], «یدعون» [711], «ندع» [712], and «دعاء الرسول» [713], which appear in the Qur'an, none of them are related to shirk. However, not every invocation is valuable. If a sick person calls a doctor, it is right, but if he calls a fortune-teller, it is false.

«یعلم ما بین ایدیهم و ما خلفهم» Allah knows all realities, and intercession before the Lord is not an attempt to prove someone's innocence, but rather an effort for salvation and forgiveness. Allah's awareness should cause man to feel a little shame. He knows what is inside the chests, and He is aware of hidden and manifest secrets. He knows what is inside the mothers' wombs, whether the newborn is male or female. He knows the knowledge of the Day of Judgment and the measure of everything. [714] Whereas our human knowledge is limited. We hear sounds to a certain extent, we see sights to a certain extent, and we do not know the secrets, except for the part that Allah wills and permits.

«وسع كرسیّه السّموات والارض ولایؤده حفظهما و هوالعلى العظیم» In a narration from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, it is stated: The meaning of the 'Arsh (Throne) is the knowledge of Allah that He has made known to the Prophets and Messengers [715], but the Kursi (Footstool/Seat) refers to knowledge that no one knows. [716] Some consider the Kursi to be a metaphor for the power and sovereignty of Allah that encompasses the heavens and the earth. Although the universe is vast, and the earth compared to it is like a ring in a desert, yet with all this, preserving and maintaining them is not burdensome for Allah. Ayat al-Kursi makes people understand that Allah is not only the Owner of the universe and Knower of all His possessions, but the entire existence, with all its creatures, is under His dominion and power. [717] And people should not compare the unlimited power of the Creator with the power of the created. Because fatigue is a consequence of matter, but the ability of Allah is His very Essence. «ان ربّك هو القوى العزیز» [718]. Ali عليه السلام says: Neither the passage of time makes Allah old and worn out, nor does He Himself change or transform. [719] Yes, attention to the unlimited power of Allah creates tranquility in the soul of the believer, and the believer finds himself in immunity and safety through it. Each of Allah's attributes: Qayyum, Hayy, Alim, Qadir, and Azim, plays a constructive role in the education of man. Because the best school of thought is one that gives hope and love to its followers, clarifies their destiny and future, and whose followers know that they are under observation every moment, their slips are forgivable, and their Ruler is Kind. In summary, a God worthy of worship is one who is Living, Ever-Abiding, and Indomitable; who is not afflicted by weakness or fatigue, nor overcome by sleep or slumber; one before whose Will no one has the power to accomplish anything. One who knows everything and has comprehensive control and dominance over everything. Such a God can be Worshipped and Loved, and in such a God one can place trust, feel love, and have hope. The God introduced by the Qur'an in this manner is incomparable to the god of the distorted Gospel and Torah. How do those who are trapped in the narrow and dark dead ends of the material system of the world think? «الحمدللّه الّذى هدانا لهذا و ما كنّا لنهتدى لولا ان هدانا اللّه»

Messages

1- No being other than Him is worthy of being worshipped. «لااله الاّ هو»

2- Real and eternal Life, and all other lives, belong to Him. «الحىّ»

3- Everything is constantly dependent on Him and is never outside of His management for a moment. «القیوم»

4- Everything is from Him. «له ما فى السموات...»

5- Not only does existence belong to Him, but the efficacy of existence is also from Him. «من ذا الّذى یشفع»

6- By your imagination and delusion, nothing becomes an intercessor or mediator. «من ذا الّذى»

7- No one is kinder than Him. The affection and kindness of every intercessor are from Him. «الاّ باذنه»

8- Allah is aware of everything and in every state, so we should feel shame before Him regarding sin. «یعلم مابین ایدیهم وما خلفهم...»

9- He encompasses everything, but others, without His Will, do not encompass even a corner of His knowledge. [720] «لا یحیطون بشى ء من علمه»

10- His sovereignty and power are not limited. «وسع كرسیّه»

11- The protection of existence is not burdensome for Him. «ولایؤده»

12- He who protects existence can protect us against dangers. «حفظهما» Hence, the recitation of Ayat al-Kursi is recommended for protection.

720) «الم یعلم بانّ اللّه یرى» Al-'Alaq, 14.

Ayah

لَآ إِكْرَاهَ فِى ٱلدِّينِ ۖ قَد تَّبَيَّنَ ٱلرُّشْدُ مِنَ ٱلْغَىِّ ۚ فَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِٱلطَّـٰغُوتِ وَيُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ فَقَدِ ٱسْتَمْسَكَ بِٱلْعُرْوَةِ ٱلْوُثْقَىٰ لَا ٱنفِصَامَ لَهَا ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲٥٦)
There is no compulsion in religion: rectitude has become distinct from error. So one who disavows fake deities and has faith in Allah has held fast to the firmest handle for which there is no breaking; and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (256)

Key Points

Sincere faith (Iman) is not achieved through coercion, but rather through proof (Burhan), ethics, and admonition (Maw'izah) one can penetrate hearts. However, this does not mean that everyone is free to commit any evil deed in practice and say, "I am free, and no one has the right to stop me from the path I have chosen." Islamic penal laws, such as discretionary punishments (Ta'zirat), fixed punishments (Hudud), blood money (Diyat), and retribution (Qisas), and obligations such as enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar) and Jihad, indicate that even if someone lacks internal belief, he still has no right to be a harmful individual to society.

How could Islam, which tells the disbelievers: «هاتوا برهانكم ان كنتم صادقین» [721] (Bring your proof if you are truthful in your claim), possibly coerce people into accepting Islam?

Jihad in Islam is either for fighting against tyrants (Taghuts) and breaking oppressive regimes that do not allow nations the freedom of thought, or it is for eradicating polytheism (Shirk) and superstition, which is essentially a disease, and silence in the face of it is an injustice to humanity.

According to narrations, one of the manifestations of holding fast to the «عروة الوثقى» (the firmest handhold) and the strong Divine rope is connection with the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah) and the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام.

The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله said to Hadrat Ali علیه السلام: «انت العروة الوثقى» (You are the firmest handhold). [722]

721) بقره، ۱۱۱.

722) تفسیر برهان، ج ۱، ص ۱۴۱ .

Messages

1- A religion that possesses proof and logic has no need for compulsion or coercion. «لااكراه فى الدین»

2- The effect of force is on actions and movements, not on thoughts and beliefs. «لااكراه فى الدین»

3- The path of truth has been separated from falsehood so that the proof (Hujjah) may be complete for the people. The clarification of the path of truth is achieved through intellect, revelation, and miracles. «قد تبیّن الرشد من الغى» Islam is the religion of guidance (Rushd).

4- Religion is the source of humanity's growth (Rushd). «قد تبیّن الرشد من الغى»

5- Islam does not compromise with arrogance (Istikbar). «یكفر بالطاغوت»

6- Monotheism (Tawhid) will not manifest until the tyrants (Taghuts) are eradicated. First disbelief in the Taghut, then faith in Allah. «فمن یكفر بالطاغوت ویؤمن باللّه»

7- Disbelief in the Taghut and faith in Allah must be continuous. «یكفر، یؤمن» (the imperfect tense) indicates continuity.

8- The strength of the Divine rope is not enough; holding fast to it firmly is also a condition. «فقد استمسك بالعروة...»

9- Reliance on tyrants (Taghuts) and everything non-Divine is breakable and perishable. The only bond that is never broken is faith in Allah. «لا انفصام لها»

10- Faith in Allah and connection with the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah) are eternal. «لاانفصام لها» But the tyrants will disavow their followers on the Day of Judgment.

11- Faith in Allah and disbelief in the Taghut must be genuine, not hypocritical. For Allah knows and hears. «واللّه سمیع علیم»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ وَلِىُّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يُخْرِجُهُم مِّنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَوْلِيَآؤُهُمُ ٱلطَّـٰغُوتُ يُخْرِجُونَهُم مِّنَ ٱلنُّورِ إِلَى ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ ۗ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۲٥۷)
Allah is the wali of the faithful: He brings them out of darkness into light. As for the faithless, their awliya are the fake deities, who drive them out of light into darkness. They shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they will remain in it [forever]. (257)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that He is the Owner of everything: «له مافى السموات و...» (His is what is in the heavens and...). And in this Ayah, we read that His relationship with the believers is a special, protective (Wilayi) relationship. «ولىّ الذین آمنوا» (Allah is the Protector of those who believe).

A glimpse of the characteristics of those who have accepted the Wilayah (Guardianship) of Allah

  • Whoever accepts the Wilayah of Allah, his actions take on a Divine color. «صبغة اللّه» (The Dye of Allah) [723]

  • He chooses a Divine leader for himself. «ان اللّه قد بعث لكم طالوت ملكاً» (Indeed, Allah has appointed Saul [Talut] as a king over you) [724]

  • His path is clear, his future is known, and he is reassured by his deeds. «یهدیم ربّهم بایمانهم» (Their Lord guides them by their faith) [725], «انّا الیه راجعون» (Indeed, to Him we will return) [726], «لانضیع اجرالمحسنین» (We do not allow to be lost the reward of the doers of good) [727]

  • In wars and hardships, he looks to the help of Allah and does not fear non-Divine powers. «فزادهم ایماناً» (It increased them in faith) [728]

  • He does not fear death and considers being killed under Divine Wilayah (Guardianship) to be felicity. Imam Husayn عليه السلام said: «انّى لا ارى الموت الاّ السعادة» (Indeed, I do not see death except as felicity/happiness). [729]

  • Loneliness in life is not bitter or unpleasant for him, because he knows he is under the supervision of Allah. «ان اللّه معنا» (Indeed, Allah is with us) [730]

  • He is not worried about spending and giving charity (Infaq), because he entrusts his wealth to his Protector (Wali). «یقرض اللّه قرضاً حسناً» (Who will loan to Allah a goodly loan) [731]

  • Negative propaganda is ineffective against him, because his heart is set on the certain Divine promises: «والعاقبة للمتّقین» (And the [good] outcome is for the righteous) [732]

  • Everything besides Allah is small in his view. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «عظم الخالق فى أنفسهم فصغر مادونه فى أعینهم...» (The Creator is magnified in their souls, so everything besides Him is belittled in their eyes...). [733]

  • He is not confused by multiple laws and commands, because he has accepted only the law of Allah and thinks only of that. And if hundreds of paths to income open up for him, he chooses only the path that Allah has specified, based on the Divine standard. «ومن لم یحكم بماانزل اللّه» (And whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed...) [734]

733) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 193.

734) Ma'idah, 44 and 45.

Messages

1- Believers have one Guardian (Protector), which is Allah, while disbelievers have multiple guardians, who are the Taghuts (tyrants/false deities), and accepting one Guardian is easier. [735] Regarding the believers, He says: «اللّه ولىّ» (Allah is the Protector), but regarding the disbelievers, He says: «اولیائهم الطاغوت» (Their protectors are the Taghut).

2- The previous Ayah stated: «قد تبیّن الرّشد من الغى» (The right course has become distinct from error). This Ayah presents an example of the right course (Rushd) and error (Ghayy), where the Wilayah of Allah is the right course, and the Wilayah of the Taghut is deviation. «اللّه ولىّ... اولیائهم الطاغوت»

3- The path of truth is one, but the paths of deviation are multiple. The Quran uses the word Nur (Light) for the path of truth, but refers to the crooked and deviant paths as Zulumat (darknesses). «النور، الظلمات»

4- The path of truth is light, and in light, there is the possibility of movement, growth, hope, and tranquility. «النور»

5- The believer does not reach a dead end. «یخرجهم من الظلمات» (He brings them out from the darknesses)

6- The Taghuts have the power to maneuver in the atmosphere of disbelief (Kufr) and polytheism (Shirk). «الذین كفروا اولیائهم الطاغوت» (As for those who disbelieve, their protectors are the Taghut)

7- Whoever does not come under the Wilayah of Allah will inevitably find the Taghuts gaining Wilayah over him. «اللّه ولىّ... اولیائهم الطاغوت»

8- Any Wilayah other than the Divine Wilayah is the Wilayah of the Taghut. «اللّه ولىّ... اولیائهم الطاغوت»

9- Attention to the consequence of accepting the Taghut leads man toward accepting the truth. «اولئك اصحاب النّار» (Those are the companions of the Fire)

735) Joseph عليه السلام also used this meaning to argue when inviting the disbelievers: «ءأربابٌ متفرّقون خیرٌ أم اللّه الواحد القهّار» Are scattered, multiple lords better, or Allah, the One, the Irresistible? (Yusuf, 39).

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِى حَآجَّ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ فِى رَبِّهِۦٓ أَنْ ءَاتَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُلْكَ إِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ رَبِّىَ ٱلَّذِى يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ قَالَ أَنَا۠ أُحْىِۦ وَأُمِيتُ ۖ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَأْتِى بِٱلشَّمْسِ مِنَ ٱلْمَشْرِقِ فَأْتِ بِهَا مِنَ ٱلْمَغْرِبِ فَبُهِتَ ٱلَّذِى كَفَرَ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۲٥۸)
Have you not regarded him who argued with Abraham about his Lord, [only] because Allah had given him kingdom? When Abraham said, ‘My Lord is He who gives life and brings death,’ he replied, ‘I [too] give life and bring death.’ Abraham said, ‘Indeed Allah brings the sun from the east; now you bring it from the west.’ Thereat the faithless one was dumbfounded. And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (258)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that Allah is the Guardian (Wali) of the believers and brings them out of darknesses into the light. This Ayah and the subsequent Ayahs present examples of Allah's guardianship and the act of bringing people out of darknesses into the light.

It is mentioned in historical narrations that Nimrod was one of the kings of Babylon and held a powerful rule. He debated and argued with Prophet Abraham concerning Allah. When Prophet Abraham said, "My Lord is He Who gives life and causes death," he replied, "I also give life and cause death." He then ordered two prisoners to be brought forward; he freed one and ordered the other to be killed. When Abraham, عليه السلام, observed this sophistry and fallacy—seeing what interpretation this person had of giving life and causing death, and how he intended to mislead the thoughts of others—he said: "Allah causes the sun to rise from the East. If you claim that you rule over the universe and that everything is under your control and power, then bring the sun out from the West!" At that moment, Nimrod was dumbfounded and had no response but silence.

Messages

1- Whatever anyone possesses is from Him. Even disbelievers benefit from His blessings in this world, but they misuse them. «اتاه اللّه»

2- Power and governance can become a cause for arrogance and pride. «اتاه اللّه الملك... أنا اُحى و امیت...»

3- Attention to public opinion and people's judgment is necessary. Although the phenomenon of life is one of the ways to know God, and Nimrod's answer was incorrect, those debates and arguments did not satisfy public opinion. «ربّى الذى یحیى و یمیت»

4- Fallacy and sophistry are the tricks of the people of falsehood. «انا اُحى و اُمیت»

5- Truth, if presented logically and with evidence, is victorious and dominant over falsehood. «فأت بها من المغرب فبهت الذى كفر»

6- In debates, use innate, rational, and universally accepted arguments, so that even the stubborn opponent is subdued and dumbfounded. «فبهت الذى كفر»

7- The disbeliever is an oppressor (Zalim), because he has oppressed himself and the status of humanity. «لا یهدى القوم الظالمین»

Ayah

أَوْ كَٱلَّذِى مَرَّ عَلَىٰ قَرْيَةٍ وَهِىَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا قَالَ أَنَّىٰ يُحْىِۦ هَـٰذِهِ ٱللَّهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۖ فَأَمَاتَهُ ٱللَّهُ مِا۟ئَةَ عَامٍ ثُمَّ بَعَثَهُۥ ۖ قَالَ كَمْ لَبِثْتَ ۖ قَالَ لَبِثْتُ يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ ۖ قَالَ بَل لَّبِثْتَ مِا۟ئَةَ عَامٍ فَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰ طَعَامِكَ وَشَرَابِكَ لَمْ يَتَسَنَّهْ ۖ وَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰ حِمَارِكَ وَلِنَجْعَلَكَ ءَايَةً لِّلنَّاسِ ۖ وَٱنظُرْ إِلَى ٱلْعِظَامِ كَيْفَ نُنشِزُهَا ثُمَّ نَكْسُوهَا لَحْمًا ۚ فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُۥ قَالَ أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۲٥۹)
Or him who came upon a township as it lay fallen on its trellises. He said, ‘How will Allah revive this after its death?!’ So Allah made him die for a hundred years, then He resurrected him. He said, ‘How long did you remain?’ Said he, ‘I have remained a day or part of a day.’ He said, ‘No, you have remained a hundred years. Now look at your food and drink which have not rotted! Then look at your ass! [This was done] that We may make you a sign for mankind. And now look at its bones, how We raise them up and clothe them with flesh!’ When it became evident to him, he said, ‘I know that Allah has power over all things.’ (259)

Key Points

The previous Ayah recounted the dialogue between Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) and Nimrod, where they discussed Monotheism (Tawhid), and guidance was achieved through argumentation (reasoning). Ibrahim was victorious through his argument. The second example of emerging from darkness into light is in this Ayah, where the discussion revolves around the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and guidance is achieved through demonstration, in a tangible and practical manner. It is mentioned in the exegeses (Tafasir) and some narrations (Riwayat) that the name of this person was 'Uzayr. We read in Tafsir al-Mizan that this person must have been a Prophet, because Allah spoke to him.

Messages

1- One must look upon the ruined civilizations of the past with an eye of admonition (Ibrah) and learn new lessons. «مرّ على قریة»

2- Even if you know, still increase your knowledge through experience and questioning. «انّى یحى هذه اللّه»

3- Dying for a hundred years is appropriate for understanding a single point. «فاماته اللّه مأة عام»

4- The passage of a long time does not impair the power of Allah. «فاماته اللّه مأة عام ثم بعثه»

5- The return (Raj'ah) and the revival of the dead in this very world, before the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), is acceptable. «أماته... بعثه»

6- Demonstration is one of the best ways to convey religious knowledge (Ma'arif). «اُنظر الى طعامك و شرابك... و انظر الى... و انظر الى العظام...»

7- By Divine Will, a strong bone disintegrates, but food that spoils quickly remains healthy for a hundred years. «لم یتسنّه»

8- The demonstration of the Lord's power is for the guidance and instruction of people, not for entertainment or even mere display of power. «ولنجعلك آیة للناس»

9- Allah has brought a scene of the Resurrection into this world. «فاماته... ثم بعثه... وانظر الى العظام كیف ننشزها»

10- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical (corporeal). If the Resurrection were merely spiritual, there would be no mention of bones. «وانظر الى العظام»

11- A handful is a sample of the whole (a drop in the ocean). Allah has displayed parts of His power concerning the Resurrection in this world. «فلمّا تبیّن له قال أعلم انّ اللّه على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ رَبِّ أَرِنِى كَيْفَ تُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ ۖ قَالَ أَوَلَمْ تُؤْمِن ۖ قَالَ بَلَىٰ وَلَـٰكِن لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِى ۖ قَالَ فَخُذْ أَرْبَعَةً مِّنَ ٱلطَّيْرِ فَصُرْهُنَّ إِلَيْكَ ثُمَّ ٱجْعَلْ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ جَبَلٍ مِّنْهُنَّ جُزْءًا ثُمَّ ٱدْعُهُنَّ يَأْتِينَكَ سَعْيًا ۚ وَٱعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۲٦٠)
And when Abraham said, ‘My Lord! Show me how You revive the dead,’ He said, ‘Do you not believe?’ He said, ‘Yes indeed, but in order that my heart may be at rest.’ He said, ‘Catch four of the birds. Then cut them into pieces, and place a part of them on every mountain, then call them; they will come to you hastening. And know that Allah is all-mighty and all-wise.’ (260)

Key Points

It is narrated only from the singular great man of history after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, meaning Ali عليه السلام, that He said: «لو كشف الغطاء ما ازددت یقیناً» (If the veils were removed, my certainty would not increase.) [738] But all other people like to observe their hearings and even their beliefs visually. Just as everyone knows that sugar is extracted from beets, but they like to observe how it is done. It is mentioned in the commentaries (Tafasir): Prophet Abraham was passing by a sea and saw a carcass lying by the shore, part of it in the water and part on the land, and birds, marine, desert, and aerial animals were feeding on it from every direction. The Prophet said to himself: If this happens to a human being and the particles of the human body are scattered among other creatures, how will they be gathered and brought back to life all at once on the Day of Judgment? Therefore, he requested Allah to observe the manner in which the dead are brought back to life. Allah, through this demonstration, guided Abraham toward the light of certainty and assurance.

738) Ghurar al-Hikam and Tafsir Ruh al-Bayan, Vol. 1, p. 416.

Messages

1- Bringing the dead back to life is among the attributes of Allah’s Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ربّ ارنى كیف تحیى»

2- For deep learning, the use of demonstrations and sensory observations is necessary. «أرنى»

3- Revelation (Kashf) and witnessing (Shuhud) are only for those who have traversed stages of knowledge, faith, and reasoning. Abraham’s request «أرنى» is answered, not everyone else’s.

4- We should strive to elevate our faith and certainty until we reach the threshold of assurance (Itmi’nan). Research and curiosity are valuable. «لِیطمئنّ قلبى»

5- Faith (Iman) has stages and degrees. «لیطمئن قلبى»

6- The heart (Qalb) is the center of tranquility. «لیطمئنّ قلبى»

7- The friends of Allah (Awliya) possess the power to influence creation, which is called inherent guardianship (Wilayah Takwini). «ثمّ ادعهنّ یأتینك سعیاً»

8- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical, and on the Day of Judgment, the soul will return to these very particles of the body. «یأتینك سعیاً»

Ayah

مَّثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ كَمَثَلِ حَبَّةٍ أَنۢبَتَتْ سَبْعَ سَنَابِلَ فِى كُلِّ سُنۢبُلَةٍ مِّا۟ئَةُ حَبَّةٍ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يُضَـٰعِفُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ وَٰسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲٦۱)
The parable of those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah is that of a grain which grows seven ears, in every ear a hundred grains. Allah enhances severalfold whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-knowing. (261)

Key Points

The command for spending (infaq) and the prohibition of extravagance (israf) and wastefulness (tabdhir) is the best way to resolve class differences. Just as the emergence and spread of usury (riba) is the groundwork and creator of [social] classes. Therefore, in the Qur'an, the verses mandating spending (infaq) and prohibiting usury (riba) are mentioned side by side. [739] Not every seed, in every soil, will sprout seven ears, each containing a hundred grains. Rather, the seed must be healthy, the soil fertile, the time appropriate, and the protection complete. Similarly, spending lawful wealth (mal halal), with the intention of seeking closeness [to Allah] (qurbat), without obligation (minnah), and in a good manner, will yield all those effects.

739) Tafsir al-Mizan, Vol. 2, p. 406.

Messages

1- Spending (infaq) does not only have an otherworldly reward; rather, it causes the existential growth and perfection of the person himself. «مثل الذین ینفقون»

2- The Qur'an praises those for whom spending is a constant practice. «ینفقون» (the imperfect verb) indicates continuity.

3- Spending is valuable when it is in the way of Allah. In Islam, economics is not separate from ethics. «فى سبیل اللّه»

4- The use of natural examples never becomes outdated and is understandable to all people, regardless of their age or condition. [This includes] the comparison of wealth to a seed and the comparison of the effects of spending to multiple ears full of grain. «كمثل حبّة انبتت سبع سنابل»

5- Encouragement and the promise of reward are the strongest factors for action. A seven-hundred-fold reward. «واللّه یضاعف»

6- The favor of Allah has no limits. «واللّه یضاعف ... واللّه واسعٌ علیم»

7- If spending wealth has the potential for growth up to seven hundred times; «كمثل حبّة انبتت سبع سنابل فى كلّ سنبلة مأة حبّة» then what must be the reckoning for those who spend their lives in the way of Allah?! «واللّه واسعٌ علیم»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ لَا يُتْبِعُونَ مَآ أَنفَقُوا۟ مَنًّا وَلَآ أَذًى ۙ لَّهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (۲٦۲)
Those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah and then do not follow up what they have spent with reproaches and affronts, they shall have their reward near their Lord, and they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (262)

Key Points

The goal of Islam in spending (in charity/Infaq) is not merely to fill the stomachs of the poor. Rather, this beautiful act must be performed by good people, through a good method, and accompanied by good intentions. Sometimes a person takes a step for Allah and begins an action virtuously, but due to side effects such as arrogance, reminding (of the favor/mann), expectation, or similar issues, the value of the deed is destroyed. With reminding (of the favor/mann), the effect of the charity is lost, because the goal of charity is the purification of the soul from stinginess, but the result of reminding is the contamination of the soul. The one who reminds (of the favor) is either seeking to aggrandize himself and humiliate others, or he wishes to attract the attention of the people, which in either case is far from sincerity (Ikhlas). Has Allah reminded us of the favor for all the blessings He has given us, so that we should remind His creation of the favor when giving a small portion of it? A Hadith is narrated in Majma' al-Bayan that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will not look upon those who remind (of their favors) with a gaze of mercy.

Messages

1- A good start is not enough; a good completion is also a condition. «ینفقون... ثمّ لایُتبِعون... منّاً»

2- Islam safeguards the dignity of the deprived and the poor, and considers the crushing of the poor's dignity through reminding (of the favor) to be the cause of nullifying the deed. «لا یتبعون... منّاً و لا اذىً»

3- Human actions influence one another; meaning one action can neutralize another. Charity is meant to cure poverty, but reminding (of the favor) turns it into a source of pain for the poor. «ینفقون... لا یتبعون... منّاً و لا اذىً»

4- Allah has guaranteed the future of the one who spends in charity. «لا خوف علیهم ولا هم یحزنون»

5- The one who spends in charity without reminding (of the favor) or causing harm, and only for the sake of Allah, enjoys divine tranquility. «ینفقون اموالهم فى سبیل اللّه... لا خوف علیهم ولا هم یحزنون»

Ayah

۞ قَوْلٌ مَّعْرُوفٌ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِّن صَدَقَةٍ يَتْبَعُهَآ أَذًى ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَنِىٌّ حَلِيمٌ (۲٦۳)
An honourable reply [in response to the needy] and forgiving [their annoyance] is better than a charity followed by affront. Allah is all-sufficient, most forbearing. (263)

Key Points

The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله said: If a beggar comes to you, act according to one of these two methods; »بذل یسیر او ردّ جمیل« [740] either give him something within your capacity, or refuse him in a proper manner. He also said: If you cannot attend to people with wealth, attend to them with good character. [741]

740) Tafsir Qurtubi and Namuneh, regarding the Ayah. 741) Tafsir Kashif, Vol. 1, p. 272.

Messages

1- The honor and dignity of the poor person is more valuable than feeding the stomach and preserving the person of the poor. «قول معروف... خیر من صدقة»

2- Guide the poor person, with affection and compassion, toward a useful job that secures his livelihood. [742] «قول معروف»

3- Spending (in charity) must be accompanied by good character. «قول معروف... خیر من صدقة»

4- Pleasant conversation with the poor person brings him comfort and is a factor in human growth, whereas charity accompanied by reproach (or reminding of the favor) achieves neither. «قول معروف... خیر من صدقة»

5- If a poor person says something inappropriate due to pressure and hardship, forgive him. «مغفرة»

6- Observing the common practice (or custom) of society is necessary in speech and action. «قول معروف»

7- Allah responds to the harm inflicted upon the poor, but not hastily. «حلیم»

742) Tafsir Partovi az Quran, regarding the Ayah.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تُبْطِلُوا۟ صَدَقَـٰتِكُم بِٱلْمَنِّ وَٱلْأَذَىٰ كَٱلَّذِى يُنفِقُ مَالَهُۥ رِئَآءَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَا يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۖ فَمَثَلُهُۥ كَمَثَلِ صَفْوَانٍ عَلَيْهِ تُرَابٌ فَأَصَابَهُۥ وَابِلٌ فَتَرَكَهُۥ صَلْدًا ۖ لَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ مِّمَّا كَسَبُوا۟ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۲٦٤)
O you who have faith! Do not render your charities void by reproaches and affronts, like those who spend their wealth to be seen by people and have no faith in Allah and the Last Day. Their parable is that of a rock covered with soil: a downpour strikes it, leaving it bare. They have no power over anything of what they have earned, and Allah does not guide the faithless lot. (264)

Key Points

This Ayah also contains a similitude and reveals the inner reality of individuals who spend (in charity) for the purpose of showing off (riya') and ostentation. The outward appearance of their deed is soft, like soil, but its inner reality is hard, like rock, which lacks the capacity for penetration (or growth). Due to the hardness and severity of their heavy hearts, they gain no benefit from their spending.

Messages

  1. Reminding (of the favor done) and harming the poor destroys the reward of spending (infaq) and charities (sadaqat). «لا تبطلوا»
  2. Showing off (riya') is a sign of a lack of true faith in the Lord and the Day of Judgment. «ینفق ماله رئاء النّاس و لایؤمن باللّه»
  3. The spending (infaq) itself is not important; the motive and spirit of the spender are important. «رئاء الناس»
  4. The deeds of the person who reminds (of the favor), the show-off (riya'kar), and the disbeliever (kafir) are ruined. The word «فمثله» (So his likeness) is applicable to all three groups.
  5. The show-off (riya'kar) is ultimately disgraced, and events reveal the realities. «فتركه صلداً»
  6. The show-off is not only deprived of the reward in the Hereafter but has also failed to achieve spiritual growth. «لا یقدرون على شیى ء»
  7. The person who reminds (of the favor) and the show-off fall within the scope of disbelief (kufr) and are subject to threat. «لایهدى القوم الكافرین»

Ayah

وَمَثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَٰلَهُمُ ٱبْتِغَآءَ مَرْضَاتِ ٱللَّهِ وَتَثْبِيتًا مِّنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ كَمَثَلِ جَنَّةٍۭ بِرَبْوَةٍ أَصَابَهَا وَابِلٌ فَـَٔاتَتْ أُكُلَهَا ضِعْفَيْنِ فَإِن لَّمْ يُصِبْهَا وَابِلٌ فَطَلٌّ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۲٦٥)
The parable of those who spend their wealth seeking Allah’s pleasure and to confirm themselves [in their faith], is that of a garden on a hillside: the downpour strikes it, whereupon it brings forth its fruit twofold; and if it is not a downpour that strikes it, then a shower, and Allah watches what you do. (265)

Messages

1- If the goal is to attain the pleasure of Allah, along with spiritual growth and perfection, then actions become fruitful. «ابتغاء مرضات اللّه... فاتت اُكلها ضعفین»

2- Sincerity (Ikhlas) is not easily attained; one must actively seek it. «ابتغاء مرضاة اللّه»

3- Sincere actions are like a farm situated on high ground, protected from the destruction of floods. «بربوة اصابها»

4- If the goal is Allah, we are not deprived of splendor and beauty. The sincere (Mukhliṣīn) are more beloved in society than the ostentatious (Riyākārān). «جنّة بربوة» Sincere charity is like a farm on a hillside or high ground, which all people see and enjoy.

5- More important than resources is the utilization of those resources. Whether the rain is heavy or light is not important; what is important is that the ground can absorb it. «فان لم یصبها وابل فطلّ»

Ayah

أَيَوَدُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَن تَكُونَ لَهُۥ جَنَّةٌ مِّن نَّخِيلٍ وَأَعْنَابٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ لَهُۥ فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ وَأَصَابَهُ ٱلْكِبَرُ وَلَهُۥ ذُرِّيَّةٌ ضُعَفَآءُ فَأَصَابَهَآ إِعْصَارٌ فِيهِ نَارٌ فَٱحْتَرَقَتْ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۲٦٦)
Would any of you like to have a garden of palm trees and vines, with streams running in it, with all kinds of fruit for him therein, and old age were to strike him while he has weakly offspring; whereupon a fiery hurricane were to hit it, whereat it lies burnt? Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you so that you may reflect. (266)

Key Points

When a person becomes old and dependent (has a family to support), his capacity for production is lost, but his needs and consumption become intense. Now, if his source of income is also destroyed, he is dragged into humiliation. On the Day of Judgment, man, on the one hand, lacks the ability to perform righteous deeds, and on the other hand, his need is intense. In this state, if his righteous deeds have been erased by showing favor (mann) and hypocrisy (riya), what humiliation will he reach? Dates and grapes are among the most beneficial fruits, and their names have been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever says "لا اله الاّ اللّه" (There is no god but Allah), a tree is planted for him in Paradise because of that deed, and the same applies to whoever says "اللّه اكبر" (Allah is the Greatest). A person said to the Prophet: Then our trees in Paradise are numerous! He replied: Yes, provided that you do not send fire after it and burn it. [743]

743) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 186.

Messages

1- Put yourself in the place of others so that you may understand the issues well. «أیَودّ احدكم ...»

2- We should use natural examples that are not affected by time, place, era, or generation. «جنّة من نخیل و...»

3- Showing favor (mann) and causing harm after spending (in charity) is like a scorching wind upon a lush garden, and a fire upon a verdant orchard. «فاحترقت»

4- Growth is gradual, but destruction and the nullification of deeds happen in an instant. «فاصابها اعصار فیه نار فاحترقت»

5- The examples in the Quran are for reflection. «لعلّكم تتفكّرون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَنفِقُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا كَسَبْتُمْ وَمِمَّآ أَخْرَجْنَا لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَلَا تَيَمَّمُوا۟ ٱلْخَبِيثَ مِنْهُ تُنفِقُونَ وَلَسْتُم بِـَٔاخِذِيهِ إِلَّآ أَن تُغْمِضُوا۟ فِيهِ ۚ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌ (۲٦۷)
O you who have faith! Spend of the good things you have earned [through trade and the like] and of what We bring forth for you from the earth, and do not be of the mind to give the bad part of it, for you yourselves would not take it, unless you ignore. And know that Allah is all-sufficient, all-laudable. (267)

Key Points

The previous verses discussed the conditions of the one who gives charity (infaq), and this verse explains the conditions of the things that are given in charity to the deprived. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this verse: Some Muslims had unlawful incomes, such as usury (riba), which they spent on the poor. This verse forbade them from doing so. [744]

744) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 255 and Rahnamā.

Messages

1- The goal of infaq (charity) is liberation from stinginess, not liberation from worthless and undesirable items. «انفقوا من طیّبات ما كسبتم»

2- In infaq, there is no difference between cash or goods. «ما كسبتم... ما اخرجنا»

3- Giving away easily acquired wealth is not important; rather, giving away from one's hard-earned income is valuable. «ما كسبتم»

4- In infaq, the dignity of the deprived must be preserved. «ولا تیمّموا الخبیث»

5- Not only should you refrain from giving impure and base things in charity, but you should not even think about doing so. «ولاتیمّموا»

6- Human conscience is the best criterion for distinguishing between desirable and undesirable. Whatever you would like to be given to you in charity, it is worthy for you to give; and if you do not like it, it is not worthy of infaq. «لستم باخذیه الا ان تغمضوا»

7- Every wealthy person should consider the possibility of becoming poor; perhaps one day your giving hand will become a receiving hand. «آخذیه»

8- Accepting low-quality goods is not a sign of the poor's satisfaction, but rather a sign of their desperation. «تغمضوا» means that you too might accept something with tolerance and difficulty, but this is not a sign of your satisfaction.

9- The effect of infaq returns to you, otherwise Allah is Self-Sufficient and Free of Need. «ان اللّه غنى حمید»

10- Divine Self-Sufficiency (Ghinā) is accompanied by perfection and praiseworthiness. «غنى حمید»

Ayah

ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ يَعِدُكُمُ ٱلْفَقْرَ وَيَأْمُرُكُم بِٱلْفَحْشَآءِ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ يَعِدُكُم مَّغْفِرَةً مِّنْهُ وَفَضْلًا ۗ وَٱللَّهُ وَٰسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲٦۸)
Satan frightens you of poverty and prompts you to [commit] indecent acts. But Allah promises you His forgiveness and bounty, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-knowing. (268)

Key Points

At the time of spending (in charity/Infaq), Satan approaches man and suggests that if you spend today, you will become poor tomorrow. It is better to save your wealth so that you can spend it during old age, sickness, and so on. These are satanic suggestions and promises. Our need for Divine forgiveness on the Day of Judgment is far greater than our need for wealth in the future of this world. Beware lest we exchange Divine forgiveness for the illusion of comfort in life. Because: 1. Firstly: Perhaps I will not grow old or become needy, but my need on the Day of Judgment is certain. 2. Secondly: Perhaps the wealth I save today for tomorrow will not remain, but the charity (Infaq) of today will certainly remain for the Day of Judgment. 3. Thirdly: Perhaps I will not be able to benefit from the wealth saved today during my old age tomorrow, but benefiting from the charity of today is certain in the Hereafter. 4. Fourthly: Man's benefit from his savings during old age is temporary, but man's benefit from charity in the Hereafter is eternal. [745]

745) Tafsir Fakhr Razi, concerning the Ayah.

Messages

  1. Satan prevents man from spending desirable wealth (in charity). «انفقوا من طیّبات... الشیطان یعدكم الفقر...»

  2. Your stinginess leads the poor toward corruption and indecency (Fahsha). «یعدكم الفقر ویأمركم بالفحشاء»

  3. Man is free to choose the path of Allah or the path of Satan. «الشیطان یعدكم... واللّه یعدكم»

  4. Associates who prevent man from spending (in charity) and frighten him about the future, yet encourage sins, are devils (satans). «الشیطان یعدكم»

  5. There is a relationship between poverty and indecency (Fahsha). Many sins occur due to the fear of poverty. «یعدكم الفقر ویأمركم بالفحشاء»

  6. Not spending (in charity) or spending from undesirable wealth are examples of ugliness and indecency (Fahsha). «لا تیمموا الخبیث... الشیطان یعدكم...»

  7. Spending (in charity) increases wealth and is a cause for the forgiveness of sins. «انفقوا... اللّه یعدكم مغفرة منه وفضلاً»

  8. Fight against the obstacles to charity and satanic thoughts. In the face of the fear of poverty, remember the grace and favor of Allah. «مغفرة منه و فضلاً...»

  9. Every thought or illusion that brings you narrow-mindedness, fear, and negative consequences is satanic, and every thought or inspiration that brings you movement, vitality, and broad-mindedness is Divine. «واللّه یعدكم مغفرة منه و فضلا...»

Ayah

يُؤْتِى ٱلْحِكْمَةَ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَمَن يُؤْتَ ٱلْحِكْمَةَ فَقَدْ أُوتِىَ خَيْرًا كَثِيرًا ۗ وَمَا يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّآ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۲٦۹)
He grants wisdom to whomever He wishes, and he, who is given wisdom, is certainly given an abundant good, and none takes admonition except those who possess intellect. (269)

Key Points

Al-bāb (ألباب) is the plural of lubb (لُبّ), meaning 'the core' or 'the brain/marrow.' Not all humans are called Ulū al-Albāb (those of understanding); rather, this term is reserved only for the wise who utilize their intellect and find the path to their true life and felicity. Wisdom (ḥikmah) has been defined as knowledge, recognition of secrets, awareness of realities, and attainment of the Truth. Allah grants this to certain individuals due to their purity, piety, and effort, so that they may distinguish Satanic whispers from Divine inspirations, the pit from the path, and mere slogans from true consciousness. And this is the great bounty (khayr kathīr). In narrations, we read: Wisdom is knowledge and deep understanding (tafaqqūh) in religion. Wisdom is obedience to Allah, recognition of the Imam, and avoidance of major sins. [746]

746) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 287.

Messages

1- Although wealth and property are a bounty (khayr), the great bounty (khayr kathīr) is having insight and the power of discernment. Those who spend from that bounty will attain the great bounty. «انفقوا...یؤتى الحكمة»

2- Satan promises poverty, while Allah promises forgiveness and grace. However, discerning and choosing between these two paths requires wisdom. «یؤتى الحكمة»

3- The entire world is a meager provision, [747] but wisdom is the great bounty. If all material resources are sacrificed to attain wisdom and correct insight, it is worthwhile. «فقد اوتى خیراً كثیرا»

4- Wisdom is a key gift and the mother of all bounties. Whoever possesses it will possess many things. «ومن یؤتى الحكمة فقد اوتى خیراً كثیراً»

5- Not everyone realizes the value of wisdom. «مایذّكّر الاّ اولوا الالباب» (None remember except those of understanding.) Materialists rely on wealth, statistics, and material calculations, measuring profit and loss based on physical assets. However, the far-sighted wise and the thoughtful pathfinders calculate from a different dimension.

747) متاع الدنیا قلیل (An-Nisā', 77).

Ayah

وَمَآ أَنفَقْتُم مِّن نَّفَقَةٍ أَوْ نَذَرْتُم مِّن نَّذْرٍ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُهُۥ ۗ وَمَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مِنْ أَنصَارٍ (۲۷٠)
Allah indeed knows whatever charity you may give, or vows that you may vow, and the wrongdoers have no helpers. (270)

Messages

1- Since Allah is aware of our spending (infaq), we should spend the best of our wealth with the noblest intentions. «ما انفقتم... فان اللّه یعلمه»

2- Infaq is not exclusive to the wealthy or large amounts of money; spend even small amounts. «من نفقة» Sometimes a single yellow autumn leaf becomes a ship for several ants in a pond.

3- Belief that Allah knows is the best encouragement for spending (infaq) and fulfilling commitments and vows (nadhr). «فان اللّه یعلمه»

4- Encouragement and warning, side by side, are factors for growth. The phrase «فانّ اللّه یعلمه» is an encouragement, and the phrase «ما للظالمین من انصار» is a warning.

5- Fulfilling a vow (nadhr) is obligatory, and abandoning it is oppression (zulm). «نذرتم... ما للظالمین»

6- Oppression against oneself has a path to repentance (tawbah) and expiation (kaffarah), but in the oppression committed against the deprived due to neglecting spending (infaq), the oppressor has no helper. «وما للظالمین من انصار»

7- Intercession (shafa'ah) does not include the stingy. [748] «وما للظالمین من انصار»

8- If the motive for stinginess is seeking aid and help from wealth and people, know that whenever Allah's wrath comes, neither wealth nor people will be effective. «ماللظالمین من انصار»

9- The crime must be commensurate with the penalty. Whoever is not a helper to others through spending (infaq) in this world, others will not be his helpers in the Hereafter. «ما للظالمین من انصار»

748) Just as it is stated in Surah Al-Muddaththir, Ayah 48: »ولم نك نطعم المسكین... فما تنفعهم شفاعة الشافعین« The intercession of the intercessors will not benefit them (those who do not spend/give charity).

Ayah

إِن تُبْدُوا۟ ٱلصَّدَقَـٰتِ فَنِعِمَّا هِىَ ۖ وَإِن تُخْفُوهَا وَتُؤْتُوهَا ٱلْفُقَرَآءَ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۚ وَيُكَفِّرُ عَنكُم مِّن سَيِّـَٔاتِكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۲۷۱)
If you disclose your charities, that is well, but if you hide them and give them to the poor, that is better for you, and it will atone for some of your misdeeds, and Allah is well aware of what you do. (271)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Pay the obligatory Zakat openly, but give the recommended (Mustahabb) Zakat secretly. [749] Perhaps the reason for this is that obligatory Zakat is a public, common duty, and far removed from ostentation (Riya). Although the Ayah concerns financial aid, in Islamic culture, any good deed is called Sadaqah (charity). Even if we remove a stone from the path of Muslims, it is Sadaqah. Therefore, helping the deprived through knowledge, reputation (honor/influence), and intercession is also considered Sadaqah.

749) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 289.

Messages

1- Open spending (Infaq) encourages others, removes the accusation of stinginess from the person, and is a form of practical propagation. «فنعمّاهى»

2- The condition for assisting the poor is not that they must be Muslim. «فقراء»

3- Although Sadaqat (charities) and Zakat have many uses, the mention of the poor in the Ayah indicates their priority. «تؤتوها الفقراء»

4- You must go to the poor, not wait for them to come to you. «تؤتوها الفقراء» (You give it to the poor) and He did not say: «یأتكم الفقراء» (The poor come to you).

5- Secret spending is far from ostentation (Riya) and showing off, closer to sincerity (Ikhlas), and preserves the dignity of the recipient of the charity. «فهو خیر لكم»

6- Man possesses the instinct for seeking benefit and benevolence, although sometimes in practice he pursues evil. Therefore, the Divine Prophets have clarified the instances and examples of good for mankind. «فهو خیر لكم»

7- Helping the poor forgives a portion of minor sins. «یكفّر عنكم من سیئاتكم» (He will remove from you some of your evil deeds). The term Sayyi’at (سیّئات) in the Quran is used in contrast to major sins (Kabirah) and refers to minor sins.

8- What [750] is important is the knowledge of Allah, not the information or awareness of people. «واللّه بما تعملون خبیر» (And Allah is well-acquainted with what you do).

750) ان تجتنبوا كبائر ما تنهون... نكفّر عنكم سیئاتكم...« (If you avoid the major sins which you are forbidden... We will remove from you your minor sins...) Surah An-Nisa, 31.

Ayah

۞ لَّيْسَ عَلَيْكَ هُدَىٰهُمْ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا۟ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَلِأَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ وَمَا تُنفِقُونَ إِلَّا ٱبْتِغَآءَ وَجْهِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا۟ مِنْ خَيْرٍ يُوَفَّ إِلَيْكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تُظْلَمُونَ (۲۷۲)
It is not up to you to guide them; rather, it is Allah who guides whomever He wishes. Whatever wealth you spend, it is for your own benefit, as you do not spend but to seek Allah’s pleasure, and whatever wealth you spend will be repaid to you in full and you will not be wronged. (272)

Key Points

In the Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Kabir by Fakhr Razi, occasions of revelation (Shān Nuzūl) are mentioned for this Ayah, the implication of which is that Muslims were doubtful about giving charity (Infaq) to poor polytheists and non-Muslims. When they asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, this Ayah was revealed. Furthermore, in the practical conduct (Sīrah) of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and his Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, many examples of helping non-Muslims are observed, showing that they were compassionate and benevolent even towards those who insulted them or usurped their rights.

Messages

1- Do not use economic pressure or the withholding of charity (Infaq) as a means to make disbelievers embrace faith. «لیس علیك هداهم... وما تنفقوا من خیر فلانفسكم»

2- Guidance is a divine grace (Tawfīq) that only encompasses prepared hearts. «یهدى من یشاء» (He guides whom He wills.)

3- Attending to the deprived is a human duty. Therefore, give charity (Infaq) even to non-Muslims. «وما تنفقوا من خیر فلانفسكم»

4- Islam is a school of humanism and does not approve of poverty and deprivation, even for non-Muslims. «وما تنفقوا من خیر فلانفسكم»

5- The benefit of charity (Infaq) returns to yourselves and revives the spirit of generosity within you. Charity prevents the emergence of class differences and social explosions, and it creates affection in society. Spending is not a cause of your deprivation, but rather a source of your immunity/protection. «فلانفسكم»

6- Do not spend except for the sake of Allah. Because all worldly benefits and effects will disappear sooner or later, but if the spending is for Allah, you will benefit from its blessings forever. «الا ابتغاء وجه اللّه»

7- Wealth and possessions are good (Khayr). «ما تنفقوا من خیر»

8- Be generous and open-handed in spending. Because whatever you spend, you will receive back in full, without reduction. «یوفّ الیكم»

9- Benefiting in the Hereafter occurs when the sole purpose of spending is the pleasure of Allah. «الاّ ابتغاء وجه اللّه... یوفّ الیكم»

Ayah

لِلْفُقَرَآءِ ٱلَّذِينَ أُحْصِرُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ ضَرْبًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يَحْسَبُهُمُ ٱلْجَاهِلُ أَغْنِيَآءَ مِنَ ٱلتَّعَفُّفِ تَعْرِفُهُم بِسِيمَـٰهُمْ لَا يَسْـَٔلُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ إِلْحَافًا ۗ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا۟ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِهِۦ عَلِيمٌ (۲۷۳)
[The charities are] for the poor who are straitened in the way of Allah, not capable of moving about in the land [for trade]. The unaware suppose them to be well-off because of their reserve. You recognize them by their mark; they do not ask the people importunately. And whatever wealth you may spend, Allah indeed knows it. (273)

Key Points

In some exegeses, such as Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr al-Razi, Majma' al-Bayan, and Qurtubi, it is stated that this Ayah was revealed concerning the Ashab al-Suffah (People of the Platform). The Ashab al-Suffah were approximately four hundred people who had migrated from Mecca to Medina. Since they had no homes, dwellings, or acquaintances in Medina, they lived beside the Prophet's Mosque on the Suffah (a large platform). These individuals were always ready for Jihad in the way of Allah.

Messages

  1. The poor have a right in the wealth of the rich. «للفقراء»

  2. Necessary attention must be paid to the Mujahidin (strugglers), those who are besieged in prisons and places of exile due to political activities aimed at fighting tyrants (Taghuts), the defenseless migrants, and all those who have become poor in the way of Allah and lack the opportunity to strive to earn a living. «اُحصروا فى سبیل اللّه لایستطیعون ضرباً...»

  3. Those who can secure their livelihood by traveling or migrating to another place should not wait for the charity (infaq) of the people. «لایستطیعون ضرباً فى الارض»

  4. Chaste, virtuous, and honorable poor people are praised by Allah. «یحسبهم الجاهل اغنیاء»

  5. Anonymous and respectable poor people are a priority. «تعرفهم بسیماهم»

  6. Even if a necessity arises, do not ask people with insistence. «لایسئلون الناس الحافاً» Begging is disliked.

  7. It is not fair that one group sacrifices themselves and all their existence and becomes besieged, while another group is stingy even in giving charity (infaq) to them. [751] «للفقراء الّذین اُحصروا»

751) As soon as I finished reviewing this Ayah, before I had even put down my pen, a person entered and handed me a newspaper containing an article about the characteristics of the wise (Simay-e Farzanegan). I found it highly compatible with this Ayah, so I included it in the footnote. Ayatollah Mirza Ali Aqa Qadhi قدس سره, the master of Irfan (Gnosticism) of Allamah Tabataba'i قدس سره, was seen in Najaf al-Ashraf buying wilted and low-quality cabbages from a greengrocer. They asked him, "Why are you doing this?" He replied, "This shopkeeper is a poor man, and I want to help him, but I wish for his honor (reputation) to be preserved and for him not to become accustomed to receiving unearned wealth! Therefore, I buy these cabbages from him so that he may achieve some prosperity, and it makes little difference to me whether I consume fresh or old cabbage."

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَٰلَهُم بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً فَلَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (۲۷٤)
Those who give their wealth by night and day, secretly and openly, they shall have their reward near their Lord, and they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (274)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the Tafsirs of Safi, Majma' al-Bayan, Qurtubi, and Kabir (by Fakhr al-Razi) that this Ayah was revealed concerning the status of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. When that noble personage only had four dirhams saved, He spent one dirham during the day, one dirham during the night, the third secretly, and the fourth openly in the way of Allah. Of course, the promise of this Ayah includes all those who act in this manner. Such individuals neither fear poverty and destitution, because they believe in the promises of Allah and rely upon Him, nor do they grieve because of their spending, since they pay attention to Divine satisfaction and the otherworldly effects of charity.
