Perhaps the reason that layl (night) precedes nahar (day) or sirr (secret) precedes alaniyah (openness) is that hidden acts of charity in the depth of the night hold greater value. [752] This Ayah serves as a summary of the past fourteen verses which discussed charity (infaq). This is a topic that has not been discussed in such detail anywhere else in the Quran. The effects of charity are clear to everyone, including: the adjustment of wealth and reduction of class differences, the creation of affection, the flourishing of the spirit of generosity, and, above all, proximity to Allah. Charity, besides the spending of wealth and property, also includes the giving of knowledge, reputation (honor), and position. The word infaq (charity), linguistically means filling a pit, and technically, it means filling and removing financial deficiencies. It is necessary to remember that Islam's attention to the issue of charity does not mean fostering beggars or promoting mendicancy. This is because, in many narrations, those who ask others for help without being in need are condemned, and on the other hand, the best type of charity is introduced as providing tools for work instead of giving money.

752) Tafsir al-Maraghi.

Messages

1- Possessing the spirit of charity and generosity is important, not just giving charity once or twice out of pity. The verb «ینفقون» (they spend) is in the present tense, indicating continuity.

2- The lack of specification regarding the Divine reward is a sign of its vastness. «اجرهم» (their reward)

3- Divine promises are the best encouragement for humans in good deeds. «فلهم اجرهم» (then for them is their reward)

4- Tranquility and security are among the blessings of charity. «لاخوف علیهم ولا هم یحزنون» (there shall be no fear upon them, nor shall they grieve)

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ ٱلرِّبَوٰا۟ لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ ٱلَّذِى يَتَخَبَّطُهُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ مِنَ ٱلْمَسِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا ٱلْبَيْعُ مِثْلُ ٱلرِّبَوٰا۟ ۗ وَأَحَلَّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْبَيْعَ وَحَرَّمَ ٱلرِّبَوٰا۟ ۚ فَمَن جَآءَهُۥ مَوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ فَٱنتَهَىٰ فَلَهُۥ مَا سَلَفَ وَأَمْرُهُۥٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۲۷٥)
Those who exact usury will not stand but like one deranged by the Devil’s touch. That is because they say, ‘Trade is just like usury.’ While Allah has allowed trade and forbidden usury. Whoever relinquishes [usury] on receiving advice from his Lord shall keep [the gains of] what is past, and his matter will rest with Allah. As for those who resume, they shall be the inmates of the Fire and they shall remain in it [forever]. (275)

Key Points

Riba, linguistically, means excess and increase. In Islamic Sharia, it means taking an excess amount in a loan or a sale. The subject of Riba is either money or goods. Sometimes one lends money and takes back more than what was given, which is Riba in loans, and sometimes one gives goods and receives a greater quantity of the same goods, which also becomes Riba in certain cases. The usurer is likened to someone whom Satan has afflicted with madness. Khabaṭ (خَبط) means falling and rising and lack of balance during movement. The usurer will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment like the insane, because in this world, his method caused the disruption of societal balance. The worship of wealth has blinded the eye of his reason, and by his action, he incites such class differences and hatred that poverty and resentment lead to explosion and destabilize the very principle of ownership. For these individuals, it is as if Riba is the principle and buying and selling is secondary; therefore, they say: Sale and transaction are just like Riba and there is no difference between them. Criticism of usury, taking Riba, and giving Riba has been raised since the beginning of Islam. In Surah Ar-Rum, which was revealed in Mecca, He says: «و ما اوتیتم من ربا لیربوا فى اموال النّاس فلایربوا عند اللّه» [753] Meaning, what you give with the intention of Riba so that it may increase for you in the wealth of the people, know that it does not increase with Allah. Then in Surah Aal-Imran, it was prohibited with the command «لاتأكلوا الرّبا» [754], and the greatest criticism of usury is found in these very verses. Furthermore, the Ayah «وأخذهم الرّبا وقد نُهوا عنه» [755] reminds us that Riba was also forbidden in the Jewish religion, just as this prohibition is mentioned in the Torah [756]. The verses related to Riba followed the verses of Infaq (charity) to present the two aspects of good and evil that arise through wealth and property. Infaq means giving without compensation, and Riba means taking without compensation. Every good effect that Infaq has is countered by the negative effects that Riba creates in society. For this reason, the Quran says: «یمحق اللّه الرّبا و یُربِى الصّدقات» (Allah destroys Riba but increases Sadaqat (charity)). The threats mentioned in the Quran for taking Riba and accepting the sovereignty of Taghut (tyranny) have not been mentioned for murder, oppression, drinking wine, gambling, or adultery. [757] The prohibition of Riba is definitive among all Islamic sects and is considered one of the major sins. When Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was informed that a certain person was a usurer, he said: If I had the power, I would strike his neck. [758] Similarly, when Hadrat Ali عليه السلام encountered a usurer, he asked him to repent. When he repented, he released him and subsequently said: The usurer must be made to repent from his action, just as one is made to repent from polytheism (Shirk). It is narrated from Imam Baqir عليه السلام that he said: The most foul of earnings is usury. [759] And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Whenever Allah intends the destruction of a village, usury appears in that village. [760] And Allah has cursed the usurer, his agent, the witness, and the scribe of Riba. [761] We read in a Hadith: Usurers will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment like the insane. [762] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام considers the reason for the repetition of the verses on Riba to be the preparation of the wealthy for good deeds and charities, and He says: Since Riba is forbidden on the one hand, and Kanz (hoarding) and accumulating wealth in a stagnant manner is also forbidden on the other hand, then no choice remains for the wealthy except Infaq or useful productive work. [763] Similarly, regarding the reason for the prohibition of Riba, it has been said: Usury prevents the flow of money into production and public welfare projects, and instead of effort, thought, and labor, only the profit of money is utilized; hence, Riba has been prohibited. It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that he said: «لو كان الرّبا حلالا لترك الناس التجارات» If Riba were lawful, people would abandon trade and business. [764] And it is also narrated from Imam Ridha عليه السلام that he said: If Riba becomes widespread, the path of lending (Qardh) will be closed. [765] Furthermore, since the probability of falling into the trap of Riba in economic matters is high, we read in a Hadith: «من اتّجر بغیر فقه فقد ارتطم فى الربا» [766] Whoever engages in trade without knowledge (of jurisprudence) will fall into Riba. Effects of Riba: Taking extra money without performing useful work or participating in production is a form of oppression and injustice, which leads to the emergence of enmity and hardness of heart. The borrower, due to escalating debts, sometimes goes bankrupt and is forced to accept various forms of humiliation and servitude. [767] Riba disrupts the balance of society and causes the division of society into two poles: the arrogant (Mustakbir) and the oppressed (Mustadh'af). [768] Considering these destructive effects, Riba has been prohibited not only in the Islamic Sharia but in all divine religions. But some try to justify Riba under various pretexts and look for loopholes. Creating religious loopholes (Kolah Shar'i), similar to the trick used by the Jews to catch fish on the Sabbath, the story of which was mentioned in previous verses, is nothing more than a game, and the Quran has criticized such games. Riba has its destructive effects, even if human societies have accepted it in their economic systems. The reason for the progress of Western societies is their attention to science and industry, not that usury has caused their advancement.

753) Ar-Rum, 39. 754) Aal-Imran, 130. 755) An-Nisa, 161. 756) Torah, Exodus, Chapter 23, sentence 25, and Leviticus, Chapter 25. 757) Tafsir Al-Mizan, regarding the Ayah. 758) Wasail, Vol. 12, p. 429. 759) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 147. 760) Kanz al-Ummal, Vol. 4, p. 104. 761) Wasail, Vol. 12, p. 430. 762) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 2, p. 102. 763) Wasail, Vol. 12, p. 423. 764) Wasail, Vol. 12, p. 424 and Bihar, Vol. 103, p. 119. 765) Al-Hayat, Vol. 4, p. 334. 766) Nahj al-Balaghah, Qisar 447. 767) Tafsir Maraghi, regarding the Ayah. 768) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

  1. Usurers lack spiritual and psychological balance and also remove society from economic balance. «كما یقوم الذى یتخبّطه الشیطان»
  2. Liking lawful sale (Bay') to forbidden Riba is a sign of their intellectual imbalance. «یتخبّطه الشیطان... بانّهم قالوا انّما البیع مثل الربا»
  3. Satan has the power to influence the inner self of man. «یتخبّطه الشیطان من المَس»
  4. Justifying sin opens the way for committing sin. «انّماالبیع مثل الربا»
  5. There is no responsibility until the obligation (Taklif) is conveyed. «فمن جائه موعظة من ربّه»
  6. Divine rulings are aimed at advising and educating people. «جائه موعظة من ربّه»
  7. Today's law does not cover the past actions of individuals. «فله ما سَلف»
  8. The sins of the ignorant are overlooked, but never those of the knowing, malicious, and persistent. «ومَن عاد فاولئك اصحاب النّار»

Ayah

يَمْحَقُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلرِّبَوٰا۟ وَيُرْبِى ٱلصَّدَقَـٰتِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ أَثِيمٍ (۲۷٦)
Allah brings usury to naught, but He makes charities flourish. Allah does not like any sinful ingrate. (276)

Key Points

The word 'mahq' (محق) means gradual decrease, and muhāq (مُحاق) refers to the moon whose light has diminished so much in its final nights that it is no longer visible. In contrast, 'ribā' (ربا) means gradual growth. This Ayah reminds us that although the usurer takes ribā from others with the intention of accumulating wealth, Allah removes the blessing (barakah) and the positive effects that the increase in wealth should have from that ribā. It is not necessary for the usurious wealth itself to be destroyed; rather, the goals intended by the increase in wealth are destroyed. In the usurious system, there is no happiness, affection, or security. How many wealthy individuals gain no comfort, peace, or popularity from their capital? However, in a system where infāq (spending for Allah's sake), sadaqah (charity), and Qardh al-Hasanah (interest-free loans) are common, that society benefits from many blessings. In that system, the poor are not desperate, and the rich are not preoccupied with excessive accumulation. The deprived do not contemplate revenge and theft, and the wealthy are not worried about guarding and protecting their assets. The society will enjoy a relative balance accompanied by affection, mercy, understanding, and security. It is stated in the Tafsīr Kabīr of Fakhr al-Rāzī: When the usurer erases human emotions and justice within himself, he and his wealth become subject to the curses of the poor, and resentment, revenge, and plots of theft constantly threaten him. This is an example of the destruction mentioned in the Ayah.

Messages

1- Do not be dazzled by the apparent growth of wealth; the economic system based on ribā is heading towards destruction. «یمحق اللّه الربا»

2- The destruction of usurious wealth is one of the Divine traditions. «یمحق اللّه الربا» The imperfect verb (present/future tense) indicates continuity.

3- The prevalence of sadaqah and zakāt is the cause of economic growth and stability. «یربى الصدقات»

4- The usurer is deprived of Divine mercy and affection. «انّ اللّه لایحبّ كلّ كفّار اثیم»

5- The usurer is extremely ungrateful and sinful. By taking ribā, he makes himself liable to the people, makes his life unlawful (haram), invalidates his acts of worship, and allows greed, avarice, and hardness of heart to dominate him. «كفّار اثیم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتَوُا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (۲۷۷)
Indeed those who have faith, do righteous deeds, maintain the prayer and give the zakat, they shall have their reward near their Lord, and they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (277)

Key Points

| This Ayah, in contrast to the usurers who are "sinful disbelievers" (كفّار اثیم), portrays the characteristics of the believers who perform righteous deeds, establish prayer, and pay Zakat. This indicates that the foundation for eradicating usury from society lies in focusing on faith, righteous deeds, and reviving prayer and Zakat. People are four groups: 1- A group that believes and performs righteous deeds; these are the "believers" (مؤمنان). 2- A group that neither believes nor performs worthy deeds; these are the "disbelievers" (كافران). 3- A group that believes but lacks righteous deeds; these are the "transgressors" (فاسقان). 4- A group that does not believe but expresses faith and performs good deeds outwardly; these are the "hypocrites" (منافقان). If usurers have cut themselves off from Allah and people, conversely, there are those who possess faith and righteous deeds, who are connected to Allah through prayer, and who are linked to people by paying Zakat.

Messages

1- Islam pays attention to economic and social issues alongside devotional and individual matters. Prayer and Zakat are mentioned together. «اقاموا الصلوة و اتوا الزكاة»

2- Mentioning prayer and Zakat after righteous deeds indicates that the status of these two is distinct among worthy actions. «عملوا الصالحات و اقاموا الصلوة و اتوا الزكاة»

3- Encouraging the virtuous following the threat against the wicked is an educational principle. «لهم اجرهم عند ربّهم»

4- The intelligent person is one who, in their calculations, does not look only at the current assets they possess, but pays attention to the future and the reserves that are with Allah. «عند ربّهم»

5- The Lord has a special regard for believers who are devoted to righteous deeds, prayer, and Zakat. The word «ربّهم» (their Lord) points to His special grace.

6- Divine promises are the motivation for righteous deeds. «لهم اجرهم عند ربّهم»

7- True security and tranquility are found under the shade of faith, righteous deeds, and connection with Allah and people. «لاخوف علیهم و لا هم یحزنون»

8- The factors leading to tranquility are faith, righteous deeds, prayer, and Zakat. «امنوا و عملوا الصالحات و اقاموا الصلوة و اتوا الزكوة... لاخوف علیهم ولا هم یحزنون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَذَرُوا۟ مَا بَقِىَ مِنَ ٱلرِّبَوٰٓا۟ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۲۷۸)
O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and abandon [all claims to] what remains of usury, should you be faithful. (278)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the harms of usury (ribā) were mentioned, showing that usury throws the individual and society out of balance and causes turmoil; «یتخبّطه الشیطان» (Satan confounds him). And it became clear that usury is, in reality, a decrease, not an increase; «یمحق الربا» (Allah destroys usury). Now, He explicitly states the prohibition of usury. «ذروا مابقى من الربا» (Give up what remains of usury).

It is narrated in the commentaries Majma‘ al-Bayān, Al-Mīzān, and Al-Marāghī that when the verse prohibiting usury was revealed, some of the Companions, such as Khālid ibn Walīd, Abbās, and Uthmān, had outstanding debts owed to them from usury. They sought clarification from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله regarding their claims, and the above verse was revealed. After the revelation of this verse, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Abbās, My uncle, also does not have the right to demand usury, and before anyone else, My relatives must abandon usury. Just as He said during a sermon: «وكلّ ربا فى الجاهلیة موضوع تحت قدمىّ هاتین و اوّل ربا اَضع ربا العباس» (And all usury established during the Age of Ignorance is placed beneath these two feet of Mine, and the first usury I abolish is the usury of Abbās.) I cast down all usury established during the Age of Ignorance, and foremost among them is the usury belonging to Abbās. [769]

769) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an.

Messages

1- Usury was among the customs of the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah), and early Muslims were also tainted by it. «یا ایها الذین آمنوا... ذروا»

2- Piety (Taqwā) is a stage higher than faith (Īmān). «یا ایها الذین آمنوا اتّقوا اللّه»

3- The usurer does not own the interest, and Islam does not recognize usurious profit. «ذروا مابقى من الربا»

4- The prerequisite of faith and piety is waiving the claim to unlawful wealth. «ان كنتم مؤمنین»

Ayah

فَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلُوا۟ فَأْذَنُوا۟ بِحَرْبٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ ۖ وَإِن تُبْتُمْ فَلَكُمْ رُءُوسُ أَمْوَٰلِكُمْ لَا تَظْلِمُونَ وَلَا تُظْلَمُونَ (۲۷۹)
And if you do not, then be informed of a war from Allah and His apostle. And if you repent, then you will have your principal, neither harming others, nor suffering harm. (279)

Key Points

In Islam, neither usury (riba), exploitation, nor unilateral confiscation of people's wealth is permitted. In some systems, ownership is abolished and all wealth is taken from its owners, while in others, exploitation, profiteering, and usury are freely allowed in any form.

Messages

1- The usurer is at war with Allah. He must know that in this war, he is on one side, and the Almighty God (Allah) is on the other. «فأذنوا بحرب من اللّه»

2- Usury is a major sin, because it is a declaration of war against Allah. «بحرب من اللّه»

3- The usurer should not imagine that he is dealing only with the deprived people; rather, Allah has risen in support of the deprived and defends their rights. «بحرب من اللّه»

4- Since the usurer is at war with Allah, fighting usury is among the duties of the Islamic government. «فأذنوا بحرب من اللّه»

5- The path of repentance is open even for those who wage war against Allah. «بحرب من اللّه... وان تبتم»

6- The usurer owns only the principal amount, not the interest. «فلكم رؤس اموالكم»

7- In order to save the deprived, do not disregard the principle of people's ownership; private ownership is accepted in Islamic economics. «فلكم رؤس اموالكم»

8- Both accepting domination and exercising domination are condemned; neither inflict injustice nor suffer injustice. «لاتَظلِمون ولا تُظلمون»

9- Vengeance is forbidden. Even the repentant usurer must not be wronged. «فلكم رؤس اموالكم لا تَظلمون ولا تُظلمون»

10- Repentant individuals must not be wronged because of their past transgressions. Reproaching those who were previously wrongdoers is forbidden. «فان تبتم... لاتَظلمون ولاتُظلمون»

11- Establishing economic justice is among the duties of the Islamic government. «لاتَظلِمون ولا تُظلمون»

Ayah

وَإِن كَانَ ذُو عُسْرَةٍ فَنَظِرَةٌ إِلَىٰ مَيْسَرَةٍ ۚ وَأَن تَصَدَّقُوا۟ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۲۸٠)
If [the debtor] is in straits, let there be a respite until the time of ease; and if you remit [the debt] as charity, it will be better for you, should you know. (280)

Key Points

Although this Ayah recommends that the debtor be given respite, the debtor must not misuse this provision. Therefore, if he delays paying his debt without a valid excuse, he is sinful. It is stated in a Hadith: For those who do not pay their debt without an excuse, the sin of a thief is recorded. Just as for those who grant respite, a reward like the reward of martyrs is registered. For every day that the debtor is given respite, the creditor receives the reward of giving that same amount in charity (Sadaqah). [770] Forgiving the poor debtor is better for you, because: A: Perhaps this situation will arise for you tomorrow as well. B: Wealth is forgotten, but forgiving the poor is never forgotten. C: Gaining the satisfaction of the deprived heart and the pleasure of the Creator is far better than earning income. It is stated in the rulings of Fiqh (jurisprudence): Imprisoning a debtor who has a valid excuse is forbidden, and in cases where the debtor truly possesses no wealth to pay his debt, it is the duty of the Islamic government to pay his debt.

770) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- Islam is the protector of the oppressed (Mustad'afin). «و ان كان ذوعسرة» 2- Not only should you not take usury (Riba), but you should also be lenient in collecting the principal amount and the original capital. «فنظرة الى میسرة» 3- The basis for setting the repayment schedule is the debtor's ability. «فنظرة الى میسرة» 4- There is no objection to compelling a debtor who has the ability to pay. «ان كان ذو عسرة فنظرة» 5- Forgiving a poor debtor is charity (Sadaqah). «وان تصدقوا خیر لكم» 6- The legal and economic system of Islam is linked to its moral system. Although reclaiming a loan is a right, granting respite and forgiving the debtor must be observed. «فنظرة... تصدقوا» 7- Human limitations and his love for wealth prevent man from realizing the truth of values and perfections. «ان كنتم تعلمون»

Ayah

وَٱتَّقُوا۟ يَوْمًا تُرْجَعُونَ فِيهِ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ ثُمَّ تُوَفَّىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (۲۸۱)
And beware of a day in which you will be brought back to Allah. Then every soul shall be recompensed fully for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged. (281)

Key Points

This Ayah serves as a summary and a general warning at the conclusion of the verses concerning usury (Riba). It is mentioned in exegeses (Tafasir) such as Kashshaf, Al-Mizan, Majma' al-Bayan, Kabir Fakhr Razi, and Burhan that this Ayah was the last verse revealed to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, but it was placed here by the command of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is an important day. The tanwin (nunation) on the word «یوماً» (Yawman) indicates the greatness of that Day.

2- Taking usury (Riba) and attaining worldly gains is simple and fleeting; what is eternal and lasting is the Day of Judgment. «واتقوا یوماً»

3- Piety (Taqwa) and the remembrance of the Day of Judgment are the strongest factors for staying away from usury (Riba) and other forbidden acts. «و اتقوا یوماً ترجعون فیه»

4- The guarantee for the implementation of the Islamic economic system is the faith and piety of the people. «واتقوا یوماً»

5- In the transactions of this world, there is the possibility of deficiency and loss, but in dealing with Allah, there is absolutely no deficiency or loss. «توفّى كلّ نفس ما كسبت»

6- The criterion for recompense and reward is action, not mere wishes. «ماكسبت»

7- Do not worry about spending (in charity/Infaq) and abandoning your usurious profits; nothing will be diminished from your good deeds. «توفّى كلّ نفس... لا یظلمون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا تَدَايَنتُم بِدَيْنٍ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى فَٱكْتُبُوهُ ۚ وَلْيَكْتُب بَّيْنَكُمْ كَاتِبٌۢ بِٱلْعَدْلِ ۚ وَلَا يَأْبَ كَاتِبٌ أَن يَكْتُبَ كَمَا عَلَّمَهُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ فَلْيَكْتُبْ وَلْيُمْلِلِ ٱلَّذِى عَلَيْهِ ٱلْحَقُّ وَلْيَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ رَبَّهُۥ وَلَا يَبْخَسْ مِنْهُ شَيْـًٔا ۚ فَإِن كَانَ ٱلَّذِى عَلَيْهِ ٱلْحَقُّ سَفِيهًا أَوْ ضَعِيفًا أَوْ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَن يُمِلَّ هُوَ فَلْيُمْلِلْ وَلِيُّهُۥ بِٱلْعَدْلِ ۚ وَٱسْتَشْهِدُوا۟ شَهِيدَيْنِ مِن رِّجَالِكُمْ ۖ فَإِن لَّمْ يَكُونَا رَجُلَيْنِ فَرَجُلٌ وَٱمْرَأَتَانِ مِمَّن تَرْضَوْنَ مِنَ ٱلشُّهَدَآءِ أَن تَضِلَّ إِحْدَىٰهُمَا فَتُذَكِّرَ إِحْدَىٰهُمَا ٱلْأُخْرَىٰ ۚ وَلَا يَأْبَ ٱلشُّهَدَآءُ إِذَا مَا دُعُوا۟ ۚ وَلَا تَسْـَٔمُوٓا۟ أَن تَكْتُبُوهُ صَغِيرًا أَوْ كَبِيرًا إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلِهِۦ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَقْسَطُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَأَقْوَمُ لِلشَّهَـٰدَةِ وَأَدْنَىٰٓ أَلَّا تَرْتَابُوٓا۟ ۖ إِلَّآ أَن تَكُونَ تِجَـٰرَةً حَاضِرَةً تُدِيرُونَهَا بَيْنَكُمْ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَلَّا تَكْتُبُوهَا ۗ وَأَشْهِدُوٓا۟ إِذَا تَبَايَعْتُمْ ۚ وَلَا يُضَآرَّ كَاتِبٌ وَلَا شَهِيدٌ ۚ وَإِن تَفْعَلُوا۟ فَإِنَّهُۥ فُسُوقٌۢ بِكُمْ ۗ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۖ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۲۸۲)
O you who have faith! When you contract a loan for a specified term, write it down. Let a writer write with honesty between you, and let not the writer refuse to write as Allah has taught him. So let him write, and let the one who incurs the debt dictate, and let him be wary of Allah, his Lord, and not diminish anything from it. But if the debtor be feeble-minded, or weak, or incapable of dictating himself, then let his guardian dictate with honesty, and take as witness two witnesses from your men, and if there are not two men, then a man and two women—from those whom you approve as witnesses—so that if one of the two defaults the other will remind her. The witnesses must not refuse when they are called, and do not consider it wearisome to write it down, whether it be a big or small sum, [as a loan lent] until its term. That is more just with Allah and more upright in respect to testimony, and the likeliest way to avoid doubt, unless it is an on-the-spot deal you transact between yourselves, in which case there is no sin upon you not to write it. Take witnesses when you make a deal, and let no harm be done to the writer or witness, and if you did that, it would be sinful of you. Be wary of Allah and Allah will teach you, and Allah has knowledge of all things. (282)

Key Points

This Ayah is the greatest Ayah in the Qur'an, dealing with legal matters and the method of drafting commercial documents. This Ayah is a sign of the meticulousness and comprehensiveness of Islam, which introduced the most precise legal issues during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) and among a backward people. The word "tadayantum" (تداینتم), derived from "dayn" (دَین) meaning debt, includes any non-cash transaction, loan, or forward purchase (salaf). It is stated in a Hadith: If someone does not take a document or a witness in a transaction, and his property is exposed to loss, whatever he prays for, Allah will not answer, and He will say: Why did you not act upon My recommendations?! [771] If precision in safeguarding individual rights is necessary, safeguarding the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal) is necessary by many degrees more! Drafting a document is useful even in cash transactions, but its necessity is not as great as in credit transactions. Because in transactions with a fixed term, the parties to the transaction or the witnesses might pass away or forget, it is therefore necessary that it also be written down. From the command to write, it becomes clear that drafted documents accompanied by the testimony of a just witness are admissible as evidence. This Ayah implies that the Islamic society must support one another in safeguarding rights. This is because every transaction requires a scribe and several witnesses. At a time when, according to historians, there were no more than 17 literate people in the entire Arabian Peninsula, all this talk of writing is a sign of Islam's attention to knowledge and the preservation of rights.

771) Kanz al-Daqā'iq, Vol. 2, p. 467.

Messages

1- Faith is the foundation and prerequisite for acting upon divine rulings, and the address, "Yā ayyuhal-ladhīna āmanū" (یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا), is an encouragement to implement the rulings.

2- The duration of the debt must be clear. "ilā ajalim-musammā" (الى اجل مسمّى)

3- Debts must be written down to preserve mutual trust and optimism, ensure the spiritual peace of both parties, and prevent forgetfulness, denial, and suspicion. "faktubūh" (فاكتبوه)

4- To ensure confidence and protect the contract from potential interference by either party, the document must be written in the presence of both parties and by a third person. "wa liyaktub baynakum kātib" (ولیكتب بینكم كاتب)

5- The contract writer must consider justice and write the exact reality. "bil-'adl" (بالعدل). The condition for selecting the document writer is having justice in the pen.

6- In gratitude for the literacy and knowledge that Allah has granted us, we should facilitate matters for others. "wa lā ya'ba kātibun an yaktuba kamā 'allamahullāh" (ولا یأب كاتب ان یكتب كما علّمه اللّه)

7- People of every art and profession have a specific responsibility; whoever holds a pen must write for the people. "wa lā ya'ba kātibun an yaktub" (ولا یأب كاتب ان یكتب)

8- The debtor, who bears the obligation, must dictate the text of the contract for the scribe to write, not what the creditor claims. "wa liyumlilil-ladhī 'alayhil-ḥaqq" (و لیملِل الّذى علیه الحقّ)

9- A person's confession against himself is acceptable. "falyumlilil-ladhī 'alayhil-ḥaqq" (فلیملل الذى علیه الحقّ)

10- The debtor, when dictating the contract, must keep Allah in mind, omit nothing, and state all the details of the debt. "wa liyattaqillāha rabbah" (ولیتّق اللّه ربّه)

11- Legislation must be drafted in such a way that the right of the incapable is not lost. If the debtor is foolish (safīh), weak, incapable, or mute, his guardian (walī) must dictate the text of the contract for the scribe to write. "fa in kānal-ladhī 'alayhil-ḥaqqu safīhan aw ḍa'īfan... falyumlil walīyyuh" (فان كان الذى علیه الحقّ سفیهاً او ضعیفاً... فلیملل ولیّه)

12- The foolish and the incapable require a guardian (walī) in social interactions. "falyumlil walīyyuh" (فلیملل ولیّه)

13- Attention to the rights of the deprived class and weak segments of society is necessary on the part of officials and guardians. "falyumlil" (فلیملل) (the imperative form) indicates the necessity of this action.

14- Men are given precedence over women in becoming witnesses and giving testimony. "shahīdayni mir-rijālikum" (شهیدین من رجالكم)

15- Witnesses must be just, trustworthy, and acceptable to both parties. "mimman tarḍawn" (ممن ترضون)

16- Each man can be a witness individually, but if the witnesses are one man and two women, the two women must give testimony together, so that if one errs or makes a mistake, the second one reminds her. "fa tudhakkira iḥdāhumā" (فتذكّر احدیهما)

Two women instead of one man in testimony is either due to their delicate and emotional nature, or because women usually do not have an active presence in market and economic matters, and their familiarity with the methods of transactions is less.

17- If you are invited to be a witness, do not refuse, although becoming a witness without invitation is not obligatory. "wa lā ya'bash-shuhadā'u idhā mā du'ū" (ولا یأب الشهداء اذا ما دعوا)

18- The amount of the contract is not important; preserving trust and the rights of the people (Ḥaqq an-Nās) is important. "wa lā tas'amū an taktubūhu ṣaghīran aw kabīran" (ولاتسأموا ان تكتبوه صغیراً او كبیراً)

19- Accurate and just registration of the document has three benefits: A: It guarantees the implementation of justice. "aqsaṭ" (اقسط) B: It causes the witnesses to be more courageous in giving testimony. "aqwam" (اقوَم) C: It prevents the creation of suspicion in society. "adnā allā tartābū" (ادنى ألاّ ترتابوا)

20- If the benefits and secrets of the divine rulings are explained to the people, accepting them becomes easier. "dhālikum aqsaṭ..." (ذلكم اقسط...)

21- Delaying today to write the contract is better than the strife (fitnah) and disagreement of tomorrow. "adnā allā tartābū" (ادنى الا ترتابوا)

22- In cash transactions, there is no necessity for writing. "illā an takūna tijāratan ḥāḍirah" (اِلاّ أن تكون تجارة حاضرة)

23- In cash transactions, although writing is not necessary, take witnesses. "fa laysa 'alaykum junāḥun allā taktubūhā wa ash-hidū idhā tabāya'tum" (فلیس علیكم جناح ألاّ تكتبوها واشهدوا اذا تبایعتم)

24- The scribe and the witness are safe and should not be harmed or harassed by either party for writing the truth or speaking the truth. "lā yuḍārra kātibun wa lā shahīd" (لایضارّ كاتب ولاشهید)

25- If giving testimony or writing the contract requires time or enduring hardship, the witness and the scribe must be compensated financially in some way, otherwise both will suffer loss. "wa lā yuḍārra kātibun wa lā shahīd" (ولا یضارّ كاتب ولا شهید)

26- The scribe and the witness do not have the right to draft the document in a way that causes harm to either party. "lā yuḍārra kātibun wa lā shahīd" (لایضارّ كاتب ولاشهید)

27- Any tampering with the document, by whomever it may be, is transgression (fisq) and sin. "fa innahu fusūq" (فانّه فسوق)

28- The scribe and the witness are the guardians of people's rights, and breaking these boundaries is equivalent to crossing the limit and transgression (fisq). "fa innahu fusūq" (فانّه فسوق)

29- A pure and pious heart, like a mirror, receives knowledge and truths. "wattaqullāh wa yu'allimukumullāh" (واتقواللّه و یعلّمكم اللّه)

30- Allah knows all your material and spiritual needs, and based on that, He explains the rulings and laws to you. The Legislator must possess deep and extensive knowledge. "wallāhu bikulli shay'in 'alīm" (واللّه بكل شى ء علیم)

Ayah

۞ وَإِن كُنتُمْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ وَلَمْ تَجِدُوا۟ كَاتِبًا فَرِهَـٰنٌ مَّقْبُوضَةٌ ۖ فَإِنْ أَمِنَ بَعْضُكُم بَعْضًا فَلْيُؤَدِّ ٱلَّذِى ٱؤْتُمِنَ أَمَـٰنَتَهُۥ وَلْيَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ رَبَّهُۥ ۗ وَلَا تَكْتُمُوا۟ ٱلشَّهَـٰدَةَ ۚ وَمَن يَكْتُمْهَا فَإِنَّهُۥٓ ءَاثِمٌ قَلْبُهُۥ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ (۲۸۳)
If you are on a journey and cannot find a writer, then a retained pledge [shall suffice]. And if one of you entrusts [an asset] to another, let him who is trusted deliver his trust, and let him be wary of Allah, his Lord. And do not conceal your testimony; anyone who conceals it, his heart will indeed be sinful, and Allah knows well what you do. (283)

Key Points

Whenever there is no scribe available to draw up the debt document, a pledge (collateral) or security can be taken from the debtor, whether we are traveling or not. Therefore, the phrase «ان كنتم على سفر» (if you are on a journey) is mentioned because people usually encounter shortages (or difficulties) during travels. This is similar to the phrase «اذا حضر احدكم الموت الوصیة» (when death approaches one of you, [make] the will), meaning: make a will when death comes to you. Although we know that making a will is not exclusive to the time of death, its last opportunity is the moment death arrives.

Messages

1- The condition for collateral is that it must be taken possession of and be under control. «مقبوضة» [772]

2- Being thorough does not hurt; take collateral against the debt. «فرهان مقبوضة»

3- The status of individuals makes a difference in rulings. A pledge (collateral) is required for the unknown, but not for trustworthy individuals. «فرهان مقبوضة فان أمن...»

4- Do not allow trust and confidence to be removed from transactions. «فان امن بعضكم بعضاً فلیؤدّ الذى اؤتمن» The philosophy behind taking a pledge is assurance; if that assurance exists without it, taking a pledge is unnecessary.

5- Individuals aware of people's rights are responsible toward the oppressed. «ولاتكتموا الشهادة»

6- Where stating the truth is necessary, silence is forbidden (haram). «ومن یكتمها فانّه اثمٌ قلبه»

7- Inner deviation leads to outer deviation. A sinful heart causes the concealment of testimony. «ولم یكتمها فانّه آثم قلبه»

8- Belief that Allah knows all our deeds leads to piety (taqwa) and the declaration of truths. «واللّه بما تعملون علیم»

(772) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: »لا رهن الاّ مقبوضة« A pledge (rahn) is not valid without possession (qabdh). Tahdhib, Vol. 7, p. 176.

Ayah

لِّلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَإِن تُبْدُوا۟ مَا فِىٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ يُحَاسِبْكُم بِهِ ٱللَّهُ ۖ فَيَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۲۸٤)
To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Whether you disclose what is in your hearts or hide it, Allah will bring you to account for it. Then He will forgive whomever He wishes and punish whomever He wishes, and Allah has power over all things. (284)

Key Points

Allamah Tabataba'i states in Tafsir al-Mizan: The states of the human heart are of two types: Sometimes it is a khutur (passing thought), where something comes to mind involuntarily, and the person makes no decision regarding it. That khutur is not a sin because it is not within our control. However, those spiritual states which become the source of a person's bad deeds and are rooted deep within our psyche are subject to accountability. It is possible that the intent of the Ayah regarding the punishment for a person's inner states refers to sins that are inherently internal and are actions of the heart, such as disbelief (kufr) and concealing the truth (kitman al-haqq), and not cases that are merely preliminaries to external sins. Man is responsible for his spiritual dispositions, states, and motivations, and this matter is repeatedly mentioned in the Quran. Among these are:

    • «یؤاخذكم بماكسبت قلوبكم» [773] Allah holds you accountable for what your hearts have earned.
    • «فانّه اثم قلبه» [774] Indeed, the one who conceals (the truth), his heart is sinful.
    • «انّ السّمع و البصر والفؤاد كلّ اولئك كان عنه مسئولاً» [775] Indeed, the hearing, the sight, and the heart—each of those will be questioned.
    • «انّ الّذین یحبّون ان تشیع الفاحشة فى الّذین امنوا لهم عذابٌ ألیمٌ فى الدّنیا والأخرة» [776] The inner desire for the spread of sin and corruption among the believers is a cause for painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter.

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: People are punished based on their thoughts and intentions.

773) Baqarah, 225. 774) Baqarah, 283. 775) Isra, 36. 776) Nur, 19.

Messages

[777] 1- Allah is both the Absolute Owner; «للّه ما فى السموات و ما فى الارض» and the Absolute Knower; «ان تبدوا...اوتخفوه» and the Absolutely Powerful. «واللّه على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

2- What is in the jar is what flows out of it. The deeds of humans indicate their way of thinking and their inner beliefs. «تبدوا ما فى انفسكم»

3- The educator must keep people between fear and hope. «یغفر، یعذّب»

777) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 75.

Ayah

ءَامَنَ ٱلرَّسُولُ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِۦ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۚ كُلٌّ ءَامَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتِهِۦ وَكُتُبِهِۦ وَرُسُلِهِۦ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّن رُّسُلِهِۦ ۚ وَقَالُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۖ غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۲۸٥)
The Apostle and the faithful have faith in what has been sent down to him from his Lord. Each [of them] has faith in Allah, His angels, His scriptures and His apostles. [They declare,] ‘We make no distinction between any of His apostles.’ And they say, ‘We hear and obey. Our Lord, forgive us, and toward You is the return.’ (285)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the fundamentals of religion (Tawhid [Monotheism], Prophethood, Resurrection) are stated, and in the next Ayah, man's readiness to perform divine duties and the request for mercy and forgiveness from Allah are presented. Therefore, it is mentioned in a Hadith that this Ayah and the next Ayah have special value, and reciting them is like a treasure. Honoring the Prophet is necessary, and the status of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله is separate from others. Therefore, in this Ayah, the name of the Messenger of Allah and His faith came first, followed by the names of the believers and their beliefs.

Messages

1- The manifestation of the school of thought is in the leader. «آمن الرّسول بما انزل الیه»

2- Religious preachers must believe in what they invite others to. «آمن الرسول...»

3- The revelation of heavenly books is for the nurturing and training of human beings. «من ربّه»

4- Belief in all Prophets is necessary because all Prophets share one goal. «كتبه ورسله»

5- The prerequisite for receiving divine forgiveness is faith and action. «سمعنا واطعنا غفرانك ربّنا»

6- Alongside faith and action, one must still look hopefully toward divine mercy and forgiveness. «سمعنا و اطعنا غفرانك ربّنا»

7- Forgiveness is among the affairs of Lordship (Rububiyyah) and the basis for nurturing. «غفرانك ربّنا»

Ayah

لَا يُكَلِّفُ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۚ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا ٱكْتَسَبَتْ ۗ رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَآ إِن نَّسِينَآ أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَآ إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُۥ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِۦ ۖ وَٱعْفُ عَنَّا وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَٱرْحَمْنَآ ۚ أَنتَ مَوْلَىٰنَا فَٱنصُرْنَا عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۲۸٦)
Allah does not task any soul beyond its capacity. Whatever [good] it earns is to its own benefit, and whatever [evil] it incurs is to its own harm. ‘Our Lord! Take us not to task if we forget or make mistakes! Our Lord! Do not place upon us a burden as You placed on those who were before us! Our Lord! Do not lay upon us what we have no strength to bear! Excuse us, forgive us, and be merciful to us! You are our Master, so help us against the faithless lot!’ (286)

Key Points

Sometimes the source of forgetfulness is the person's own negligence, for which he is accountable. As Allah says: «كذلك أتَتك ایاتنا فنَسیتَها و كذلك الیوم تنسى» [778] Just as Our signs came to you and you forgot them, so too today you shall be forgotten. Therefore, in this Ayah, forgiveness is sought for forgetfulness alongside error. Furthermore, pardon is requested from the heavy punishments that were imposed upon previous nations due to their transgression and corruption. In this Ayah, man asks Allah for all stages of grace: The first stage is 'Afw (pardon), which is the erasure of the effects of sin and punishment. The second stage is Maghfirah (forgiveness), which is the erasure of the effects of sin from the soul, and the third stage is benefiting from the Lord's Mercy and achieving victory over the disbelievers. «لایكلّف اللّه نفساً الاّ وُسعها» In other Ayahs, He has also stated: «ما جعل علیكم فى الدین من حرج» [779] and «یرید اللّه بكم الیسر» [780] The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله also said: I have been sent with an easy and lenient religion. [781]

778) Taha, 126. 779) Hajj, 78. 780) Baqarah, 185. 781) «بعثنى بالحنیفیة السهلة السمحة» Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 22, p. 263.

Messages

1- Divine obligations are not beyond human capacity. «لا یكلّف اللّه نفساً الا وسعها» Islam is a religion of ease, not hardship.

2- Our deeds have consequences whose reflection returns to us. «لها ما كسبت وعلیها ما...»

3- Man is free and possesses agency. «لها ما كسبت»

4- Performing or abandoning Divine commands, its benefit and harm are for ourselves. «لها... علیها»

5- Those who say: «سَمِعنا واطعَنا» and are entirely just and pious, are still concerned about their errors and forgetfulness and supplicate. «ان نسینا او أخطأنا»

6- Let us look at the history of past generations and their bitter events so that we may learn lessons and seek refuge in Allah. «كما حَمَلتْه على الّذین من قبلنا»

7- The etiquette of supplication is to first acknowledge our weakness; «لا طاقة لنا» then bear witness to the greatness of Allah; «انت مولینا» and only then present our request. «واعف عنّا واغفرلنا وارحمنا»

8- The victory of Islam over disbelief is the constant desire of the believers. «وانصرناعلى القوم الكافرین»
